name: LiquidHALLMARK
qualityRationale: Record is largely accurate, current, and well-cited against peer-reviewed validation (PLoS One 2022), the LIQUIK prospective study (JCO PO 2025), and the Mayo Clinic Labs catalog. Gene architecture (80 ctDNA genes, 10 ctRNA fusion targets), MSI reporting, LoD, and sensitivity figures are corroborated by primary sources. Test confirmed still offered (Mayo Clinic Labs collaboration live as of Jan 2026). Points deducted for an internal CPT-code contradiction (0571U vs 0409U across two fields), a clearly wrong NCCN-alignment citation pointing to Luminex Corp (an unrelated company), and an unverified TAT override (7 days vs Mayo-listed 8-12 days).

defects.issue: Internal contradiction: top-level cptCodes = '0571U' but medicareCoverage.cptCode = '0409U'. Both PLA codes appear in public sources (the $2,919 reimbursement is tied to 0409U; Lucence's current patient page cites 0571U as the currently-covered code). The record carries both without reconciliation, so at least one field is stale/wrong.

Citation is 'https://www.luminexcorp.com/' — Luminex Corporation (now part of DiaSorin) is an unrelated company with no connection to Lucence or LiquidHALLMARK. This is an erroneous/copy-paste citation that does not support the NCCN-alignment claim.

Record states '7 Business Days' (vendor-verified override) but the Mayo Clinic Labs catalog lists 8-12 days. The override is documented in submissionInfo but the public-source figure conflicts; should be flagged or reconciled.

Lists L38043 ('MolDX: Plasma-Based Genomic Profiling'); some sources cite the DEX Z-code Z00V8 for the ctDNA assay. Policy/code mapping is plausible but not independently re-verified to current LCD revision.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Points to Luminex Corp, an unrelated diagnostics company; cannot substantiate the NCCN-alignment claim for a Lucence test.
Conflicts with medicareCoverage.cptCode (0409U) in the same record; the $2,919 Medicare rate in sources is associated with 0409U, raising doubt about which code is currently active for billing.
Mayo Clinic Labs catalog (the record's own primary citation for many fields) states 8-12 days; the 7-day figure rests solely on a vendor self-report.

suspectClaims.currentValue: First Asian company to secure Medicare coverage for liquid biopsy in US (Feb 2023 for NSCLC, expanded 2024). CPT code 0571U.
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Marketing-style superlative ('first Asian company') is unverifiable and the embedded CPT (0571U) again conflicts with the 0409U used elsewhere in the record.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 0571U (current covered PLA code per Lucence; 0409U is the predecessor/alternate PLA code associated with the $2,919 MolDX rate)
Add HBV/EBV viral oncogenic signature detection (panel detects HBV and EBV, relevant to liver and nasopharyngeal cancer) alongside SNVs, indels, CNAs, fusions, MSI
Per validation study: SNV/indels in 77 genes, CNAs in 18 genes, structural rearrangements/fusions in 10 genes, plus MSI and HBV/EBV detection; ~58.8 kb genomic coverage

summary: LiquidHALLMARK (Lucence) is a still-offered ctDNA+ctRNA liquid CGP assay (80 DNA genes, 10 RNA fusion targets, MSI), accurately and well-cited overall, but the record carries a self-contradictory CPT code (0571U vs 0409U), a clearly wrong NCCN citation pointing to unrelated company Luminex, and a vendor-self-reported TAT that conflicts with the Mayo Clinic Labs catalog.

name: Cancerguard
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and unusually well-evidenced. All headline facts verify against the Exact Sciences launch press release and vendor test-info page: $689 self-pay, Quest Diagnostics ~7,000 sites, Sept 10 2025 launch, 64% overall / 68% six-deadliest sensitivity, 97.4% specificity, 50+ cancer types, CLIA LDT, not covered. Vendor label "Abbott (Exact Sciences)" is now correct — Abbott completed its $21B acquisition of Exact Sciences on March 23, 2026, making it a wholly owned subsidiary. Per-cancer/per-stage sensitivity tables are sourced to the Clinician Brochure, and the record commendably flags the specificity/PPV tradeoff vs Galleri. Points off for: PPV/NPV borrowed from the DETECT-A study (CancerSEEK predecessor, not Cancerguard itself) without clear labeling that these are not Cancerguard-specific; targetPopulation imprecise vs the labeled 50-84 with 3-year cancer-free criterion; and a few provenance notes (dataSourceNotes, medicareCoverage.lastVerified Jan 2026) predating the acquisition close.

defects.issue: PPV 19.4% and NPV 99.3% are sourced from the DETECT-A study (Science 2020, a CancerSEEK-predecessor study using a different/older assay), not from Cancerguard's own validation. ppvNotes/npvNotes label them as 'from DETECT-A' but the values are presented as if they characterize Cancerguard; they are a proxy, not a direct Cancerguard metric, and should be flagged as such.

States 'Adults 50+ at average or elevated risk.' Vendor labeling is more specific: ages 50-84 with no known cancer diagnosis in the past three years, considered annually. Missing the upper age bound (84) and the 3-year cancer-free exclusion.

lastVerified 2026-01-13 and dataSourceNotes 'Added December 2025' predate the Abbott acquisition close (March 23, 2026). Coverage status is still correct (NOT_COVERED), but provenance stamps are stale relative to the now-correct Abbott ownership.

Labeled 'ASCEND-2 est.' (estimate) while the main cancerTypeSensitivity table is sourced to the Test Development Study (n=729/590). Two different per-cancer datasets coexist with one marked as an estimate; source attribution is muddy and the 'est.' values are not clearly tied to a citable public table.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Derived from the DETECT-A/CancerSEEK study, not Cancerguard's own data; PPV is highly prevalence-dependent and the DETECT-A population (women 65-75) differs from Cancerguard's intended 50-84 mixed-sex population, so 19.4% may not represent Cancerguard real-world PPV.

Sums FALCON (~25,000, still enrolling) + DETECT-A (~10,000). DETECT-A studied a predecessor assay, not Cancerguard; counting it inflates the apparent Cancerguard evidence base, and FALCON is prospective/incomplete.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 14 cancers with pct/n values, source 'ASCEND-2 est.'
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Marked as an estimate with small n values (e.g., Lymphoma n=1, Ovary n=2) and no direct public citation; some entries (Endometrial 20% n=24) are hard to reconcile with the Test Development table values and may be modeled rather than observed.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Adults ages 50-84 with no known cancer diagnosis in the past 3 years; may be considered annually as a complement to (not replacement for) guideline-recommended single-cancer screening
Cancerguard is owned by Abbott Laboratories; Abbott completed its acquisition of Exact Sciences (now a wholly owned subsidiary, HQ Madison WI) on March 23, 2026, for $105.00/share (~$21B).

PPV/NPV shown are extrapolated from the DETECT-A study of the predecessor CancerSEEK assay, not from Cancerguard's own validation; treat as a proxy. Cancerguard-specific PPV in a screening population depends on the assumed ~1% prevalence and 97.4% specificity (~2.6% FPR).

summary: Accurate, current, well-cited ECD record for Exact Sciences' Cancerguard MCED test; vendor label "Abbott (Exact Sciences)" is now correct after Abbott's March 2026 acquisition close, and headline performance/price/availability/FDA facts all verify against vendor sources. Main weaknesses are minor: PPV/NPV and some per-cancer estimates are borrowed from the predecessor DETECT-A/CancerSEEK study rather than Cancerguard itself, and targetPopulation omits the labeled 50-84 age cap and 3-year cancer-free criterion.

name: NavDx
qualityRationale: Record is current and well-sourced. Vendor (Naveris), method (ddPCR TTMV-HPV DNA / fragmentomic), 5 HPV genotypes (16/18/31/33/35), tumor-naive approach, and headline performance (sensitivity 90.4%, specificity 98.6%, PPV 97.9%, NPV 95.7% for recurrent OPSCC) all match vendor FAQs and the PMC9955790 analytical-validation paper. Medicare coverage milestones (OPSCC Nov 2023 via Palmetto MolDX, ASCC Nov 2025, ADLT April 2024, CPT 0356U) are confirmed against Naveris press releases. Deductions for a wrong-vendor citation (biodesix.com), an LOD value that does not match the published paper, and minor internal inconsistencies around the gynecologic (NavDx+Gyn) genotype count.

defects.issue: Points to https://www.biodesix.com/ — a different, unrelated vendor (Biodesix). Should cite Naveris/NavDx physician ordering page. Likely copy-paste error from another record.

Value '0-1.10 copies/µL' does not match the published analytical-validation paper (PMC9955790), which reports LOD 0.56–1.31 copies/µL (LOB 0–0.32, LOQ <1.20–4.11). The record appears to conflate LOB with LOD. The lodNotes correctly cite LOB/LOQ but the headline lod figure is wrong.

Method says NavDx 'detects HPV genotypes 16,18,31,33,35' (correct for base NavDx) but cancerTypes lists HPV-driven gynecologic cancers served by NavDx+Gyn, which detects 14 high-risk HPV types via 34 biomarkers. Internal inconsistency: a reader could think the gyn indication uses the 5-genotype assay.

policyNumber is 'MolDX' (generic) while coverageCrossReference.medicare.policies lists 'L38779' and the older note references 'L38779'. Inconsistent / non-specific LCD identifier across nested coverage objects.
Says 'Nearly 20 peer-reviewed publications' while top-level numPublications is 36 (+). Stale/contradictory publication count within the same record.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Published NavDx analytical validation (PMC9955790) gives LOD 0.56–1.31 copies/µL across the 5 types; the record's value matches neither LOD nor cleanly LOB/LOQ, suggesting it is a conflated or transcribed figure.
Vendor FAQ states ≥89% sensitivity for newly diagnosed OPSCC; the precise 91.5% figure is not surfaced on the FAQ and should be tied to a specific citation/timepoint to be verifiable.

totalParticipantsNotes describes PPV 95% (76/80) and NPV 95% (1,198/1,256) — denominators (~1,336 tests) don't correspond to a 1,076-patient cohort; mixes per-test and per-patient counts, making the figure ambiguous.
No public Naveris/CMS source cited for a $1,800 ADLT rate; ADLT pricing is set via CMS gapfill/market data and should carry a citation. Unverifiable as stated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://navdx.com/physician-faqs/
0.56-1.31 copies/µL (LOD); LOB 0-0.32, LOQ <1.20-4.11 copies/µL

Base NavDx (5 high-risk HPV types: 16,18,31,33,35) is validated for HPV+ oropharyngeal and anal squamous cell carcinoma. HPV-driven gynecologic cancers (cervical, vaginal, vulvar) are served by the separate NavDx+Gyn assay, which detects 14 high-risk HPV types using 34 DNA biomarkers via ddPCR.

Medicare coverage via Palmetto GBA MolDX for HPV+ oropharyngeal cancer surveillance (Nov 2023) and anal squamous cell carcinoma MRD surveillance (Nov 2025; first/only Medicare-covered ASCC MRD test). CMS ADLT designation effective April 2024; CPT 0356U.

summary: NavDx by Naveris is accurately represented and still actively offered — a tumor-naive ddPCR TTMV-HPV DNA MRD assay (5 HPV genotypes) for HPV+ oropharyngeal and anal SCC, with Medicare coverage (OPSCC Nov 2023, ASCC Nov 2025) and ADLT status; the gyn indication uses the separate 14-type NavDx+Gyn assay. Main defects are a wrong-vendor sample citation (biodesix.com), an LOD value that contradicts the published analytical-validation paper, and minor internal inconsistencies in publication count and LCD identifiers.

name: CellSight DNA
qualityRationale: Core descriptive fields (CTC-based whole-tumor-cell approach, 500+ genes DNA-only, MSI/TMB reported, CLIA LDT, blood sample) are accurate and match the vendor's official page. However the record contains a self-contradicting and factually wrong medicareCoverage note ("Vendor may not have US-available test or may be inactive") despite the test being commercially available and vendor-verified; the turnaround time is stale/inconsistent (record says 5-7 days while the current vendor pages state 10-14 days); and all performance numbers (>99% sensitivity, >97% PPV, 5% LOD) are unverifiable vendor marketing claims with zero peer-reviewed publications backing them. Currency and evidence backing drag the score down despite good descriptive accuracy.

defects.issue: Record states '5-7 days' but the current vendor homepage and cellsight-dna page state 'results delivered in just 10-14 days'. The vendor's own copy is internally inconsistent (one blurb says 5-7), but the structured/headline figure is now 10-14 days, making the stored 5-7 days stale/optimistic.

Says 'Unable to locate information. Vendor may not have US-available test or may be inactive.' This is factually wrong and contradicts the same record: the test is commercially available in the US (vendor site has live 'Request a Sample Report' CTA) and the record is vendorVerified:true with an active vendor submitter (Kyle Dunn, Jan 2026). Misleading.

>99% analytical sensitivity, >97% PPV, and 5% LOD are vendor self-reported marketing figures from the product webpage only. numPublications is 0, so none are independently validated or peer-reviewed; should be flagged as vendor-claimed, not established performance.

A directly relevant MolDX LCD exists — L38643 'MolDX: Phenotypic Biomarker Detection from Circulating Tumor Cells' — which the UNKNOWN status note ignores; coverage landscape for CTC phenotypic/genomic assays is not captured.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Current vendor pages (homepage and cellsight-dna page) state 10-14 days; 5-7 appears in an older blurb only. Likely revised by vendor after submission.
Vendor-page marketing claim with no peer-reviewed validation (numPublications:0); analytical sensitivity for a CTC-capture 500-gene NGS LDT at 5% LOD is not independently corroborated.
PPV depends on prevalence/cohort; quoted as a fixed vendor figure with no study, cohort, or citation beyond the product webpage.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Vendor may not have US-available test or may be inactive
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Directly contradicted by live vendor site and the record's own vendorVerified:true + 2026 vendor submission; the test is US-available.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 10-14 days

No test-specific Medicare/MolDX policy identified for CellSight DNA. Relevant umbrella policy: MolDX LCD L38643 'Phenotypic Biomarker Detection from Circulating Tumor Cells' (Palmetto/MolDX). Coverage status for this specific CTC genomic LDT not established.
10 mL blood draw
Reports SNVs, indels, MSI, and TMB across 500+ cancer-related genes; HRD not reported. DNA-only assay on captured intact circulating/whole tumor cells.

summary: CellSight DNA is a still-offered, US-available CLIA LDT from Cancer Cell Diagnostics performing 500+ gene DNA-only genomic profiling (with MSI/TMB) on captured intact circulating/whole tumor cells; descriptive fields are accurate but the record carries a contradictory/wrong "may be inactive" Medicare note, a stale turnaround time (5-7 vs the vendor's current 10-14 days), and unvalidated vendor-only performance claims with zero supporting publications.

name: StrataNGS
qualityRationale: Core CGP facts (429 genes, tissue FFPE, multiplex PCR/semiconductor, TMB/MSI, CLIA LDT, ~7-10 day TAT) are accurate and match the validation literature and vendor materials. But the record carries two critical accuracy failures: a cited PMID (34723565) that resolves to an unrelated PTSD/suicide psychology paper rather than StrataNGS, reused in three citation fields; and a medicareCoverage block that describes an entirely different test (a plasma/liquid-biopsy 55-gene panel under LCD L38043 'MolDX: Plasma-Based Genomic Profiling'), which is wrong for this tissue 429-gene assay. Several citation URLs (strataoncology.com/stratangs, stratangs.com) 404 or are unverified, and the CPT/method fields have verifiability problems. Test is still offered, so currency is acceptable, but evidence backing is materially compromised.

defects.issue: PMID 34723565 is a PTSD/suicide causal-diagrams paper in Psychological Trauma (2021), NOT StrataNGS. Wrong citation reused in 3 fields. Correct StrataNGS validation paper is Tomlins et al., J Mol Diagn 2021, PMID 34454112 (DOI 10.1016/j.jmoldx.2021.07.013).

Block describes the WRONG test: status COVERED under LCD L38043 'MolDX: Plasma-Based Genomic Profiling' with notes 'Liquid biopsy CGP panel. 55 genes for therapy selection.' StrataNGS is a TISSUE 429-gene assay covered under Palmetto MolDX 'NGS for Solid Tumors,' not the plasma LCD. Appears copy-pasted from a liquid-biopsy record.

geneListUrl https://www.strataoncology.com/stratangs returns HTTP 404; the dedicated StrataNGS page no longer exists. Many fields cite this dead URL and the unverified stratangs.com. Vendor homepage now leads with treatment-response scores, not StrataNGS.

CPT 0391U is associated in PLA sourcing with 'Strata Select' (immunotherapy test), not confirmed as StrataNGS's code. cptCodesNotes references regional 'BCBSM EI status.' Unverifiable as the StrataNGS PLA code.
Omits that StrataNGS co-isolates DNA AND RNA; gene fusion detection is RNA-based. Record implies DNA-only with 'select fusions,' understating the RNA component documented in the validation paper.

medicareRate 3600 and medicareEffective '2025-Q4' are unverifiable from public CMS CLFS data in this review; medicareRate is null inside the conflicting medicareCoverage block, an internal inconsistency.

suspectClaims.currentValue: L38043 — MolDX: Plasma-Based Genomic Profiling; '55 genes for therapy selection'
suspectClaims.whySuspect: This is a plasma/liquid-biopsy policy describing a 55-gene panel; StrataNGS is a 429-gene tissue test. Coverage is under Palmetto MolDX 'NGS for Solid Tumors,' not L38043.
Public PLA sourcing links 0391U to 'Strata Select,' not StrataNGS; not independently confirmed as the StrataNGS code.
No public CMS CLFS/ADLT pricing for StrataNGS's CPT located to confirm $3,600; also contradicted by null reimbursementRate inside the same record's medicareCoverage block.
Supporting citation is the wrong (PTSD) PMID; the count is plausible but its evidence backing is invalid.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34454112/
proposedFills.source: Tomlins SA et al. Development and Validation of StrataNGS, a Multiplex PCR, Semiconductor Sequencing-Based Comprehensive Genomic Profiling Test. J Mol Diagn. 2021;23(11):1515-1530.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Multiplex PCR / semiconductor sequencing CGP performed on co-isolated DNA and RNA from FFPE tumor tissue; 429 genes; detects SNVs/MNVs, indels, CNAs, RNA-based gene fusions, TMB, and MSI; optimized for small samples (>=2 mm2 tumor surface area).

Covered by Palmetto GBA / MolDX under 'Next-Generation Sequencing for Solid Tumors' for patients with advanced (stage III/IV), recurrent, relapsed, refractory, or metastatic solid tumors (tissue CGP).
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34454112/
proposedFills.source: Tomlins et al., J Mol Diagn 2021 (429-gene co-isolated DNA/RNA assay)

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8384401/
proposedFills.source: Real-World Performance of a Comprehensive Genomic Profiling Test Optimized for Small Tumor Samples (StrataNGS), open-access PMC.

summary: StrataNGS is still offered as a 429-gene co-isolated DNA+RNA tissue CGP test (orderable via Strata Assistant), but the record has two critical errors: a cited PMID (34723565) that is an unrelated PTSD paper instead of the real validation study (PMID 34454112), and a medicareCoverage block that actually describes a different plasma/55-gene liquid-biopsy test under LCD L38043. Citation URLs 404, the CPT 0391U attribution is unverified, and the method understates the RNA component.

name: Latitude
qualityRationale: Core performance figures (58% landmark, 81%/84.4% surveillance/longitudinal sensitivity, 97.2% sample / 92.1% patient specificity, 4.6mo lead time, HR=10) are accurate and match the Jan 2026 npj Precision Oncology validation (PMID 41555007). Approach, tumor-naive methylation method, CRC-only scope, and tissue-free differentiator are correct. Score is dragged down by an internal contradiction on commercial availability, an unsupported VAF LOD, a stale/missing primary citation, and reimbursement fields that lag the published roadmap.

defects.issue: States 'Commercially available via Natera for colorectal cancer patients' but Natera's own 2026 roadmap schedules Latitude tissue-free MRD for a Q3 2026 launch and the medicareCoverage.notes in this same record says 'Still in clinical validation, not yet commercially available.' As of June 2026 the test is not yet broadly commercially launched. Internal contradiction and likely factually wrong.

'0.01% VAF' is not supported by the published validation (npj Precision Oncology, PMID 41555007 does not report a VAF-based LOD). Latitude is a methylation/DMR assay, not a variant-allele-frequency assay, so a VAF LOD is methodologically mismatched and likely fabricated.

Only the vendor page is cited; the actual peer-reviewed validation (npj Precision Oncology, 2026-01-19, PMID 41555007, DOI 10.1038/s41698-026-01277-5) is not cited anywhere despite being the source of nearly every performance number.

medicareCoverage.status NOT_YET_AVAILABLE with lastVerified 2026-01-13 predates the Jan 2026 publication that explicitly supports a MolDX submission; coverage section is stale relative to the published regulatory roadmap.
clinicalTrials text references 'CIRCULATE-JAPAN GALAXY Study' but cites only the vendor URL; should cite the GALAXY validation paper directly.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Commercially available via Natera for colorectal cancer patients
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Natera's 2026 roadmap lists Latitude tissue-free MRD launching Q3 2026 and MolDX submission still pending; same record's medicareCoverage.notes says not yet commercially available.
Not reported in the validation publication; methylation/DMR assay does not natively report VAF. Appears assumed from Signatera rather than measured for Latitude.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Latitude-positive patients who did not receive ACT had 100% relapse rate (26/26 PPV)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Published paper reports 100% specificity in the MRD window among non-ACT patients (63/63); the specific '26/26' PPV figure is not directly confirmed in the validation and may be a different subgroup or misstated.
Defensible (paper cites 58% landmark, 84.4% longitudinal, ~81% surveillance) but the single headline value 81 conflates distinct windows; should specify which window.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Validation: Nakamura et al., 'Validation of a methylation-based, tissue-free MRD assay in colorectal cancer patients from the GALAXY study,' npj Precision Oncology, 2026-01-19. PMID 41555007; DOI 10.1038/s41698-026-01277-5.
97.2% sample-level and 92.1% patient-level specificity (surveillance setting)
58.5% sensitivity in post-surgical landmark/MRD window (38/65); 84.4% longitudinal sensitivity (54/64) in surveillance; median 4.6-month lead time before radiographic recurrence
Clinical validation published Jan 2026; tissue-free MRD launch planned Q3 2026 with MolDX submission supported by GALAXY data; additional cancer indications in development for 2026

Methylation-based NGS assay detecting cancer-specific differentially methylated regions (DMRs); tumor-naive/tissue-free; validated on 1,230 plasma timepoints from 195 stage I-IV CRC patients in the GALAXY arm of CIRCULATE-Japan

GALAXY (CIRCULATE-Japan): 195 stage I-IV CRC patients, 1,230 plasma timepoints, median follow-up 28 months; ctDNA-positivity associated with worse DFS in MRD window (HR=10.0, p<0.001) and surveillance window (HR=31.9, p<0.001)

summary: Natera's Latitude is a real, currently-named tumor-naive methylation tissue-free MRD test for CRC whose performance figures match the Jan 2026 npj Precision Oncology GALAXY validation (PMID 41555007), but the record wrongly states it is 'commercially available' (Natera's roadmap shows a Q3 2026 launch, contradicting the record's own coverage note), reports an unsupported 0.01% VAF LOD, and fails to cite the primary publication.

name: CancerVision
qualityRationale: Core facts (vendor, target-enhanced WGS approach, ~40x WGS backbone + ~500x on key genes, tumor-normal paired, TMB/MSI/HRD reporting, CLIA LDT, ~2 week TAT, not US-insurance-covered) match the vendor's official provider page and Oct-2024 validation announcement, and the test is actively offered (expanded to 10 hospitals July 2025). Score is held down by an overstated publication count (evidence is two medRxiv preprints, not 5 peer-reviewed papers), a headline 100% sensitivity that rests on a single 49-patient concordance set while the vendor's own analytical-validation figure is 99%, an unverifiable cfDNA/liquid-biopsy option, and an unsourced $4,000 self-pay price.

defects.issue: Record claims numPublications:5 with '5+' and 'multiple publications.' Public evidence is two medRxiv PREPRINTS (2023.12.18.23300049 and 2023.12.20.23300156) plus press releases/trade news — no 5 peer-reviewed indexed publications found. Overstated and not backed by the cited clinicaltrialsarena source.

sensitivity:100 reflects a single 49-patient TSO500 concordance set (498 variants). The vendor's own provider page states 99% sensitivity/PPV. Headline 100% is from a small validation cohort and is more favorable than the vendor's stated analytical sensitivity.

cfDNAOption:true ('liquid biopsy option available when tissue sample fails') is unverifiable. Vendor patient and provider pages describe tissue + blood-as-matched-normal only; no ctDNA/liquid tumor-profiling product is documented for CancerVision (liquid is associated with the separate MRDVision product).

genesAnalyzed:20000 frames the WGS/exome as '20,000 genes.' Some vendor materials cite '600+ clinically relevant genes' at high depth while the provider page says ~500 key biomarker genes at 500x; the record uses 500 in notes. Minor inconsistency between 500 (notes) and the 600+ figure seen elsewhere.
$4,000 (patient self-pay) is not disclosed on vendor public pages (price withheld; 'contact us'). Unsourced and unverifiable.

specificity:99 is described qualitatively ('high specificity via tumor-normal') with vendor homepage citation; vendor cites 99% PPV/specificity but the specific 99 value is not independently anchored to a peer-reviewed source.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Based on n=49 concordance only; vendor's own provider page states 99% sensitivity/PPV. 100% is the best-case concordance figure, not the vendor analytical-validation number.
Only two medRxiv preprints and press coverage are findable; no set of 5 peer-reviewed publications identified.
No documented liquid-biopsy/ctDNA option for CancerVision on vendor pages; liquid biopsy is tied to the separate MRDVision product.
Vendor does not publish a price; figure is unsourced.
validationCohortSize is 49; the basis for totalParticipants:500 (and '500+') is unclear and not tied to a citation.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Target-enhanced WGS: ~40x whole-genome backbone with ~500x focused depth on ~500 key biomarker genes (vendor also markets '600+ clinically relevant genes' at high depth); tumor 40x / germline 20x.

Analytical and clinical validation reported in two medRxiv preprints (2023): 'Analytical and clinical validation of a target-enhanced whole genome sequencing-based comprehensive genomic profiling test' and 'Target-enhanced whole-genome sequencing (TE-WGS) shows clinical validity equivalent to commercially available targeted oncology panel'; head-to-head with TSO500 showed 100% concordance (498 variants, n=49), VAF r=0.978.
10 FFPE tissue sections plus paired normal blood (2 Streck tubes) or buccal swab for germline comparison.
Commercially available CLIA/CAP LDT; expanded to 10 hospitals (announced July 2025).

summary: CancerVision is an actively offered Inocras CLIA-LDT target-enhanced WGS tumor-profiling test (~40x WGS + ~500x on ~500 key genes, tumor-normal paired, TMB/MSI/HRD), and the record's core facts are accurate. Main quality issues are an overstated publication count (evidence is two medRxiv preprints), a headline 100% sensitivity drawn from a 49-patient set versus the vendor's stated 99%, and an unverifiable cfDNA/liquid-biopsy option and self-pay price.

name: NEO PanTracer Tissue
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and verifiable against the vendor: 517-gene DNA+RNA tissue CGP from FFPE, SNV/indel/CNV/fusion/splice plus MSI and TMB, CLIA LDT (not FDA-approved), NY State approval, and the rebrand from "Neo Comprehensive" to "NEO PanTracer Tissue" is correctly captured via previousName. Score is held below 90 because the record's supporting narrative and citations are anchored to the March 2023 "Neo Comprehensive" launch press release (#235) rather than the actual PanTracer rebrand (ASCO 2025 / available June 30 2025), so the dating and several citations are stale by one product generation. TAT and the gene-count breakdown (517 DNA SNV/indel, 59 DNA CNV, 55 RNA) are slightly imprecise versus current vendor data. No fabricated performance numbers detected; the LBx sensitivity/specificity figures belong to the sibling liquid test and are labeled as such.

defects.issue: Record says "7-10 days"; current vendor messaging (June 2025 launch PR) states results in as little as 8 days. Not contradictory but mildly stale/imprecise.

Narrative and citations anchor the PanTracer name to the March 2023 press release (#235, "Neo Comprehensive" launch). The PanTracer rebrand was announced at ASCO 2025 and made available June 30, 2025. Dating is stale by one product generation; PR #235 does not support the PanTracer name.

States "517 genes via both DNA and RNA" but vendor breakdown is 517 genes by DNA (SNV/indel), 59 genes by DNA for CNV, and 55 genes by RNA for fusions/splice — not all 517 are RNA-profiled. Slightly misleading.
HRD/genomic instability score not listed; offered via the companion product NEO PanTracer Tissue + HRD. Defensible as a separate test, but worth a note since the portfolio now includes HRD.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No specific peer-reviewed publications are cited for the PanTracer/Neo Comprehensive tissue panel; the only citation is a press release. The "6+" count is unverifiable from the sources provided.
Vendor's current launch PR advertises "as little as 8 days," suggesting the 7-10 day figure derives from the older Neo Comprehensive era rather than the current PanTracer offering.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Results in as little as 8 days (FFPE tissue CGP)
517 genes by DNA for SNVs/InDels, 59 genes by DNA for CNVs, and 55 genes by RNA for known and novel fusions and splice variants; includes TMB and MSI.
Comprehensive genomic profiling of FFPE tissue using DNA and RNA NGS, rebranded from Neo Comprehensive Solid Tumor to NEO PanTracer Tissue and made available June 30, 2025 (debuted at ASCO 2025).
NEO PanTracer Tissue + HRD (adds homologous recombination deficiency / genomic instability score, targeted at ovarian cancer therapy selection); NEO PanTracer LBx (liquid biopsy); NEO PanTracer Pro.

summary: NEO PanTracer Tissue is accurately described as a 517-gene DNA+RNA FFPE tissue CGP (SNV/indel/CNV/fusion/splice + MSI/TMB), CLIA LDT, NY-approved, and still offered under this current name. Main weakness is currency: the record's narrative and citations are anchored to the March 2023 Neo Comprehensive launch rather than the June 2025 PanTracer rebrand, and TAT plus the RNA gene-count breakdown are slightly imprecise.

name: Avantect Pancreatic Cancer Test
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-cited. The enhanced Feb-2026 performance figures (82.6% overall sensitivity, 76.8% early-stage I/II, 97.5% specificity) match ClearNote Health's official press release and the ASCO 2026 abstract exactly. FDA status (LDT/CLIA, NYSDOH, not cleared), CPT 0410U, $1,160 CLFS rate, and "not Medicare-covered" are all correct as of 2026. Vendor-verified with documented correspondence. Deductions: (1) PPV ~25% and NPV 99% are cited to vague/unverifiable sources and no peer-reviewed source supports 25% PPV; (2) medicareCoverage block lists "coveredIndications" under a NOT_COVERED status, which is internally contradictory/misleading; (3) clinicalTrials field asserts NCI Vanguard MCED selection citing only the about-us page, while the stronger, recently-confirmed affiliations are SAFE-D (NHS) and PRECEDE; (4) headline performance remains "Data on File"/conference-only (ASCO+DDW 2026), peer-reviewed publication still pending — record notes this correctly but the numbers carry that caveat.

defects.issue: Status is NOT_COVERED yet the block lists two 'coveredIndications' (new-onset T2DM; family history). Listing covered indications under a not-covered policy is internally contradictory and misleading — these are the target/intended-use population, not Medicare-covered indications.
PPV 25% cited to 'ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06803771; Avantect Official Site' — no peer-reviewed or vendor publication substantiates a 25% PPV; value is prevalence-dependent and unverifiable as stated.
NPV 99% cited only to 'ClearNote Health clinical data; high-risk screening population' — vague, unverifiable source.

Claims selection for 'NCI Vanguard Multicancer Detection study' citing only the vendor about-us page; vendor's current/primary trial affiliations in 2026 press materials are NHS SAFE-D and PRECEDE Consortium. NCI Vanguard claim is weakly sourced.

Headline enhanced-test metrics remain 'Data on File'/conference presentations (ASCO 2026 n=1,445; DDW 2026); no peer-reviewed publication yet. Record discloses this but the primary numbers should be read with that caveat.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited to NCT06803771 + official site but no published source gives a 25% PPV; PPV is highly prevalence-dependent and not reported as a validated figure in the validation studies.

suspectClaims.currentValue: ['Age 50+ with new-onset Type 2 diabetes (within 36 months)','Age 50+ with family history of pancreatic cancer']
suspectClaims.whySuspect: These are the intended-use population, not Medicare-covered indications — status is NOT_COVERED, so labeling them coveredIndications is incorrect.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Selected for NCI Vanguard Multicancer Detection study; PRECEDE Consortium member
suspectClaims.whySuspect: 2026 vendor press releases emphasize SAFE-D (NHS) and PRECEDE; NCI Vanguard selection is sourced only to the about-us page and not corroborated in recent releases.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Selected for NHS SAFE-D study; PRECEDE Consortium member; multi-cohort validation presented at ASCO 2026 and Digestive Disease Week 2026

ASCO 2026: 'Multi-cohort validation of a multi-analyte liquid biopsy test for early-stage pancreatic cancer detection' — independent validation cohort n=1,445 with multiple risk factors (T2DM, family history, genetic predisposition); plus secondary cohorts n=338 enriched for new-onset T2DM (presenter A. Bergamaschi/A. Bergamaschi PhD)

CLFS rate $1,160 (CPT 0410U); no active Medicare coverage policy as of March 2026. The new-onset T2DM and family-history criteria describe the intended-use population, not Medicare-covered indications.

summary: Accurate, current, vendor-verified record for ClearNote Health's single-cancer pancreatic ECD test; enhanced Feb-2026 performance (82.6%/76.8%/97.5%) matches the official release and ASCO 2026 abstract, and the test remains actively offered. Main weaknesses are a self-contradictory medicareCoverage block (NOT_COVERED but lists 'coveredIndications'), an unverifiable 25% PPV/99% NPV, a weakly-sourced NCI Vanguard claim, and headline metrics that are still conference-only (publication pending).

name: Cologuard Plus
qualityRationale: Core scientific and regulatory facts verified accurate and well-cited (FDA PMA Oct 4 2024, BLUE-C, 93.9% sens / ~91% spec, CPT 0464U, NEJM 2024). Vendor ownership reflects real Abbott acquisition. Deductions for wrong age range, stale list price, self-contradictory Medicare block, over-precise per-stage numbers, and a participant-count discrepancy (26758 in record vs ~20176 published).

defects.issue: States 'average-risk adults 45-75 years'. FDA label covers age 45+; Medicare 45-85. Upper bound 75 is incorrect.

Embedded object status 'UNKNOWN' with notes 'Need to verify coverage' and null fields, contradicting top-level cptCode 0464U / medicareRate 591.92 / medicareStatus 'Priced'. Stale and internally inconsistent.
Record says 26758; published BLUE-C enrollment is ~20,176 (primary average-risk cohort ~19,000). The 26758 figure also appears appended to the clinicalTrials string with no basis.
Lists $790; published Cologuard Plus self-pay price is $599 (cologuard.com/GoodRx 2025). Likely inherited from original Cologuard.
Per-stage values 87/94/97/100% are over-precise and not in primary reporting (which gives I-III combined 92.7%); Stage IV 100% rests on very few cases.
Only Humana listed; notes concede other payers merely 'anticipated' and unvalidated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Average-risk adults 45-75 years for CRC screening at home
suspectClaims.whySuspect: FDA indication is 45+; Medicare 45-85. Upper bound 75 unsupported.
Published BLUE-C enrollment is ~20,176; source of 26758 unclear.
Published self-pay price is $599.
Tiny Stage IV subgroup; primary trial reports I-III combined 92.7%.
Contradicts populated top-level Medicare/CPT pricing fields; CMS confirms coverage effective Oct 3 2024.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Average-risk adults 45 years and older (FDA label); Medicare covers ages 45-85
COVERED (HCPCS 0464U, effective for services on/after Oct 3 2024; every 3 years, ages 45-85, no copay/deductible)
92.7% for treatable-stage (I-III) CRC vs FIT 64.6%

summary: Cologuard Plus is correctly captured on core science (single-cancer CRC mt-sDNA, FDA PMA Oct 2024, BLUE-C 93.9% sens / ~91% spec, CPT 0464U) and its real Abbott acquisition, but contains a wrong target age range (45-75 vs 45+/45-85), a stale list price ($790 vs $599), a self-contradictory embedded Medicare-coverage block, over-precise unverified per-stage sensitivities, and a participant count (26758) that conflicts with published BLUE-C enrollment (~20,176).

name: Tempus xT CDx
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and current: 648-gene tumor-normal tissue FDA-approved IVD (P210011), CDx claims for CRC (MSI + KRAS/NRAS for cetuximab/panitumumab) correctly count as 2, ADLT $4,500 and CPT 0473U verified, and the record already captures the May 29 2026 tumor-only PMA amendment. Deductions for a stale conflicting Medicare CPT (81479 vs 0473U), a slightly wrong FDA approval date, and an internally inconsistent clinical-availability date.

defects.issue: Record states 2023-05-01; FDA's official approval date for P210011 is 2023-04-28 (FDA device page and SSED).
Lists CPT 81479 (unlisted molecular pathology) which conflicts with the correct, top-level PLA code 0473U assigned exclusively to xT CDx. 81479 is stale/superseded for this test.

States 'nationwide since January 2025' but the FDA-approved xT CDx national launch press (and fdaStatus field) is January 2026, and medicareEffective is 2025-Q4. The 2025 date appears to be a typo for January 2026, creating an internal inconsistency.

Says 'Medicare rate $4,500' as if static, but $4,500 was the initial 9-month ADLT rate (Jul 2024-Mar 2025); since Apr 1 2025 CMS sets the rate from the weighted median of private-payer amounts. Rate may now differ.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: xT CDx has a dedicated PLA code 0473U (effective 2025); 81479 is an unlisted code and inconsistent with the record's own top-level cptCodes value.
ADLT rate of $4,500 is known and populated elsewhere in the record (listPrice/medicareRate); leaving null here is an unverifiable gap rather than reflecting unknown data.
Round '50+' figure cited only to a single 2019 validation paper (PMID 31040929); the aggregate count is not directly verifiable from that source.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Commercially available nationwide as FDA-approved xT CDx since January 2026 (national launch); tumor-only indication added May 2026
CMS ADLT designation (granted 2024). Initial ADLT rate $4,500 for Jul 2024-Mar 2025; from Apr 1 2025 CMS rate set on weighted median of private-payer rates.

summary: Tempus xT CDx is still offered and the record is accurate and current — correctly capturing the 648-gene tumor-normal FDA IVD (P210011), 2 CRC CDx claims, ADLT $4,500/CPT 0473U, and the May 2026 tumor-only approval. Main defects are a stale conflicting Medicare CPT (81479), a slightly-off FDA approval date (should be 2023-04-28), and an inconsistent 'since January 2025' availability date that should read January 2026.

name: OmniSeq INSIGHT
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate: 523-gene tissue CGP, DNA+RNA, immune profiling with PD-L1 and immune signatures, CLIA LDT (correctly not FDA-approved, so 0 CDx). Test is verifiably still offered under the same name (Labcorp test 831177, Jan 2025 brochure, June 2025 gene list). But several fields are stale or incomplete: TAT stated as 10-14 days when vendor materials say 7-10 days; HRD/HRR reporting (14 HRR genes) is omitted from biomarkersReported though the assay reports it; vendor URLs (omniseq.com and the /providers/order-a-test/ path) appear dead/redirected; and the CPT PLA code 0473U is missing.

defects.issue: Record says '10-14 days'. Vendor materials (Labcorp/OmniSeq) state 7-10 days. Stale/incorrect turnaround.
Omits HRD. OmniSeq INSIGHT reports homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) via 14 HRR genes when clinically indicated; HRD should be listed alongside TMB/MSI.

Multiple fields cite https://oncology.labcorp.com/providers/order-a-test/omniseq-insight, which appears to be a dead/redirected path. Live URLs are https://oncology.labcorp.com/cancer-care-team/test-menu/omniseq-insight and https://www.labcorp.com/tests/831177/omniseq-insight.
Cites https://www.omniseq.com/ — the standalone omniseq.com domain post-acquisition redirects to Labcorp; stale citation.
No CPT/PLA code present. PLA code 0473U applies to OmniSeq INSIGHT (Medicare MolDX, effective July 2024).

Method describes DNA 523-gene panel but does not explicitly capture the RNA component (55 fusions/splice variants) or that PD-L1 is measured by IHC and immune signatures via 64 immune-gene mRNA expression.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor materials consistently state 7-10 days; current value likely stale.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Medicare and commercial coverage; widely available via Labcorp network.
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vague and unverified; no specific MolDX policy or CPT (0473U) cited to substantiate the Medicare claim.
'10+' with a single PMC citation; unverifiable as stated and the round number suggests an estimate rather than a counted figure.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 7-10 days
SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Fusions, TMB, MSI, HRD, PD-L1, Immune Signatures
https://www.labcorp.com/tests/831177/omniseq-insight

NGS assay sequencing the full coding regions of 523 genes by DNA (SNVs, indels, CNAs, including 14 HRR/HRD genes, MSI, TMB) plus RNA sequencing for 55 fusions/splice variants; immune profiling via 64 immune-gene mRNA expression signatures and PD-L1 by IHC.

summary: OmniSeq INSIGHT is verifiably still offered under the same name (Labcorp test 831177, current 2025 materials); the record's core 523-gene DNA+RNA/immune-profiling CLIA-LDT facts are accurate, but TAT is stale (10-14 vs 7-10 days), HRD reporting and the 0473U CPT code are missing, and several citation URLs are dead/redirected.

name: RaDaR ST
qualityRationale: Record is current and substantially accurate: RaDaR ST launched Feb 25, 2026, tumor-informed up to 48 variants, breast sensitivity/specificity (95.7%/91.0%) verified against PMC10870111, and Natera litigation status correct. Score held below 85 by a method-field error (names the unrelated InVision plasma-only assay and omits the actual WES discovery step), an imprecise LOD (~10 vs vendor-official LOD95 11 ppm), a TNBC-vs-breast generalization, and stale/vague CPT and commercial-payer fields with a non-specific citation.

defects.issue: Method describes the assay as running on the 'InVision/ RaDaR platform.' InVision is Inivata's separate plasma-only (tumor-naive) ctDNA assay, not the RaDaR ST tumor-informed platform. The field also omits that variant discovery is done by whole-exome sequencing (WES) of tumor tissue, which is the defining method per the vendor's official launch release and product page.
Listed as '~10 ppm'. Vendor's official figure is LOD95 = 11 ppm (with study-specific detection down to 1 ppm). Should state 11 ppm LOD95 to match the primary source.

95.7% sensitivity / 91.0% specificity are attributed to 'breast' generally, but the cited source (PMC10870111) reports these specifically for early-stage triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), not all breast subtypes.
States 'MolDX coverage announced July 2023.' The RaDaR MolDX coverage for HR+/HER2- breast cancer was effective March 24, 2023 per NeoGenomics. Date is wrong.

indicationsNotes asserts Medicare coverage for HPV-negative head and neck cancer, but the Feb 2026 launch release states only 'initial launch indications' are Medicare-approved with 'two additional indications submitted for reimbursement' — head & neck coverage appears pending, not confirmed. medicareCoverage object lists only the breast indication, creating an internal inconsistency with indicationsNotes.
'PLA under consideration.' Vague/stale; no specific code provided. RaDaR is billed clinically, so a current billing code or removal is warranted.

Only 'Blue Shield of California' listed, citation is the decibio.com homepage (non-specific). Vendor product page claims '300+ in-network commercial insurance contracts,' so the single-payer list understates and the citation is unverifiable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Tumor-informed MRD assay on the InVision/ RaDaR platform...
suspectClaims.whySuspect: InVision is Inivata's distinct tumor-naive plasma assay; conflating it with RaDaR ST is misleading. Actual method is WES of tumor tissue building a patient-specific panel of up to 48 variants.
NeoGenomics states RaDaR MolDX breast coverage was effective March 24, 2023, not July 2023.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Medicare coverage for ... HPV-negative head and neck cancer
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Feb 2026 launch release indicates additional indications were submitted/pending, not yet covered; head & neck Medicare coverage is unconfirmed.
Vendor-official LOD95 is 11 ppm.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Tumor-informed MRD assay: whole-exome sequencing (WES) of tumor tissue plus matched normal identifies somatic variants, used to build a patient-specific panel of up to 48 variants tracked in plasma cfDNA by deep amplicon NGS with error suppression.
LOD95 11 ppm (detection down to 1 ppm under study-specific conditions)

MolDX coverage for HR+/HER2- breast cancer effective March 24, 2023; RaDaR ST launched Feb 25, 2026 with select solid tumor indications Medicare-approved and two additional indications submitted for reimbursement.
In early-stage triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC): sensitivity 95.7% (95% CI 84.3-99.3) and specificity 91.0% (95% CI 80.9-96.3); median lead time to relapse 6.1 months vs 3.9 for dPCR.
NeoGenomics reports 300+ in-network commercial insurance contracts for RaDaR ST (specific payer list not enumerated by vendor).

summary: RaDaR ST is a current, still-offered NeoGenomics tumor-informed MRD assay (launched Feb 25, 2026; WES-derived ≤48-variant panel, LOD95 11 ppm) and the record is mostly accurate, with verified breast/TNBC performance and correct litigation status. Main fixes: the method field wrongly invokes the unrelated InVision platform and omits WES, the MolDX date is wrong (March not July 2023), head & neck Medicare coverage is overstated as confirmed, and CPT/commercial-payer fields are stale/vague.

name: Freenome SimpleScreen CRC
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and current. PREEMPT CRC v1 numbers (79.2% sensitivity, 91.5% specificity, stage-specific 57.1/100/82.4/100%, 12.5% advanced adenoma) all match Freenome topline release and JAMA 2025 publication. Updated assay (85% CRC / 22% APL at 90% specificity) confirmed via ASCO GI 2026 release. ACS 2026 guideline inclusion confirmed (caac.70083). FDA PMA submitted Aug 2025, approval expected mid-2026, confirmed. Minor staleness in ownership framing (Exact Sciences->Abbott merger closed ~March 2026) and several thin/speculative fields (price, TAT, screening interval, CPT) flagged as expected/unconfirmed. PPV/NPV citations are vaguely attributed.

defects.issue: References 'Exact Sciences/Abbott exclusive US licensing agreement'. Abbott completed its acquisition of Exact Sciences (merger closed ~March 2026), so the live licensee is Abbott. Phrasing is historically accurate but should be updated to reflect Abbott as the current commercialization partner to avoid implying both are still separate parties.

Cites 'PREEMPT CRC JAMA 2025; Freenome Newsroom' as a bare text string with no URL/PMID/DOI. Same for npvCitations and performanceCitations. Should point to the JAMA 2025 article DOI or a specific URL.

$895 is an expectation extrapolated from Exact Sciences' Shield ($895), not a published SimpleScreen CRC list price (test is pre-launch). listPriceNotes correctly hedges this, but the numeric field reads as established.
TAT (14 days), screening interval (every 3 years), and CPT (UNKNOWN) are all unconfirmed/placeholder pre-launch estimates. Notes hedge appropriately but values are speculative.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: This JCO supplement abstract URL (ASCO GI 2025, J Clin Oncol 43:4_suppl) is plausible but not directly verified in this audit; should be confirmed as a live citable abstract rather than assumed.

Correct as of June 2026 post-merger, but the underlying license was originally signed with Exact Sciences; ensure provenance is documented so the lineage (Freenome -> Exact Sciences license -> Abbott acquisition) is traceable.
Extrapolated from Shield, not an official SimpleScreen CRC price; test is not yet commercially launched.

proposedFills.proposedValue: PMA application submitted (final module August 2025); FDA approval expected mid-2026. US commercialization rights held by Abbott following its acquisition of Exact Sciences (which originally signed the exclusive license with Freenome; Abbott-Exact Sciences merger closed ~March 2026).
PREEMPT CRC, JAMA 2025 (n=27,010 evaluable; 79.2% CRC sensitivity, 91.5% specificity); updated assay 85% CRC / 22% APL at 90% specificity (ASCO GI 2026).
https://acsjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.3322/caac.70083
proposedFills.source: Colorectal cancer screening: An update to the American Cancer Society guideline, 2026 (Wolf et al., CA Cancer J Clin)

summary: Accurate, current, well-cited ECD record for Freenome's SimpleScreen CRC (single-cancer blood-based CRC screening, still offered, pre-FDA LDT at pilot sites). Main defects are minor: ownership phrasing predates the Abbott/Exact Sciences merger close, several pre-launch fields (price/TAT/CPT/interval) are speculative placeholders, and a few citations are bare text strings lacking DOIs/URLs.

name: LymphoVista
qualityRationale: Record is well-researched, current (Nov 2024 launch, vendor-verified Dec 2025), and correctly captures the tumor-informed duplex-sequencing method, lymphoma-specific niche, LOD (~6.5-6.7e-6, matches vendor), and cancer types. But two fields conflict with the vendor's own page cited as their source: turnaround (record 10 days vs vendor "2-4 weeks") and pricing (record asserts specific euro prices the cited vendor page does not show). The top-line sensitivity=100 overstates the vendor's validated 91-94% genotyping sensitivity. These accuracy issues on cited fields pull the score down despite strong overall completeness.

defects.issue: Record states 10 days for both genotyping and MRD monitoring, but the official vendor page (liqomics.com/en/lymphovista/, cited elsewhere in the record) states 'Time to Result: 2-4 weeks' for both LymphoVista and LymphoVista HL. The 10-day claim contradicts the vendor's stated TAT.

Record asserts EUR 2,965 for genotyping and EUR 1,985 per MRD sample, citing https://liqomics.com/en/lymphovista/. That vendor page and the launch press release disclose NO pricing. The euro figures are unverifiable from the cited source and appear fabricated or from an unstated source.

Top-line sensitivity=100 reflects a single point estimate at one MRD threshold (7.0e-5) in a small cohort, not a robust validated sensitivity. The vendor's headline/validated genotyping sensitivity is 91-94% (93.86% LymphoVista, 91.27% LymphoVista HL at >=0.5% mAF). Presenting 100% as the headline sensitivity is misleadingly optimistic.

Citation is 'https://lymphovista.com/' which is not the vendor's verified product URL (liqomics.com/en/lymphovista/). Domain appears wrong/unverified; the NCCN DLBCL ctDNA-MRD recommendation also does not name LymphoVista, which the note acknowledges but the boolean vendorClaimsNCCNAlignment=true overstates.

Listed as 2 conference abstracts (ASH 2024, EHA 2024) with peer-reviewed manuscripts 'under review.' Evidence base is conference-abstract-only; no peer-reviewed full publication confirmed, which the prognostic claims (HR 6.9, PFS figures) lean on.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor's validated sensitivity is 91-94% (genotyping). 100% is a point estimate at MRD level 7.0e-5 in a small n=160 cohort, not generalizable headline sensitivity.
Official vendor page states 'Time to Result: 2-4 weeks' for both assays; 10 days is contradicted by the vendor's own stated TAT.
Cited vendor page shows no pricing; launch PR shows no pricing. Euro figures not verifiable from any cited public source.
NCCN DLBCL ctDNA-MRD guideline does not name LymphoVista; citation domain (lymphovista.com) is not the verified vendor URL.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 2-4 weeks
2-4 weeks
Genotyping sensitivity 93.86% (LymphoVista) / 91.27% (LymphoVista HL) at >=0.5% mAF; MRD sensitivity reported up to 100% at 7.0e-5 threshold in validation cohort
November 2024

summary: LymphoVista (LIQOMICS, Cologne) is a still-offered (launched Nov 2024) tumor-informed duplex-sequencing ctDNA MRD assay for B-cell and Hodgkin lymphomas; the record is thorough and current but has three accuracy problems: turnaround time (10 days vs vendor's stated 2-4 weeks), specific euro pricing not supported by the cited vendor page, and a headline sensitivity=100 that overstates the vendor's validated 91-94% figures.

name: Pathlight (FoundationOne MRD)
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and current on the major facts that matter: tumor-informed WGS-to-dPCR two-step workflow, up to ~16 patient-specific structural variants, ~2-3 week initial / 1-3 day follow-up TAT, MolDX/Medicare coverage for early-stage breast cancer across all subtypes (TRACER, 100% sens/spec, 13.7-mo lead time), CRC launch (Jan 12, 2026), Roche/Foundation Medicine acquisition (~$595M, announced Apr 2026) and June 2026 relaunch as FoundationOne MRD. All confirmed against vendor, Roche, Foundation Medicine, GenomeWeb and BioSpace sources. Points deducted for internal inconsistency in Medicare policy numbers (L38779 vs L38822), a 'Multi-solid' cancer-type label that exceeds the validated/covered breast+CRC scope, and headline 100% performance metrics resting on a small single-arm cohort (n=100-121) sourced only to vendor materials.

defects.issue: Internal inconsistency: medicareCoverage.policyNumber says 'L38779' while coverageCrossReference.medicare.policies says 'L38822'. Both are presented as the governing MolDX MRD LCD for the same test. One (or both) is wrong; the correct controlling LCD should be reconciled to a single verified number.

Lists 'Multi-solid' alongside 'Breast'. Validated/covered evidence is breast (TRACER) and colorectal (Jan 2026 launch). 'Multi-solid' is aspirational platform positioning, not a validated/offered indication, and overstates current scope. CRC is offered but not listed in cancerTypes.

All four reported as 100%, sourced to a vendor press release, on a small single-arm cohort (n=100, expanded n=121). smallSampleWarning is correctly set, but presenting 100/100/100/100 as headline numbers risks overstating performance; should be framed as point estimates from a small cohort pending larger validation.

Field correctly notes the FoundationOne MRD relaunch, but the test 'name' bundles both Pathlight and FoundationOne MRD; the test is effectively renamed under Foundation Medicine (FoundationOne MRD, June 2026). stillOffered is best characterized as 'renamed' rather than simply 'yes'.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Conflicts with coverageCrossReference.medicare.policies value 'L38822' for the same MolDX MRD coverage. Cannot both be the governing LCD; neither was independently confirmed against the MCD in this audit.
No validation or coverage exists beyond breast and colorectal. 'Multi-solid' is marketing positioning, not a delivered/validated indication; CRC (an actual offered indication) is omitted.

Sourced only to a SABCS vendor poster (Data on File); FoundationOne MRD launch materials do not disclose a numeric LOD. 0.001% VAF = 10 ppm, while lod95 claims ~5 ppm — the relationship is plausible but the figures rest entirely on vendor analytical claims, not peer-reviewed LoD validation.

sensitivity/specificity notes cite n=100 (TRACER CCR Jan 2025) while validationCohortSize=121 (SABCS 2025 expanded). The 100% metrics are attributed to the n=100 cohort, so the cohort size backing the headline numbers is ambiguous.

proposedFills.proposedValue: FoundationOne MRD (formerly Pathlight, SAGA Diagnostics)
Breast, Colorectal
Personalized tumor fingerprint and initial blood test result within approximately 2-3 weeks of sample receipt; subsequent blood tests within 1-3 days (Foundation Medicine FoundationOne MRD launch).
Pathlight CRC: 3-year relapse-free interval 19% (ctDNA-positive) vs 95% (ctDNA-negative) post-treatment; launched Jan 12, 2026; CRC/colon/rectal data presented at ASCO GI 2026 and ASCO 2026.

summary: Accurate, current record for SAGA's tumor-informed WGS-to-dPCR MRD assay, now relaunched by Roche/Foundation Medicine as FoundationOne MRD (June 2026); core facts on method, ~16 SVs, TAT, breast Medicare coverage and CRC launch all verify against vendor and Roche sources. Main issues are an internal Medicare LCD number conflict (L38779 vs L38822), an overreaching 'Multi-solid' cancer-type label, and headline 100% performance metrics resting on a small vendor-sourced cohort.

name: CancerDetect Oral & Throat
qualityRationale: See defects/suspectClaims: both citations independently verified; minor issues are optimistic vendor-vs-peer-reviewed performance figures, unverifiable vendor-corrected PPV/NPV, and a likely stale list price.

defects.issue: Lists $599 base price; current vendor page implies a lower base (~$449.75) with DETECT code giving $300 off (financing 'as low as $149.75'). The $599 figure appears stale and the record's promo note ($299 with DETECT) is inconsistent with the current $300-off offer.

Headlines 95% specificity and 90% OPSCC sensitivity (vendor figures). Peer-reviewed Oral Oncology 2023 abstract (PMID 37454545) reports 94% specificity and 84.2% OPSCC sensitivity. Record's notes do acknowledge the ranges, but the top-line numbers favor the more optimistic vendor values.

PPV 5.4% and NPV 99.9% cite the Oral Oncology DOI but are not in the published abstract; record self-describes them as 'vendor-reported from full paper' added via a 2026 vendor correction. Plausible for a low-prevalence screen but not independently verifiable.

States validation study 'n=1,175'; the 2023 paper total is 1,175 (945 training + 230 validation), which is correct, but the validation cohort itself is 230 — wording could imply a 1,175-person validation. Cosmetic clarity issue only.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Peer-reviewed Oral Oncology 2023 (PMID 37454545) abstract states 94% specificity; 95% is the vendor's rounded/optimistic figure. 97.9% appears only in the earlier 2021 discovery study, not the validation cohort.
Not reported in the published abstract; sourced to a vendor correction citing the 'full paper.' Cannot be independently confirmed from public peer-reviewed source.
Not in published abstract; vendor-correction value. Plausible given low prevalence but unverifiable from cited public source.
Current vendor site indicates a lower base price with a $300-off DETECT code (financing 'as low as $149.75'), suggesting $599 is outdated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Base price not explicitly stated on current vendor page; DETECT code gives $300 off, financing 'as low as $149.75' (implying ~$449.75 base). Recommend re-verifying.
94 (peer-reviewed validation cohort, n=230); vendor headlines 95%

summary: Viome's CancerDetect Oral & Throat is a saliva metatranscriptomic single-cancer screen still actively offered as a CLIA LDT with FDA Breakthrough designation; the record is accurate and well-sourced (both primary PMIDs verified). Main caveats: headline specificity/OPSCC sensitivity use optimistic vendor figures vs. peer-reviewed 94%/84.2%, PPV/NPV are unverifiable vendor corrections, and the $599 list price appears stale.

name: clonoSEQ
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-sourced against vendor pages, the FDA De Novo authorization, MolDX coverage news, and NCCN. Core facts verified: tumor-informed immunosequencing of B/T-cell receptor rearrangements; 10⁻⁶ LOD; FDA-cleared IVD for MM/B-ALL (bone marrow) and CLL (blood/bone marrow); DLBCL/MCL via LDT/MolDX; $8,029 episode pricing; MCL surveillance coverage (every 6 mo for 5 yr); NCCN named for MM/ALL/CLL/DLBCL/MCL. Single id, no duplicate. Points deducted for internal inconsistencies in cash-pay/Medicare rate values and a couple of unverifiable aggregate counts.

defects.issue: Lists Medicare rate and cash-pay both as '$1,950', conflicting with the top-level medicareRate of $2,007.25 and listPrice $2,007. The 0364U CLFS rate effective Jan 2025 is ~$2,007; '$1,950' appears to be a stale or conflated value. Internal inconsistency should be reconciled.

States 'Medicare CLFS rate: $2,007 per test (PLA 0364U); episode pricing $8,029' which is consistent, but the coverageCrossReference block still carries the older '$1,950' figure — two coverage structures disagree.
Cites https://www.clonoseq.com/technical-summary/ — verify this exact path is live; vendor restructured to adaptivebiotech.com/clonoseq-mrd-test/. Some clonoseq.com deep links may now redirect.

Aggregate counts (>250 publications, >5,000 patients in totalParticipants vs '51,000 patients evaluated' in clinicalTrials, '190 clinical trials') are vendor-derived marketing aggregates and internally inconsistent (totalParticipants=5000 contradicts the 51,000 figure in the clinicalTrials narrative); not independently verifiable.
Claims EU availability 'via Roche and regional partners' and 'MedGenome (India)' — the Roche/MedGenome distribution claims are not corroborated in current vendor sources and may be stale.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Conflicts with top-level medicareRate $2,007.25 and 2025 CLFS 0364U pricing; appears stale or conflated with cash-pay.
Same record's clinicalTrials field states 'more than 51,000 patients evaluated across clinical and real-world settings' — the 5,000 figure understates and contradicts the narrative.

suspectClaims.currentValue: EU: CE-marked, available via Roche and regional partners. International availability via MedGenome (India)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Roche/MedGenome distribution partnerships for clonoSEQ are not confirmed in current vendor or news sources; possibly outdated.

~99%+ analytical specificity is plausible but the cited PMC7325652 is a validation paper; the specific 99% clinical specificity figure should be tied to a precise LoB/specificity claim rather than rounded.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Medicare CLFS rate ~$2,007 per test (PLA 0364U) effective Jan 1, 2025; full-episode pricing $8,029 across covered indications.
NGS-based MRD testing is incorporated in NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines for ALL, CLL, DLBCL, MCL and multiple myeloma; >82,000 clonoSEQ results delivered in 2025.

summary: clonoSEQ (Adaptive Biotechnologies) is still actively offered and the record is accurate and current — tumor-informed immunosequencing, 10⁻⁶ LOD, FDA-cleared for MM/B-ALL/CLL with DLBCL/MCL via LDT/MolDX, all verified against vendor and FDA sources. Only minor defects: internal inconsistencies in Medicare/cash-pay rate ($1,950 vs $2,007) and in patient-count aggregates (5,000 vs 51,000), plus some unverified EU/India distribution claims.

name: K-4CARE
qualityRationale: Core identity, vendor, and analytical performance (>99% SNV/indel sensitivity, 94% fusion, 100% TSO500 concordance) are accurate and well-sourced to the 2024 Frontiers validation (PMID 38404964). But the record is stale and incomplete versus the current vendor page: gene count (504) matches neither the publication (473) nor the current vendor page (515 tissue); it omits PD-L1, HRD, and the DNA+RNA transcriptomic dimension from biomarkers; it understates the dual tissue+liquid/MRD nature; availability is stale (lists 2 of 14 countries, omitting the now-listed US); and turnaround (8 days) is missing.

defects.issue: Listed as 504. Vendor's current official page states 515 genes for tissue CGP; the cited 2024 publication states 473. 504 matches no public source — likely an interim figure now superseded.

Omits PD-L1 and HRD (Homologous Recombination Deficiency), both explicitly reported per the vendor page. For a CGP test these are clinically important missing biomarkers. Also omits RNA/transcriptomic-based reporting (tissue-of-origin, viral DNA markers) the vendor advertises.

Lists only Singapore and Vietnam. Vendor page now lists 14 countries (incl. Thailand, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Philippines, Malaysia, Indonesia, Turkey, India, Australia, Brazil, China, and the United States). Stale.

Marked Tissue / 'Tissue CGP' only. The test is dual-function (tissue CGP + liquid ctDNA/MRD per vendor and publication title); approach understates the liquid/MRD component (acknowledged in notes but not in structured fields).
Turnaround time absent; vendor page states 8 days.
Method text says 504-gene/1.7 Mb but 1.7 Mb is the 473-gene publication figure; panel size and panel size in Mb should be reconciled with current vendor spec (515 genes).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Neither the cited publication (473) nor the current vendor page (515 tissue) reports 504. The vendorRequestedChanges note claims '504 commercial vs 473 publication' but the live vendor page now says 515.
Vendor page lists 14 countries including the US; record is missing 12 markets.

suspectClaims.currentValue: SNVs, Indels, Gene fusions, Amplifications, TMB, MSI, Germline variants
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor page additionally lists PD-L1, HRD, viral DNA markers, and tissue-of-origin; these are absent.

proposedFills.proposedValue: SNVs, Indels, Gene fusions, Amplifications, TMB, MSI, PD-L1, HRD, Germline variants
Singapore, Thailand, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Vietnam, Philippines, Malaysia, Indonesia, Turkey, United States, India, Australia, Brazil, China
8 days
Jan 2026: strategic collaboration with Pangea Laboratory to perform analytical/clinical validation of LDT ctDNA tests in CLIA/CAP setting for US market; not yet FDA cleared.
100% somatic-variant concordance with TruSight Oncology 500; TMB concordance 89% (97% correlation with WES); MSI concordance 98%; ctDNA plasma detection rate 79%

summary: K-4CARE is still offered by Gene Solutions and its core performance data are accurately sourced to the 2024 Frontiers validation, but the record is stale/incomplete: gene count (504) matches neither the publication (473) nor the current vendor page (515), and it omits PD-L1/HRD biomarkers, the liquid/MRD dimension, 12 of 14 availability markets (including the US), and turnaround.

name: xM for TRM
qualityRationale: Core description (tumor-naive ctDNA tumor-fraction monitoring for ICI response, ensemble CNV+VAF algorithm, pan-cancer advanced solid tumors, runs on xF/xF+ liquid panels) is accurate and well-sourced to the real June 2025 Tempus launch and a genuine 2024 peer-reviewed paper. Two material problems pull the score down: (1) currency — the record is frozen at June 2025 and still states "RUO as of June 2025" and "clinical availability expected later in 2025," which is now stale at June 2026 (Tempus has since clinically launched its broader xM MRD portfolio; the TRM/ICI-monitoring variant's current clinical status is unconfirmed); (2) a wrong journal citation (record says Clinical Cancer Research 2024; the cited PMC11333675 paper is actually in Oncology and Therapy 2024). LOD/sensitivity/specificity are all null though the validation paper does report a limit-of-blank (95th-pct TF 0.6%) and an MR threshold (>=50% TF reduction).

defects.issue: Stale: still states 'RUO as of June 2025' and 'clinical availability expected later in 2025' as of a June 2026 audit. Tempus has since clinically launched its xM MRD portfolio; the current clinical status of the TRM (ICI-response) variant is unconfirmed and should be re-verified rather than asserted as future-2025.

Wrong journal attribution. Record states 'Clinical Cancer Research 2024 publication' but the cited article (PMC11333675) is published in Oncology and Therapy (2024), titled 'Dynamic Changes in Circulating Tumor Fraction as a Predictor of Real-World Clinical Outcomes...'. Journal name is factually wrong.

States 'Specific LOD not publicly disclosed,' but the validation paper reports a Limit of Blank (95th-percentile tumor fraction of 0.6% in 29 healthy subjects) and a molecular-response threshold of >=50% TF reduction. These quantitative thresholds are available and omitted.

sampleCitations points to the xF page (xf/), not an xM/xM-for-TRM page; given xM runs on xF this is defensible but the sample-collection details are inherited from xF rather than verified for the xM-for-TRM workflow specifically.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Counts a 2024 peer-reviewed paper + an ASCO 2025 abstract. The peer-reviewed paper's journal is mis-stated (Oncology and Therapy, not Clinical Cancer Research), so the supporting note is unreliable even if the count of 2 is plausible.

suspectClaims.currentValue: RUO (Research Use Only) ... as of June 2025; clinical availability expected later in 2025
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Forward-looking 2025 statement asserted in a June 2026 record. Tempus clinically launched xM MRD in the interim; whether xM-for-TRM (ICI monitoring) is still RUO, launched, or folded into the broader xM portfolio is unverified.

Tempus now markets 'xM' as an umbrella spanning xM MRD (tumor-naive CRC), xM (NeXT Personal Dx, tumor-informed WGS via Personalis), and xM Monitor / xM-for-TRM (ICI response). Risk that this record conflates or overlaps with a separately-tracked xM MRD entry; the precise current product name for the TRM variant should be reconfirmed.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Key validation: 'Dynamic Changes in Circulating Tumor Fraction as a Predictor of Real-World Clinical Outcomes in Solid Tumor Malignancy Patients Treated with Immunotherapy,' Oncology and Therapy, 2024 (PMC11333675). Molecular response (>=50% TF reduction) correlated with improved rwOS (HR 0.4, P=0.004) and rwPFS (HR 0.4, P=0.005).

Validation paper reports a Limit of Blank with 95th-percentile tumor fraction of 0.6% across 29 healthy subjects; molecular response defined as >=50% reduction in ctDNA tumor fraction. Algorithm linearity vs tumor-informed ctDNA Pearson R=0.9.

Runs on Tempus' xF (105-gene) and xF+ (523-gene) liquid biopsy panels; xM Monitor quantifies molecular changes in ctDNA tumor fraction by dynamically weighting somatic VAFs and copy-number variants, using germline information to inform estimates.

summary: Record accurately describes Tempus' xM-for-TRM tumor-naive ctDNA immunotherapy-response assay as it stood at the June 2025 RUO launch, but is now stale (still promises 'clinical availability later in 2025' at a June 2026 audit) and mis-cites the key validation paper's journal as Clinical Cancer Research when it is Oncology and Therapy 2024. LOD/sensitivity/specificity are null despite the paper reporting a limit-of-blank (95th-pct TF 0.6%) and a >=50% TF-reduction response threshold.

name: TSO 500
qualityRationale: Core facts (523 genes, DNA+RNA, TMB, MSI, FFPE tissue, RUO status, distinction from FDA-approved TSO Comprehensive IVD) all match Illumina's official documentation and are accurate/current. The product is still actively offered. Points deducted for: two unsourced round-number metrics (totalParticipants 50000, numPublications 200) that appear estimated/fabricated, an incomplete platform list, and several thin/unverifiable fields (TAT, reimbursement). Accuracy is good; evidence backing for the quantitative claims is weak.

defects.issue: Value 50000 is an unsourced round number with no citation. No public Illumina figure supports a specific '50,000 participants' count for TSO 500. Appears fabricated/estimated.
'200+' publications is an unsourced round figure. No citation provided; not verifiable against any registry. Looks like a placeholder estimate.
Listed as 'NextSeq 500/550/550Dx' only. TSO 500 portfolio also runs on NovaSeq 6000/6000Dx, NextSeq 1000/2000, and NovaSeq X Series per Illumina. Platform list is incomplete.

'7-10 days' is plausible as a lab-level estimate but unsourced; Illumina cites the sequencing+bioinformatics portion at ~2.5-4 days. Field conflates assay runtime with lab reporting and lacks a citation.
'Coverage varies' is vague and uninformative; for an RUO kit there is generally no direct reimbursement (billing is done by the lab running it), which the record does not clarify.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Round number with no source; no Illumina publication or registry cites a 50,000-participant figure for TSO 500.
Round, unsourced figure; not traceable to any publication count registry.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Hybrid-capture NGS covering 523 genes from DNA and RNA; ...
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Slightly imprecise: per Illumina, small variants are called across 523 genes (DNA), CNAs in 59 genes, and fusions/splice variants in 55 genes (RNA). The '523 from DNA and RNA' phrasing overstates RNA coverage.

proposedFills.proposedValue: NextSeq 500/550/550Dx, NextSeq 1000/2000, NovaSeq 6000/6000Dx, NovaSeq X Series

Hybrid-capture NGS: small variant (SNV/indel) detection across 523 genes from DNA, copy number alterations in 59 genes, fusions and splice variants in 55 genes from RNA; reports TMB and MSI genomic signatures from FFPE tissue.
Minimum 30 ng DNA and 40 ng RNA from FFPE tissue (>=2 mm3 tumor)
TSO Comprehensive (IVD version) received FDA approval August 2024 with two CDx indications: NTRK fusions (larotrectinib) and RET fusion-positive NSCLC (selpercatinib).

summary: Illumina's RUO TSO 500 record is factually accurate on its core CGP specs (523 genes, DNA+RNA, TMB/MSI, FFPE, RUO, correctly distinguished from the FDA-approved TSO Comprehensive IVD) and the product is still offered, but it carries two unsourced round-number metrics (50,000 participants, 200+ publications) that appear fabricated and several thin/incomplete fields (platform list, TAT, reimbursement).

name: GeneseeqPrime
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: 425-gene DNA-only hybrid-capture panel on FFPE tissue, reports TMB+MSI, FDA 510(k) cleared (K250003, announced Sep 2025), CE and NMPA — all verified against the vendor page, PRNewswire, GenomeWeb, and the FDA 510(k) record. Vendor-verified Jan 2026. Score held down by a now-stale "K-number pending" note, a slightly off clearance date, and several unsourced/promotional numeric claims (50,000 participants, 100+ publications, 7-10 day TAT, "competitive pricing expected").

defects.issue: Says 'K-number pending publication' — the K-number is now public: K250003 (FDA 510(k) database, accessdata.fda.gov/cdrh_docs/pdf25/K250003.pdf). Should be updated.
States 'Aug 2025'. FDA decision letter is dated Aug 29, 2025 but the clearance was publicly announced Sep 2, 2025. Acceptable but imprecise; no K-number cited.
Value 50000 has no citable source; appears to be an unverifiable cohort/validation figure. The FDA validation was across multiple US labs but no 50,000-patient figure is published.
'100+' publications claim is unsourced and unverifiable for this specific assay.
'7-10 days' turnaround not stated on vendor FDA-clearance page or 510(k) summary; unverified.
'Competitive pricing expected' is promotional/speculative, not a factual differentiator.
'Emerging - new FDA clearance' is vague; no MolDX/Medicare/CPT (e.g., 0037U / PLA) status confirmed.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No public source for a 50,000-patient figure tied to GeneseeqPrime validation; FDA summary describes multi-lab analytical/clinical validation without this number.
Unsourced; no citable count of publications specific to this assay.
Not stated on vendor clearance page or 510(k) summary; as a decentralized IVD kit, TAT depends on the running lab.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Plausible marketing claim but not independently verified; phrased as a superlative without source.
Stale — K250003 is now published; date is the letter date not the announcement date.

FDA 510(k) cleared as GENESEEQPRIME NGS Tumor Profiling Assay (FFPE) under K250003 (Sep 2025); qualitative IVD for solid malignant neoplasms detecting SNVs, indels, selected amplifications/translocations, plus TMB and MSI. Also CE-IVDD and NMPA (China) approved.
Hybrid-capture NGS of DNA from FFPE tumor tissue covering 425 cancer-related genes; detects SNVs, indels, selected gene amplifications and translocations; reports TMB and MSI (DNA-only assay).

summary: GeneseeqPrime is accurately represented as a 425-gene DNA-only FFPE-tissue CGP kit reporting TMB+MSI, with FDA 510(k) clearance (K250003, Sep 2025), CE-IVDD, and NMPA — all verified and the test is still actively offered. Main weaknesses are a now-stale 'K-number pending' note and several unsourced promotional metrics (50,000 participants, 100+ publications, 7-10 day TAT, expected pricing).

name: Reveal TRM
qualityRationale: Core science is accurate and current: tumor-naive methylation-based epigenetic tumor fraction on Guardant Infinity, tissue-free, 7-day TAT, pan-cancer response monitoring, RADIOHEAD validation. But several specific factual claims are off: the vendor name "Reveal TRM" is not an official Guardant product (it is marketed as "Guardant Reveal" therapy response monitoring, an expansion of the existing Reveal test, announced Nov 10 2025 — not a separate Dec 2025 "Reveal TRM" launch). Loci count (19,000) understates the vendor's stated >20,000. The "replaces Guardant360 Response" framing is unverified — G360 Response still appears in Guardant's portfolio and 2026 Annual Notice. LOD "0.01% TF" is unverified for this product (vendor page cites 0.005% in CRC MRD). These accuracy/currency gaps cap the score.

defects.issue: "Reveal TRM" is not an official Guardant product name. Guardant markets this as "Guardant Reveal" with therapy response monitoring (an expansion of the existing Reveal tissue-free test, announced Nov 10 2025). No vendor materials use "Reveal TRM" or position it as a distinct standalone product.
