States "commercially available December 2025" and "Launched December 2025." The Guardant press release announcing the Reveal therapy-response-monitoring expansion is dated Nov 10, 2025, not December.

Claims it "Replaces Guardant360 Response." This is unverified — Guardant360 Response still appears on guardantcomplete.com and in the 2026 Annual Notice to Physicians. The launch is framed by Guardant as an expansion of Reveal, not a confirmed retirement/replacement of G360 Response.
States "over 19,000 differentially methylated loci." Guardant's official figure is ">20,000 epigenomic signals." Understated/imprecise.
"0.01% TF" is not sourced to this product's response-monitoring use; the Guardant Reveal vendor page cites a limit of detection to 0.005% (CRC MRD context). The 0.01% figure is unverified for TRM.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No Guardant marketing or press material uses "Reveal TRM"; the official offering is "Guardant Reveal" therapy response monitoring.
Guardant press release announcing the expansion is dated Nov 10, 2025.

suspectClaims.currentValue: replacing genomic VAF-based Guardant360 Response with epigenetic tumor fraction (epiTF) quantification
suspectClaims.whySuspect: G360 Response still listed in Guardant portfolio and 2026 Annual Notice; replacement not confirmed by vendor.
Vendor states >20,000 epigenomic signals.
Vendor page cites LOD to 0.005% (CRC MRD); 0.01% TF for TRM unsourced.
RADIOHEAD data give concrete lead times (ICI response detected up to 5 months earlier; progression up to 18 months earlier, median 2.3 months) that could populate this field but are not captured.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Guardant Reveal (Therapy Response Monitoring)
Clinical LDT – therapy response monitoring expansion announced November 10, 2025

Epigenetic tumor fraction (epiTF) quantification on Guardant Infinity, tracking >20,000 epigenomic (methylation) signals / thousands of differentially methylated regions; mutation-agnostic, tissue-free.
RADIOHEAD: immunotherapy response detected up to 5 months earlier than imaging; disease progression detected up to 18 months earlier (median lead time 2.3 months).
LOD to 0.005% (Guardant Reveal, CRC MRD context); response monitoring measures relative epiTF change over time.

summary: The record accurately captures the underlying tumor-naive, methylation-based epigenetic tumor fraction (Guardant Infinity) science and RADIOHEAD-backed therapy response monitoring, but the product name "Reveal TRM" is an internal label (vendor calls it "Guardant Reveal" therapy response monitoring), the launch date is off (Nov not Dec 2025), the loci count (19,000 vs >20,000) and "replaces Guardant360 Response" claim are inaccurate/unverified, and the 0.01% TF LOD is unsourced for this use.

name: FoundationOne Tracker (TRM)
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: tumor-informed personalized ctDNA assay co-developed with Natera, mPCR-based, reports MTM/mL, Medicare coverage via Palmetto MolDX effective June 17 2023 for ICI response monitoring in all solid tumors, broad clinical launch Oct 2023, still offered under this name in 2026. Main deductions are for conflating the assay with Natera's Signatera (a distinct product), citing the generic MRD LCD L38779 without the ICI-specific qualifier in the structured Medicare field, a broken/truncated primary sample citation URL, and missing facts (2-16 variant panel size, FDA Breakthrough Device Designation, CPT/PLA code).

defects.issue: States Tracker 'uses Signatera platform' / 'Powered by Natera Signatera technology.' Misleading: FoundationOne Tracker is a distinct co-developed assay that designs a personalized 2-16 variant panel from FoundationOne CDx tumor profiling and runs Natera's mPCR ctDNA chemistry. It is not the Signatera product. Conflating the two is a factual inaccuracy.

Cites L38779 (the generic MolDX MRD LCD) with empty coveredIndications. Tracker's Medicare coverage is specifically for immune-checkpoint-inhibitor response monitoring across all solid tumors, effective June 17 2023. The structured field omits this ICI qualifier, overstating breadth of covered indications.
Does not state the personalized panel size (2-16 patient-specific variants selected from FoundationOne CDx), a defining and publicly documented spec of the assay.

Describes only 'CLIA clinical LDT – not FDA cleared/approved' and omits that FoundationOne Tracker was granted FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for its ctDNA monitoring assay, an established public fact relevant to regulatory status.
sampleCitations URL is truncated/broken (ends mid-path: '.../1iqyMQwcJ0jGCYhUxc'). Primary citation does not resolve.
'specific PLA code TBD' is stale; the test has been commercially billed since Oct 2023. CPT/PLA code unverified but should be researched rather than left TBD.
null; aggregate validation cohort size (IMpower131 / OMICO-MoST) not captured though publications are cited.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Partnership with Natera - uses Signatera platform
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Tracker is a separate co-developed assay (2-16 variants from FoundationOne CDx + Natera mPCR), not the Signatera product; vendor materials describe it as distinct from Signatera.

MTM/mL is a tumor-burden quantity, not a true analytical LoD; the underlying Natera mPCR chemistry is typically described at ~0.01% VAF LoD. The '≥5 MTM/mL' threshold is plausible as a detection threshold but is cited to a tech-spec PDF whose companion sampleCitations URL is broken, so it is not independently verifiable here.

suspectClaims.currentValue: median lead time of 11.5 months in complete responders (OMICO-MoST)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Specific 11.5-month figure not confirmed in retrieved sources; OMICO-MoST coverage emphasizes 4-week ctDNA decline predicting OS, not an 11.5-month imaging lead time. Possible misattribution.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Personalized tumor-informed ctDNA assay: 2-16 patient-specific somatic variants are selected from prior FoundationOne CDx tumor comprehensive genomic profiling, then tracked in plasma via Natera multiplex-PCR chemistry, quantifying mean tumor molecules per mL (MTM/mL) over serial draws for treatment-response monitoring in advanced solid tumors.
Monitoring of response to immune-checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) therapy for Medicare beneficiaries with all solid tumors (Palmetto GBA MolDX, effective June 17, 2023)
CLIA laboratory-developed test for treatment response monitoring (not FDA cleared/approved); granted FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for its ctDNA monitoring assay.

summary: Accurate, current record for a still-offered test (Foundation Medicine/Natera FoundationOne Tracker, Medicare-covered for ICI response monitoring since June 2023, broad launch Oct 2023). Main issues: it conflates the assay with Natera's distinct Signatera product, cites the generic MRD LCD L38779 without the ICI-specific qualifier, has a broken primary citation, and omits the 2-16 variant panel size and FDA Breakthrough Device Designation.

name: Shield
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate, current, and well-sourced against primary references: FDA PMA P230009 (July 26 2024, first blood test for primary CRC screening), ECLIPSE 83.1% sensitivity / 89.6% specificity / 13.2% advanced-adenoma sensitivity (NEJM PMID 38477985), NCCN inclusion (June 2025) and ACS inclusion (May 27 2026), Medicare ADLT $1,495 rate, CPT 0537U (PLA, Shield-specific), and Quest national ordering (March 2026) / TRICARE / VA coverage — all independently corroborated. Single ecd-1 record (no duplicate). Score held below 90 due to a stale list price and a minor internal cptCode inconsistency, not factual errors.

defects.issue: Listed as $895 with 2023-2024 AAFP/CNN citations. Current and consistently reported cash-pay/list price is $1,495 (matches the ADLT Medicare rate). $895 reflects an outdated 2024 launch figure and is now stale/misleading.
Set to null while the top-level cptCode field is 0537U. Internal inconsistency; should be 0537U for consistency.

Mild inconsistency between fields: sampleVolumeMl=40 and sampleVolume='4 tubes (min 2 mL plasma)' coexist with sampleCollectionNotes stating FDA approved reduction from 4 to 2 tubes (Q1 2026) and sampleTubeCount=2. The 40 mL / 4-tube descriptors are superseded by the 2-tube configuration.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Multiple 2026 sources (CLP, Quest partnership, Guardant) report the cash-pay price as $1,495, identical to the ADLT Medicare rate. $895 appears to be the superseded 2024 introductory price.

100% sensitivity for stages II-IV reflects small per-stage subgroup counts in ECLIPSE (few late-stage cases in an average-risk screening cohort) rather than a robust population estimate; technically traceable to the FDA SSED but should be read with caution given wide confidence intervals.
tatNotes cite a 15-day median in a 20,000-patient real-world study; the headline '~14 days' is slightly inconsistent with its own supporting note (15 days).

proposedFills.proposedValue: As of March 2026 Shield is orderable through Quest Diagnostics' national network with a $1,495 cash-pay rate when not covered; ACS inclusion (May 2026) and NCCN inclusion (June 2025) are expected to accelerate commercial payer adoption.

summary: The Shield (ecd-1) record is accurate, current, and well-sourced — FDA PMA, ECLIPSE performance, NCCN/ACS guideline inclusion, Medicare ADLT, CPT 0537U, and Quest/TRICARE/VA availability all verify against primary sources. Main issues are a stale list price ($895 should be $1,495) and a minor internal cptCode inconsistency; no duplicate.

name: IUrisure
qualityRationale: The test is real and still offered: IUrisure (urine AL021918.2 + VIM dual-methylation MSP, single-cancer urothelial) by Wuhan Ammunition Life-tech, with an active recurrence-surveillance trial (NCT06564363) and a 2025 validation publication. The cited PMID 37455255 is genuine (CMJ 2024, DOI 10.1097/CM9.0000000000002783) but it is a brief 3-page Correspondence (vol 137(2):232-234) proof-of-concept, NOT the 477-patient validation study the record describes. The record's headline performance figures (93.94% sensitivity, 92.02% specificity, 94.44% Ta-stage, n=477 with a 264 UC / 213 control breakdown) do not match any locatable published source; the peer-reviewed validation (Clinica Chimica Acta 2025; AUA 2025 GG01-11) reports 91.19%/92.16% sensitivity and 90.03%/92.66% specificity across training/validation cohorts and 79.55%/90.00% Ta-stage. Numbers appear conflated or invented, and PPV/NPV citations are vague placeholders. NMPA-approval claim is unverified. Scope (single-cancer, not MCED) is correctly characterized.

defects.issue: Headline numbers (93.94% sensitivity, 92.02% specificity, 94.44% Ta-stage) cannot be verified against any located source. Cited PMID 37455255 is a 3-page CMJ correspondence (pages 232-234), not the n=477 study claimed. Peer-reviewed validation (Clinica Chimica Acta 2025, S0009898125000439; AUA 2025) instead reports ~91-92% sensitivity and ~90-93% specificity. Figures appear conflated or fabricated.

Claims a 477-sample validation (264 UC: 199 bladder/39 renal pelvis/26 ureter; 213 controls) attributed to PMID 37455255. That PMID is a brief correspondence and does not contain this cohort. The n=477 breakdown is not verifiable in any located publication.

States numbers are 'from clinical validation study (n=477) published in Chinese Medical Journal 2024' — the CMJ 2024 item is correspondence, not a validation study. The actual dual-cohort validation is a separate 2025 Clinica Chimica Acta paper not cited.

Listed as 1; at least two relevant peer-reviewed sources exist (CMJ 2024 correspondence + Clinica Chimica Acta 2025 validation), plus AUA 2025 abstract. Understated and citing the wrong paper for validation claims.
PPV 31% and NPV 99% cite only 'Clinical trial data' with no traceable source; unverifiable placeholders.
'NMPA approved (China)' is unverified — no NMPA registration record located. May be in-development/registration-stage given the still-recruiting recurrence trial NCT06564363.
Cites only ChiCTR2200066864; the active, more relevant surveillance/recurrence trial NCT06564363 (ClinicalTrials.gov) is not referenced.
$450 USD-equivalent given with no source; unverifiable.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Published validation reports 91.19%/92.16% (training/validation); 93.94% not found in any source and is attributed to a PMID that is a brief correspondence.
Published validation reports 90.03%/92.66%; 92.02% not located in cited or other sources.
Published Ta-stage sensitivity is 79.55%/90.00% across cohorts; 94.44% is higher than any reported figure and unverifiable.
Attributed to PMID 37455255, which is a 3-page correspondence without this cohort; the n=477 / 264 UC + 213 control breakdown is not in any located publication.
No NMPA registration record found; an active recruiting recurrence trial suggests the test may still be in validation/registration rather than fully approved.
Sourced only to 'Clinical trial data'; no traceable citation and population-dependent figure stated without prevalence basis.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 91.19% (training) / 92.16% (validation cohort)
90.03% (training) / 92.66% (validation cohort)
Ta-stage sensitivity 79.55% (training) / 90.00% (validation)
NCT06564363 — The Efficacy of the IUrisure for Detection of Recurrent Urothelial Carcinoma (recurrence surveillance vs cystoscopy/pathology)

Original dual-marker proof-of-concept: Wu et al., Chin Med J 2024;137(2):232-234, DOI 10.1097/CM9.0000000000002783 (brief correspondence). Full validation: Clinica Chimica Acta 2025, S0009898125000439.
In the surveillance cohort, IUrisure predicted recurrence with 100% sensitivity and 82.86% specificity, with a median lead time of 4.5 months before clinical confirmation.

summary: IUrisure is a real, still-offered single-cancer (urothelial) urine dual-methylation (AL021918.2 + VIM) test with an active recurrence-surveillance trial (NCT06564363) and a 2025 validation publication. The record's headline performance figures (93.94%/92.02%/94.44%, n=477) and its NMPA-approval claim are unverifiable and conflict with the actual peer-reviewed numbers (~91-92% sensitivity, ~90-93% specificity, 79.55%/90.00% Ta-stage), and the cited PMID is a brief correspondence rather than the validation study claimed.

name: IEsohunter
qualityRationale: Core clinical claims are accurate and well-sourced: overall sensitivity 87.4%, specificity 93.3%, per-stage sensitivities (78.5/87.3/92.5/96.9%), AUC 0.903, HGIN ~52%, n=1,116, 5 China centers, NCT05680077, and two real PMIDs (38890756 validation; 37650127 marker discovery) all match the peer-reviewed J Hematol Oncol 2024 study. Markers (OTOP2+KCNA3), method (multiplex qPCR, algorithm-free), and single-cancer esophageal scope are correct. Points deducted because: (1) the vendor citation URL (whaltglobal.com) does not match the live vendor domain (waltglobal.com) and is effectively stale/dead; (2) CE-IVD/EU availability and NMPA approval claims are asserted without any regulatory-database citation and could not be independently verified; (3) PPV/NPV are labeled as calculated/clinical-trial-derived but no explicit prevalence assumption is given, making 18%/98% only loosely supportable.

defects.issue: Cited vendor URL https://www.whaltglobal.com/iesohunter/ does not match the live vendor domain. The active product page is https://www.waltglobal.com/product-category/iesohunter/ (WALT = Wuhan Ammunition Life-tech). The whaltglobal.com domain appears stale; waltglobal.com product-category root currently resolves to a domain-sale page in one fetch, indicating the site is in flux. Citation should point to a stable, verified vendor page.

Claims 'NMPA approved (China); CE-IVD (EU)' but no regulatory citation is provided and neither the NMPA registration nor a CE-IVD listing could be verified through public sources. NCT05680077 is described as the registration/validation trial of the kit, suggesting it may still be a regulatory-pathway/registrational product rather than a fully approved, EU-marketed IVD. Approval status and EU availability are unverifiable as stated.

PPV 18% and NPV 98% are cited as 'calculated from clinical trial data' / 'clinical trial data' but no prevalence assumption is stated. In the case-enriched trial cohort (334 EC of 1,116) PPV would be far higher; in a true high-risk screening setting the real PPV depends on assumed prevalence, which is undisclosed. Values are plausible but the derivation is non-transparent.

Source study frames the assay as 'non-invasive diagnosis of esophageal cancer' and a diagnostic aid in symptomatic/high-risk referral populations (5 tertiary referral centers, case-enriched), not a population screening test. With HGIN (precancer) sensitivity only ~52%, the record's framing as a screening alternative to endoscopy slightly overstates early-detection performance for precancerous lesions.

Vendor page states 92.98% specificity; record uses 93.3% (the study's 'vs all controls' figure). Both trace to the same study but the small discrepancy reflects different control subsets and should be noted.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No regulatory citation; NMPA registration and CE-IVD listing not independently verifiable. NCT05680077 reads as the registrational validation trial, suggesting approval may be pending or recent rather than established.
EU availability depends on the unverified CE-IVD claim. Vendor materials emphasize China high-incidence screening; no evidence of active EU commercial distribution was found.
Stated as calculated from trial data but no prevalence assumption disclosed; the trial cohort was case-enriched (~30% EC), so 18% implies an unstated low-prevalence screening assumption.
'Approximately $500 USD equivalent' has no citation; pricing for a China/EU IVD is hard to verify and may be an estimate.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://www.waltglobal.com/product-category/iesohunter/
proposedFills.confidenceNote: live WALT vendor product page found via search

proposedFills.proposedValue: Vendor reports 92.98% specificity; study reports 93.3% vs all controls (healthy, benign GI, other malignancies). Per published validation, ESCC sensitivity 87.14% and precancerous-lesion sensitivity 51.35%.
Note that the primary validation paper (PMID 38890756) received a published Correction in Dec 2024 (PMID 39709429, doi:10.1186/s13045-024-01653-3); verify which figures the correction affects.

summary: IEsohunter (WALT / Wuhan Ammunition Life-tech) is a single-cancer esophageal cfDNA methylation assay (OTOP2+KCNA3); the record's clinical performance, study design, PMIDs, and trial ID accurately match the peer-reviewed J Hematol Oncol 2024 validation, but the vendor citation URL is stale (live domain is waltglobal.com) and the NMPA/CE-IVD approval plus EU availability claims are uncited and unverifiable. A published Correction to the primary paper (PMID 39709429) exists and should be reconciled.

name: PGDx elio plasma focus Dx
qualityRationale: Core technical facts are accurate and well-cited: 33 genes (SNV/indel), 5 CNA genes (CCND1, CD274, ERBB2, FGFR2, MET), 3 translocation genes (ALK, NTRK1, RET), kitted IVD on Illumina NextSeq, FDA De Novo Aug 2, 2024, still actively offered (co-commercialized with Illumina as of March 2026). Deductions: internal contradiction in the De Novo identifier (DEN230046 cited vs DEN240016 in notes), the "sensitivity 96" field actually represents assay/clinical success rate not analytical sensitivity, and specificity/LoD/list price are null. No fabricated genes or false ownership.

defects.issue: Inconsistent FDA De Novo number: sampleCitations links to DEN230046.pdf while fdaStatusNotes states DEN240016. At most one is correct; the DEN230046 sample-collection PDF citation appears mismatched to this device. The record contradicts itself on the regulatory identifier.

Value 96 is labeled as sensitivity but the note says 'clinical success rate >96%' — success/QC yield rate is not analytical sensitivity. Mislabels a throughput metric as a performance metric; misleading.
Both null. FDA De Novo summary contains analytical PPA/specificity and LoD by variant type; these are obtainable from the FDA decision summary but not populated.

Omits MSI. PGDx elio plasma focus product line advertises proprietary MSI detection; unclear whether the FDA-authorized Dx variant reports MSI. Worth verifying against the FDA labeling — if MSI is in the intended use it is a missing biomarker; if not, current omission is correct.
numPublications=1 with 'AACR 2025 presentation' — a peer-reviewed analytical validation poster/abstract exists on Labcorp Oncology site; evidence backing is thin and likely understated.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: DEN230046 conflicts with DEN240016 stated in fdaStatusNotes; the device was authorized in 2024 (DEN24xxxx series is plausible), making the DEN230046 link likely the wrong De Novo record.
Sourced to 'clinical success rate >96%' which is an assay success/yield rate, not analytical or clinical sensitivity. Variant-level sensitivity (PPA) differs by alteration type per FDA summary.
Date is correct, but pairing it with the unverified/contradictory DEN number undermines the regulatory citation; needs the single correct DEN identifier.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Yes — actively offered; in March 2026 Illumina and Labcorp expanded their collaboration to co-commercialize the FDA-authorized PGDx elio plasma focus Dx liquid biopsy.
FDA De Novo marketing authorization granted August 2, 2024; first and only kitted pan-solid tumor liquid biopsy IVD
33 genes for SNVs/indels; CNA amplifications in 5 genes (CCND1, CD274, ERBB2, FGFR2, MET); translocations in 3 genes (ALK, NTRK1, RET)

summary: PGDx elio plasma focus Dx is accurately characterized (33-gene kitted FDA De Novo liquid biopsy, Aug 2 2024) and is still actively offered and being co-commercialized with Illumina as of 2026. Main defects: a self-contradictory FDA De Novo number (DEN230046 vs DEN240016) and a "sensitivity=96" field that actually reports assay success rate, not analytical sensitivity.

name: FoundationOne Liquid CDx
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and densely cited against primary sources (FDA PMA P200006 label, vendor page, CMS CLFS). Core technical facts all verify: 324 genes analyzed / 311 reported, 8 rearrangement + 3 CNA genes, bTMB + MSI, PMA Aug 2020, CPT 0239U PLA, ADLT, $3,500, 7-day TAT (vendor-verified 2026-02-14). Single record under this id (no duplicate). Main deduction: fdaCompanionDxCount of 11 is stale and internally inconsistent — the vendor's own page now enumerates ~15 distinct CDx therapy indications, and the record's own notes append a June 2026 approval without updating the count. Minor: ctDNA tumor fraction now reported by vendor but absent from biomarkersReported.

defects.issue: Value is 11 but the vendor's official test page now lists approximately 15 distinct CDx therapy indications (5 NSCLC, 5 prostate, 2 breast, 2 pan-tumor NTRK, 1 CRC). The count is stale; the field's own notes even document a June 2026 TALZENNA+XTANDI approval and a Jan 2026 BRAFTOVI+cetuximab mCRC approval that postdate the count of 11, making it internally inconsistent.
Vendor page now lists ctDNA tumor fraction as a reported output; it is not in the biomarkersReported array (lists SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Rearrangements, bTMB, MSI).

Free-text fdaStatus singles out two CDx approvals (Akeega Feb 2026, encorafenib+binimetinib NSCLC) but omits the more recent June 2026 TALZENNA+XTANDI and Jan 2026 mCRC approvals captured elsewhere in the record — narrative is partial/uneven rather than wrong.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor official page enumerates ~15 distinct CDx therapy indications as of June 2026; count appears frozen at an earlier date while the notes field has accreted newer approvals.
Single citation is the 2020 Woodhouse PLoS One validation paper; the specific figure of 51 is not directly traceable to a public source and may be an internal estimate.

proposedFills.proposedValue: SNVs, Indels, Select CNAs, Select Rearrangements, bTMB, MSI, ctDNA tumor fraction

summary: FoundationOne Liquid CDx is still actively offered and the record is accurate and well-cited on all core technical, regulatory, and reimbursement facts. The main defect is a stale, internally inconsistent fdaCompanionDxCount of 11 (vendor now lists ~15 CDx therapy indications), with a minor gap omitting ctDNA tumor fraction from reported biomarkers.

name: OncoCompass Target
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced: 168-gene ctDNA capture-based NGS panel for NSCLC/pan-solid (confirmed by vendor page, GenomeWeb, and the 2021 Merck KGaA CDx announcement), blood-only sample type (corrected in a prior audit), and a real peer-reviewed validation (Fan et al., Cancer Medicine 2024, DOI 10.1002/cam4.70078, PMID 39385536) plus the real BENEFIT trial (Lancet Respir Med 2018, PMID 30017884, NCT02282267). Currency is good. Main weaknesses: (1) the headline performance/LOD figures and the 101-gene cohort are validation for a DIFFERENT panel (101-gene) than the offered 168-gene LDT — the record flags this but still surfaces blended numbers; (2) several distinctive claims (100% specificity for the 168-gene panel, sensitivity 96%+, 129 publications, 9-day TAT, pricing) rest solely on unverifiable vendor email data; (3) FDA/CDx status is correctly stated as none in the US, but TMB reporting is unaddressed.

defects.issue: Headline analytical performance (sensitivity 96%+, specificity 99.9-100%, LOD values) is drawn from the 101-gene panel validation (Fan 2024, n=904) and vendor email, not from a validation of the actually-offered 168-gene LDT. The record notes the panel mismatch but still presents these as the test's performance, which conflates two distinct products.

'129 publications' / '100+ clinical studies' is sourced only to a vendor-provided list (Dec 2025) with no independent citation; unverifiable and likely conflates platform-wide Burning Rock literature with this specific assay.
'Vendor reports 100% for 168-gene panel' is an unsourced vendor claim; 100% specificity is implausibly clean and should be treated as suspect.

States CLIA LDT / CE-IVD; Burning Rock is a China-based lab and the 168-gene test is primarily a China/international LDT — 'CLIA LDT' framing may overstate US lab availability. Clinical availability field more accurately says China + international markets.
Lists MSI but not TMB; whether the 168-gene panel reports TMB is unaddressed (168 genes is generally below the ~300-gene threshold for reliable TMB, so omission may be correct but is unstated).
'Varies by region' with vendor-only note provides no actual figure; effectively a placeholder.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: 100% specificity is statistically implausible and sourced only to vendor email; the 99.9% figure actually belongs to the 101-gene cohort, a different product.
Sourced only to a vendor-provided publication list; no independent verification and likely platform-wide rather than assay-specific.

Training-cohort sensitivity reported elsewhere as 79.9% (GenomeWeb/PMC6864073 panel validation); 96%+ appears to be a cherry-picked by-variant analytical figure from the 101-gene panel, not clinical sensitivity of the 168-gene test.
Vendor-email-only; no public source confirms a 9-calendar-day turnaround for this assay.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Co-development companion diagnostic for tepotinib (MET inhibitor) with Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany, for mainland China market (announced Nov 2021); OncoCompass Target also participated in the FDA-led SEQC2 consortium.

Fan F et al., 'Analytical and clinical validation of a NGS panel in detecting targetable variants from ctDNA of metastatic NSCLC patients,' Cancer Medicine 2024 (101-gene panel, n=904): LOD 0.38% SNV / 0.33% InDel / 0.33% fusion; by-type sensitivity 98.3% SNV, 100% InDel, 100% fusion; ctDNA-tissue concordance 72.2% (457/633). Note: validates the 101-gene panel, NOT the 168-gene LDT.
Hybrid capture-based NGS targeting 168 clinically actionable predictive biomarkers from cfDNA/ctDNA, including MET.

summary: OncoCompass Target is a real, still-offered 168-gene ctDNA NGS panel from China-based Burning Rock Dx for NSCLC/pan-solid tumors; the record's gene count, sample type, and the Merck-KGaA tepotinib CDx context are accurate and the cited Fan 2024 (PMID 39385536) and BENEFIT (PMID 30017884) sources are genuine. Its main weakness is that headline performance/LOD figures and the '129 publications' claim derive from the 101-gene panel and vendor email rather than from a validation of the actual 168-gene LDT, making several quantitative fields unverifiable.

name: Harbinger RESOLVE
qualityRationale: The record is current (captures the April 2026 RESOLVE rebrand, $100M Flagship round, and ASCO 2026 suspected-cancer repositioning) and the headline ASCO 2026 performance figures (54.8% sensitivity, ~98% specificity, LR 29.6, 209 adults aged 45-79, 155 cancer / 54 non-cancer) are verified accurate against the vendor PR. The repositioning from asymptomatic MCED screening to diagnostic aid for suspected cancer is correctly captured. Score is held down by a material methodology error (claims protein+cfDNA when the Harbinger HX platform is cfDNA methylation + ML per vendor, Fierce Biotech and 360Dx), internal inconsistency on cancer-type count (15+ vs ~12), near-total absence of citations on individual fields (almost every *Citations field is null), and null clinicalTrials despite two known NCT IDs.

defects.issue: Claims 'Blood-based protein + cfDNA MCED'. Harbinger's HX platform is cfDNA methylation + machine learning, not protein biomarkers. No vendor, Fierce Biotech, or 360Dx source supports a protein-biomarker component; this appears fabricated/erroneous.

States 'multi-analyte platform combining protein biomarkers and cfDNA analysis'. Same error — the platform uses methylation-based cfDNA analysis + ML to screen ~12 cancer types (360Dx, Fierce Biotech). The protein-biomarker claim is unsupported.

Lists '15+ tumor types' but fdaStatusNotes and vendor/360Dx sources say ~12 cancer types. Internal inconsistency; the 15+ figure traces to the 2023 case-control pilot, not the current RESOLVE product.

Describes the test as illustrating 'real-world screening' performance gap, but RESOLVE is explicitly NOT a screening test (repositioned to suspected-cancer evaluation). The framing conflates the retired screening positioning with the current diagnostic-aid product.
Null despite two named, citable trials: CORE-HH (NCT05435066) and PROCARES (NCT07046260). totalParticipants and validationCohortSize also null though CORE-HH analyzed 209 adults.

Top-level sensitivity=25.8 reflects the early-stage CORE-HH screening cohort, while the headline current-product (ASCO 2026 suspected-cancer) sensitivity is 54.8%. The single numeric field is ambiguous about which population it represents; only the notes disambiguate.
Duplicated/garbled text: 'Aetna: Not covered (CPB 0352). Aetna: denies (CPB 0352 — not covered...).' Redundant phrasing.
Nearly every per-field citation (cancerTypesCitations, sensitivityCitations, specificityCitations, fcc.) is null; evidence is concentrated in free-text notes rather than structured citations.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor and trade press (Fierce Biotech, 360Dx) consistently describe cfDNA methylation + ML only; no source corroborates a protein-biomarker analyte.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 15+ tumor types including hepatobiliary, upper GI, colorectal, lung
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Current RESOLVE/HarbingerHx product is described as ~12 cancer types; 15+ derives from the older 2023 case-control pilot and contradicts the record's own fdaStatusNotes.

This is the early-stage screening-cohort figure; the current suspected-cancer product (the actual RESOLVE indication) reports 54.8% sensitivity at ASCO 2026, so the headline value misrepresents the live product.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Blood-based cfDNA methylation MCED (Harbinger HX platform: methylation-based cell-free DNA analysis + machine learning)
Methylation-based cell-free DNA analysis combined with machine learning (Harbinger HX platform) to detect multiple cancer types and infer tissue of origin
~12 cancer types
CORE-HH (NCT05435066, completed); PROCARES (NCT07046260, ongoing, >88% enrolled as of mid-2026)
209 (CORE-HH prospective suspected-cancer cohort: 155 with confirmed cancer, 54 without)
Not yet commercially available; first RESOLVE product expected to launch H2 2026

summary: Harbinger RESOLVE is an investigational, pre-commercial cfDNA methylation + ML multi-cancer test (Harbinger HX platform, ~12 cancers) repositioned in 2026 as a diagnostic aid for suspected cancer (not asymptomatic screening), with verified ASCO 2026 CORE-HH performance (54.8% sensitivity, ~98% specificity, LR 29.6). The record is reasonably current but mislabels the methodology as 'protein + cfDNA', is internally inconsistent on cancer-type count (15+ vs ~12), and lacks structured citations and known NCT trial IDs.

name: TrueMRD
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: Veracyte acquired C2i Genomics ($70M + $25M milestones, closed Feb 2024), branded TrueMRD, WGS tumor-informed method, MolDX Medicare coverage announced May 15 2026 for MIBC recurrence monitoring, commercial launch June 1 2026 — all confirmed by Veracyte investor PR, businesswire, and GenomeWeb. Performance (91% sens / 92% spec, 131-day lead) traces to a real Eur Urol publication (PMID 38811314). Score is held down by internal contradictions: the top-level fdaStatus/medicareCoverage fields were updated to COVERED/launched, but the coverageCrossReference block, reimbursement, regulatoryStatusNotes, indicationsNotes, and clinicalAvailability/clinicalTrials notes still describe the test as pre-commercial / NOT_COVERED / 'expected H1 2026' — stale and now self-inconsistent. A wrong vendor citation (Guardant URL) and a softly-sourced LOD round out the defects.

defects.issue: Entire block (lastVerified 2026-01-15) still states medicare NOT_COVERED, status 'Pre-commercial research platform', and analysis 'Not yet available for clinical use... expected H1 2026'. This directly contradicts the now-correct top-level medicareCoverage (COVERED, MolDX, May 2026) and the June 1 2026 launch. Stale and self-contradictory.
Cited as 'https://www.guardanthealth.com/' — wrong vendor (Guardant) on a Veracyte/C2i test. Copy-paste artifact (same URL appears on neighboring records). Should be a Veracyte source.
Says 'Not applicable' with reimbursementNote 'Pre-commercial' — stale; test is now commercially launched (June 1 2026) with MolDX coverage.
Still reads 'Pre-commercial investigational WGS-based MRD platform... First clinical test for MIBC expected H1 2026' — outdated now that the test has launched.
indicationsNotes says 'launch expected H1 2026' (now occurred); clinicalAvailability is correct but adjacent notes (cptCodesNotes 'None (pre-commercial)') are stale.
LOD '0.001% TF (10 ppm)' sourced only to 'Veracyte investor communications; C2i Genomics technical specifications' — not a verifiable public/peer-reviewed citation.
All null despite COVERED status; MolDX policy/CPT identifiers not captured.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Sourced only to 'investor communications / technical specifications,' not a peer-reviewed LOD. WGS-based MRD LOD is typically reported as a tumor-fraction range and the precise 10 ppm figure is not independently verifiable from public literature.
Listed as 100 (TOMBOLA), but the record also cites larger CRC (Frydendahl) and rectal (Manca) cohorts; the single value of 100 understates the validation evidence and is tied only to one trial.
Vendor page states '<4 mL plasma' and notes 1-2 mL plasma possible; 4 mL is the upper bound, not a verified fixed requirement.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://www.veracyte.com/innovation/truemrd-platform/
COVERED
Medicare covered (MolDX) for MIBC recurrence monitoring; commercial launch June 1, 2026

CLIA LDT, not FDA-cleared. Commercially launched June 1, 2026 as Veracyte's first MRD test (whole-genome MRD) on the C2i Genomics platform; MolDX Medicare coverage for MIBC recurrence monitoring announced May 15, 2026.
Veracyte acquired C2i Genomics for up to $95M ($70M in stock at closing Feb 5, 2024, plus up to $25M in performance milestones).

summary: TrueMRD (Veracyte, ex-C2i Genomics) is a real, currently offered tumor-informed WGS MRD test — MolDX Medicare coverage (May 15 2026) and June 1 2026 commercial launch for muscle-invasive bladder cancer are correctly captured. The record is undermined by stale, self-contradictory coverage/regulatory sub-blocks (coverageCrossReference, reimbursement, regulatoryStatusNotes still say 'pre-commercial / NOT_COVERED / H1 2026') and a wrong Guardant sampleCitations URL.

name: Hedera Profiling 2 ctDNA Test Panel
qualityRationale: The record is well-researched and accurate against both the official vendor page (hederadx.com/profiling-2-ctdna-test-panel) and the peer-reviewed analytical validation (Journal of Molecular Diagnostics, Dec 2025; the cited PMID 41015181 resolves to a real Hedera Profiling 2 study). Gene count (32), DNA-only liquid CGP, biomarkers (SNV/indel/CNV/fusion/MSI), sensitivity (96.92%), specificity (99.67%), LOD (0.5% VAF), cfDNA input (10ng min/30ng rec), 137 clinical samples, 5-day TAT, Streck BCT tubes, and the performance-studies-only/CE-IVDR-local-validation regulatory framing all match sources. Minor deductions for an over-specific platform field, a broken sample citation URL, and US-centric fields (FDA/CPT/Medicare) that are N/A for this EU performance-studies kit but not explicitly marked.

defects.issue: Record states 'Illumina NextSeq 2000' specifically, but the vendor page lists the supported instruments more broadly as 'Illumina NextSeq and NovaSeq Series.' Overly narrow.
URL is truncated/broken: 'https://www.dlongwood.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Hedera-' ends mid-filename and does not resolve to a usable source.
Listed as 1 tube, but vendor specifies a minimum input of 5 mL plasma; a single Streck BCT (~10 mL whole blood) is plausible but the plasma-volume requirement is the binding spec and is not captured.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor page states the assay runs on 'Illumina NextSeq and NovaSeq Series' instruments generally; pinning it to NextSeq 2000 is narrower than the official spec and may mislead labs running NovaSeq.

Accurate but worth flagging: the panel itself is NOT CE-IVD — it is 'for performance studies only.' Only the Hedera Prime software is CE-IVD. The combined phrasing could be misread as the kit being CE-IVD.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Illumina NextSeq and NovaSeq Series
Minimum 5 mL plasma; 10 ng cfDNA minimum, 30 ng recommended
5 calendar days sample-to-report; ~3 hours hands-on time
Analytical Validation of a Pan-Cancer NGS Assay for In-House Liquid Biopsy Testing: An International Multicenter Study, J Mol Diagn 2025 (also medRxiv 2024.10.17.24313324)

summary: Accurate, well-sourced record for Hedera Dx's 32-gene DNA-only ctDNA CGP kit; still offered under this name as a performance-studies-only / CE-IVDR-local-validation product (panel not CE-IVD; only the Hedera Prime software is). Main issues are minor: an over-specific platform field (should be NextSeq/NovaSeq Series), a broken sample-citation URL, and incomplete plasma-input capture.

name: Tempus xM MRD
qualityRationale: Record correctly identifies the test as Tempus's tumor-naive, blood-only, methylation+genomic-variant CRC MRD assay and correctly distinguishes it from the separate tumor-informed xM (NeXT Personal Dx) product — a non-trivial distinction Tempus's own naming muddles. Core sensitivity (61.1% landmark, 83.3% longitudinal) and longitudinal specificity (89.5%) match the peer-reviewed CCR 2025 paper. But the headline landmark specificity (94%) directly contradicts the published value of 87.9%, the LOD (0.01% VAF) appears to be a generic placeholder rather than a sourced figure for this methylation-based assay, performance citations point to BusinessWire press releases rather than the peer-reviewed CCR paper, and tumor-tissue requirement is left null despite being definitively "no" for a tumor-naive assay. Coverage block is reasonably current (UHC 2026 update present).

defects.issue: Record states 94% landmark specificity, but the peer-reviewed source (Clin Cancer Res 2025;31(2):328-338, GALAXY subset, n=80) reports 87.9% (29/33). The 94% value traces only to a BusinessWire press release and is contradicted by the published paper.

'0.01% VAF' (=100 ppm) is not a value from the published GALAXY paper or vendor spec for this methylation-based tumor-naive assay. lodNotes admits it is approximate 'based on trial performance.' Likely an unsourced placeholder; possibly conflated with the separate NeXT Personal Dx product (which Tempus markets at 1.67 ppm).
Performance figures cite BusinessWire press releases, not the peer-reviewed validation (CCR 2025;31(2):328-338, PMC equivalent). The authoritative publication exists and is not cited.

Null, but the assay is explicitly tumor-naive (blood-only) per vendor and publication — should be false. Leaving null on a defining attribute is an unverified gap that mislabels a core differentiator.
Null; assay applies CHIP/germline filtering algorithmically without a matched-normal requirement per vendor description — should be false.
'Contact vendor for current billing guidance' — vague placeholder; an MRD CRC LDT typically bills under a MAAA/Z-code (MolDX), unverified here.
fdaStatus says 'as of 2025'; record otherwise reflects 2026 data — currency wording stale. numPublications:4 unverified against an enumerated source list.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Published GALAXY subset (CCR 2025;31(2):328-338) reports 87.9% (29/33). 94% appears only in a press release and conflicts with peer review.
Not reported in the methylation-based GALAXY validation; lodNotes calls it approximate. Possible conflation with the distinct NeXT Personal Dx (1.67 ppm WGS assay).

suspectClaims.currentValue: PARTIAL / CPB 0715 / covered for CRC Stage II-III
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Aetna CPB 0715 generally lists most ctDNA MRD assays as experimental/investigational; a specific 'covered' status for Tempus xM CRC is unverified and may be carried over from a generic ctDNA/Signatera entry.
L38779 is a MolDX MRD LCD; whether Tempus xM is specifically listed under it (vs. pending technical assessment) is not confirmed from a primary source in the record.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Landmark specificity 87.9% (29/33 nonrecurrent patients MRD- at landmark) per published GALAXY (CIRCULATE-Japan) subset analysis, n=80 resected stage II-III CRC. Landmark sensitivity 61.1% (22/36).

Kotani et al., A Tumor-Naive ctDNA Assay Detects MRD in Resected Stage II/III CRC: Subset Analysis from GALAXY/CIRCULATE-Japan. Clin Cancer Res 2025;31(2):328-338. https://aacrjournals.org/clincancerres/article/31/2/328/751096/
false

Tumor-naive (tissue-free), plasma-only MRD assay delivering a binary MRD call from combined DNA-methylation and genomic-variant classifiers with algorithmic CHIP/germline/artifact filtering; landmark (4 wks post-op) uses both methylation and variant data, longitudinal surveillance uses methylation only. Marketed for resected stage II-III colorectal cancer.

summary: The record correctly characterizes Tempus xM as a still-offered, tumor-naive, methylation+variant blood-only CRC MRD assay and rightly separates it from the tumor-informed xM (NeXT Personal Dx). However the headline landmark specificity (94%) contradicts the peer-reviewed GALAXY value of 87.9%, the LOD is an unsourced placeholder, the validation is cited to press releases instead of the CCR 2025 paper, and the defining tumor-tissue/matched-normal flags are left null.

name: cobas KRAS Mutation Test
qualityRationale: The record correctly identifies the test, vendor, single-gene KRAS PCR method, platform (cobas 4800), and CDx status for cetuximab/panitumumab in mCRC. But it contains multiple critical factual errors: it is labeled Blood/Plasma and Tumor-agnostic with requiresTumorTissue=No, when the FDA-approved test (PMA P140023) is a tissue-only (FFPET) assay specific to colorectal cancer. The LoD ("1% MAF") and the FDA citation URL (P110027C, a different PMA) are also wrong. Several performance/scale fields (sensitivity 95, specificity 99, 20,000 participants, 100+ publications) are presented without correct sourcing. Net: structurally on the right test but materially misleading on sample type, approach, and detection limit.

defects.issue: Listed as 'Blood/Plasma'. The cobas KRAS Mutation Test (PMA P140023) is a tissue assay run on DNA from formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPET) colorectal tumor tissue. There is no plasma/ctDNA version of this product. Misclassifies a tissue IVD as liquid biopsy.
Listed as 'Tumor-agnostic'. The test is a single-gene CDx specific to metastatic colorectal cancer (anti-EGFR therapy selection); it is not tumor-agnostic.
Set to 'No'. FDA-approved use requires FFPET tumor tissue with ≥10% tumor content, 5 µm sections. Should be 'Yes'.
Stated as '1% MAF'. Published/FDA analytical data describe a limit of detection of <5% mutant alleles, not 1%. Cited source (P110027C) does not support 1%.

All cite https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/cdrh_docs/pdf11/P110027C.pdf — wrong PMA. The cobas KRAS Mutation Test PMA is P140023 (approved May 2015). P110027 corresponds to a different Roche assay. Citation does not substantiate the stated numbers.

Claims detection of codons 12, 13, AND 61. The assay design covers codons 12/13/61, but the FDA-approved CDx indication is restricted to codons 12 and 13 for ruling out anti-EGFR therapy. Record should distinguish assay-reported content from the FDA CDx claim.

States 'KRAS wild-type required for anti-EGFR therapy eligibility.' The approved intended use is the inverse framing: the test identifies mCRC patients who should NOT be treated with cetuximab/panitumumab when a KRAS codon 12/13 mutation IS detected. Borderline but imprecise vs label.
Values 95 / 99 are generic round numbers attributed to a wrong FDA citation; not verifiable as stated against P140023 summary data (codon-specific PPA/NPA differ, e.g., codon 61 PPA ~85.7%).
20000 appears inflated/unsourced for this CDx pivotal program; no citation provided.
'100+' is unsourced and likely overstated for this specific product.
'1-2 days' — Roche markets the assay as <8 hours run time; '1-2 days' is plausible lab turnaround but unsourced.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: FDA P140023 and Roche product page describe FFPET tumor tissue only; no plasma version exists.
Single-gene CDx limited to colorectal cancer / anti-EGFR therapy.
Published analytical performance is <5% mutant alleles; 1% unsupported by cited source.
Round value tied to wrong PMA citation; codon-specific agreement varies.
Round value tied to wrong PMA citation.
No source; implausibly large for this CDx validation.
Unsourced; overstated for a single-gene legacy CDx kit.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Tissue (FFPE)
Tumor tissue, single-gene (CRC-specific)
Yes
<5% mutant alleles in wild-type background
FDA-approved CDx (PMA P140023, approved May 2015) for cetuximab/panitumumab patient selection in mCRC
FDA-approved companion diagnostic to identify mCRC patients who should NOT receive cetuximab (Erbitux) or panitumumab (Vectibix) when a KRAS codon 12 or 13 mutation is detected.
Real-time PCR (TaqMelt) detection of KRAS mutations in codons 12 and 13 (FDA CDx claim); assay also reports codon 61
<8 hours run time on cobas 4800 System
Medicare coverage under MolDX FDA-Approved KRAS Tests policy (CMS billing/coding article A55162)

summary: The record audits to the right product (Roche cobas KRAS Mutation Test, FDA CDx PMA P140023, still marketed) but is critically miscategorized as a tumor-agnostic Blood/Plasma test requiring no tissue — it is actually a tissue-only (FFPET) single-gene PCR CDx specific to colorectal cancer, with a <5% (not 1%) LoD and performance figures cited to the wrong PMA (P110027 instead of P140023).

name: OverC Multi-Cancer Detection Blood Test
qualityRationale: Core record is accurate and well-sourced against the THUNDER study (Annals of Oncology 2023, Gao et al.) and primary press releases. Headline performance (69.1% sensitivity / 98.9% specificity, MCDBT-1; MCDBT-2 higher-sens alternative; 83.2% TPO1), the 50-75 average-risk population, the 6-cancer panel, FDA BDD (Jan 2023) covering 5 cancers excluding CRC, and NMPA BDD all verified against vendor and FDA-pathway sources. Deductions: several commercially material fields (PPV 12.3%, NPV 99.9%, $900 list price, 10-15 day TAT) rest solely on a single unverifiable Dec 2025 vendor email; and the record does not reflect Burning Rock's Aug 2024 NASDAQ ADS delisting notice / HFCAA risk, which is a currency gap on corporate status even though the test itself remains offered.

defects.issue: Four commercially relevant fields (PPV 12.3%, NPV 99.9%, $900 list price, 10-15 business day TAT) are sourced only to a single private vendor email (Liankai Chi, Burning Rock, Dec 2025) with no public citation. PPV/NPV in particular are prevalence-dependent and cannot be independently verified; flag as vendor-asserted.

Record states corporate normalcy but does not capture that Burning Rock filed an expected-delisting notice for its NASDAQ ADSs (Aug 2024) and carries HFCAA/China risk per FY2025 SEC filings. Does not change test availability (CE-IVD/China) but is a currency gap on vendor status.

stageISensitivity is populated as 35.4 while stageISensitivityNotes states 'Overall stage I sensitivity not reported' and gives only per-cancer figures. The 35.4 value is internally inconsistent with its own note and its provenance for an overall stage I figure is unclear.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Sourced only to a private vendor email; PPV is prevalence-dependent and no screening-population (asymptomatic) prevalence assumption is stated. THUNDER is case-control (50% prevalence), under which PPV would be far higher, so 12.3% implies an unstated real-world prevalence model that is not citable.

The accompanying note explicitly says overall stage I sensitivity was not reported in the source; the numeric value contradicts its own note and lacks a clear citation for an aggregate stage I figure.
Single private vendor email; no public price list. For a China/EU self-pay test, USD $900 is plausible but unverifiable from any public source.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Burning Rock Biotech (NASDAQ: BNR) announced an expected delisting of its American Depositary Shares (Aug 2024) and discloses HFCAA/China operational risk in FY2025 SEC 6-K/20-F filings; company continued operating and reporting revenue (RMB148.5M Q2 2025) through Dec 2025. OverC remains offered as CE-IVD in China/EU.

FDA Breakthrough Device Designation (Jan 2023) covers five cancer types — esophageal, liver, lung, ovarian, pancreatic — and explicitly does not include colorectal; intended use is adults 50-75 at average risk.

summary: Accurate, well-cited MCED record matching the THUNDER study (69.1% sens / 98.9% spec, MCDBT-1) and FDA/NMPA Breakthrough Device Designations; OverC remains offered as a CE-IVD test in China/EU and is investigational in the US. Minor weaknesses: four commercial fields (PPV/NPV/price/TAT) rest on an unverifiable vendor email, an internally inconsistent stage I sensitivity value, and no reflection of Burning Rock's 2024 NASDAQ delisting/HFCAA status.

name: Precise MRD
qualityRationale: The audited Precise MRD record is factually accurate and current: tumor-informed WGS, up-to-1,000 variants, ultrasensitive LOD ~1 ppm, breast launch March 2026 with CRC/renal to follow, CLIA LDT, and the Lancet Oncology publication are all confirmed against Myriad's vendor page and press releases. Score is held down by (1) a CRITICAL shared id — a second, unrelated record (Quest Flow Cytometry MRD) also carries id mrd-30; and (2) thin/null analytical fields (specificity, TAT, blood volume, CPT) where Myriad has actually published values (e.g., 99.66% specificity, LOD 1.45 ppm eVAF).

defects.issue: id mrd-30 is shared by two distinct records in mrd.json: this 'Precise MRD' (Myriad) and 'Quest Flow Cytometry MRD Blood Test' (Quest Diagnostics). Duplicate primary key — one must be reassigned a unique id.
null, but Myriad's vendor page publishes 99.66% clinical specificity. Verifiable value is missing.
Recorded as '1 ppm'; vendor page states 1.45 ppm estimated VAF (eVAF). Rounded/oversimplified rather than wrong.
null; not yet established, acceptable for a March 2026 launch but flagged as a gap.
All null; vendor materials do not yet publish specific TAT or draw-volume figures, so unverifiable rather than fabricated.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Myriad's official Precise MRD page states a limit of detection of 1.45 ppm eVAF; '1 ppm' is a rounded approximation, not the published analytical figure.

Correct for the current commercial launch (breast only, March 2026), but note the headline Lancet Oncology validation was in clear-cell RCC, not breast — the evidence base and the launch indication differ.

Likely understated; Myriad presented 6 Precise MRD posters at ASCO 2026 plus prior AACR/2025 data readouts. '1' counts only the Lancet Oncology paper, which is defensible for peer-reviewed full papers but thin given the broader dataset.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Limit of detection 1.45 ppm estimated VAF (eVAF) per Myriad; ~60% of post-surgical MRD detections occur at ultrasensitive levels other assays may miss.
Accepts core needle biopsy or FFPE; requires only 4 x 5µm slides plus 1 H&E section.
Personalized WGS panels of up to 1,000 somatic variants were successfully created for 97.3% of patients tested.
Primary validation: Tang et al., metastasis-directed radiotherapy in oligometastatic ccRCC, Lancet Oncology 2025; DOI 10.1016/S1470-2045(25)00380-8 (94% baseline ctDNA <100 ppm).

summary: Myriad's Precise MRD record is accurate and current — tumor-informed WGS, ~1,000 variants, ultrasensitive LOD, March 2026 breast launch, CLIA LDT, Lancet Oncology data all verified — but it shares id mrd-30 with an unrelated Quest flow-cytometry record (CRITICAL duplicate key) and omits published values like 99.66% specificity and the 1.45 ppm eVAF LOD.

name: Liquid Trace Solid Tumor
qualityRationale: Record is accurate on the core architecture (combined cfDNA + cfRNA liquid CGP, CSF capability, monitoring, CLIA LDT status) and is backed by a real GTC publication (Albitar et al., Heliyon 2023, PMID 37251903). Test confirmed still offered under this exact name. Deductions: the gene-count field is misleading (uses cfRNA's >1,600 as the headline while claiming the cfDNA count is "not specified," but GTC publishes a ~318-gene cfDNA panel); HRD is reported by the vendor but omitted from biomarkers; the NCCN-alignment citation points to an unrelated company's domain; and Medicare/MolDX coverage detail is thin (null policy name and CPT).

defects.issue: genesAnalyzed=1600 uses the cfRNA gene count as the headline; the note claims 'cfDNA gene count not specified separately,' but GTC's own gene-list page enumerates ~318 cfDNA genes and the Liquid Trace overview states '>300 cfDNA genes / >1600 cfRNA genes.' The field conflates two distinct panels and asserts something the vendor actually does specify.
Citation URL is https://imagenedx.com/ which is Imagene AI (a separate digital-pathology company), not a GTC NCCN-alignment source. Wrong/broken citation for the NCCN-alignment claim.
GTC's Liquid Trace page lists HRD as a reported biomarker, but the record omits HRD. MSI status is also not addressed (neither reported nor explicitly excluded).
Medicare COVERED status asserted via MolDX/Palmetto LCD but policyName and cptCode are null; coverage detail is unverified beyond a generic statement.
numPublications=15 is asserted with a generic publications-category URL; the specific count is not independently verifiable from the cited page.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: This is the cfRNA gene count, not a unified panel size. The cfDNA solid-tumor panel is ~318 genes per GTC's published gene table; presenting 1600 as 'genesAnalyzed' overstates the DNA mutation panel.
Domain belongs to Imagene AI, unrelated to GTC; cannot substantiate any NCCN-alignment claim for this test.

suspectClaims.currentValue: SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Fusions, Gene Expression, TMB, ... (no HRD)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: GTC's Liquid Trace capability list includes HRD; its omission makes the biomarker list incomplete versus the vendor's own page.

proposedFills.proposedValue: cfDNA panel covers ~318 genes (tested for mutations, fusions, and expression); cfRNA covers >1,600 genes. GTC describes the assay as '>300 cfDNA genes / >1600 cfRNA genes.'
Add 'HRD' to reported biomarkers
Albitar M, et al. Combining cell-free RNA with cell-free DNA in liquid biopsy for hematologic and solid tumors. Heliyon. 2023. PMID 37251903

summary: GTC's Liquid Trace Solid Tumor is accurately captured as a combined cfDNA+cfRNA liquid CGP with CSF capability and is still offered under this name, backed by a real GTC publication. Main quality issues: the gene-count field conflates cfRNA (>1,600) with the panel size while wrongly claiming the cfDNA count (~318) is unspecified, HRD is omitted despite vendor reporting it, and the NCCN-alignment citation points to an unrelated company's domain.

name: Oncotype DX Breast Recurrence Score
qualityRationale: Core clinical content is accurate, current, and well-sourced: 21-gene RT-PCR (16 cancer + 5 reference genes correctly listed), HR+/HER2- early breast cancer indication, NCCN Category 1 status, TAILORx/RxPONDER Level 1 evidence, CPT 81519, and CLIA LDT status are all correct. Score is reduced for a wrong codeType classification, an internal contradiction in the Medicare rate fields, and a vendor label that anticipates a not-yet-closed acquisition. This is a gene-expression prognostic assay, not a comprehensive genomic profiling (mutation) test, so its placement in the CGP category is a category-fit oddity though the record itself flags this.

defects.issue: Listed as 'PLA' but CPT 81519 is a Tier 1 Multianalyte Assay with Algorithmic Analysis (MAAA / Category I) code, not a Proprietary Laboratory Analysis (PLA) code. The PLA code 0045U mentioned in cptCodesNotes is for the separate DCIS Score test, not this one.

Internal contradiction: numeric value is 3873 but medicareRateNotes states 'Medicare reimbursement rate approximately $3,416.' One of the two is stale/wrong; the values cannot both be the current CLFS amount.

'Abbott (Exact Sciences)' presents the Abbott acquisition as completed. The deal was only announced 2025-11-20 ($105/share, ~$21B) and remains pending regulatory close; Exact Sciences (Genomic Health) is still the operating lab entity. Label is forward-dated.

Classified as CGP, but this is a 21-gene RT-PCR gene-expression prognostic/predictive assay, not comprehensive genomic profiling (no mutation/SNV/CNV/TMB/MSI panel). The record's own technologyDifferentiator notes it is 'fundamentally different methodology from CGP tests.'
Value 501 with note citing a 2016 bibliometric analysis (530+) and 'pubmed-estimate' source; the precise figure of 501 is unverifiable and somewhat inconsistent with its own note of 530+.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: CPT 81519 is a Tier 1 MAAA / Category I CPT code, not a PLA (U-code). PLA codeType is factually misclassified.
Conflicts with own note ($3,416). Historical gapfill for 81519 ran $2,000-$3,400; the exact current CLFS figure could not be confirmed from a public source and the two stated numbers disagree.

81519 is a Tier 1 MAAA code; ADLT (Advanced Diagnostic Laboratory Test) status under PAMA typically attaches to single-source/PLA-coded tests. ADLT designation for the 81519-billed Oncotype DX could not be verified and may be conflated with its single-source pricing history.
Abbott acquisition announced Nov 2025 but not yet closed as of the audit date; Exact Sciences remains the operator.

proposedFills.proposedValue: MAAA
Exact Sciences (Genomic Health); pending acquisition by Abbott (announced Nov 2025)

summary: High-quality, well-sourced record for Oncotype DX Breast Recurrence Score; the test is still offered (Exact Sciences/Genomic Health, with an Abbott acquisition pending close). Main defects are a wrong codeType ('PLA' vs MAAA for CPT 81519), a self-contradictory Medicare rate ($3,873 vs note's $3,416), and a vendor label that pre-empts an unclosed acquisition.

name: Caris Detect
qualityRationale: The record is accurate, current (reflects March 31, 2026 finalized Achieve 1 results and the May 7, 2026 Q1 earnings call), and unusually well-sourced for a pre-launch MCED. Every headline performance number verified against multiple independent sources: Stage I-II 60.3%, asymptomatic specificity 99.2%, n=3,014, $3,500 price, Everlywell distribution, H1/Q2 2026 launch. The record exercises strong discipline in separating Caris Detect from the commercially available Caris Assure (a common conflation trap) and flags cohort enrichment (~7% cancer rate vs ~0.5-1% screening prevalence) honestly. Score held below 90 because all clinical data rests on press releases (zero peer-reviewed Caris Detect publications), several per-cancer sensitivities derive from small/undisclosed n, and the cancer-type list, TAT, CPT, and PPV/NPV are unknown — appropriate for a pre-launch product but limiting evidentiary backing.

defects.issue: Zero peer-reviewed publications specific to Caris Detect; all Achieve 1 performance data (sensitivity, specificity, per-cancer) is from company press releases and earnings slides only. Correctly disclosed in record, but means every clinical claim is unverifiable against independent peer review.

Per-cancer Stage I+II sensitivities (e.g., Breast 53%, Colon 62.2%, Head&Neck 100%) all have n=null. dataSourceNotes admits several cancer types have n<=7, making these point estimates statistically fragile and potentially misleading without confidence intervals or denominators.
Cancer-type panel still 'not yet disclosed' — the full list of cancers the test detects is unknown, a core MCED scoping fact.

Value 70.1% retained from Feb 2026 interim; finalized release reports combined Stage I-II ~60.3% but does not separately confirm Stage II. With Stage I at 56.8% and combined at 60.3%, a 70.1% Stage II is arithmetically plausible only with a particular stage mix — flagged in notes but is a stale interim figure.
Multiple operational/performance fields null (CPT, turnaround time, PPV, NPV, screening interval). Expected for pre-launch but drives the 0.71 completeness.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: From Feb 2026 interim, not confirmed in finalized data; finalized release only reports combined Stage I-II 60.3%. With Stage I 56.8% and combined 60.3%, the Stage II figure is unverified.

Accurate but the 99.2% applies only to a defined asymptomatic subgroup excluding subjects later diagnosed within 1 year; the broader benign/high-risk population specificity is 96.0%. The headline 99.2% reflects a favorable subpopulation, not the full evaluable cohort — appropriately noted but worth scrutiny for cross-test comparison.
100% sensitivity almost certainly reflects a very small n (record admits n<=7 for several types); not a robust estimate.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Beta launch underway as of Q1 2026 earnings (May 7, 2026); full commercial launch expected H1/Q2 2026 via Everlywell direct-to-consumer platform at $3,500
Combined Stage I-II 60.3%; Stage I 56.8%, Stage IV 98.6% (asymptomatic specificity 99.2%, benign/high-risk specificity 96.0%) — Achieve 1 finalized n=3,014

summary: Caris Detect (Caris Life Sciences) is a still-active, pre-launch blood-based MCED test; the record is accurate and current to the finalized March 31, 2026 Achieve 1 results (n=3,014; Stage I-II 60.3%, asymptomatic specificity 99.2%, $3,500 via Everlywell, H1 2026 launch), all verified against vendor and press sources. Main weaknesses are reliance on press-release-only data with zero peer-reviewed Caris Detect publications, small/undisclosed per-cancer n values, and an undisclosed cancer-type panel.

name: Liquid Trace Hematology
qualityRationale: Record is largely accurate and well-cited against the vendor's official Liquid Trace Hematology page. Sample requirements, LOD tiers, biomarkers (T/B clonality, TTV, CAR-T, HLA), cancer types, and CLIA/NYSDOH/CE/UKCA status all match vendor materials. Medicare/MolDX (Palmetto GBA) coverage independently confirmed via PRNewswire/BioSpace press releases. The Rowley et al. Cancers 2025 publication DOI resolves to a real MDPI article. Main weakness: the headline genesAnalyzed=1600 reflects the cfRNA panel, while the cfDNA driver-detection panel is >300; for a CGP/treatment-decision test the cfDNA figure is the more conventional metric, making 1600 potentially overstated/misleading without the cfDNA number surfaced. A few cancer types (EBV-related Neoplasms, Hypereosinophilia) appear in the record but were not all confirmed on the fetched vendor page.

defects.issue: Set to 1600, which is the cfRNA panel size (vendor: '>1600 cfRNA genes'). The cfDNA panel that drives mutation/variant detection is '>300 genes' per the vendor's Liquid Trace page. Using the larger cfRNA number as the headline CGP gene count overstates the assay's variant-calling breadth and is not directly comparable to DNA-panel competitors. The cfDNA count (>300) is absent from structured fields.
policyName and cptCode are null. The coverage is real (Palmetto GBA MolDX) but the specific LCD/CPT is not captured, leaving the coverage entry partially unverifiable at the policy level.
Claims 15 publications citing a general GTC publications-category URL rather than an enumerated list; the count is not independently verifiable from the cited page.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor's Liquid Trace page states '>300 cfDNA genes' and '>1600 cfRNA genes'. 1600 is the RNA expression/fusion panel, not the cfDNA variant panel; presenting it as the single gene count is misleading for a CGP comparison.
No enumerated source; cited URL is a general publications category page, not a list supporting the count of 15.

The fetched vendor page listed MM, lymphoma, ALL, AML, MDS, CMML, MPN, VEXAS but did not explicitly confirm EBV-related Neoplasms or Hypereosinophilia; these may be from a fuller page version but were not verified.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Combined approach: >300 cfDNA genes for variant/CNA detection plus >1600 cfRNA genes for expression and fusion detection.
Palmetto GBA (MolDX) covered; GTC announced as first Palmetto GBA-covered liquid biopsy for hematologic neoplasms (177-gene cfDNA hematology profile), per company press release.
Albitar M, et al. Combining cell-free RNA with cell-free DNA in liquid biopsy for hematologic and solid tumors. Heliyon 2023. PMID: 37251903.

summary: Accurate, well-cited record for an actively offered GTC test; Medicare/MolDX coverage and the Rowley 2025 chimerism publication check out. Primary defect: genesAnalyzed=1600 reflects the cfRNA panel while the cfDNA variant panel is >300, which overstates/obscures the comparable CGP gene count.

name: Reveal MRD
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: tumor-naive epigenomic (methylation) MRD on Guardant Infinity/Smart Liquid Biopsy, COSMOS 81% longitudinal sensitivity / 98% specificity, CRC+breast+NSCLC indications, CPT 0569U (effective 7/1/2025), MolDX/Medicare CRC-only coverage, and the JCO 2026 N0147 study all verified against vendor and primary sources. Score held below 90 by an internally inconsistent lead-time field (159 days/"4.77 mo" vs vendor's 5.3 mo), a weakly sourced fixed LOD, a tumor-informed CPT/policy mismatch in the Blue Shield CA cross-reference, and the landmark study's pivotal JCO publication being cited only by name with no PMID/DOI despite being real (PMID 41616224).

defects.issue: Internally inconsistent and inconsistent with source. Value is 159 days; the note says '4.77 months'; but the cited Guardant COSMOS press release reports a 5.3-month median lead time. 159 days = ~5.2 months, matching neither figure.

Lists tumor-INFORMED CPT codes 0306U/0307U and a 'Tumor-Informed ctDNA UM Policy' for Reveal, which is a tumor-NAIVE methylation test (its code is 0569U). Wrong policy framework/codes attributed to this test.

Cites the pivotal N0147 study as 'JCO Feb 2026 (Sinicrope et al.)' but provides no PMID/DOI; the real citation is PMID 41616224 / DOI 10.1200/JCO.2025.43.16_suppl.3504 (full paper) and should be linked.
Set to 2000. The N0147 trial enrolled 3,084; the Reveal-analyzed cohort was 2,260 with 461 ctDNA-positive. '2000' is an approximation that understates/misstates the cohort.
States 'over 19,000 differentially methylated loci'; AMA/AAPC PLA descriptor for 0569U states the assay examines 'more than 20,000 regions' of DNA methylation. Mild understatement of panel breadth.
LOD of '0.005% Tumor Fraction' is cited only to the bare homepage 'https://www.guardantcomplete.com', not a specific validation page or publication.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Single fixed LOD with only a homepage citation. Vendor/literature describe methylation detection down to ~0.001% tumor fraction, and LOD for cfDNA assays is input-dependent; the precise 0.005% LOD95 is not corroborated by a citable validation source.
Conflicts with the cited COSMOS press release figure of 5.3 months and is internally inconsistent (159d != 4.77mo).
81% is specifically the COSMOS longitudinal sensitivity for stage II+ COLON cancer; rectal sensitivity is ~60%. Labeling it 'Stage II-IV combined' across CRC slightly overstates generality.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 5.3 months median lead time from ctDNA detection to recurrence (COSMOS)

PMID 41616224 / DOI 10.1200/JCO-26-00369 region — Sinicrope et al., 'Tissue-Free Circulating Tumor DNA Assay and Patient Outcome in a Phase III Trial of FOLFOX-Based Adjuvant Chemotherapy (Alliance N0147),' Journal of Clinical Oncology 2026
NGS interrogation of more than 20,000 DNA regions for aberrant methylation patterns
2260 (Reveal-analyzed N0147 cohort; 461 ctDNA-positive post-surgery; full trial enrolled 3,084)
0569U is the unique PLA code for Guardant Reveal (effective 2025); MolDX/Palmetto Medicare coverage for CRC surveillance effective 7/1/2025

summary: Reveal MRD (Guardant Health) is accurately characterized as a still-offered tumor-naive epigenomic ctDNA MRD test with verified COSMOS performance (81%/98%), correct CPT 0569U, and a now-real JCO 2026 N0147 validation; main defects are an inconsistent/incorrect lead-time value, a tumor-informed CPT/policy mismatch in the Blue Shield CA cross-reference, a weakly sourced fixed LOD, and missing PMID for the pivotal study.

name: Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus GX
qualityRationale: Core technical facts are accurate and verifiable against the Thermo Fisher vendor catalog page (A47013) and product page: 517 genes, DNA+RNA, FFPE solid tumor, RUO ("For Research Use Only. Not for use in diagnostic procedures"), HRD(GIM)/TMB/MSI reported, ~24h Genexus TAT. RUO status is correctly flagged (no FDA/CDx claimed — the FDA-approved Genexus clinical product is the separate Oncomine Dx Express Test, 23 genes, not this assay). Score is held below ~80 because: (1) this is an RUO research kit, not a clinical/orderable test, sitting in a clinical treatment-decision/CGP database, with reimbursement/clinicalAvailability null (appropriately, but limits usefulness); (2) totalParticipants (20000) and numPublications (80) are explicitly inherited from the GeneStudio S5 sibling (tds-kit-4) rather than GX-specific, so they overstate evidence for this variant; (3) sample input stated as "20 ng DNA/RNA" conflicts with vendor catalog ("as little as 30 ng DNA and/or 20 ng RNA"); (4) all performance numbers trace to a single vendor RUO marketing flyer, not peer-reviewed/independent validation.

defects.issue: States '20 ng DNA/RNA from FFPE tissue'; vendor catalog (A47013) specifies 'as little as 30 ng DNA and/or 20 ng RNA' — DNA input requirement (30 ng) is understated.

totalParticipants=20000 and numPublications=80 are inherited from the GeneStudio S5 sibling (tds-kit-4), not specific to the GX/Genexus variant; record itself notes 'shared publication base'. These overstate the evidence base actually tied to the GX assay.

This is an RUO research-use-only kit ('Not for use in diagnostic procedures'), not a clinical treatment-decision test. Acceptable to catalog but should be clearly distinguished from clinical CGP tests; reimbursement and clinicalAvailability correctly null but make the CGP/treatment-decision framing partly aspirational.
All analytical performance figures cite a single Thermo Fisher RUO marketing flyer; no peer-reviewed or independent validation for the GX/Genexus variant specifically.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Inherited from GeneStudio S5 OCA Plus (tds-kit-4) per the record's own notes; not a count of participants studied on the Genexus GX variant.
Record states 'Shared publication base with Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus (GeneStudio S5)'; not GX-specific publications.
Vendor catalog A47013 states 'as little as 30 ng DNA and/or 20 ng RNA'; DNA input appears understated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: As little as 30 ng DNA and/or 20 ng RNA from FFPE tissue (including fine needle biopsies)

RUO — labeled 'For Research Use Only. Not for use in diagnostic procedures.' No FDA approval/clearance; the FDA-approved Genexus clinical assay is the separate Oncomine Dx Express Test (23-gene IVD CDx), not this 517-gene RUO panel.
SNVs, indels, CNVs, fusions; HRD via genomic instability metric (GIM), TMB, MSI

summary: Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus GX is a real, currently offered Thermo Fisher RUO research kit (catalog A47013): 517-gene DNA+RNA amplicon NGS for FFPE solid tumors on the Ion Torrent Genexus platform, reporting HRD/TMB/MSI with ~24h TAT, and the record's core technical/RUO facts are accurate. Main weaknesses are inherited (non-GX-specific) evidence counts, an understated DNA input requirement, and reliance on a single vendor marketing flyer for all performance numbers.

name: Guardant360 CDx
qualityRationale: Core static facts (74 genes targeted / 55 reportable, liquid ctDNA via Streck tubes, PMA P200010, Aug 2020 approval, CPT 0242U, Medicare NCD 90.2) are accurate and well-cited against the FDA SSED. But the record is now materially stale and self-contradictory: (1) in May 2026 FDA approved a successor "Guardant360 Liquid CDx" with ~740 genes (100x footprint) and transferred the CDx indications to it, which the record never mentions; (2) the fdaStatus field says "26 CDx indications" while fdaCompanionDxCount says 8 — an internal contradiction, and neither matches the verifiable counts (vendor "26th combo indication" May 2026, or "7 FDA-approved CDx indications across multiple tumor types"); (3) biomarkersReported omits MSI-High which is on the FDA label. Strong sourcing keeps it above failing, but the CDx-count contradiction and missed successor product are significant accuracy/currency defects.

defects.issue: Internal contradiction: fdaStatus says '26 CDx indications' but fdaCompanionDxCount=8. Both are questionable. Verifiable framings are: vendor counts drug×tumor combinations ('25th' in Jan 2026 BRAFTOVI PR, '26th' May 2026 vepdegestrant PR), while clinical/FDA framing is '7 FDA-approved companion diagnostic indications across multiple tumor types'. The value 8 matches neither; the field and its parent fdaStatus disagree with each other.

Product effectively superseded/renamed: FDA approved 'Guardant360 Liquid CDx' (~740 genes, 100x footprint) in May 2026 with the 7 CDx indications transferring to it. The 74-gene 'Guardant360 CDx' described here is the legacy version; record gives no indication of the successor.

Omits MSI-High. The Guardant360 CDx FDA label reports MSI status (MSI-High); record lists only SNVs, Indels, Select CNAs, Select Fusions. TMB is not on the original CDx label so its absence is correct, but MSI-H should be present.

Free-text crams approval history, indication list, and a specific 'May 2026 VEPPANU' claim into one field with a count ('26') that conflicts with fdaCompanionDxCount; should be reconciled and trimmed.
medicareRate=5000 and medicareEffective='2025-Q4' are plausible (ADLT 0242U) but uncited in the record; no direct CMS gapfill/ADLT citation provided.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Neither vendor combo-count (25th/26th as of 2026) nor the clinical '7 CDx indications across multiple tumor types' framing equals 8. Likely a stale or miscounted value.

suspectClaims.currentValue: ...26 CDx indications including ESR1 for vepdegestrant...
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Contradicts the same record's fdaCompanionDxCount=8; '26' reflects vendor drug×tumor combo counting, not the 7-indication clinical framing.
Correct for the LEGACY 74-gene CDx, but suspect as the current offering given the May 2026 ~740-gene Guardant360 Liquid CDx supersedes it.

proposedFills.proposedValue: SNVs, Indels, Select CNAs, Select Fusions, MSI-High
Superseded by Guardant360 Liquid CDx (~740 genes, 100x genomic footprint), FDA-approved May 2026; the 7 prior CDx indications transferred to the new test.

As of Jan 2026 (BRAFTOVI/encorafenib mCRC), Guardant Health described this as the '25th companion diagnostic indication across multiple tumor types'; vepdegestrant (May 2026) was the 26th. These are drug×tumor-type combinations, not distinct from the ~7 core CDx indications.

summary: Record accurately captures the legacy 74-gene Guardant360 CDx liquid biopsy (PMA P200010) with strong FDA citations, but is now stale and internally contradictory: fdaStatus ('26 CDx indications') conflicts with fdaCompanionDxCount (8), MSI-High is missing from biomarkers, and the May 2026 ~740-gene successor 'Guardant360 Liquid CDx' (which inherits the CDx indications) is not reflected.

name: LymphoTrack Dx IGH Assay
qualityRationale: The record correctly identifies the vendor (Invivoscribe), platform (Illumina MiSeq), tumor-informed approach, and CE-IVD status — these are accurate and the product is still offered. However accuracy is undermined by: (1) a regulatory conflation — the LymphoTrack Dx (CE-IVD/IVDR) intended use is B-cell clonality/lymphoproliferative-disease identification and prognosis, while MRD tracking is delivered via separate LymphoTrack MRD Software (historically RUO), so the record's framing of a CE-IVD MRD product overstates the regulatory status; (2) the primary citation URL (used for sensitivity, specificity, and LOD) returns HTTP 404 — IGH is actually sold as separate FR1/FR2/FR3 panels under catalog.invivoscribe.com; (3) precise sensitivity 98% / specificity 99% figures are not supported by the cited literature (which reports 100%/85-of-85 clonality concordance vs PCR GeneScan/Sanger), making the exact numbers unverifiable/likely fabricated; (4) round-number totalParticipants (5000) and numPublications (30) carry no citation; (5) cancer types omit multiple myeloma / plasma cell neoplasms, the most prominent IGH-MRD application.

defects.issue: Precise values of 98% and 99% are presented as vendor/validation figures but the cited peer-reviewed studies report 100% (85/85) clonality concordance vs PCR GeneScan/Sanger, not 98/99. The exact percentages are unverifiable and appear fabricated; sensitivity/specificity also conflate clonality-detection performance with MRD performance.
