The cited URL https://www.invivoscribe.com/products/lymphotrack-dx-igh-assay-miseq returns HTTP 404 (dead/incorrect link). The IGH assay is actually marketed as separate FR1/FR2/FR3 panels under catalog.invivoscribe.com. All three performance fields rely on this single dead citation.

Conflates CE-IVD clonality (Dx) intended use with MRD. The LymphoTrack Dx CE-IVD/IVDR intended use is identification of clonal IGH rearrangements / lymphoproliferative disease and prognosis; MRD tracking is provided via separate LymphoTrack MRD Software (historically RUO). Record presents an integrated CE-IVD MRD product, overstating regulatory standing.
Lists only B-cell lymphomas, CLL, B-ALL. Omits multiple myeloma / plasma cell neoplasms, a major validated IGH-MRD application repeatedly described in the literature and Invivoscribe MRD materials.
Value 5000 is an uncited round number with no supporting source; unverifiable.
Value 30 is an uncited round number; unverifiable.

'FDA clearance pending' is a forward-looking, unverifiable claim with no evidence of an active 510(k)/De Novo for the IGH MRD assay; LeukoStrat CDx FLT3 is FDA-approved but the LymphoTrack Dx IGH assay is RUO in the US.
'Coverage varies; often reimbursed as clonality assay' is vague and uncited; no CPT/MolDX reference provided.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited clonality-concordance literature reports 100% (85/85) detection vs PCR GeneScan/Sanger, not 98%. Exact figure unverifiable and conflates clonality with MRD sensitivity.
'>99%' is asserted from an unspecified 'NGS validation study' behind a dead vendor URL; no locatable source provides this exact figure for the IGH assay.
Round number, no citation; cannot be tied to any aggregated study population.
Round number, no citation.
No evidence of a pending FDA submission for the IGH MRD assay; US status is RUO. 'Pending' is unsubstantiated.

Plausible per literature (10^-5 verified standardized; 10^-6 achievable with high DNA input/reads), but cited via a 404 URL; the realistic routine sensitivity floor is ~10^-5, with 10^-6 only under optimized contrived-template conditions.

proposedFills.proposedValue: B-cell lymphomas, CLL, B-ALL, Multiple myeloma / plasma cell neoplasms

Standardized sensitivity of 10^-5 verified using triplicate measurements with 1 µg DNA input and ~1 million sequencing reads on the LymphoTrack-MiSeq platform; 10^-6 achievable only with high DNA input (up to ~6.5 µg) using contrived spiked templates.

Clonality-detection concordance reported as 100% (85/85 clonal IGH rearrangements detected by PCR GeneScan and/or Sanger were also detected by LymphoTrack) in an ALL cohort; this is clonality concordance, distinct from quantitative MRD sensitivity.

CE-IVD/IVDR kit intended for NGS-based identification of clonal IGH gene rearrangements in suspected lymphoproliferative disease; MRD monitoring performed using separate LymphoTrack MRD Software (Research Use Only).
Illumina MiSeq (also validated on MiSeq i100, NextSeq, and Complete Genomics DNBSEQ-G99 per vendor)

summary: The LymphoTrack Dx IGH assay is a real, still-offered Invivoscribe CE-IVD clonality kit on Illumina MiSeq, but the record overstates it as an integrated CE-IVD MRD product (MRD is actually delivered via separate, historically-RUO software), relies on a dead 404 citation for its 98%/99%/LOD performance figures (literature supports ~100% clonality concordance and 10^-5 standardized sensitivity instead), omits multiple myeloma from cancer types, and carries uncited round-number participant/publication counts. Recommend correcting performance claims, cancer types, and regulatory framing.

name: MSK-IMPACT Flex powered with SOPHiA DDM
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and verifiable against the vendor's official page: RUO IVD kit, tissue/tumor-only, MSI/TMB/HRD reported, DNA+RNA modular CGP, NovaSeq/NextSeq platforms, Sep 2025 launch. The record loses points for a thin/imprecise gene count (505 reflects the MSK-IMPACT subset, not the full 533-gene Flex DNA panel), a null turnaround time despite the vendor publishing concrete numbers, empty citations, and missing RNA-panel detail and additional supported sequencers. No fabricated performance numbers, which is good. Completeness is low (0.33) but the data present is largely correct.

defects.issue: Record states 505 genes. Vendor's official page states the Flex DNA panel is 533 genes (of which 505 are derived from MSK-IMPACT), plus a separate 140-gene RNA panel. 505 understates the actual Flex DNA content and omits the RNA panel entirely.
Null despite vendor publishing concrete figures: library prep 1.5 days (DNA only) / 2 days (DNA+RNA); analysis <10h (DNA only) / <15h (DNA+RNA).
Empty. The MSK-IMPACT backbone has a real foundational validation (Cheng et al. 2015, J Mol Diagn, PMID 25801821) that could be cited as heritage/context.
Lists only Illumina NovaSeq/NextSeq. Vendor page also lists MGI and Element Biosciences platform compatibility.
No RNA panel field captured; vendor specifies a distinct 140-gene RNA capture panel for fusions/exon-skipping/expression, which the modular DNA+RNA design centers on.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor's official product page lists the Flex DNA panel as 533 genes (505 from the MSK-IMPACT subset) plus a separate 140-gene RNA panel; 505 is the legacy MSK-IMPACT count, not the full Flex DNA content.
Accurate but worth flagging: SOPHiA DDM products are CE-IVD in Europe while RUO in the US, so 'RUO' is region-dependent; the record does not note the CE-IVD European availability.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 533 (DNA panel; 505 derived from MSK-IMPACT) plus 140-gene RNA panel
Library prep 1.5 days (DNA only) / 2 days (DNA+RNA); analysis <10h (DNA only) / <15h (DNA+RNA)
Illumina NextSeq 550/1000/2000, NovaSeq 6000/X; also compatible with MGI and Element Biosciences platforms
25801821 (Cheng et al., J Mol Diagn 2015 — foundational MSK-IMPACT hybridization-capture validation; backbone heritage, not Flex-specific)
RUO in the US; SOPHiA DDM products available as CE-IVD in European markets

summary: A real, currently-offered SOPHiA GENETICS RUO tissue CGP kit (launched Sep 2025) whose record is accurate on regulatory status, sample type, and biomarkers but understates the gene panel (505 vs the vendor's 533-gene DNA panel plus a 140-gene RNA panel), leaves turnaround time null despite published figures, and carries empty citations. No fabricated data and no duplicate.

name: CanCatch Custom
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and unusually well-sourced. Core claims verified against peer-reviewed sources: trade name "CanCatch Custom" (vendor uses "CanCatch® Custom") matches the PROPHET academic name; WES + up to 50 patient-specific variants + 100,000x/ultra-deep UMI-NGS + LoD 0.004% + 299-day median lead time + 45% landmark sensitivity + MEDAL (NCT03634826, n=181, 760 plasma samples) all corroborated by Chen et al. Cancer Cell 2023 (PMID 37683638). OSCC application confirmed in Molecular Cancer 2025 (perioperative nivolumab study). Test confirmed still actively offered (Burning Rock 2025/2026 AACR presentations). Vendor (NASDAQ: BNR) correct. Deductions: a few unverifiable vendor-only headline metrics (98.7% sensitivity, >99% specificity), an unsupported "LSE: BNR" listing claim, and "GIST" cancer type lacking visible published corroboration.

defects.issue: States 'NASDAQ: BNR, LSE: BNR'. Burning Rock Biotech is listed as ADS on NASDAQ (BNR) only; no London Stock Exchange (LSE) listing is supported by SEC filings or company IR. The 'LSE: BNR' appears fabricated/erroneous.

Lists 'GIST' as a validated cancer type, attributed to 'ASCO 2025' in cancerTypesNotes, but no published GIST validation for CanCatch Custch was located via search. Weakly sourced relative to NSCLC/CRC/esophageal which are confirmed.

Headline 98.7% sensitivity is vendor-attributed only ('Liankai Chi, Burning Rock, Dec 2025') and not reconcilable with the published stage-specific preoperative detection rates (IA 15.7% up to III 82.6%) or 45% landmark sensitivity in the same record; the 98.7% figure is context-free and not independently verifiable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Burning Rock Biotech Limited (NASDAQ: BNR, LSE: BNR)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: No evidence of an LSE listing; Burning Rock trades as ADS on NASDAQ only. 'LSE: BNR' is likely erroneous.
Vendor-only figure with no defined cohort/timepoint; inconsistent with published 45% landmark and stage-specific preoperative rates in the same record.
Listed as validated but no published GIST validation located; only conference-presentation attribution, weaker than the other tumor types.
Vendor-attributed (Dec 2025) only; not independently confirmable from peer-reviewed sources located.

proposedFills.proposedValue: OSCC validation derives from a perioperative nivolumab+chemo phase 2 RCT (65 patients, 324 blood samples; 2,533 variants designed, 84.4% patient-specific) published in Molecular Cancer 2025.
PROPHET/CanCatch Custom uses UMI-based ultra-deep NGS of a patient-specific 50-SNV panel derived from tumor/normal WES, with a reported limit of detection of 0.004%.

summary: High-quality, well-sourced MRD record for Burning Rock's CanCatch Custom (PROPHET) tumor-informed assay; trade name, method, LoD, lead time, and MEDAL/OSCC validation all verified against peer-reviewed literature and the test is confirmed still offered. Minor defects: an unsupported 'LSE: BNR' listing claim, a weakly-sourced GIST cancer type, and vendor-only headline sensitivity/specificity figures.

name: EPISEEK
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-cited against the vendor's own tests page and the ASCO 2025 abstract (JCO 2025;43(16_suppl):3144). Key facts confirmed: MCED methylation-specific PCR (not NGS), 10 biomarkers, 60+ cancer types, 99.5% specificity, PPV 64.9%, NPV 99.5%, $699 self-pay, CLIA LDT (not FDA approved), still offered under this name from Tucson, AZ. Sensitivity figures (IAS 54%, Stage I/II 45%, Stage III 60%, Stage IV 74%, observed overall 66.5%) are consistent with the published abstract and vendor press materials. Minor internal inconsistencies and a couple of unverifiable/derived fields hold it short of the 90s. Deterministic completeness 0.84 understates a richly-populated record.

defects.issue: List contains 19 entries but cancerTypeSensitivity object reports 21 cancer types including Testis and Thyroid, which are omitted from detectedCancerTypes. Inconsistent counts; Testis/Thyroid (with reported per-type sensitivities) should be included for consistency.
Naming inconsistency: detectedCancerTypes uses 'Gastric' and 'Uterus', cancerTypeSensitivity uses 'Stomach' and 'Uterus'. Harmonize labels.
Citation is a truncated WordPress upload directory URL (.../uploads/2024/04/) with no file, so it does not resolve to a verifiable source document.

Listed as UNKNOWN at top level while medicareCoverage.cptCode is null. No CPT identified in vendor materials; acceptable but flagged as a completeness gap for an MCED LDT (likely uses a MAAA/PLA or unlisted 81479).
'Not specified' — vendor materials position as annual-style screening but interval is genuinely unstated by vendor; acceptable but thin.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Record itself notes a discrepancy: a simple SEER-weighted average of per-type observed sensitivities yields ~64%, while the regression-model IAS is 54%. The 54% is sourced to the ASCO poster and is defensible, but the gap between the two methods is large and worth flagging — different stage/incidence weighting assumptions drive a 10-point swing.

stageISensitivity and stageIISensitivity are both set to 45 (the combined IAS Stage I/II figure), not distinct Stage II values. Observed Stage II was 60.6%. Using the same 45% for both stages conflates the SEER-weighted combined early-stage metric with a per-stage value.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Brain (glioma) detection believed unique among blood-based MCED tests
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor marketing claim ('believed unique'); not independently verified and is a comparative superiority claim that should be attributed to the vendor rather than stated as fact.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Modeled PPV of 40% in screening population age 50+
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor tests page frames PPV 64.9% as the predicted value for patients 50+; the record's separate '40% modeled screening PPV' is not clearly corroborated by the located vendor/ASCO sources.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Add Testis and Thyroid to the list (both have reported per-type observed sensitivities of 60% and 16.7% respectively in the ASCO 2025 validation), bringing detected types into alignment with cancerTypeSensitivity.
Adults 45+ with elevated cancer risk; not recommended for pregnant individuals, those under 21, or those undergoing active cancer treatment.
Not included in USPSTF, ACS, or NCCN screening guidelines; no MCED test is yet endorsed for general screening (consistent with all blood-based MCED tests as of 2026).

summary: EPISEEK is a still-offered, accurately-described methylation-PCR MCED LDT ($699 self-pay, CLIA, not FDA approved) whose performance fields are well-cited to the ASCO 2025 abstract and the vendor's own page. Defects are minor: an inconsistent cancer-type count (19 vs 21), Stage I and Stage II sensitivities both set to the combined 45% IAS value, and a couple of vendor superiority/modeling claims that should be attributed rather than stated as fact.

name: OncoScreen Focus CDx Tissue Kit
qualityRationale: Core facts (13 genes with named list, NSCLC/CRC/GIST indications, CE-IVD status, SNV/indel/fusion/CNV biomarkers, FFPE sample type) are corroborated by both the Burning Rock vendor page and LARVOL VERI. But the record is weakened by: (1) implausible 100%/100% sensitivity/specificity from a single 117-sample vendor study with no independent peer-reviewed backing; (2) the cited vendor deep-link URLs now 308-redirect to the bare homepage (broken citations); (3) a self-admitted estimated publication count; and (4) a minor CE-date discrepancy. No fabricated gene/CDx counts detected. This is a non-US CE-marked IVD kit with zero US FDA CDx indications and no Medicare/MolDX coverage, which the record handles reasonably but the fdaStatus framing is slightly misleading.

defects.issue: 100% sensitivity AND 100% specificity claimed from a single 117-sample vendor validation, sourced only to the vendor marketing page. Perfect dual 100% performance is implausible and unverifiable by any independent peer-reviewed source. Reads as vendor marketing, not validated analytical performance.

The cited URL https://us.brbiotech.com/p_details.php?class_id=102101101 now returns a 308 permanent redirect to the bare homepage https://www.brbiotech.com/en — the deep-link citation no longer resolves to the product page. Same for the fdaStatusCitations details.php URL. Citations are stale/broken.

Field named 'fdaStatus' holds 'CE-IVD (June 2022)'. Test has NO US FDA approval and zero FDA CDx indications; framing under an fdaStatus field could mislead. LARVOL lists CE mark date as May 7, 2022 (vendor header says June 1, 2022) — minor date inconsistency.
Value 5 is explicitly self-described as 'estimated at 5+' with a generic PubMed search citation, not enumerated OncoScreen Focus-specific papers. Unverifiable as stated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 100 ("100% accuracy with no missed mutations in 117 clinical samples")
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Perfect 100% sensitivity from a single small vendor cohort with no independent/peer-reviewed source; implausibly perfect and likely a marketing summary rather than a per-variant-class analytical sensitivity.
Same 117-sample vendor source; dual 100%/100% is not credible for an NGS CGP assay across SNV/indel/fusion/CNV and is unverifiable externally.
Self-labeled estimate ('estimated at 5+'); no enumerated test-specific publications provided.
LARVOL VERI gives CE mark date as May 7, 2022; vendor page header referenced June 1, 2022. Exact month is inconsistent across sources.

proposedFills.proposedValue: No US FDA approval; zero FDA companion-diagnostic indications. CE-marked IVD kit (CE date reported as May 7, 2022 per LARVOL VERI). Distinct from Burning Rock's larger OncoScreen Plus CDx (520-gene), which gained Japan MHLW CDx approval for capivasertib in breast cancer in September 2025.

13-gene DNA-only targeted panel (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, RET, MET, ERBB2, BRAF, PIK3CA, KRAS, NRAS, HRAS, KIT, PDGFRA); detects SNV, indel, rearrangement/fusion and CNV from FFPE-extracted DNA. Does not report MSI, TMB, or HRD (focused CDx panel, not pan-tumor CGP).

summary: A CE-marked (2022) 13-gene DNA-only FFPE-tissue CDx kit for NSCLC/CRC/GIST that is still offered by Burning Rock; core facts are corroborated by vendor and LARVOL, but the record carries unverifiable 100%/100% performance claims, broken/redirecting citation URLs, and an estimated publication count. It has no US FDA approval and no US coverage.

name: clonoSEQ Assay
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced: clonoSEQ is the first/only FDA-cleared IVD MRD test (De Novo DEN170080, 2018), tumor-informed immunosequencing of Ig/TCR rearrangements, 10^-6 LOD, CPT 0364U, $2,007 Medicare CLFS rate, and the heme-only indication set (MM, B-ALL, CLL FDA-cleared; MCL/DLBCL as LDT/Medicare-covered). FDA review and the PMC7325652 analytical validation citations are real and correctly support the concordance numbers. Score is held down by: (1) misleading US distribution framing — clonoSEQ is a central-lab send-out service in the US, not a distributable in-house kit; the kit/reagent-set format is EU CE-IVD only; (2) DLBCL omitted from cancerTypes despite being an actively marketed LDT indication; (3) likely-wrong medicareEffective date (2025-Q4 vs Jan 1 2025); (4) vague flat sensitivity/specificity (95/99) that conflate concordance metrics; (5) unverified specific instrument claim (MiSeqDx).

defects.issue: Frames clonoSEQ as a distributed 'Laboratory IVD Kit' available for in-house testing on MiSeqDx. In the US, clonoSEQ is exclusively a central-lab send-out service to Adaptive's CLIA lab; only the EU CE-IVD B-Cell Reagent Set is a distributable kit. The 'enabling in-house testing' claim is misleading for the primary (US) market.

Omits DLBCL, which is an actively marketed clonoSEQ indication (CLIA LDT, ctDNA blood-based, 7x sensitivity enhancement launched March 2025) and is listed in the record's own medicareCoverage.coveredIndications. Inconsistent with the rest of the record.
States '2025-Q4'. The $2,007.25 CLFS rate for 0364U was effective January 1, 2025 per Adaptive's own announcement, not Q4 2025.
MCL is grouped with FDA-cleared types but is an LDT / Medicare-covered indication, not FDA-cleared. The three FDA-cleared types are MM, B-ALL, CLL only.

States 'Illumina MiSeqDx' as a specific instrument; clonoSEQ's IVD service has historically run on Illumina NextSeq/MiSeq and the claim of a specific MiSeqDx requirement is unverified against vendor/FDA documentation.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: A flat '95%' sensitivity is not a published clonoSEQ headline metric. The real validated figures are flow-cytometry concordance (97.4% ALL, 97.9% MM) and a 10^-6 analytical LOD; the 95 appears to be a conflation or placeholder.

suspectClaims.currentValue: FDA-cleared IVD kit; also available as central lab service / distributed IVD kit for labs with MiSeqDx
suspectClaims.whySuspect: US clonoSEQ is a send-out service only; no in-house distributable kit exists in the US. Only the EU reagent set is CE-IVD distributed.
Specific MiSeqDx requirement not confirmed on vendor NGS-platform page or FDA review; likely NextSeq/MiSeq family.
Internally inconsistent with the record's own medicareRate of $2,007.25 (the 2025 CLFS rate). $1,950 is stale or a different cash-pay figure.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Add 'Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL)' as an LDT (ctDNA, blood-based) indication
FDA-cleared via De Novo (DEN170080, Sept 2018) for MM and B-ALL bone marrow; expanded clearance for CLL blood/bone marrow (Aug 2020). MCL and DLBCL offered as CLIA LDTs.
Concordance with flow cytometry 97.4% (ALL) and 97.9% (MM); analytical LOD 10^-6 (one malignant cell per million). DLBCL ctDNA assay enhanced March 2025 for ~7-fold sensitivity gain.
US: central-lab send-out service performed at Adaptive's CLIA laboratory only (not distributed). EU: clonoSEQ B-Cell Reagent Set CE-marked as IVD for qualified labs.

summary: clonoSEQ is still offered and the record's core facts (first/only FDA-cleared MRD IVD, tumor-informed Ig/TCR immunosequencing, 10^-6 LOD, CPT 0364U, $2,007 Medicare rate) are accurate and well-cited. Main problems: it misleadingly frames the US product as a distributable in-house kit (it is a central-lab send-out; kit format is EU-only), omits the marketed DLBCL LDT indication from cancerTypes, and has a wrong Medicare effective date and a vague/possibly-conflated 95% sensitivity figure.

name: OncoScreen Plus Cancer Mutation Profiling Tissue Kit
qualityRationale: Core facts are real and verifiable: the CE-IVD tissue kit exists and is still offered, the Sept 2025 Japan MHLW CDx approval for capivasertib is confirmed, and the anchor publication (PMID 35305253) checks out exactly (title/journal/year/cohort). Sensitivity 97 / specificity 99 match the official product page. Score is held down by internal contradictions the record never reconciles: genesAnalyzed=520 vs the official CE-IVD label's 518; an HRD biomarker the vendor page does not support; and sampleRequirements claiming blood/ctDNA/effusion support that contradicts both the product's own name ('Tissue Kit') and the official spec (FFPE tissue only). The fdaCompanionDxCount sits in an FDA-named field but the only CDx approval is Japanese (no US FDA approval), which risks misleading readers.

defects.issue: Claims the kit accepts blood (ctDNA+WBC Streck tube), pleural/ascitic/CSF/pericardial fluid, contradicting both the test name ('Tissue Kit') and the official CE-IVD product page which specifies FFPE tissue only (100-200ng tumor DNA input). Vendor-asserted (Liankai Chi) but unsupported by the public product spec.

Record states 520, but the official CE-IVD product page repeatedly labels it a '518-gene panel' / '518 gene targets'. The 520 figure comes from the RUO/research panel name (PMID 35305253). fdaStatusNotes itself says '518-gene version', creating an unreconciled internal contradiction.

Lists HRD as a reported biomarker, but the official product page does not mention HRD output (only references 'HRR genes' for ovarian/prostate). PMID 35305253 validates TMB and MSI, not HRD. HRD reporting is unverified.

fdaCompanionDxCount=1 and the FDA-named status field describe a Japan MHLW (PMDA) CDx approval, not a US FDA approval. There is no US FDA approval. Field naming risks implying FDA CDx status; notes do clarify it is Japan MHLW.
States 'Burning Rock Biotech Limited (NASDAQ: BNR, LSE: BNR)'. Ticker is BNR on NASDAQ; a London Stock Exchange (LSE) listing under the same ticker is not substantiated and appears erroneous.

Cites tissue detection rate 97.9% but omits that the same PMID 35305253 reported plasma sensitivity of only ~45.7% / 72.2% plasma detection — a material omission given the record asserts blood-sample support.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Official CE-IVD page lists FFPE tissue only; product is named 'Tissue Kit'. Blood/fluid support is vendor-asserted, not in public spec.
Official CE-IVD spec says 518 genes; 520 is the research-panel figure. Record contradicts itself (fdaStatusNotes says 518).
Vendor page does not list HRD as a reported output; only 'HRR genes' referenced. Validation paper covers TMB/MSI, not HRD.
Vendor-asserted (Liankai Chi, Jan 2026), not independently verifiable.
Vendor-asserted for a kitted IVD where TAT depends on the running lab; not a fixed product attribute.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Promotional vendor phrasing ('outstanding') without a citation to the L-MOCA result for this panel.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Capivasertib (Japan MHLW, Sept 24 2025) - PIK3CA/AKT1/PTEN - unresectable/recurrent HR+/HER2- breast cancer after endocrine therapy; via OncoGuide OncoScreen Plus CDx System (Riken Genesis partnership)
Tissue (FFPE only per CE-IVD spec; DNA input 100-200ng tumor, 30-200ng paired)
Illumina NextSeq 500/550Dx (PE150)
In the concordance study, plasma/ctDNA average detection rate was 72.2% with ~45.7% variant sensitivity vs 97.9% tissue detection — material limitation if liquid use is claimed

summary: A real, still-offered Burning Rock CE-IVD tissue CGP kit with a verified Sept 2025 Japan MHLW CDx approval (capivasertib) and a solid anchor publication (PMID 35305253), but the record carries unreconciled inconsistencies: 520 vs the official 518-gene count, an unsupported HRD biomarker, and a blood/ctDNA sample claim that contradicts the kit's own 'Tissue Kit' name and FFPE-only CE-IVD spec. The Japan CDx sits in FDA-named fields despite there being no US FDA approval.

name: FirstLook Lung
qualityRationale: Record is accurate on the core science (cfDNA fragmentomics, single-cancer lung, 80% sensitivity, 71% stage I, NPV 99.7%, LDT/CLIA status, USPSTF-eligible population) and well-cited to the DELFI-L101 Cancer Discovery 2024 paper (PMID 38829053) and real ClinicalTrials.gov NCT numbers. Points off for: specificity value (58%) not matching the published 53% screening-population figure; the primary vendor citation URL now 404s; availability described as a limited "Early Experience Program" when the test is now broadly commercially available across all 50 states (NY DOH permit obtained); and an unverifiable $300 list price.

defects.issue: Record states 58% but the cited DELFI-L101 Cancer Discovery 2024 paper (PMID 38829053) reports 53% specificity in the screening/validation population (FIRSTLUNG press cites 56%). The 58% value is not supported by the attached citation.
Primary vendor citation https://delfidiagnostics.com/firstlook-lung/ now returns HTTP 404. Product moved to delfidiagnostics.com/our-products/. Stale dead link.

Describes a limited 'Early Experience Program at select health systems (OSF; City of Hope; Indigenous Pact)'. As of 2026 FirstLook Lung is broadly commercially available; DELFI obtained a NY State DOH clinical lab permit completing authorization across all 50 states. Description is stale and understates availability.
Stated $300 list price is not publicly verifiable; vendor does not publish a price (self-pay 'several hundred dollars'). Unsourced specific figure.
'FDA IVD submission planned' is forward-looking and unverifiable; current status is correctly LDT/CLIA but the planned-submission claim should be sourced or removed.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Published DELFI-L101 (Cancer Discovery 2024, PMID 38829053) reports 53% specificity in screening population; FIRSTLUNG press reports 56%. 58% matches neither source it cites.
Vendor does not publish a list price; figure appears estimated and is unverifiable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Early Experience Program at select health systems (OSF HealthCare; City of Hope; Indigenous Pact)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Test is now broadly commercially available across all 50 states following NY DOH permit; 'Early Experience Program' framing is outdated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://delfidiagnostics.com/our-products/

Commercially available LDT (CLIA); ordered through health systems and providers nationwide. DELFI completed authorization across all 50 states after receiving NY State DOH clinical laboratory permit (2026).
https://aacrjournals.org/cancerdiscovery/article/14/11/2224/749197/Clinical-Validation-of-a-Cell-Free-DNA-Fragmentome

summary: Accurate, well-cited record for DELFI's FirstLook Lung — a single-cancer cfDNA fragmentomics LDT for pre-LDCT lung screening, still offered and now broadly available across all 50 states. Main defects: specificity (58%) doesn't match the cited 53% paper, the primary vendor citation URL 404s, and the availability field is stale ("Early Experience Program").

name: GALEAS Bladder
qualityRationale: The narrative content (scope, intended use as hematuria triage not screening, UKCA status, ~90% sens / ~85% specificity, 23-gene panel, vendor still offering via Informed Genomics) is accurate and well-aligned with the vendor page and the Ward 2023 European Urology Oncology validation. However, BOTH performance citation PMIDs are fabricated/wrong: 36335043 is a dermatology Frey-syndrome case report and 31125144 is a tuberculosis ATP-synthase paper — neither relates to bladder cancer. This is a critical evidence-integrity failure (per the project's no-fake-PMID rule) that undermines the entire performance section, even though the underlying numbers happen to be correct. Several values are vendor-estimated (price) or now slightly stale vs. newer real-world data.

defects.issue: PMID 36335043 cited for sensitivity, specificity, performance, clinicalTrials and totalParticipants is a dermatology case report ('Frey syndrome', An Bras Dermatol 2023), NOT the GALEAS bladder study. The correct PMID for Ward et al. 'Highly Sensitive and Specific Detection of Bladder Cancer via Targeted Ultra-deep Sequencing of Urinary DNA' (Eur Urol Oncol 2023;6(1):67-75) is 35410825.

PMID 31125144 cited as the 'Ward et al. BJU International 2019' 23-gene panel paper is actually a tuberculosis ATP-synthase molecular-dynamics paper (J Cell Biochem 2019). The correct PMID for Ward et al. 'Targeted deep sequencing... a 23-gene panel' (BJU Int 2019;124(3):532-544) is 31077629.

Method states '451 somatic mutations'; the Ward 2023 validation paper and vendor RUO materials describe 443 somatic mutations across 23 genes. Vendor IVD page now states 86% specificity vs. record's 85 (minor).

Validation cohort numbers are imprecise: the Eur Urol Oncol study sequenced 591 haematuria-clinic + 293 NMIBC-surveillance urine DNAs (884 total). Record's 772/'384 cancer + 388 controls' is a defensible subset but conflated with the '>770 samples / 96% detection' claim; could be clarified.
listPrice 750 is explicitly an estimate ('$600-$900 range'), not a sourced vendor price.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: PPV is not stated on the current Nonacus IVD product page (which lists sensitivity/specificity only). The ~52% figure is unverifiable from the cited source and is prevalence-dependent.
Current vendor IVD page states 86%; newer 2026 real-world NHS multicentre data (medRxiv 2026.02.06.26345752) reports 92.0% specificity. The 85 value is stale/imprecise.
Cited to the wrong (dermatology) PMID; the actual validation used 884 urine DNAs (591+293). The 772 number is unverifiable as cited.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35410825/
proposedFills.source: Ward DG et al. Highly Sensitive and Specific Detection of Bladder Cancer via Targeted Ultra-deep Sequencing of Urinary DNA. Eur Urol Oncol 2023;6(1):67-75. PMID 35410825

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35410825/ | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31077629/
proposedFills.source: Ward 2023 Eur Urol Oncol (PMID 35410825) and Ward 2019 BJU Int 23-gene panel (PMID 31077629)

proposedFills.proposedValue: Ward et al. European Urology Oncology 2023 (PMID 35410825); Ward et al. BJU International 2019 (PMID 31077629, 23-gene panel development)
Commercially available in UK (private); GALEAS Bladder service testing provided by Informed Genomics Ltd; US availability anticipated via LDT route

Real-world multicentre UK NHS haematuria-clinic data (2026) report sensitivity 92.2% (95% CI 84.0-96.4), specificity 92.0% (90.0-93.6), NPV 99.3%; grade-dependent sensitivity 71% (G1), 87% (G2), 97% (G3).

summary: GALEAS Bladder (Nonacus) is accurately described as a UKCA-marked, urine-based 23-gene NGS hematuria-triage test (not asymptomatic screening) and is still offered (via Informed Genomics). However both performance PMIDs are fabricated — one is a dermatology case report and one a tuberculosis paper — a critical evidence-integrity defect; correct citations are PMID 35410825 (Ward 2023) and PMID 31077629 (Ward 2019).

name: MSK-ACCESS powered with SOPHiA DDM
qualityRationale: Every core claim in the record matches the vendor's official page: 147 genes, RUO/IVD-kit status, ~5 day TAT, 99.4% PPA at ≥0.5% VAF vs centralized MSK-ACCESS, matched WBC normal for CHIP/germline filtering, liquid/ctDNA approach, and the cited vendor URL is live and on-point. No fabricated performance numbers or stale ownership. Score held below ~90 because the platform list and biomarker list are incomplete vs the vendor spec sheet (missing MGI DNBSEQ-T7 / Element AVITI platforms; missing TERT promoter and MET exon 14 skipping biomarkers), and citationPmids is empty despite a peer-reviewed real-world validation existing.

defects.issue: Lists only Illumina platforms (NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 550/1000/2000). Vendor page lists additional supported sequencers: MGI DNBSEQ-T7 and Element AVITI; also vendor cites NextSeq 2000 specifically rather than 550/1000.
Omits TERT promoter mutations and MET exon 14 skipping, which the vendor explicitly lists as reported. CNV/fusion scope is also narrower than implied (CNVs in 71 genes, DNA fusions in 10 genes).
Empty. A peer-reviewed real-world validation of the decentralized MSK-ACCESS powered with SOPHiA DDM solution exists (Journal of Liquid Biopsy 2024) and could be cited.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Not wrong, but sources are inconsistent: vendor page states 147 in spec text while some vendor/marketing materials say 146 'key cancer-associated genes.' Worth a confirmation note.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Illumina NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 2000; MGI DNBSEQ-T7; Element AVITI
SNVs, Indels, CNVs (71 genes), DNA-based gene fusions (10 genes), TERT promoter mutations, MET exon 14 skipping
No MSI or TMB reporting
Co-developed by SOPHiA GENETICS, Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, and AstraZeneca

summary: An accurate, vendor-verified RUO record for SOPHiA GENETICS' decentralized 147-gene MSK-ACCESS liquid biopsy kit; all core claims match the live vendor page and the test is still actively offered. Defects are minor: incomplete platform and biomarker lists (missing MGI/Element platforms and TERT/MET exon 14 biomarkers) and an empty citation list.

name: Foresight CLARITY Lymphoma
qualityRationale: Core facts (PhasED-Seq method, tumor-informed approach, 0.7 ppm LOD95, 90.62% PPA, NCCN inclusion, Natera Dec 2025 acquisition for $275M+$175M, CLIA LDT status) are accurate and well-sourced against the real validation paper (Klimova et al. Oncotarget 2025) and acquisition press release. However the record contains a fabricated/wrong specificity citation (points to an unrelated glomangiosarcoma case report), a specificity value (97.65%) that contradicts the cited paper's NPA of 77.78%, a wrong first-author attribution (Boehm vs Klimova), overstated validated cancer types (myeloma/follicular/Hodgkin not validated in cited evidence), and an internal Medicare-coverage contradiction. These are accuracy-grade defects, not mere incompleteness.

defects.issue: Citation URL https://www.oncotarget.com/article/28654/text/ resolves to an unrelated BRAF V600E glomangiosarcoma case report (Arregui et al.), NOT a DLBCL/PhasED-Seq validation. The correct article is /article/28719/text/ (Klimova et al.). Same wrong URL is reused in sensitivityCitations. Fabricated/mismatched source.

Value 97.65% (NPA 95% CI 87.43-99.94%) is not supported by the cited validation paper, which reports negative percent agreement of 77.78% (95% CI 52.73-93.59%). The 97.65% and its CI appear fabricated; only the 0.24% false-positive rate is real.

Author attributed as 'Boehm N et al. Oncotarget 2025;16:329-336' — the actual first author is Klimova N (Nina Klimova), corresponding author Laura Hyland. Wrong author name; pages 329-336 are correct.

Lists Follicular lymphoma, Classic Hodgkin lymphoma, and Multiple myeloma as validated cancer types. The cited analytical/clinical evidence and NCCN inclusion are DLBCL/LBCL-specific; multiple myeloma in particular is out of scope for this B-cell-lymphoma SHM-region assay and is unverified.

Internal contradiction: medicareCoverage.status = 'COVERED' (LCD L38779, lymphoma MRD) while coverageCrossReference.medicare.status = 'NOT_COVERED' ('coverage not yet established'). L38779 covers lymphoma ctDNA-MRD generically but this specific test's billing pathway pre-2026 clinical launch is unverified; the two blocks must be reconciled.
Citation points to natera.com/oncology/signatera-advanced-cancer-detection/ (a Signatera page) rather than a Foresight CLARITY source for the 200-day lead-time claim.

States 'Negative percent agreement 97.65%... False positive rate 0.24%' — these two are mutually inconsistent (0.24% FPR implies ~99.76% specificity, not 97.65%) and neither matches the paper's 77.78% NPA.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited paper (Klimova et al. Oncotarget 2025) reports NPA 77.78% (95% CI 52.73-93.59%), not 97.65%. Value and its CI unverifiable in any located source.

suspectClaims.currentValue: includes Follicular lymphoma, Classic Hodgkin lymphoma, Multiple myeloma
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Validation paper and NCCN inclusion are DLBCL/LBCL-specific; myeloma especially is not a B-cell-lymphoma SHM target and is not validated in cited evidence.
Actual first author is Klimova N; 'Boehm' does not appear on the paper. Likely fabricated author name.
Contradicts the coverageCrossReference block stating coverage not established; test described elsewhere in record as research/trial use with clinical launch expected 2026.
Unverified count; numPublicationsCitations only references 15 ASH 2025 abstracts and 'Foresight website,' not 40 publications.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Negative percent agreement 77.78% (95% CI 52.73-93.59%) vs SNV-based reference in DLBCL analytical validation. Separately, overall false positive rate 0.24% and background error rate 1.95E-08.
https://www.oncotarget.com/article/28719/text/ | Klimova N et al. Oncotarget 2025;16:329-336 | PMID 40353795
https://www.oncotarget.com/article/28719/text/ | Klimova N et al. Oncotarget 2025;16:329-336 | PMID 40353795
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL); Large B-cell lymphoma (LBCL)
proposedFills.source: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12068320/ and NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas guideline inclusion

proposedFills.proposedValue: LOD95 = 0.7 ppm (6.61E-07 PVAF at 120 ng input) for the lymphoma CLARITY assay per analytical validation. Foresight's headline 0.3 ppm LOD95 / <0.1 ppm detection figures refer to the WGS-based solid-tumor assay in development.

summary: Record's core profile (PhasED-Seq, 0.7 ppm LOD95, 90.62% PPA, NCCN DLBCL inclusion, Natera Dec-2025 acquisition, CLIA LDT) is accurate, but it carries a fabricated specificity figure (97.65% vs the paper's 77.78% NPA) backed by a citation pointing to an unrelated glomangiosarcoma case report, a wrong first-author name (Boehm vs Klimova), overstated validated cancer types (myeloma/follicular/Hodgkin), and a Medicare coverage self-contradiction. Test is still offered under this name (now a Natera asset).

name: Guardant LUNAR (RUO platform)
qualityRationale: Core scientific facts are accurate and well-sourced: the Parikh 2021 study (PMID 33926918, Clin Cancer Res 2021;27(20):5586-5594) is real and correctly cited, with sensitivity (56% landmark / 69% longitudinal), ~100% specificity, cohort sizes (n=103, 84 evaluable, 15 ctDNA+ all recurred), tumor-naive/plasma-only design, methylation+genomic integration, and 0.01% VAF LOD all corroborated by the source and secondary literature. The record correctly distinguishes the RUO research platform (LUNAR-1) from the clinical Guardant Reveal LDT. Points deducted because the "LUNAR" brand has effectively been retired/superseded — LUNAR-1 became Guardant Reveal and the underlying tech is now marketed as the "Smart Liquid Biopsy" platform (2024-2025), so "Guardant LUNAR (RUO platform)" as a current standalone offering is stale framing; and the record substantially overlaps the same underlying technology already tracked as mrd-6 (Reveal MRD), raising a near-duplicate concern.

defects.issue: The 'LUNAR' brand has been effectively retired. LUNAR-1 was rebranded to Guardant Reveal (commercial MRD), LUNAR-2 to the Shield/ECLIPSE screening program, and by 2024-2025 the underlying technology is marketed as Guardant's 'Smart Liquid Biopsy' platform. 'Guardant LUNAR (RUO platform)' is no longer a current standalone product name; it persists only as historical/program nomenclature. Record should be flagged as renamed/superseded.

This RUO-platform record describes the same underlying LUNAR-1 technology already tracked as the clinical product mrd-6 (Reveal MRD). Not a strict duplicate id, but a near-duplicate concept; risk of double-counting Guardant MRD offerings in the database.

Some fields (TAT, clinical availability) are sourced to the clinical Guardant Reveal product page rather than the RUO platform itself, blending RUO and clinical-product attributes; TAT for the RUO research platform is described as 'study-specific' yet a 7-10 day value is asserted.
Citation is a bare homepage (https://www.guardanthealth.com/) rather than a specific page documenting the LUNAR RUO platform; not independently verifiable.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: LUNAR is no longer an active product brand; it has been superseded by Guardant Reveal and the 'Smart Liquid Biopsy' platform. Listing it as a current offering overstates its standalone availability.
Generic count with no enumerated citation list; only the Parikh 2021 paper is concretely cited. Unverifiable as stated.

Attributed to the RUO research platform but actually sourced from the clinical Guardant Reveal product page; the record itself notes RUO TAT was 'study-specific,' making the asserted value a borrowed clinical-product figure.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Guardant LUNAR (RUO platform; LUNAR-1, now Guardant Reveal / Smart Liquid Biopsy)

In the Parikh 2021 CRC cohort, plasma-only landmark sensitivity was 56% at ~1 month post-therapy and longitudinal sensitivity 69%; all 15 patients with detectable ctDNA at the 1-month landmark recurred.
Limit of detection approximately 0.01% VAF; secondary reporting indicates ctDNA detection in early-stage CRC at 91% sensitivity down to 0.01% VAF.

LUNAR-1 RUO platform was commercialized as Guardant Reveal (launched 2021); underlying epigenomic/genomic technology now runs on Guardant's Smart Liquid Biopsy platform (2024-2025). LUNAR persists primarily as program/trial nomenclature.

summary: A scientifically accurate, well-cited record of Guardant's LUNAR-1 RUO MRD research platform (tumor-naive, plasma-only, methylation+genomic, Parikh 2021 PMID 33926918), but it presents a retired brand as a current offering — LUNAR-1 became Guardant Reveal and the tech is now the 'Smart Liquid Biopsy' platform — and substantially overlaps the clinical Reveal record (mrd-6).

name: Caris Assure
qualityRationale: Core facts accurate and well-sourced (Nature 2025 + vendor). Held down by therapy-selection metrics misapplied as MRD sensitivity/LOD, omission of the MRD HR=33.4 result, and an overstated top-level Medicare 'COVERED' status that contradicts the record's own cross-reference.

defects.issue: 93.8% PPA and >99.99% specificity are therapy-selection concordance vs matched tissue (driver-mutation detection), not MRD/monitoring sensitivity. Presenting them as the headline performance of an MRD/TRM record conflates a different application. The actual MRD evidence (HR=33.4, p<0.005) is omitted entirely.

'0.5% VAF' is a therapy-selection variant-detection threshold cited to a 2022 ASCO press release, not an MRD limit of detection. MRD/monitoring positioning implies a much lower LOD (competitors claim <0.01–0.1% VAF). 0.5% VAF is implausibly high for genuine MRD and is stale-sourced.

Top-level status 'COVERED' with rate $3,649 conflicts with the record's own coverageCrossReference (medicare status 'LIMITED', 'case-by-case', 'not broadly established'). The $3,649 MolDX/gapfill price is for therapy selection (0485U), not MRD/monitoring. Overstates coverage for this TRM/MRD record.
Cites monitoring HR=4.39 but omits the stronger and more relevant MRD HR=33.4 (p<0.005) from the same Nature 2025 paper, which is the headline MRD result for a record housed in mrd.json.
References 'BCBSM EI status Jan 2025' — stale/narrow framing; 0485U has since been gapfill-priced by CMS at $3,649. Note does not reflect current MAC pricing.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: This is a therapy-selection variant-detection threshold (>95% sensitivity at >=0.5% VAF) cited to a 2022 ASCO PR, not an MRD LOD. For an MRD/monitoring test it materially understates required analytical sensitivity; no MRD-specific LOD in ppm is published in the cited Nature paper for the buffy-coat-corrected MRD model.
This is therapy-selection PPA vs matched tissue within 30 days, not MRD or monitoring sensitivity. Misattributed to an MRD/TRM record.
Contradicted by the record's own coverageCrossReference (LIMITED, case-by-case). The covered/priced application is therapy selection, not MRD/monitoring.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Commercially available in US since late 2022
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Caris Assure launched at ASCO 2022 as therapy-selection; the MRD/monitoring application validated in the 2025 Nature paper. 'Since late 2022' may overstate availability of the MRD/TRM use this record represents.

proposedFills.proposedValue: In the AI-enabled WES/WTS validation (Abraham et al., Sci Rep 2025), the MRD model (trained on 3,439 patients, validated on 86) predicted recurrence with HR=33.4 (p<0.005); the monitoring model (validated on 101 patients) gave HR=4.39 (p=0.008) for shorter disease-free survival. MCED sensitivities 83.1%-95.7% across stages I-IV at 99.6% specificity. CHIP subtraction filtered ~22% of DNA-repair-gene mutations arising from clonal hematopoiesis.

93.8% PPA / 96.8% PPV applies to therapy-selection driver-mutation detection vs matched tissue collected within 30 days (>=5ng input, CHIP subtraction); distinct from MRD/monitoring performance, which is reported as hazard ratios (MRD HR=33.4; monitoring HR=4.39) rather than PPA.
Liquid biopsy therapy selection (solid tumors) only; MRD/monitoring not separately established — MolDX gapfill price $3,649 attaches to therapy-selection PLA code 0485U.
Tumor-naive (tumor-agnostic) blood-based WES/WTS liquid biopsy; bypasses tumor-tissue sequencing. Plasma cfDNA/cfRNA plus buffy-coat WBC for CHIP/germline subtraction.

summary: Caris Assure is correctly identified as a still-offered, tumor-naive WES/WTS blood biopsy with accurate vendor/method/CPT facts well-sourced to the Nature 2025 validation. The main defects are application conflation: the headline sensitivity/specificity and 0.5% VAF LOD are therapy-selection metrics misapplied to this MRD/TRM record, the MRD HR=33.4 result is omitted, and the top-level Medicare 'COVERED' status overstates monitoring coverage versus the record's own cross-reference.

name: ClarityDX Prostate
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and well-sourced. All performance metrics (95% sensitivity, 35% specificity, 54% PPV, 91% NPV at >=25% threshold; n=3,448 development cohort; n=1,257 validation) match the cited peer-reviewed source (Hyndman et al., npj Digital Medicine 2024, PMID 38902526, DOI 10.1038/s41746-024-01167-9) exactly. Vendor/ownership relationship (Nanostics developed/validated, Protean BioDiagnostics markets in US), CLIA-LDT status, single-cancer scope, reflex-after-elevated-PSA intended use, and 3-5 day TAT all verified against the vendor page and press releases. Deductions for omitted concrete PLA code value and missing EU (CE Mark) availability.

defects.issue: States 'PLA code granted by AMA (2025)' but omits the actual code. The code is 0550U, effective April 1, 2025 (confirmed by AMA PLA code list and Nanostics/Protean announcement).
Lists only US and Canada. Nanostics received a CE Mark for ClarityDX Prostate (EU/IVDR), so EU availability is omitted; record understates geographic availability.

Generic 'Machine learning risk prediction'. Source specifies inputs more precisely: total PSA + free PSA plus up to five clinical features (age, prior negative biopsy, DRE, prostate volume, PI-RADS/imaging score) on a gradient-boosting platform; method field captures gradient-boosting but omits the imaging/volume clinical inputs.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor page reviewed does not confirm tube type/count or the specific stability handling notes (5 days 2-8C, 24h RT, freeze -20C); these collection details are not corroborated by the public sources located and may be inferred rather than vendor-stated.

Only the 2024 npj Digital Medicine validation paper was located as a peer-reviewed publication for the named ClarityDX Prostate test; the count of 3 is unverified from the sources found (earlier 2022 conference/press data may be being counted).

proposedFills.proposedValue: AMA granted PLA code 0550U for ClarityDX Prostate, effective April 1, 2025. Coverage varies by payer; Medicare/commercial reimbursement still developing.
US, Canada, EU (CE Mark granted to Nanostics for ClarityDX Prostate)

Machine learning (gradient-boosting) risk prediction combining total PSA and free PSA with up to five clinical features (age, prior negative biopsy, DRE findings, prostate volume, PI-RADS imaging score)
International multi-site validation; >0.82 AUC for csPCa (grade group >=2) vs 0.58 AUC for PSA alone; could avoid up to ~35-37% of unnecessary biopsies as a reflex test

summary: Accurate, well-sourced record for ClarityDX Prostate, a single-cancer ML-based reflex risk test (developed/validated by Nanostics, marketed in US by Protean BioDiagnostics) that stratifies risk of clinically significant prostate cancer in men with elevated PSA; all performance metrics match the cited npj Digital Medicine 2024 validation. Minor gaps: missing PLA code 0550U value, EU (CE Mark) availability, and a couple of unverified sample-handling/publication-count details.

name: Oncodetect
qualityRationale: Core performance data (78%/80% landmark, 91%/94% surveillance from Alpha-CORRECT; LOD 15 ppm; up to 200 variants; WES-based tumor-informed approach), Medicare MolDX coverage, and the now-current Abbott/Exact Sciences ownership are all accurate and well-cited against vendor and press sources. Points deducted for an internally contradictory and factually wrong Aetna coverage entry (claims PARTIAL/covered for CRC under the wrong policy CPB 0715, when Aetna deems ctDNA MRD experimental under CPB 0352), a method field that mischaracterizes the assay as hybrid-capture rather than WES-driven, and Medicare indication that omits resectable Stage IV.

defects.issue: Claims Aetna status PARTIAL, policy 'CPB 0715', coveredIndications ['CRC Stage II-III'], notes 'CRC coverage only'. This is factually wrong on two counts: (1) CPB 0715 is 'Pharmacogenetic and Pharmacodynamic Testing', not the applicable tumor-marker policy; (2) Aetna actually classifies ctDNA MRD (including Signatera for Stage II/III CRC) as experimental/not medically necessary, so Oncodetect is NOT covered. This directly contradicts the record's own commercialPayersNotes, which correctly cites CPB 0352 / not covered.

Medicare MolDX coverage (July 2025, Palmetto GBA LCD L38779) is for stage II, III, AND resectable stage IV CRC. Record's notes say only 'Stage II-III' / 'Stage II-III CRC post-surgical', omitting resectable Stage IV.

Describes Oncodetect as a 'targeted hybrid-capture ctDNA assay'. Exact Sciences explicitly markets it as whole-exome-sequencing (WES) driven: tumor + matched-normal WES to select up to 200 patient-specific variants. The requiresMatchedNormalNotes field correctly says WES, so the method field is inconsistent and understates the WES basis.
Listed as 'PLA pending'. A MolDX-covered LDT with an active LCD typically has an assigned billing code; current value is vague/unverified.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: CPB 0715 is Pharmacogenetic/Pharmacodynamic Testing; Aetna deems ctDNA MRD experimental/not medically necessary. Should be NOT_COVERED / EXPERIMENTAL under CPB 0352 (Tumor Markers), consistent with the record's own commercialPayersNotes.

Alpha-CORRECT cohort was primarily Stage III CRC (124 patients, 19 western-PA sites, 2016-2020). The 78%/91% figures derive mainly from a Stage III population; labeling them 'Stage II-III combined' may overstate Stage II representation. Beta-CORRECT (GALAXY subset) covers Stage II-IV separately.
Unverifiable; a MolDX-covered test with active LCD L38779 would typically bill under an assigned code.

proposedFills.proposedValue: status NOT_COVERED / EXPERIMENTAL; policy 'CPB 0352' (Tumor Markers) and/or CPB 0715; Aetna classifies ctDNA-based MRD (including Signatera for stage II/III CRC) as experimental, investigational, or unproven; coveredIndications []

Medicare MolDX coverage (July 2025) for serial use in stage II, III, and resectable stage IV colorectal cancer in adjuvant and recurrence-monitoring settings over a 5-year period; Palmetto GBA LCD. $0 patient responsibility for Medicare FFS patients meeting criteria.

Tumor-informed whole-exome-sequencing (WES) MRD assay: WES of tumor tissue plus matched-normal (buffy coat) to select up to ~200 patient-specific somatic variants, then bespoke targeted ctDNA panel sequencing of plasma; LOD 15 ppm (0.0015% tumor fraction).

summary: Oncodetect is an actively offered tumor-informed WES-based ctDNA MRD test, now an Abbott asset after the March 2026 Exact Sciences acquisition (ownership field correct). Performance, LOD, variant count, and Medicare coverage are accurate, but the Aetna coverage entry is factually wrong (claims PARTIAL/covered under the wrong policy CPB 0715, contradicting the record's own not-covered note), the Medicare indication omits resectable Stage IV, and the method field understates the WES basis.

name: cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced to the FDA PMA (P150047): single-gene EGFR test, 42 mutations across exons 18-21 including exon 19 del, L858R, T790M and exon 20 insertions; both FFPE tissue and plasma cfDNA; first FDA-approved liquid biopsy CDx (plasma label extension 2016); CDx for erlotinib/gefitinib/osimertinib. Sensitivity/specificity/LOD cite the FDA summary. Score held down by a mislabeled 'approach' field (calls an EGFR-specific NSCLC assay 'Tumor-agnostic'), and unsupported round-number marketing metrics (totalParticipants 50000, numPublications 200) with no citation.

defects.issue: Labeled 'Tumor-agnostic' — incorrect. This is a single-gene EGFR assay validated and FDA-approved only for NSCLC EGFR TKI selection; it is gene- and indication-specific, not tumor-agnostic.
Value 50000 has no citation and appears to be a fabricated round number; FDA pivotal studies (e.g. ENSURE, AURA) involved far fewer plasma-validation patients (hundreds to low thousands).
Value 200 (numPublicationsPlus true) is uncited and an implausibly tidy round figure; not verifiable.
'1-2 days' is plausible lab TAT but the assay analytical runtime is <8 hours per Roche/FDA; record does not distinguish assay time from lab logistics.
'Medicare covered as CDx' is vague and uncited; no CPT code or MolDX/NCD reference (PCR EGFR typically CPT 81235).
Says '42 specific mutations' — accurate but the v2 panel breakdown (29 exon19 del, 5 exon20 ins, etc.) and exon 18-21 span are not captured.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: EGFR-only assay approved solely for NSCLC; not tumor-agnostic.
Uncited round number; far exceeds the patient counts in the FDA plasma-validation pivotal studies.
Uncited round number with numPublicationsPlus flag; not independently verifiable.

Original cobas EGFR (v1) tissue CDx was approved 2013 (P120019, with Tarceva); v2 itself was FDA-approved 2015 (P150047) for tissue and gained plasma label extension June 2016. '2013' conflates v1 with v2.

proposedFills.proposedValue: EGFR-targeted single-gene (NSCLC-specific)
Real-time PCR detecting 42 EGFR mutations across exons 18-21 (29 exon 19 deletions, exon 20 insertions, L858R, L861Q, G719X, S768I, T790M)
FDA-approved CDx: cobas EGFR v2 tissue approved 2015 (P150047); plasma label extension June 2016 (first FDA-approved liquid biopsy CDx)
FDA CDx for erlotinib (Tarceva), gefitinib (Iressa), and osimertinib (Tagrisso); plasma supports exon 19 del, L858R, and T790M (osimertinib eligibility)

summary: Accurate, FDA-sourced single-gene EGFR PCR companion diagnostic for NSCLC (tissue + plasma, 42 mutations), still actively offered by Roche; main defects are a wrong 'Tumor-agnostic' approach label, an FDA-date conflation of v1/v2, and uncited round-number participant/publication metrics.

name: OncoScreen Focus CDx
qualityRationale: Core genomic facts are accurate and vendor-verified (Jan 2026): 13-gene count, exact gene list, mutation types (SNV/indel/CNV/fusion), cancer types (NSCLC/CRC/GIST), and CE-IVD June 2022 status all corroborated by independent sources (LARVOL VERI, vendor PRs). However the record has structural/currency problems: it is classed as a "Central Lab Service / CLIA LDT" when OncoScreen Focus CDx is fundamentally a CE-IVD kit product (the "CDx" suffix = companion-diagnostic kit); every cited us.brbiotech.com URL now 308-redirects to the China .com site, so all citations are dead/stale; and US availability cannot be confirmed (Burning Rock has retrenched toward China/kit markets). TAT is self-labeled "estimated" and unverifiable.

defects.issue: Citation URL https://us.brbiotech.com/fixed-panels/ now 308-redirects to https://www.brbiotech.com/en — the US-facing product page is decommissioned; all us.brbiotech.com citations in this record are dead/moved.

Classified as 'Central Lab Service' with 'CLIA LDT (US)'. OncoScreen Focus CDx is fundamentally a CE-IVD kit (CDx = companion-diagnostic kit per vendor and LARVOL). US CLIA LDT availability is unverifiable and the US site is gone; product type is likely mislabeled.

'7-10 business days' is self-described as 'Estimated TAT based on central lab NGS workflow' — not a vendor-published figure; unverifiable, especially given the kit (not service) nature of the product.
States 'NASDAQ: BNR, LSE: BNR'. Burning Rock's NASDAQ ticker is BNR; the LSE:BNR claim is unverified/likely incorrect (the company is NASDAQ-listed).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: OncoScreen Focus CDx is documented everywhere as a CE-IVD tissue KIT, not a central-lab service. No active US central-lab page exists (site redirects to China).

suspectClaims.currentValue: CLIA LDT (US); CE-IVD kit; NMPA-IVD kit (9-gene version)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: US CLIA LDT availability cannot be confirmed; the US site is decommissioned. The NMPA 9-gene kit is a distinct sister product (LungCure CDx), not the same assay as the 13-gene CE OncoScreen Focus, so conflating them under one record is misleading.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Burning Rock Biotech Limited (NASDAQ: BNR, LSE: BNR)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Company is NASDAQ-listed (BNR); LSE:BNR listing is unverified and probably fabricated.

'38+ per vendor' partly counts 'Lungcure' publications, which concern the separate NMPA 9-gene kit, not the 13-gene CE OncoScreen Focus; the count may be inflated by attributing sister-product literature.

proposedFills.proposedValue: No FDA approval/clearance. CE-IVD marked (OncoScreen Focus CDx Tissue Kit, June 2022). Separate NMPA-approved 9-gene LungCure CDx kit exists (China, 2022).
CE mark received June 2022

Qualitative hybridization-capture NGS detecting SNV, indel, fusion/rearrangement, and CNV in 13 genes (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, RET, MET, ERBB2, BRAF, PIK3CA, KRAS, NRAS, HRAS, KIT, PDGFRA) from FFPE tissue in NSCLC, CRC, and GIST patients

summary: OncoScreen Focus CDx's core genomic facts (13 genes, gene list, mutation types, NSCLC/CRC/GIST, CE-IVD June 2022) are accurate and vendor-verified, but the record mislabels a CE-IVD kit as a US 'Central Lab Service,' relies on now-dead us.brbiotech.com citations, and conflates the distinct NMPA 9-gene LungCure sister kit. Current US availability is unverifiable.

name: CancerVista
qualityRationale: The record is internally detailed and the vendor/test still exist under this name, but it misclassifies the single most important MRD attribute: the vendor's own CancerVista page describes a tumor-informed personalized workflow ("identification of patient-specific mutations... to create an individual mutation profile," "following initial genotyping"), whereas the record states "Tumor-agnostic" and requiresTumorTissue "No." Turnaround time is also wrong (record 10 days vs vendor "2-4 weeks"). All performance numbers trace only to private vendor communication with no independent peer-reviewed CancerVista publication, so accuracy/currency/evidence backing are weak despite high field completeness.

defects.issue: Record labels CancerVista 'Tumor-agnostic', but the vendor's official CancerVista page describes a tumor-informed personalized workflow: 'Identification of patient-specific mutations from blood or tissue sample to create an individual mutation profile' then monitoring of those known mutations 'following initial genotyping'. This misrepresents the test's fundamental MRD methodology.

Set to 'No' with note 'does not require prior tumor tissue', contradicting the vendor's tumor-informed workflow which requires initial genotyping from 'blood or tissue sample' to build a patient-specific profile. At minimum prior genotyping is required; tissue is an accepted input.
Listed as '10 days', but the vendor CancerVista page states '2-4 weeks' (stated twice, in specifications and workflow). Turnaround is materially understated.

Claims 1 publication, but no peer-reviewed CancerVista publication could be located. The nearest PMC article (PMC12674089/PMID 41346570) is a Geneplus-Beijing assay, not CancerVista. Performance claims are effectively single-source vendor data.

All key performance values (80% sens, 96.7% spec, LOD 3.30x10^-6, 94.3% accuracy, n=150) are sourced only to private vendor communication (Jens Winter, Dec 2025); the cited vendor page does not independently substantiate the n=150 cohort and there is no peer-reviewed validation.

States 'commercially available in Europe', but the vendor page only describes the assay as 'ready for use as an in-house IVD test' with no CE-IVD/IVDR claim and no explicit commercial deployment statement.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor's CancerVista page describes a personalized tumor-informed workflow requiring initial genotyping and patient-specific mutation tracking.
Tumor-informed workflow requires an initial genotyping step from blood or tissue; framing it as needing nothing prior is misleading.
Vendor page states 2-4 weeks turnaround.
Sourced only to a vendor email; no public/peer-reviewed validation cohort of this size is findable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Commercially available in Europe via LIQOMICS laboratory
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor page describes test only as 'ready for use as an in-house IVD test', not confirmed commercial/clinical deployment.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Tumor-informed
Optional (initial genotyping from blood or tissue to build patient-specific mutation profile)
2-4 weeks
Technically validated; vendor describes it as 'ready for use as an in-house IVD test' from the LIQOMICS laboratory in Cologne, Germany. No CE-IVD/IVDR certification claimed.

summary: CancerVista is a real, still-offered LIQOMICS (Cologne) solid-tumor ctDNA MRD assay, but the record critically misclassifies it as 'Tumor-agnostic' / no tumor tissue when the vendor describes a tumor-informed personalized workflow requiring initial genotyping; turnaround (10 days vs vendor's 2-4 weeks) and the claimed publication/performance evidence are also unverified single-source vendor data.

name: Galleri
qualityRationale: Record is unusually accurate, current, and well-cited. Verified externally: GRAIL submitted FDA PMA Jan 29 2026 and FDA accepted for review (matches fdaStatus exactly); list price $949 confirmed on galleri.com; MCED scope, ~51.5% sensitivity / 99.5% specificity, per-stage sensitivity (~17/40/77/90%), and Breakthrough Device status all consistent with published CCGA/Klein 2021, PATHFINDER, and PATHFINDER-2/NHS-Galleri ASCO 2026 data. Coverage (Medicare NOT_COVERED, MCED Act Feb 2026, TRICARE) is current and correctly nuanced. Minor deductions: a few aggregate/synthesized fields (totalParticipants 188,535, numPublications 27) are not individually sourced, and the record correctly flags the NHS-Galleri primary-endpoint miss which is the most decision-relevant fact.

defects.issue: 188,535 is a sum of NHS-Galleri (142,250) + PATHFINDER 2 (~23,000) + SUMMIT (13,035) plus REACH; it is a synthesized aggregate not a single citable figure, and double-counts risk exists across overlapping enrollments. Reasonable but not independently verifiable as stated.
'27' (with numPublicationsPlus true) is an unsourced count; no citation backs the specific number.
Free-text '50+ cancer types including...' is vague; detectedCancerTypes array (22 types) is the validated set. The '50+' is GRAIL marketing language and not matched to a per-type evidence list.
93% top-1 / 97% top-2 CSO accuracy cited only to galleri.com performance page and a general note; specific study/table not pinned, though figure is consistent with published CCGA data.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: This is an OpenOnco-internal calculation (scripts/calculate-ias.js) weighting CCGA-3 observed sensitivities by SEER incidence; it is a derived metric not reported by GRAIL or any peer-reviewed source, so it is unverifiable against an external source and not directly comparable across tests.

PPV ~61.6% blends PATHFINDER-2 (~62% reported) with earlier PATHFINDER PPV (~38-43%); the single value should be tied to a specific study/population. PATHFINDER-2 (ASCO/ESMO 2025) is the basis but the precise 61.6% figure is not directly cited to a primary source.

summary: The Galleri (ecd-2) record is high quality and current: FDA PMA submission/acceptance, $949 price, MCED scope, and per-stage performance all verify against GRAIL and peer-reviewed sources, and it correctly captures the NHS-Galleri primary-endpoint miss. Only minor defects remain (unsourced aggregate counts and one internally-derived IAS metric).

name: Foundation TI-WGS MRD (RUO)
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: tumor-informed (tissue-informed) WGS MRD assay, RUO via FlexOMx Lab, launched Sept 23 2025, no matched-normal required, tumor tissue required, monitors hundreds to thousands of variants, not FDA cleared, not reimbursable. Coverage cross-reference (UHC 2026 unproven) is reasonable. However the record fabricates precise sensitivity (90%) and specificity (100%) percentages that appear on neither the press release nor the monitoring-portfolio page (both state only qualitative 'high sensitivity/specificity'), and the LOD is understated by an order of magnitude versus the vendor's headline claim (vendor: 1 ppm / 10^-6 / 0.0001%; record: 10 ppm / 0.001%). These accuracy issues cap the score. Name 'Foundation TI-WGS MRD (RUO)' is a reasonable shorthand but not the vendor's exact label ('Tissue-informed WGS MRD').

defects.issue: Record states '90% analytical sensitivity at 10 ppm tumor fraction threshold in feasibility studies' citing the Sept 23 2025 press release and monitoring portfolio. Neither official source states a 90% sensitivity figure; both describe only qualitative 'high sensitivity.' This appears fabricated/unverifiable and is attributed to sources that do not support it.

Record states '100% analytical specificity reported in feasibility data' citing 'Monitoring Portfolio technical specifications.' The monitoring portfolio page does not report a 100% specificity number; it states only qualitative 'high specificity.' Fabricated/unverifiable numeric claim.

Record lists LOD as '10 ppm (~0.001%).' Foundation Medicine's official monitoring-portfolio page states the assay detects ctDNA tumor fraction 'down to 10^-6 (1 PPM, 0.0001%)' — i.e., 1 ppm, ~10x more sensitive than the record claims. (The press-release text is internally ambiguous, but the vendor's headline LOD is 1 ppm.) Record understates the vendor's stated LOD by an order of magnitude.

Vendor's official name is 'Tissue-informed Whole Genome Sequencing Molecular Residual Disease (Tissue-informed WGS MRD).' 'Foundation TI-WGS MRD (RUO)' is a serviceable shorthand but not the exact vendor label.
Listed as 'Multi-solid tumors'; vendor describes 'early to late-stage cancers' without enumerating types, so acceptable but vague — no validated tumor-type list is published.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No official Foundation Medicine source publishes a 90% sensitivity figure for this assay; press release and monitoring portfolio give only qualitative 'high sensitivity.' Cited sources do not contain this number.
No official source reports 100% specificity; monitoring portfolio states qualitative 'high specificity' only. Citation ('technical specifications') does not substantiate.
Vendor's monitoring-portfolio page states LOD down to 1 ppm (10^-6, 0.0001%), ~10x lower than recorded.
Cited to 'FlexOMx Lab specifications' but no public Foundation Medicine page discloses a turnaround time for this RUO assay; unverifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 1 ppm (10^-6, 0.0001%) tumor fraction
Tissue-informed WGS MRD

Foundation Medicine reports only qualitative 'high sensitivity, accuracy, and precision in analytical performance studies'; no specific sensitivity percentage is published as of the Sept 23 2025 launch.

Tissue-informed whole-genome sequencing MRD assay: a proprietary bioinformatics algorithm incorporates WGS of tumor tissue and longitudinal plasma to detect and quantify ctDNA across hundreds to thousands of tumor-specific variants; matched-normal sample not required for CHIP/germline filtering.

summary: Foundation Medicine's Tissue-informed WGS MRD is a real, currently-offered RUO assay (launched Sept 23 2025 via FlexOMx Lab) and the record captures its core facts correctly, but it fabricates precise 90% sensitivity / 100% specificity figures unsupported by any cited source and understates the vendor's stated LOD (1 ppm) by ~10x. The qualitative facts are sound; the headline performance numbers are the main accuracy liability.

name: Resolution ctDx FIRST
qualityRationale: The technical/regulatory facts are accurate and well-cited (109 genes; PMA P210040 approved Dec 12, 2022; KRAS G12C CDx for adagrasib/KRAZATI; FDA-approved EGFR tumor profiling; analytical performance from FDA SSED and ASCO 2022). But the record's central currency claim is wrong and misleading: Agilent announced on Aug 21, 2023 that it was winding down the entire Resolution Bioscience business ($291M charge), citing no realistic path to profitability for kitted NGS CDx; the wind-down completed in early FY2024. The test is no longer offered, yet the record presents it as actively available ('Commercially available,' Quest distribution, Medicare 'COVERED' lastVerified 2026-01-13, active commercial payers) with no mention of discontinuation. The record was actually ADDED to the database Dec 24, 2025 via an LLM pipeline that failed to catch a shutdown 2+ years prior. High evidence backing on facts, but the availability/coverage picture is materially false today.

defects.issue: States 'Commercially available since December 2022; Quest Diagnostics distribution since January 2023' with no mention that Agilent wound down the entire Resolution Bioscience business beginning Aug 21, 2023 (completed early FY2024). The test is no longer offered. Record presents a discontinued test as active.

The test is retired. Agilent CEO announced shutdown of Resolution Bioscience on the Q3 FY2023 earnings call (Aug 21, 2023), taking a $291M charge; the Kirkland/Seattle CLIA lab that performed this single-site PMA test was wound down. With the lab closed, the test cannot be run.

medicareCoverage.status 'COVERED' with lastVerified '2026-01-13' is misleading — coverage is moot for a test that is no longer performed; a 2026 verification date implies active availability that does not exist.
Reimbursement 'Medicare + Commercial' and commercial payer list (Blue Shield CA, Aetna, Blue Cross NC, crawled 2026-02-01) imply an orderable test; stale/misleading given discontinuation.
'Available via Quest Diagnostics network... 2,100+ patient service centers' describes the Jan 2023 launch but ignores the subsequent shutdown; no longer true.
'Agilent / Resolution Bioscience' — Resolution Bioscience was a wholly-owned Agilent unit that has been dissolved; vendor attribution should note defunct status.
Same FDA URL duplicated twice (pipe-separated identical links).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: 'Over 1,000 patients in ongoing prospective NSCLC study with Memorial Sloan Kettering' is sourced to a 2021 Mirati CDx press release and conflates a research collaboration with the CDx validation cohort; the FDA CDx approval rested on KRYSTAL-1 bridging samples, not a 1,000-patient MSK study. Likely inflated/mismatched.

Listed publications mix assay-specific (ASCO 2022, FDA SSED) with general ctDNA-platform papers (JNCI 2018, CCR 2016, Lung Cancer 2019) that predate ctDx FIRST and describe earlier Resolution assays; count of dedicated peer-reviewed validation papers for ctDx FIRST specifically is thin (mainly an ASCO abstract + SSED).
Sourced to a 2018 JNCI ctDNA paper (djy156), not to ctDx FIRST's own performance; TAT figure is borrowed from an earlier/different assay.
Cited to an ASCO 2022 abstract; exact per-variant PPA figures should be confirmed against the FDA SSED (P210040B) rather than relying on the abstract.

proposedFills.proposedValue: DISCONTINUED. Agilent announced wind-down of the Resolution Bioscience business on Aug 21, 2023 (Q3 FY2023 earnings; ~$291M pre-tax charge), citing no realistic path to profitability for kitted NGS companion diagnostics; the Kirkland/Seattle CLIA lab that performed this single-site PMA test was closed and the test is no longer offered. Previously: FDA-approved Dec 2022, distributed via Quest Diagnostics from Jan 2023.
no

PMA P210040 approved Dec 12, 2022 (CDx for KRAS G12C / adagrasib in NSCLC; also FDA-approved for EGFR tumor profiling). NOTE: device is no longer commercially offered following Agilent's 2023 wind-down of Resolution Bioscience; PMA approval remains on record but the test is not orderable.

summary: Technical and FDA facts (109 genes, P210040 approved Dec 12 2022, KRAS G12C CDx for adagrasib plus EGFR tumor profiling) are accurate and well-cited, but the record critically misrepresents a discontinued test as actively available: Agilent wound down the entire Resolution Bioscience business starting Aug 21, 2023, closing the lab, yet the record shows current Medicare/commercial coverage and a 2026 verification date with no mention of the shutdown.

name: Tempus xM for TRM
qualityRationale: Core identity is accurate and well-sourced: xM for TRM is a real Tempus assay (announced June 2, 2025) that is tumor-naive and estimates ctDNA tumor fraction via a multi-parametric algorithm integrating CNVs plus somatic/germline VAFs, for ICI response monitoring in advanced solid tumors — all matching the record. However the record contains an unverifiable fabricated-looking response definition (the specific "≥50% reduction... post-cycle 1" threshold is not in any public source; the vendor's claim is "molecular response prior to six weeks"), stale availability language ("expected later in 2025" — now June 2026), an unverifiable publication count, a sampleCitations URL pointing to the xM MRD (different product) PDF, and speculative payer-policy attributions. LOD is self-flagged as inferred/unvalidated. Method and approach are right; several quantitative/peripheral fields are weak or unverifiable.

defects.issue: "≥50% reduction in ctDNA tumor fraction from baseline to early on-treatment time point (e.g., post-cycle 1)" is not stated in any public Tempus source. The vendor's actual claim is detecting molecular response prior to six weeks of treatment; the specific 50%/post-cycle-1 threshold appears fabricated or unverifiable.

States clinical availability "expected later in 2025 per Tempus' June 2025 xM for TRM announcement." Current date is June 2026; the 2025 horizon is now stale and no confirmed clinical-launch status is given.
numPublications=3 (numPublicationsPlus=true) is unverifiable. The only public clinical data identified is an ASCO 2025 abstract; no peer-reviewed publications specific to xM for TRM were found.
Cites Tempus-LS_xM-MRD_Test-Information PDF, which is the xM MRD (CRC recurrence) product, not the xM for TRM/ICI-monitoring assay being described here. Wrong/mismatched supporting document.

Describes the algorithm as operating on "Tempus xF/xF+ liquid biopsy data." The TRM announcement describes a multi-parametric CNV + somatic/germline VAF algorithm but does not explicitly attribute it to the xF/xF+ panel; the xF linkage is an inference.

Aetna CPB 0715 and Cigna mm_0520 are generic tumor-profiling policies labeled EXPERIMENTAL for this assay; this is a speculative attribution rather than a policy that names xM for TRM. Presents inferred non-coverage as policy-specific.

suspectClaims.currentValue: ≥50% reduction in ctDNA tumor fraction from baseline to early on-treatment time point (e.g., post-cycle 1)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: No public Tempus or peer-reviewed source states a ≥50%/post-cycle-1 molecular-response definition for xM for TRM; the vendor frames it as detecting molecular response prior to six weeks. Likely synthesized/invented threshold.
Only an ASCO 2025 abstract was identifiable; a count of 3 publications cannot be verified from public sources.
Record itself notes LoD is inferred from xF/xF+ specs and general literature, not validated for the TRM application. Unverified.
As of June 2026 this is stale; current clinical-availability status is unconfirmed.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Detects molecular response to ICI therapy from longitudinal ctDNA tumor-fraction dynamics, with molecular response detectable prior to six weeks of treatment; longitudinal non-molecular responders show worse survival than molecular responders (ASCO 2025).

Tumor-naive multi-parametric algorithm integrating copy number variations (CNVs) and somatic and germline variant allele frequencies (VAFs) to estimate circulating tumor fraction longitudinally from plasma.

Research use only (RUO) at June 2025 launch, with clinical availability announced as planned for later in 2025; used primarily in research and biopharma collaborations. Tempus has separately submitted its tumor-naive xM assay in CRC to MolDX and expects to submit a more sensitive NSCLC version for reimbursement in 2026. Confirm current clinical/reimbursement status with vendor.
Advanced solid tumors treated with immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI), alone or in combination

summary: trm-4 correctly captures Tempus xM for TRM as a real, still-offered tumor-naive ctDNA-tumor-fraction assay for ICI response monitoring (announced June 2025, RUO), but it contains an unverifiable/likely-fabricated "≥50%/post-cycle-1" response definition, stale "available later in 2025" language, an unverifiable publication count, and a sampleCitations URL pointing to the unrelated xM MRD product. No duplicate id detected.

name: FoundationOne CDx
qualityRationale: Core facts verified against vendor and FDA: tissue FFPE hybrid-capture NGS, 324 genes, PMA P170019 approved 2017-11-30, MSI/TMB reported, CPT 0037U (PLA), ADLT, $3,500 Medicare rate, MolDX/NCD 90.2 coverage.
