The headline CDx count is the main weakness: vendor's Dec 2025 milestone was exactly 100 (57 US + 43 Japan across BOTH tissue and liquid), but the record asserts "59 US ... 100+ total including Japan," which conflates the combined tissue+liquid portfolio total with the tissue F1CDx specifically and slightly misframes the Japan split. The 59 US figure is a reasonable synthesis (57 at Dec 2025 + 2 documented 2026 approvals) but is not directly stated by the cited Dec 2025 source. Other facts are accurate and well-cited; deductions are for the CDx-count framing imprecision, the unverified-at-that-exact-number 59, and minor internal inconsistencies.

defects.issue: States '59 FDA-approved CDx indications in US ... (100+ total CDx indications including Japan).' The vendor's Dec 2025 milestone of 100 is the COMBINED total of FoundationOne CDx (tissue) AND FoundationOne Liquid CDx across US (57) + Japan (43) — not 100 attributable to F1CDx tissue alone, and not '100+ including Japan' as an add-on. Framing conflates the portfolio-wide count with this single tissue assay.

Value 59 (US) is a reasonable synthesis (57 confirmed at Dec 2025 + TEPMETKO May 2026 + TALZENNA/XTANDI Jun 2026) but the cited businesswire Dec 2025 source states 57 US, not 59. The exact 59 figure is not directly supported by any single cited source; it is an internal extrapolation.
medicareCoverage.cptCode is null while top-level cptCodes is '0037U' — internal inconsistency; the Medicare coverage block omits the PLA code it reimburses under.
1003 publications is a vendor self-estimate ('>1,000', vendor-estimate flagged). Appropriately labeled but the precise '1003' implies false precision for what is a '>1,000' vendor claim.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor's official Dec 2025 milestone press release states 57 US CDx approvals (43 Japan; 100 total across tissue+liquid). 59 requires crediting two 2026 US approvals to the tissue F1CDx; plausible but not directly cited at that number, and the 100-total is portfolio-wide not F1CDx-specific.

Vendor marketing statistic; medicareCoverage block separately claims '97% of Medicare patients have $0 financial responsibility.' The two percentages (87% vs 97%) are inconsistent point figures and both are vendor-sourced, not independently verifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: As of the Dec 2025 milestone, Foundation Medicine's portfolio (FoundationOne CDx tissue + FoundationOne Liquid CDx) reached 100 approved/active CDx indications: 57 in the US and 43 in Japan. Two additional US tissue CDx approvals followed in 2026: TEPMETKO (tepotinib, MET exon 14 skipping NSCLC, May 21 2026) and TALZENNA+XTANDI (talazoparib+enzalutamide, HRR-mutated mCRPC, Jun 1 2026).

proposedFills.proposedValue_note: US count of FoundationOne CDx + Liquid CDx as officially stated Dec 2025; if isolating tissue F1CDx post-2026 approvals, document the derivation explicitly rather than asserting 59 against a 57 source.

summary: FoundationOne CDx is accurately represented and still actively offered: 324-gene tissue CGP, PMA P170019 (2017), CPT 0037U PLA/ADLT at $3,500, MolDX/NCD 90.2 covered. The only material weakness is the FDA CDx count framing — the record's '59 US / 100+ including Japan' conflates the portfolio-wide tissue+liquid total (vendor's Dec 2025 figure was 57 US + 43 Japan = 100) and the exact 59 is an extrapolation not directly stated by the cited source.

name: Decipher Prostate
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: 22-gene RNA whole-transcriptome classifier on FFPE tissue, CLIA LDT, score 0-1 with Low ≤0.45 / High ≥0.60 thresholds matching the vendor page, Medicare MolDX coverage, and NCCN inclusion — all verified against veracyte.com. Penalized because (1) performanceCitations contains a fabricated placeholder DOI (10.1016/j.cell.2025.08.XXX) violating the no-fake-source rule, (2) the method/cancerTypesNotes frame the test narrowly as post-prostatectomy metastasis-within-5-years while the test now spans biopsy/localized, N1, and metastatic disease (Decipher Prostate Metastatic launched 2025), and (3) the NCCN note understates the evidence level (Decipher is the only gene-expression test in NCCN prostate guidelines with Level 1 / highest evidence rating). Note: the STAMPEDE finding is correctly characterized as predictive of docetaxel benefit, but it is categorized here under TDS/CGP whereas this is fundamentally a prognostic gene-expression classifier, not a comprehensive genomic profiling (DNA mutation/CDx) test.

defects.issue: Contains a fabricated/placeholder DOI 'https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2025.08.XXX' — the 'XXX' is not a real identifier and resolves to nothing. The STAMPEDE paper was published in Cell with a real URL (https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674(25)00864-5). Also 'ASTRO 2025 BALANCE abstract' is a non-URL placeholder. Violates the no-fabricated-source rule.

States score predicts 'risk of metastasis within 5 years of radical prostatectomy' — stale and overly narrow. The test is validated and offered for biopsy/localized, N1, and metastatic disease; Veracyte launched Decipher Prostate Metastatic in 2025. The method/cancerTypesNotes do not reflect current indications.

Understates evidence: Decipher is the ONLY gene-expression test in NCCN prostate guidelines and carries the highest evidence-level rating (Level 1 / Category 1 validation per Veracyte/NCCN). Record just says 'include Decipher'.
Says '100+ publications'; vendor states 'more than 75 peer-reviewed studies comprising nearly 110,000 patients.' The 100+ figure is plausible but not matched to the vendor's stated count; unverified.

Classified under CGP. This is a prognostic RNA gene-expression classifier, not comprehensive genomic profiling (no DNA mutations/CNV/fusion/MSI/TMB/CDx). approach='Tissue Gene Expression' is accurate but the CGP category is a loose fit.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Placeholder 'XXX' DOI is not a resolvable identifier; the STAMPEDE paper has a real Cell URL. BALANCE entry is a bare description, not a citable link.
Veracyte's own page states 'more than 75 peer-reviewed published studies' (nearly 110,000 patients). The 100+ count is not corroborated by the vendor.
Omits biopsy/localized and metastatic (Decipher Prostate Metastatic, 2025) indications which the test now covers.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674(25)00864-5
proposedFills.source: Cell (2025), 'Tumor transcriptome-wide expression classifiers predict treatment sensitivity in advanced prostate cancers' (STAMPEDE Decipher docetaxel ancillary study)

proposedFills.proposedValue: 22-gene RNA whole-transcriptome microarray genomic classifier (GC) on FFPE prostate tumor tissue (biopsy or radical prostatectomy, prior to radiation/hormone therapy). Generates a continuous Decipher score (0-1) across 22 biomarkers spanning seven prostate-cancer biological pathways; validated for localized, N1, and metastatic disease.
Decipher is the only gene-expression test recommended in the NCCN Prostate Cancer Guidelines and is the only such test with the highest evidence-level (Level 1) validation rating.
More than 75 peer-reviewed published studies comprising nearly 110,000 patients (per Veracyte).

Prostate cancer: risk stratification in localized disease (biopsy or post-prostatectomy), and prediction of treatment benefit (e.g., docetaxel) in metastatic/advanced disease. Decipher Prostate Metastatic launched 2025.

summary: Decipher Prostate (Veracyte) is still offered and the record's core facts (22-gene RNA classifier, FFPE tissue, LDT, 0-1 score with Low ≤0.45/High ≥0.60 thresholds, Medicare MolDX/NCCN) are accurate. Main defects: a fabricated placeholder DOI in performanceCitations (real Cell URL exists), a stale post-prostatectomy-only framing that omits the 2025 metastatic indication, and an understated NCCN evidence level.

name: Tempus xF
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and well-cited against the vendor validation white paper (Finkle et al. 2021, npj Precision Oncology) and Tempus product pages. The 105-gene count, 2-tube Streck cfDNA collection, MSI-H reporting, advanced-solid-tumor population, CLIA LDT status, and the SNV/indel/CNG/rearrangement sensitivity/specificity figures all match published sources. Correctly distinguished from the 523-gene xF+ (tds-7) and correctly omits TMB (xF does not report TMB; only xF+ reports bTMB). Minor deductions for an unbroken-out MSI sensitivity (actually only ~37.5% per validation) that the record's generic ">99%" framing could mislead on, a vendor-only numPublications=20 claim, and a Medicare LCD entry lacking CPT code and policy name.

defects.issue: States '>99% for SNVs/CNGs ... >97% for rearrangements' but omits that MSI-H detection sensitivity is only ~37.5% per the Tempus xF validation, despite MSI being listed as a reported biomarker. The high headline sensitivity could mislead on the MSI claim.
cptCode and policyName are null; LCD L38043 is asserted as COVERED but without CPT (likely 81479/0239U) or policy title, making the coverage claim harder to verify.
numPublications=20+ is sourced only to a single PubMed link (the validation paper); the '20+' aggregate is a vendor-style claim that is not independently verifiable from the cited source.
'Coverage Varies' is vague given medicareCoverage is asserted COVERED; the two fields are mildly inconsistent in tone.

suspectClaims.currentValue: >99% for SNVs/CNGs at ≥0.5% VAF; >98% for indels; >97% for rearrangements
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Accurate for SNV/indel/CNG/rearrangement but the record lists MSI as a reported biomarker while the validation reports MSI-H sensitivity of only ~37.5% (specificity >99.9%); the headline does not reflect the low MSI sensitivity.

L38043 is asserted as the covering plasma genomic profiling LCD but could not be confirmed as the specific xF policy in 2025-2026 search; MolDX liquid-biopsy LCDs have been reorganized and the assignment is unverified.

proposedFills.proposedValue: MSI-H detected with sensitivity 37.5% and specificity >99.9% (separate from SNV/indel/CNG/rearrangement metrics)
105-gene hybrid-capture NGS panel spanning ~270 kb; detects SNVs and indels in 105 genes, CNGs in 6 genes, CNLs in 2 genes (BRCA1/BRCA2), rearrangements in 6 genes, and MSI-H

summary: Tempus xF is still offered and the OpenOnco record is accurate and well-sourced: 105-gene liquid CGP, MSI-H (no TMB), 2-tube Streck collection, CLIA LDT, with validation-backed performance metrics. Only minor gaps remain (unbroken-out low MSI sensitivity, missing CPT/policy detail, and an unverified specific Medicare LCD assignment).

name: OnkoSkan
qualityRationale: Core facts (vendor, assay = DxPG80 hPG80 ELISA, LDT status, not covered, blood/EDTA plasma, LoD 1 pM, TAT, 18+ population) are accurate and match the Protean BioDx page and peer-reviewed sources. Citations are real PubMed/RSC/clinicaltrials URLs. Main weaknesses: the MCED/"cancer screening" framing overstates the evidence — the most rigorous prospective diagnostic study (ONCOPRO) reported only AUC 0.63 overall, and the vendor positions it as an aid/monitoring tool, not a standalone screen; no headline clinical sensitivity/specificity (only HCC 84% and a median-pM contrast) is given; ECS-Progastrin is now branded Biodena Care.

defects.issue: Record labels OnkoSkan an MCED screening test, but the strongest peer-reviewed evidence (ONCOPRO prospective case-control, NCT03787056, n=421 cancers vs 330 healthy) found only modest overall diagnostic accuracy (ROC AUC 0.63), with meaningful performance only in lung and HCC (AUC 0.75). Authors frame hPG80 as a complementary biomarker, and the vendor calls it an 'aid' for screening and treatment monitoring. Calling it MCED screening overstates the evidence.
ECS-Progastrin (maker of the DxPG80 assay) now operates as 'Biodena Care' (biodenacare.com). Vendor naming is stale though not wrong.

No overall multi-cancer sensitivity/specificity reported; only HCC (84%) and a median-pM comparison are given. The pivotal ONCOPRO study's AUC 0.63 and per-cancer specificity (~88% lung/HCC) are not captured.
Empty object; ONCOPRO and per-cancer publications provide AUC/specificity values that could populate this.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Cancer screening and treatment response monitoring via circulating progastrin levels
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Prospective ONCOPRO data (AUC 0.63) do not support use as a general cancer screen; both authors and the vendor position it as a complementary aid, mainly relevant in lung/HCC.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Pan-cancer biomarker applicable to 16+ cancer types
suspectClaims.whySuspect: The '16 cancer types' claim derives from ONCOPRO, which showed clinically useful accuracy in only 2 (lung, HCC); 'applicable to 16+' conflates analytic detection across origins with validated diagnostic utility.
Round figure with notes citing only 3 studies; the count is plausible but unverified against a complete bibliography.

proposedFills.proposedValue: In the prospective ONCOPRO study (NCT03787056), overall diagnostic accuracy was modest (ROC AUC 0.63, 95% CI 0.59-0.67); best performance in lung cancer (AUC 0.75; specificity 88% at hPG80 >7.73 pM in patients >58y) and HCC (AUC 0.75; specificity 88%).
Multi-cancer biomarker; positioned by vendor and authors as a complementary/adjunct aid rather than a validated standalone MCED screen
Protean BioDiagnostics / ECS-Progastrin (now Biodena Care)
eBioMedicine 2020 (n=1,546, 11 cancer types, case-control); ONCOPRO prospective case-control (n=421 cancers vs 330 healthy, 16 cancer types, AUC 0.63)

summary: The OnkoSkan record is factually sound on vendor, assay (DxPG80 hPG80 ELISA), LDT/uncovered status, and analytics, with real citations, but overstates the evidence by framing a weak prospective biomarker (ONCOPRO overall AUC 0.63) as an MCED cancer screen. Minor currency gaps: ECS-Progastrin now operates as Biodena Care, and overall sensitivity/specificity plus per-cancer data are missing.

name: Trucheck Intelli
qualityRationale: The core profile (vendor, CTC-based MCED scope, ~70 solid-tumor types, CE-IVD status, asymptomatic 40-70 target population, ~88% sensitivity / 96-99% specificity) is accurate and matches the vendor's current page. But the record is undermined by a serious citation-integrity failure: all three PubMed PMIDs (31743438, 33029899, 32898291) cited across performanceCitations, numPublicationsCitations, and performanceNotes resolve to entirely unrelated papers (retinopathy of prematurity, bomb calorimetry, IVF aneuploidy testing) — the correct Datar PMIDs are 31736065 (Akolkar), 32996712 (Gaya), and 32958586 (Ranade). The named DOI cncy.22366 is real, but it is a SUSPECTED-cancer diagnostic-triaging study, not an asymptomatic-screening validation, so it does not cleanly support the headline MCED sensitivity. Score is capped well below the data's apparent completeness because cited evidence is verifiably wrong.

defects.issue: All three PMIDs (31743438, 33029899, 32898291) point to unrelated articles (retinopathy of prematurity, bomb calorimetry, IVF preimplantation genetic testing). Fabricated/incorrect citations. Correct Datar PMIDs are 31736065 (Akolkar Int J Cancer 2020), 32996712 (Gaya Cancer Cytopathol 2021), 32958586 (Ranade Cancer Prev Res 2021).
Same three wrong PMIDs reused here; the cited PubMed IDs do not correspond to the Datar papers named in numPublicationsNotes.

88.2% sensitivity is sourced to Gaya cncy.22366, which is a pan-cancer diagnostic-triaging study in known/suspected cancer cohorts, not an asymptomatic MCED screening validation. Sensitivity figure is mis-scoped for an ECD/screening claim.

Vendor states ~70 SOLID tumour types (~81% of European cancers); record says '70+ cancer types' without making clear hematologic malignancies are excluded. Solid-tumor-only limitation should be explicit.

Record fixes specificity at 96.3% from one study; vendor reports a 96-99% range (and other studies cite up to ~99.3-100% in asymptomatic subsets). Single point value is narrower than supportable evidence.
Marked false despite an expert/vendor-affiliated submitter (jigar.pandya@datarpgx.org); verification status unresolved.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Every PMID resolves to an unrelated, non-oncology article — these are wrong/fabricated identifiers, not the Datar Trucheck studies they purport to cite.

Derived from a suspected-cancer diagnostic-triaging cohort (Gaya cncy.22366), not from asymptomatic screening; applying it as an MCED screening sensitivity overstates performance in the intended population.
Unsupported and inconsistent with the cited literature, which reports cohorts of thousands to tens of thousands; appears arbitrary.
Vendor marketing figure ('>40,000') with no peer-reviewed citation tying that exact number to Trucheck Intelli validation; unverifiable as stated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31736065/ | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32996712/ | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32958586/
proposedFills.source: PubMed records: Akolkar et al. Int J Cancer 2020 (31736065, C-ETACs hallmark); Gaya et al. Cancer Cytopathol 2021 (32996712, pan-cancer diagnostic triaging); Ranade et al. Cancer Prev Res 2021 (32958586, 230x one-year risk)

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31736065/ | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32996712/ | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32958586/
proposedFills.source: Same corrected PubMed records for the three Datar publications named in numPublicationsNotes

proposedFills.proposedValue: Asymptomatic individuals aged 40-70 with elevated cancer risk (e.g., family history), no prior cancer diagnosis and no current clinical/radiological suspicion
Multi-cancer (up to 70 solid tumour types; covers ~81% of European cancer cases; hematologic malignancies not detected)
Vendor reports specificity of 96-99% in real-world data; record's 96.3% reflects one study within that range

summary: Trucheck Intelli is a still-offered, CE-IVD CTC-based MCED test (up to ~70 solid tumours; asymptomatic 40-70 population) whose top-line vendor and performance facts are broadly accurate, but the record is critically compromised by three fabricated/incorrect PubMed PMIDs and a sensitivity claim sourced from a suspected-cancer (not screening) cohort. Correct PMIDs (31736065, 32996712, 32958586) and clearer scope/population/specificity language should replace the current values.

name: FoundationOne Heme
qualityRationale: Core clinical facts (gene counts ~406 DNA/265 RNA, LDT/not-FDA status, ~2-week TAT, sample types, TMB/MSI reporting, NCD 90.2 coverage) are accurate and well-cited to the vendor page. The major problem is billing: the cptCode 0111U is factually wrong (it is Illumina's Praxis Extended RAS Panel, a KRAS/NRAS colon test, not FoundationOne Heme), and the medicareRate ($682.29), medicareEffective ("2025-Q4"), and cptCodesNotes are all tied to that wrong code, making them unreliable. The correct PLA/MAAA code is 0016M. Secondary unverifiable items (listPrice 3500, broad international region list) further reduce currency/accuracy confidence.

defects.issue: 0111U is the wrong CPT code. 0111U = Illumina Praxis Extended RAS Panel (KRAS/NRAS, colorectal cancer), an unrelated test. FoundationOne Heme's MAAA/PLA code is 0016M (Oncology, hematolymphoid neoplasia, gene rearrangement / CGP). Misleading billing data.
$682.29 is the CLFS rate associated with the wrong code (0111U), not FoundationOne Heme. Rate is unreliable as a result and should not be presented as this test's reimbursement.

Notes claim 'FoundationOne Heme PLA code (BCBSM EST status Jan 2025)' but are attached to code 0111U, which is not the FoundationOne Heme code. The PLA-code claim contradicts the populated code value.
'2025-Q4' pricing inherits from the wrong CPT code; cannot be verified for FoundationOne Heme's actual code.
Record says '10-14 days'; vendor page states '2 weeks from receipt of specimen.' Close but not an exact match to the cited source.
Record uses 406; GTR and some FMI technical specs list 405 DNA genes ('complete coding sequence of 405 genes, selected introns of 31'). Vendor page says '>400'. Minor discrepancy worth a footnote.
listPrice 3500 cited to a patient-FAQ page that does not clearly state this figure; FMI list prices are commonly cited higher. Unverifiable against the given citation.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: 0111U maps to Illumina Praxis Extended RAS Panel per AAPC/AMA PLA list, not FoundationOne Heme. The FoundationOne Heme code is 0016M.
Matches the CLFS rate for 0111U, the wrong code; not a verified rate for FoundationOne Heme's actual code (0016M).
Cited to foundationmedicine.com/faq/patient-faqs which does not substantiate this exact figure; FMI tissue CGP list prices are generally quoted higher.

suspectClaims.currentValue: US, EU, UK, Japan, Canada, Australia, International (100+ countries via Roche)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: FoundationOne Heme is a US CLIA LDT; broad multi-region availability under this exact name is plausible via Roche but not clearly documented on the cited vendor/Roche pages for this specific assay.

proposedFills.proposedValue: ~2 weeks from receipt of specimen
405 (complete coding sequence of 405 genes plus selected introns of 31 rearrangement genes)
SNVs, indels, CNAs, rearrangements/fusions, TMB, MSI (per vendor LDT description)
FFPE tissue, peripheral whole blood, bone marrow aspirate, or extracted nucleic acid

summary: FoundationOne Heme is still offered by Foundation Medicine as a US CLIA LDT (~406 DNA / 265 RNA gene CGP for hematologic malignancies and sarcomas), and the record's clinical fields are largely accurate and vendor-cited. The record's billing data is the main failure: CPT 0111U is the wrong code (it is Illumina's Praxis Extended RAS Panel), and the associated $682.29 Medicare rate and Q4-2025 pricing inherit from that error — the correct PLA/MAAA code is 0016M.

name: Signatera Genome
qualityRationale: Record is highly accurate and current. All core technical facts verify against Natera's official Signatera Genome page and ASCO 2025 data: tumor-informed WGS approach, 64 clonal variants, 1 ppm LOD, up to 350,000x coverage per variant, 94% longitudinal sensitivity / 100% specificity, 392-patient pan-cancer cohort (>2,600 samples) across breast/NSCLC/melanoma/RCC/CRC, 4-week initial TAT, 7-10 day follow-up, new-patients-only ordering, and Medicare coverage under LCD L38779 (announced June 2025). The single material accuracy defect is the CPT code: the record lists 0239U, but Signatera's active PLA code is 0340U (0239U is an unrelated genomic-profiling PLA code). Other deductions are minor: ASCO citation is a JCO suppl URL that should be verified, and an internal cashPay vs listPrice inconsistency.

defects.issue: Lists '0239U', which is not Signatera's code. Standard Signatera's active PLA code is 0340U; 0239U is an unrelated comprehensive genomic profiling PLA code. Signatera Genome bills under MolDX/L38779 and either uses 0340U or a distinct new PLA code, not 0239U.

States cashPay '$4,150' while listPrice/medicareRate fields say $3,500 and financialAssistance cites prompt-pay cash of $349. The $4,150 cash-pay figure is not internally reconciled or sourced to a Genome-specific page.

Cites https://ascopubs.org/doi/10.1200/JCO.2025.43.16_suppl.3142 as the ASCO 2025 abstract; abstract number/DOI not independently confirmed in this audit and should be verified against the actual JCO supplement entry.

Set to placeholder ['Same as Signatera'] rather than the enumerated indication list that is actually present elsewhere in the record (reimbursementNote and coverageCrossReference.medicare.indications).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Signatera's PLA code is 0340U per AAPC/OpenPayer; 0239U maps to a different (Oncomine/comprehensive genomic profiling) assay. Wrong code for any Signatera variant.
Marked 'Expected same pricing as standard Signatera' — i.e. inferred, not confirmed for the Genome product specifically; conflicts with the $4,150 cashPay figure elsewhere in the same record.

Accurate as a pan-cancer longitudinal figure, but landmark/post-surgical sensitivity is materially lower (>70% in NSCLC/breast per ASCO); single 94% headline number may overstate post-surgical landmark performance if read without that context.

proposedFills.proposedValue: MolDX: Molecular Residual Disease Testing for Cancer (LCD L38779); Medicare coverage for Signatera Genome announced June 2025

Pan-cancer validation: 94% longitudinal sensitivity, 100% specificity across 5 tumor types (breast, NSCLC, melanoma, RCC, CRC); Signatera-negative patients had 100% DRFS at 12mo and 99% at 24mo, vs 41% (12mo) and 14% (24mo) for Signatera-positive; ~50% of surveillance-positive cases detected in ultra-sensitive range (<=100 ppm).
Broad U.S. clinical launch April 2025

summary: Signatera Genome (Natera) is a currently offered, ultra-sensitive tumor-informed WGS-designed mPCR-NGS MRD test; the record is accurate and current on method, 64-variant/1 ppm performance, ASCO 2025 cohort, and Medicare coverage (L38779). The notable defect is a wrong CPT code (0239U listed; Signatera's code is 0340U), plus minor pricing/citation inconsistencies.

name: HelioLiver
qualityRationale: Core record is accurate and well-cited: the corrected CLiMB prospective figures (47.8% overall sensitivity, 37.8% for HCC ≤4cm, 88% specificity) match the peer-reviewed Journal of Hepatology publication (May 6, 2026), and the documented vendor-correction history (downgrading from the inflated ENCORE case-control 85%/91% numbers) shows strong evidence discipline. CPT 0333U, ADLT status, and the ~$662 Medicare rate are all verified. Points deducted for stale currency: the record entirely misses the April 2026 Quest Diagnostics nationwide commercial launch and the May 2026 peer-reviewed publication, and the embedded medicareCoverage block still carries the obsolete, internally-contradictory 85%/91% numbers that the top-level fields already corrected.

defects.issue: Stale and internally contradictory: still states '85% all-stage HCC sensitivity, 91% specificity' — the very ENCORE case-control numbers the rest of the record was corrected away from in Jan 2026 (now 47.8%/88% per CLiMB). This obsolete claim directly contradicts the corrected top-level sensitivity/specificity fields.

Misses the material April 2026 Quest Diagnostics nationwide launch — HelioLiver is now distributed through Quest's ~7,700 patient service centers, plus a May 2026 Syneos Health commercialization partnership. Record only says 'Commercially available as LDT' with no mention of the expanded national channel.
Value '2' is stale; CLiMB was published as a peer-reviewed paper in Journal of Hepatology on 2026-05-06, which should be reflected (and cited) rather than left as an EASL 2024 abstract reference.
Labeled 37.8% but that figure is the published '≤4cm' value; the published CLiMB Stage I/T1 sensitivity is 40.0% (vs 10.0% ultrasound). Field conflates 'small lesion ≤4cm' with 'Stage I'.
'PMA submitted Q2 2024; currently LDT' is correct but undated against current status — as of mid-2026 PMA still not finalized (originally targeted Q2/Q3 2025); should note the slipped timeline.
listPrice $525 is explicitly an estimate ('$450-$600 based on market positioning'), not a sourced vendor/Quest price; flagged as low-confidence.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited only to 'Helio Genomics Investor Data', not a peer-reviewed source; PPV depends heavily on assumed HCC prevalence in the surveillance population and is not reported in the published CLiMB paper. Unverifiable as stated.
Cited only to 'Helio Genomics Investor Data'; not corroborated by the peer-reviewed CLiMB publication. Plausible for a low-prevalence rule-out test but unverifiable from a primary source.

Test now has a priced ADLT CPT code (0333U, ~$662) and is live through Quest nationwide; coverage posture may have advanced beyond 'expected'. Needs re-verification of actual Medicare/MAC coverage determination.
Self-described estimate, not a sourced price.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Per published CLiMB (Journal of Hepatology, May 2026): 47.8% overall HCC sensitivity, 87.6% specificity, superior to ultrasound. Earlier 85%/91% figures (ENCORE case-control) were retired as overstated.
Distributed nationwide via Quest Diagnostics (~7,700 patient service centers) effective April 2026; Syneos Health commercialization partnership announced May 2026.
CLiMB prospective study published in Journal of Hepatology, May 6, 2026 (n=1,968; validation cohort n=1,556; 42 US sites).
CPT 0333U is a Category I PLA code (effective Oct 1, 2022) specific to the HelioLiver Test from Fulgent Genetics LLC / Helio Health Inc.; Medicare-priced at ~$662.

summary: HelioLiver is still actively offered (single-cancer HCC surveillance blood test) and the record's corrected CLiMB performance figures are accurate and well-sourced to the May 2026 Journal of Hepatology publication. Main weaknesses are currency — it misses the April 2026 Quest Diagnostics nationwide launch and the peer-reviewed publication — and a stale embedded medicareCoverage note that still carries the retired 85%/91% numbers contradicting the corrected top-level fields.

name: ColoSense
qualityRationale: Core facts (single-cancer CRC stool mt-sRNA test, Geneoscopy, 93% CRC sensitivity, 88% specificity, FDA PMA, CPT 0421U, every-3-year interval) are accurate and well-aligned with the CRC-PREVENT/JAMA 2023 pivotal data and FDA PMA P230001. But the record is dragged down by a critical citation error — the PMID cited across ~7 fields (37930717) points to an UNRELATED JAMA Internal Medicine long-term-care article, not the CRC-PREVENT study (correct PMID is 37870871) — and by stale Medicare-coverage status: CMS granted ColoSense NCD coverage June 8, 2026, yet the record still says PENDING_COVERAGE. Several internal inconsistencies (n=14263 vs 8920; FDA date May 3 vs May 6/10 2024) further reduce currency.

defects.field: sensitivityCitations / stageISensitivityCitations / specificityCitations / ppvCitations / npvCitations / stageIISensitivityCitations / stageIIISensitivityCitations
defects.issue: Cited PMID 37930717 is the WRONG article — it is a JAMA Internal Medicine paper on disability/caregiving before long-term care entry, not the ColoSense CRC-PREVENT study. Correct citation is Barnell EK et al., JAMA 2023;330(18):1760-1768, PMID 37870871. This fabricated-by-error citation appears across all performance-claim fields.

Stale: states Medicare coverage PENDING / 'NCD reconsideration requested.' CMS granted ColoSense coverage within NCD 210.3 for colorectal cancer screening on June 8, 2026 (average-risk adults 45-85, 3-year interval). Record's PENDING_COVERAGE is now factually wrong as of audit date 2026-06-10.

Participant count '14263' is unverified and inconsistent with the verified analyzed cohort of n=8,920 (CRC-PREVENT, JAMA 2023). totalParticipants field correctly says 8920, creating an internal contradiction.
FDA PMA approval date listed as 'May 3 2024'; published sources/CMS cite May 6, 2024 (CMS letter dated May 10). Date is off.
medicareCoverage.cptCode is null and reimbursementRate is null even though top-level cptCode (0421U) and medicareRate (508.87) are populated — fields not reconciled.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Verified CRC-PREVENT analyzed cohort is n=8,920 (Barnell JAMA 2023). 14,263 is not supported by the publication; likely an enrollment/screening figure mislabeled as the analysis cohort.

Per-stage sensitivities (Stage I 100%, II 71.4%, III 100%) come from very small stage-stratified subgroups in CRC-PREVENT; precise per-stage values are not prominently reported in the FDA PMA labeling or JAMA primary endpoints and should be re-verified against the actual publication tables (and re-cited to PMID 37870871).
Conflicts with confirmed CMS NCD coverage decision dated June 8, 2026; the 2025-Q4 effective date may refer only to ADLT pricing, not coverage, and should be clarified.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37870871/ | https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/cdrh_docs/pdf23/P230001B.pdf
proposedFills.source: Barnell EK et al. Multitarget Stool RNA Test for Colorectal Cancer Screening. JAMA. 2023;330(18):1760-1768 (CRC-PREVENT)

proposedFills.proposedValue: COVERED
CMS granted Medicare coverage June 8, 2026 within NCD 210.3 (colorectal cancer screening) for average-risk adults 45-85 at a 3-year interval; aligns with NCCN and ACS guidelines.
FDA-approved PMA (P230001) May 6, 2024; Breakthrough Device Designation January 2020
CRC-PREVENT (n=8,920 analyzed): CRC sensitivity 94% (record 93%), advanced adenoma sensitivity 46%, specificity 88%; outperformed FIT (CRC 94% vs 78%; AA 46% vs 29%).
Included as a preferred noninvasive option in NCCN colorectal cancer screening guidelines and in the American Cancer Society 2026 CRC screening guideline update (mt-sRNA). Not yet USPSTF A/B rated.

summary: ColoSense (Geneoscopy) is accurately characterized as the first FDA-approved stool mt-sRNA CRC screening test with correct headline performance, but the record carries a critical wrong PMID (37930717 → should be 37870871) across all performance citations and a now-stale 'Medicare pending' status (CMS granted NCD coverage June 8, 2026). Test is still offered under this name.

name: Shield MCD
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced against the April/May 2025 AACR/ASCO data and Guardant IR: 60% overall sensitivity, 98.5% specificity, 10 cancer types, FDA Breakthrough Device (June 2025), NCI Vanguard Study, national US launch Oct 2025, Asia/Manulife launch March 2026, ~$895 price. A few values are imprecise or stale relative to the latest disclosed data (tumor origin accuracy now public at ~89%; alternate ASCO figures 98.6%/59.7%), and several non-vendor-page numbers (PPV 3.1%, NPV 99.9%) cite an unverifiable "HCP Page" without a precise link.

defects.issue: States cancer signal of origin accuracy is 'not yet publicly disclosed,' but Guardant's AACR 2025 data report ~89% accuracy for primary/secondary cancer signal of origin prediction. Field is stale; value is now available.

PPV 3.1% and NPV 99.9% cite a generic 'Guardant Health Shield HCP Page' with no resolvable URL. These population-dependent values are not in the cited AACR/ASCO case-control data (which cannot yield PPV/NPV without prevalence assumptions); they are unverifiable as stated.
Describes the 74% figure as 'subset of deadliest/aggressive cancers'; Guardant's release specifies it as the six most aggressive cancers. Vague phrasing vs. the precise source claim.
Listed as UNKNOWN. As an LDT add-on this may genuinely lack a unique CPT, but the field is unresolved.
Several citations point to the bare domain (guardanthealth.com) or an unnamed 'HCP Page' rather than a specific, resolvable source URL.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: PPV is prevalence-dependent and not derivable from the cited 778-person case-control study; source is an unresolvable 'HCP Page.' Plausible order of magnitude for MCED but unverifiable as a hard claim.
Same issue as PPV — cited to an unresolvable HCP page, not the validation data.
Correct for AACR data, but Guardant's ASCO 2025 cancer-signal-origin model reported 98.6% specificity / 59.7% sensitivity; record may reflect an earlier cut. Minor.
Signal-of-origin accuracy (~89%) was in fact disclosed at AACR 2025.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Per-cancer overall sensitivity ranged from 21% (prostate) to 96% (esophageal-gastric) at 98.5% specificity in the AACR 2025 case-control study
778 individuals (403 with a known cancer diagnosis) in the blinded case-control validation; separate from the 24,000-target NCI Vanguard Study
~74% sensitivity across the six most aggressive cancers (per Guardant AACR 2025 release)

summary: Shield MCD is an actively offered Guardant Health blood-based methylation MCED test (10 cancers, ~60% sensitivity / 98.5% specificity, FDA Breakthrough June 2025, NCI Vanguard, US launch Oct 2025); the record is largely accurate but has a stale tumor-origin-accuracy field (~89% is now public) and unverifiable PPV/NPV values cited to an unresolvable 'HCP Page.'

name: ProVue Lung
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and vendor-sourced: single-cancer lung test, blood-based proteomics LDT (not FDA approved), 85% sensitivity / 55% specificity / 81% Stage I / >99.8% NPV, high-risk 50+/20-pack-year population, Nov 2025 launch, self-pay/no Medicare. These match the PrognomiQ vendor page and Nov 2025 GlobeNewswire release. Score capped because (1) PPV ~44% and list price $800 are explicitly self-labeled estimates with no real source, and (2) the record predates the May 2026 real-world update (89%/54%/83% Stage I) which it does not mention.

defects.issue: PPV value of 44 is sourced to 'PrognomiQ clinical data; high-risk smoking population' but no such PPV is published on the vendor page or press releases (May 2026 release explicitly omits PPV). Appears to be an unverifiable/derived estimate presented as a cited figure.

listPrice 800 is sourced to 'Estimated based on market positioning' — fabricated/estimated value, not a real published price. Should be null or flagged clearly as unknown rather than a specific dollar figure.

Record reflects only the Nov 2025 launch figures (85%/55%/81%). A May 12 2026 real-world Early Experience Program update reported improved 89% sensitivity, 54% specificity, 83% Stage I sensitivity (n=78). Record is stale by one update cycle.
Record says 81% (per Nov 2025 press release); current vendor page now states 80% Stage I. Minor discrepancy between cited sources.
cptCode is 'UNKNOWN' — acceptable given new LDT, but unverified.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No published PPV from PrognomiQ; vendor page and both press releases omit PPV. The cited source 'PrognomiQ clinical data' is not locatable. Likely a calculated estimate dressed as a vendor figure.
Self-labeled 'Estimated based on market positioning' — not a real disclosed price.
May 2026 real-world study disclosed n=78 first cohort; pivotal validation size still undisclosed, but at least one participant count is now public.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Launch (Nov 2025): 85% all-stage sensitivity. Updated May 2026 real-world Early Experience Program (n=78): 89% all-stage sensitivity at 54% specificity, 83% Stage I sensitivity.

summary: ProVue Lung is accurately recorded as a still-offered single-cancer lung proteomics LDT (85%/55%/81% Stage I/>99.8% NPV, high-risk 50+ smokers, not FDA approved, self-pay) matching vendor sources. Main weaknesses: an unsourced PPV (44%) and estimated list price ($800) presented as cited data, plus staleness vs the May 2026 real-world update (89%/54%/83% Stage I, n=78).

name: Signal-C
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-sourced for a pre-market test. Vendor (Universal DX), single-cancer CRC scope, cfDNA methylation+fragmentation approach, FDA PMA pivotal-trial status (NCT06059963), and Quest exclusive US commercialization are all correctly captured and verified against vendor/press sources. Score held below 90 because: (1) the headline 93% sensitivity conflicts with the same 1,000-patient study being reported elsewhere as 91% — the record never reconciles this; (2) all performance data trace to press releases and conference abstracts (DDW 2023, ASCO GI 2023) — no peer-reviewed publication exists, yet numPublications=3 and stage-specific figures are presented with apparent precision; (3) PPV/NPV are internal calculations, not independently sourced.

defects.issue: Listed as 93% but the same 1,000-patient multi-cohort study is reported by BioSpace and ASCO GI 2023 materials as 91% CRC sensitivity. The record's own note acknowledges 92-94% variation but does not resolve which figure is the validated headline. Likely 91% is the all-stage value and 93% reflects a different cohort/threshold.

Set to 3 with numPublicationsPlus=true, but research found NO peer-reviewed journal publications — only conference abstracts/presentations (DDW 2023, ASCO GI 2023, AACR 2022) and press releases. Conference abstracts should not be counted as peer-reviewed publications; the count overstates evidence maturity.

Per-stage figures (91/92/91/93%) derive from a single Jan-2023 Business Wire press release with tiny per-stage denominators (e.g., 21/23, 13/14) and are not peer-reviewed; presented with more precision than the underlying n supports.
PPV ~6% and NPV ~99.9% are internal calculations at assumed 0.5% prevalence, not independently published values; citation points to a calculation plus vendor page rather than a primary source.

lastVerified 2026-01-13; company raised additional funding in Jan/May 2026 but no approval — status NOT_YET_AVAILABLE remains correct, though a re-verification of trial enrollment completion (target Dec 2025) would strengthen currency.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Same 1,000-patient study reported as 91% CRC sensitivity by BioSpace and in ASCO GI 2023 materials; 93% appears to be a later/alternate-cohort or different-specificity figure that conflicts with the 91% all-stage number.
No peer-reviewed publications located; only conference abstracts and press releases. Vendor's own science page states results are from a DDW 2023 abstract with no journal publication listed.
Refers to the validation case-control study; correct for that study but the ongoing pivotal trial targets 15,000+ — field could be misread as the registrational n. Notes clarify, so low concern.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 91 (all-stage CRC) — reconcile with 93% headline; flag both as press-release-derived, not peer-reviewed
0 peer-reviewed; 3+ conference presentations (DDW 2023, ASCO GI 2023, AACR 2022)

PMA pivotal trial (NCT06059963) first patient Jan 2024; enrollment target 15,000+ across 100 sites by Dec 2025; Universal DX raised additional funding (€62M Jan 2026; €30M La SETT May 2026) to advance the program — still pre-approval as of mid-2026

summary: Signal-C is still offered by Universal DX as a single-cancer CRC screening blood test, in an active FDA PMA pivotal trial (NCT06059963) with Quest holding exclusive US commercialization rights — all accurately captured. Main defects: the headline 93% sensitivity conflicts with the same study's 91% figure, and numPublications=3 overstates evidence since all data are from press releases/conference abstracts with no peer-reviewed publication.

name: IColocomf
qualityRationale: Core claims are strongly corroborated by the cited peer-reviewed source (PMID 34890778, J Mol Diagn 2022) and the live vendor page. Sensitivity (95.31%), specificity (96.67% vs healthy controls; 88.39% all-comers), advanced-adenoma sensitivity (63.16%), n=1,164 cohort composition, SDC2/TFPI2 dual-target methylation approach, NMPA(2022)+CE-IVD regulatory status, and "still offered" are all verifiable. Citations are real and accurate. Points deducted for stage-stratified sensitivity fields that split a combined Stage I-II / Stage III-IV figure into four identical per-stage values (overstated granularity, not cleanly verifiable from the source abstract) and a TAT discrepancy (record 3-5 days vs vendor-stated 4.5h analytical).

defects.issue: Both set to identical 96.93% derived from a 'Stage I-II combined: 158/163' figure. Splitting one combined number into two separate per-stage fields with the same value implies per-stage granularity the source does not provide; the J Mol Diagn abstract reports no stage-stratified sensitivities and states 'no statistically significant variations...for different clinical features.' The 158/163 figure is not confirmable from the abstract.

Both set to identical 93.06% from a 'Stage III-IV combined: 134/144' figure, same issue as Stage I-II: combined number duplicated across two distinct per-stage fields, granularity not supported by the cited abstract.

Record states '3-5 days from sample receipt to results'; vendor page states analytical result 'available within 4.5h since sample processing.' The 3-5 day figure is unsourced and conflicts with the vendor's stated turnaround.
$500 USD listed as 'approximately'; no citable source. Vendor page gives no price ('more reasonable than colonoscopy'). Unverifiable estimate.

Record emphasizes 'high-risk CRC screening population / needing colonoscopy'; vendor page frames intended use more broadly as adults 40+, family history, or symptomatic individuals. Slightly narrower than vendor positioning.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Stage I-II combined: 96.93% (158/163 detected). Individual stage I data not separately reported.
suspectClaims.whySuspect: The denominators 163 (Stage I-II) + 144 (Stage III-IV) = 307, but the study enrolled 320 CRC patients; the 13-patient discrepancy and the exact 158/163, 134/144 splits are not present in the J Mol Diagn abstract and could not be independently confirmed. May derive from a supplementary table but is presented as headline per-stage data.
Vendor's official page states results within 4.5 hours of sample processing; the 3-5 day claim has no citation.
No public source; vendor does not publish a price. 'Approximately $500 USD equivalent' is an unsourced estimate.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Analytical result within ~4.5 hours of sample processing (lab logistics may add days)

95.31% CRC sensitivity and 96.67% specificity (vs healthy controls; 88.39% vs all non-CRC; 90.29% accuracy) in multicenter double-blinded case-control study, n=1,164 (320 CRC, 148 adenomas, 396 interfering diseases, 300 healthy controls), 3 hospitals.
Stool DNA methylation of SDC2 and TFPI2 via methylation-specific qPCR; vendor describes an atomic-signal-capture (AS-CAP) enhancement

Adults 40+, those with CRC family history, symptomatic individuals (blood in stool, altered bowel habits), and prior colorectal disease/polyps; noninvasive alternative for those unable/unwilling to undergo colonoscopy

summary: IColocomf is a still-offered, NMPA-approved (2022) and CE-IVD-marked Chinese single-cancer stool DNA methylation test (SDC2/TFPI2) for colorectal cancer; its headline performance, cohort, and regulatory claims are accurately backed by the cited J Mol Diagn 2022 study (PMID 34890778) and the live vendor page. Main weaknesses are stage-stratified sensitivity fields that duplicate combined Stage I-II / III-IV figures into four identical per-stage values not cleanly supported by the source, plus an unsourced 3-5 day TAT (vendor states 4.5h) and an unsourced $500 price.

name: Oncoguard Liver
qualityRationale: Current and mostly well-sourced (ALTUS 2025, vendor/ownership, FDA LDT+Breakthrough, CPT 81599 confirmed) but undermined by a factual biomarker-panel error, internally inconsistent performance numbers, and estimated/unverified price and Medicare fields.

defects.issue: Biomarker panel is wrong. Official Oncoguard Liver markers are methylated HOXA1 and TSPYL5 with reference marker B3GALT6 (plus AFP and biological sex). Record lists 'HOXA1; EMX1; TSPYL5' — EMX1 is not an Oncoguard Liver marker, and the B3GALT6 reference marker is omitted.

Internally inconsistent performance figures. sensitivityNotes states 70% overall, stageISensitivity=64%, specificity=81.9%, while performanceNotes and ppvNotes still cite 77% early-stage and an obsolete Phase II 88%. Vendor headline ALTUS figures are 64% very early-stage, 77% early-stage, ~82% specificity (n>3,000). The 70% 'overall' figure is not the vendor-promoted headline and conflates stages; the record should reconcile to the published 64%/77%/82%.
listPrice=600 is explicitly flagged as an estimate ('Estimated ~$500-$700'), not a sourced vendor/CMS price. Unverifiable.

Medicare coverage marked UNKNOWN / 'Need to verify' (lastVerified 2026-01-13). No MolDX LCD or Medicare determination located; reimbursementNote says NOT covered by Medicare, which conflicts with the UNKNOWN status field — needs reconciliation.

PPV (37.5) and NPV (98.5) cite a generic 'Oncoguard Liver FAQ; Exact Sciences clinical data' without a specific figure-bearing URL; values are population-dependent estimates not clearly traceable to ALTUS.

Abbott ownership is pending: acquisition announced Nov 19 2025, expected to close Q2 2026. As of audit date the deal may not have legally closed; 'Abbott (Exact Sciences)' is reasonable but should be confirmed against close date.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 3 methylated DNA markers (HOXA1; EMX1; TSPYL5) + AFP + biological sex
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Multiple Exact Sciences / Oncoguard sources list the markers as methylated HOXA1 and TSPYL5 with reference marker B3GALT6, not EMX1. EMX1 appears to be carried over from a different assay.

Vendor and AASLD 2025 headline reporting is by stage (64% very early-stage, 77% early-stage); a single 70% 'overall' sensitivity is not the figure Exact Sciences promotes and may be a chart-derived blended value that conflicts with the record's own stage fields.
Self-labeled as an estimate based on 'market positioning'; no published list price or CMS rate located.
Conflicts with reimbursementNote ('NOT covered by Medicare'); coverage status genuinely unresolved and stale (last verified Jan 2026).

proposedFills.proposedValue: Methylated DNA markers HOXA1 and TSPYL5 + reference marker B3GALT6, combined with serum AFP and biological sex in a qualitative algorithm; developed with Mayo Clinic
77 (early-stage HCC); 64 very early-stage
Exact Sciences (acquisition by Abbott announced Nov 19 2025, expected close Q2 2026)
Adults with liver cirrhosis and/or chronic hepatitis B (HBV) at risk for HCC requiring surveillance

summary: Oncoguard Liver is still offered by Exact Sciences (Abbott acquisition announced Nov 2025, closing Q2 2026) as a single-cancer HCC surveillance LDT; the record is largely current on ALTUS 2025 data, FDA status, and CPT 81599 but contains a critical biomarker-panel error (lists EMX1 instead of the real HOXA1/TSPYL5/B3GALT6 panel), internally inconsistent sensitivity figures (70% vs 77%/88%), and unverified price/Medicare fields.

name: Cologuard
qualityRationale: Core facts about the ORIGINAL Cologuard are accurate and well-cited: 92% CRC sensitivity, 87% specificity, per-stage DeeP-C numbers (Stage I ~89.7%, Stage II 100%), 3.7% PPV, FDA PMA P130017 (2014), USPSTF Grade A, CPT 81528, $649 list price, 3-year interval, and BMP3/NDRG4/KRAS+FIT method all verified against NEJM 2014 and FDA SSED. Vendor 'Abbott (Exact Sciences)' is now correct (Abbott's $21B acquisition closed March 23, 2026). The main weakness is currency: the record describes only the original Cologuard and entirely omits Cologuard Plus (FDA-approved Oct 4, 2024; CPT 0464U; 95% CRC sensitivity / 94% specificity in BLUE-C), the marketed next-gen successor. Both tests remain available, so 'still offered' holds, but the record is incomplete on the current product line.

defects.issue: Record covers only the original Cologuard and omits Cologuard Plus (BLUE-C, FDA PMA approved Oct 4, 2024; CPT 0464U), the next-generation successor Exact Sciences is actively marketing. No field references the newer test or its improved 95% sensitivity / 94% specificity, making the record stale on the current product line.

87% specificity is a simplification. DeeP-C/FDA SSED reported ~86.6% specificity among those without advanced neoplasia and ~89.8% among those with a negative colonoscopy. The single '87%' value with '~13% false positive rate' is defensible but loses the nuance reported in the pivotal trial.
Payer list (UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, Humana) and 'most commercial insurers' is plausible but uncited; no commercialPayersCitations field. Unverifiable as written.

NPV 99.9% is cited to the DeeP-C PMID but the original NEJM 2014 paper's headline figures were sensitivity/specificity/PPV; a 99.9% NPV is reasonable given low CRC prevalence but is not clearly stated in the cited source.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited to the DeeP-C PMID, but the 2014 NEJM paper emphasized sensitivity/specificity/PPV; a precise 99.9% NPV value is not a headline figure of that source and may be an inferred/rounded estimate rather than a directly reported number.
No citation provided; specific named payers for a screening test change over time and cannot be verified against the listed sources.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Cologuard Plus (next-generation mt-sDNA, FDA PMA-approved Oct 4, 2024; CPT 0464U) demonstrated 95% overall CRC sensitivity and 94% specificity (no findings on colonoscopy) in the pivotal BLUE-C study (~19,000 average-risk participants), with 43% sensitivity for advanced precancerous lesions and >30% reduction in false positives vs original Cologuard. Both original Cologuard and Cologuard Plus remain available.
81528 (original Cologuard); Cologuard Plus uses 0464U
Abbott completed its ~$21B acquisition of Exact Sciences, closing March 23, 2026; Cologuard is now an Abbott-owned product.
~86.6% specificity in participants without advanced neoplasia; ~89.8% in those with a negative colonoscopy (DeeP-C / FDA SSED)

summary: The record accurately and credibly documents the ORIGINAL Cologuard (verified 92% sensitivity, 87% specificity, DeeP-C per-stage data, PMA P130017, USPSTF Grade A), and the 'Abbott (Exact Sciences)' vendor is now correct after the acquisition closed March 23, 2026. Its main shortfall is currency: it omits Cologuard Plus (FDA-approved Oct 2024, CPT 0464U), the marketed next-gen successor, and a few fields (NPV, named commercial payers) are uncited.

name: IHepcomf
qualityRationale: Core record is highly accurate and well-sourced: all 4 PMIDs are real and the vendor page confirms scope (single-cancer liver), GNB4+Riplet dual-target qMSP, NMPA/CE status, and high-risk population. Sensitivity 92.3%, specificity 93.4%, HCC 91.9%, ICC 93.3%, early-stage I-II 88.7%, recurrence 95.8% all verified against PubMed abstracts. Score held below 90 by a clear TAT contradiction (record says 3-5 days; vendor page says results within 4.5h of processing), an unsourced stage I figure (84.4% vs published 81.7%/78.26%), an unverified NCT-to-paper mapping, and PPV/price values lacking citable sources.

defects.issue: Record states '3-5 days from sample receipt'. Vendor page (whaltglobal.com/ihepcomf) states 'Results available within 4.5h since sample processing'. Direct contradiction with the official source; likely stale or fabricated.

Value 84.4% labeled 'per vendor data' but is not found in any cited publication. Published stage I HCC sensitivity is 81.7% (PMID 39324668) and 78.26% (PMID 38154055). The 84.4% figure is unsourced and inconsistent with peer-reviewed data.

Multicenter validation (PMID 40624654) is mapped to NCT05668793, but the analytical-validation paper (PMID 39324668) reports NCT05685524. The NCT-to-publication mapping for the main n=1,182 study is not confirmed in the abstract; trial ID may be misattributed.

PPV 38% cited only as 'Clinical trial data; high-risk liver cancer surveillance population' with no specific source. PPV is prevalence-dependent and not reported in the cited abstracts; value is an estimate presented as data.
List price $400 cited as 'Approximately $400 USD equivalent' with no source; vendor page does not disclose price. Unverifiable.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor official page states results within 4.5 hours of sample processing — a rapid qMSP turnaround, not 3-5 days.
No publication supports 84.4%; peer-reviewed stage I HCC sensitivity is 81.7% (PMID 39324668) and 78.26% (PMID 38154055).
Not reported in any cited study; PPV is prevalence-dependent and the source is vaguely 'clinical trial data'.
Cited only as 'Clinical trial data' with no specific publication; not stated in the verified abstracts.
88.7% is the combined early-stage TNM I-II PLC figure (PMID 40624654), not a stage-II-specific value; mislabeling a combined I-II metric as stage II.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Results within ~4.5 hours of sample processing
81.7 (stage I HCC per analytical/clinical validation; 78.26 in plasma discovery cohort)
Dual-target plasma methylation (PDTM) test using triplex qMSP of GNB4 and Riplet with beta-actin reference; LOD 1% methylation/100 copies/uL, within-5% CV precision

summary: Accurate, well-sourced single-cancer (liver) cfDNA methylation test (GNB4+Riplet qMSP) by Wuhan Ammunition Life-tech; NMPA-approved and CE-IVD, still offered as IHepcomf with all four cited PMIDs real and core performance verified. Main defects: TAT contradicts the vendor page (record 3-5 days vs ~4.5h), an unsourced stage I sensitivity (84.4% vs published 81.7%), and uncited PPV/NPV/price values.

name: Epi proColon
qualityRationale: Core test identity (single-cancer CRC, mSEPT9 methylation, real-time PCR, FDA PMA P130001 April 2016) is accurate, and headline ~68% sens / ~80% spec match the published literature. But the record is materially stale on the two most decision-relevant facts: (1) ownership — Epigenomics AG sold substantially all assets to New Day Diagnostics in 2023 and recalled Epi proColon; the product is now offered as an LDT rebranded 'ColoHealth'/'ColoHealth Direct.' (2) Medicare — CMS finalized a NATIONAL NON-COVERAGE determination for Epi proColon (failed the ≥74% sens / ≥90% spec bar), yet the record shows medicareStatus 'Priced', a $192 rate, and 'Coverage variable/limited' framing, which is misleading. Per-stage sensitivities (57/72/85/78) are directionally plausible but not verifiable against the cited PMID/SSED.

defects.issue: Vendor listed as 'Epigenomics.' Epigenomics AG sold substantially all assets (including Epi proColon IP) to New Day Diagnostics LLC in 2023 and wound down; Epigenomics no longer markets the test. Current owner/distributor is New Day Diagnostics.

Record implies Medicare pricing ('Priced', rate $192, 'Coverage variable/limited'). CMS issued a final NATIONAL COVERAGE DETERMINATION of NON-COVERAGE for Epi proColon for screening (test failed CMS's ≥74% sensitivity / ≥90% specificity threshold). The record materially understates this — it is non-covered by Medicare for screening, not merely 'variable/limited.'

Epi proColon as an actively marketed FDA-approved kit was recalled by Epigenomics; New Day rebranded it 'ColoHealth' / 'ColoHealth Direct' and sells it as a laboratory-developed test (self-pay ~$199). The 'Epi proColon' branded product is effectively retired/renamed; record presents it as a currently distributed IVD kit.

States 'FDA-approved IVD kit for laboratory use.' The FDA-approved kit was recalled; the current US offering is an LDT, and the FDA-approval transfer to New Day's ColoHealth brand was pending. 'FDA-approved (2016)' is technically true for the original PMA but no longer describes the marketed product's regulatory pathway.

Per-stage sensitivities (57/72/85/78) cite PMID 23408352 and the FDA SSED but cannot be verified to those exact sources; SSED/PRESEPT data are summarized differently. Possibly imported from a non-cited source.

'2025-Q4' effective date for an allegedly priced Medicare rate is inconsistent with the longstanding (2021) CMS non-coverage NCD; appears fabricated or mis-derived from a CPT 81327 fee-schedule line rather than actual screening coverage.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: CMS finalized a national NON-COVERAGE NCD for Epi proColon screening; a CPT 81327 lab-fee-schedule price does not equal Medicare screening coverage.
Likely a CLFS price for CPT 81327, not a covered screening reimbursement; presents as if Medicare pays for the screening test, which it does not.
Inconsistent with the 2021 CMS non-coverage decision; no evidence Medicare began covering Epi proColon screening in Q4 2025.
Assets acquired by New Day Diagnostics (2023); Epigenomics AG no longer the active vendor.
Exact per-stage values not confirmable against cited PMID 23408352 or FDA SSED.

proposedFills.proposedValue: New Day Diagnostics (formerly Epigenomics AG)
Renamed — Epi proColon recalled by Epigenomics; relaunched by New Day Diagnostics as 'ColoHealth'/'ColoHealth Direct' LDT (~$199 self-pay)
Non-covered (CMS national non-coverage determination; test does not meet CMS ≥74% sensitivity / ≥90% specificity bar for blood-based CRC screening)
CMS finalized a national non-coverage determination for Epi proColon for CRC screening; New Day markets the rebranded ColoHealth as self-pay (~$199) while seeking commercial reimbursement.
Original FDA PMA approval April 2016 (P130001); kit subsequently recalled — current US offering is a laboratory-developed test (ColoHealth) pending FDA approval transfer

summary: The record's CRC/mSEPT9 test identity and headline ~68%/80% performance are accurate, but it is critically stale: Epigenomics sold the asset to New Day Diagnostics (2023) and Epi proColon was recalled and relaunched as the 'ColoHealth' LDT, and CMS issued a national NON-COVERAGE determination — contradicting the record's 'Priced' $192 Medicare framing. Vendor, ownership, Medicare status, and product name all need correction.

name: IColohunter
qualityRationale: Core performance data (sensitivity, specificity, stage and adenoma/HGIN subgroups) matches the BMC Medicine 2024 validation paper (PMID 39379942) exactly, and the method/second-publication citations are correct. But the record has real accuracy problems: PPV/NPV are imported from an unrelated test (mSEPT9 / 'New Day Diagnostics'), the primary vendor citation URL is now a dead domain-for-sale redirect (waltglobal.com -> hugedomains.com; live site is whaltglobal.com), and the NMPA/CE-IVD regulatory claims for IColohunter specifically are unverified (only the sibling stool test IColocomf has confirmed NMPA certification). The test is correctly characterized as a single-cancer plasma methylation CRC test and is still offered.

defects.issue: PPV (2.5%) and NPV (99.7%) are cited to 'New Day Diagnostics; mSept9 validation data' — a different test (Epigenomics mSEPT9/Epi proColon) from a different company. These values are not IColohunter's own data and are misattributed/imported.
Vendor URL https://www.waltglobal.com/icolohunter/ now 302-redirects to a HugeDomains 'domain for sale' page. Dead link. Live vendor domain is whaltglobal.com.

Claims 'NMPA approved (China); CE-IVD (EU)'. Sources confirm NMPA certification only for the sibling stool test IColocomf, not IColohunter. IColohunter NMPA approval and CE-IVD marking are uncorroborated; the validation study (NCT05508503) describes the assay as a 'potential' diagnostic tool, suggesting it may have been investigational at publication.
Both set to 87.4 (the combined Stage I-II value from the paper); true per-stage breakdown was not reported. Notes disclose this, but the per-stage fields are not genuinely per-stage.

Record frames the test as CRC 'screening' for high-risk individuals, but the validation was a cross-sectional/case-control diagnostic-discrimination study (incl. known-CRC patients); the source paper frames it as a 'diagnostic' tool, not an asymptomatic screening trial.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited to mSEPT9/'New Day Diagnostics' data, not IColohunter. The BMC Medicine validation reported sensitivity/specificity but PPV in a screening population is not derived from IColohunter's own data — value is borrowed from an unrelated test.
Same misattribution — cited to mSEPT9 validation, not IColohunter.

Only IColocomf (stool) has confirmed NMPA certification in available sources; IColohunter's NMPA/CE status is not corroborated and the validation paper language ('may serve as a potential noninvasive diagnostic tool') implies investigational status.
Notes say 'approximately $200-$350 USD equivalent based on regional pricing' — no citable source; appears estimated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Coverage varies by region / Covered in China per regional healthcare policies
suspectClaims.whySuspect: No citation; vague and unverifiable assertion of Chinese reimbursement.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://www.whaltglobal.com/icolohunter/
proposedFills.source: WHALT (Wuhan Ammunition Life-tech) product portfolio pages list IColohunter among current offerings; waltglobal.com domain is dead/for-sale and superseded by whaltglobal.com

proposedFills.proposedValue: 91.2% overall CRC sensitivity (95% CI 88.4-94.0) from a multicenter, double-blinded, cross-sectional clinical study (n=1,194; NCT05508503), Wang et al., BMC Medicine 2024. Study was a diagnostic-discrimination design including known-CRC patients, not an asymptomatic-screening trial.

Validation study (NCT05508503, BMC Medicine 2024) describes the MethyDT/IColohunter assay as a potential noninvasive diagnostic tool. NMPA certification is documented for the sibling stool test IColocomf; independent confirmation of IColohunter-specific NMPA approval and CE-IVD marking was not located. Not FDA approved or available in US.

summary: IColohunter is a still-offered single-cancer plasma cfDNA methylation CRC test (NTMT1/MAP3K14-AS1) whose core performance numbers correctly match the BMC Medicine 2024 validation (PMID 39379942), but the record imports PPV/NPV from an unrelated mSEPT9 test, cites a now-dead vendor domain, and asserts unverified NMPA/CE-IVD approval that is only confirmed for the sibling stool test IColocomf.

name: SPOT-MAS
qualityRationale: The record is accurate, well-cited, and matches published K-DETEK (BMC Medicine) and eLife validation data: 70.8% sensitivity / 99.7% specificity, 10 cancer types, multi-omics ctDNA method, Asian LDT availability. Performance figures and per-cancer/per-stage breakdowns are backed by real DOIs/PMIDs. The principal weakness is currency: the record misses the FDA Breakthrough Device Designation granted to SPOT-MAS 10 on 2026-05-27, instead stating only "Not FDA cleared / US validation underway." The vendor-renamed product line ("SPOT-MAS 10") and the targeted late-2026 US launch are also not reflected. fdaStatus is now stale/misleading without the Breakthrough flag.

defects.issue: Stale. Says 'Not FDA cleared' with no mention of FDA Breakthrough Device Designation granted to SPOT-MAS 10 on 2026-05-27. Breakthrough status is a material regulatory milestone that should be captured.
Vendor has rebranded/extended the test as 'SPOT-MAS 10' (the FDA-designated version detecting 10 cancers). Record name 'SPOT-MAS' is not wrong but no longer reflects the current product branding.
Mentions 'US validation underway with Pangea Laboratory' but does not reflect the May 2026 announcement of targeted US launch readiness in late 2026 following Breakthrough designation.
vendorVerified=false despite a vendor submission logged 2026-01-22 by David Schlesinger (Gene Solutions); verification not completed.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Not wrong, but Gene Solutions is now described in 2026 press materials as Singapore-based and the US-facing entity/lab strategy (Pangea Laboratory) is evolving; the operating entity for any future US offering may differ. Worth confirming the legal vendor on the US Breakthrough submission.
Record cites K-DETEK interim PPV ~58-60%, but a larger 2025 real-world cohort (n=12,281) reports PPV 67%; the single ppv value of 60 may understate the most recent figure.

proposedFills.proposedValue: FDA Breakthrough Device Designation (SPOT-MAS 10), granted 2026-05-27; not FDA cleared/approved

FDA granted Breakthrough Device Designation to SPOT-MAS 10 on 2026-05-27, providing prioritized FDA engagement; intended as an adjunctive screening test in asymptomatic adults aged 40+ for 10 cancer types. Targeting US launch readiness late 2026. Still an LDT in Asian markets; >100,000 individuals tested in real-world practice.
Asymptomatic adults aged 40 years and older (adjunctive multi-cancer screening)

summary: SPOT-MAS (now branded SPOT-MAS 10) by Gene Solutions is an accurate, well-cited MCED record matching published K-DETEK/eLife data, but it is stale on regulatory status: it omits the FDA Breakthrough Device Designation granted 2026-05-27 and the targeted late-2026 US launch. The test is still actively offered. Score 78.

name: OncoXPLORE+
qualityRationale: The record is built on a real vendor (OncoDNA), a real product (OncoXPLORE), and a real validation paper (Stanley/Vermeesch, Nat Commun 2024), and many technical details (441-gene panel, 1.5 Mb, 81.62% pan-cancer accuracy at 95% specificity, 88.7% TOO, AUCs of 84.7/90.1/95.0) are corroborated by the vendor page. But the record materially mis-frames the test: the vendor positions OncoXPLORE as tumour-naive comprehensive profiling / MRD / CUP / NSAS-triage, NOT asymptomatic MCED screening, yet the record classifies it indicationGroup "MCED" and leads targetPopulation with "high-risk asymptomatic individuals." The headline 53.5% sensitivity / 99% specificity "in asymptomatic screening context" is unsourceable vendor data not present in the cited Nature paper (a case-control fragmentomics methods study reporting AUCs, not screening sensitivity). Name carries a "+" the vendor product does not use. These are accuracy/currency problems, not mere incompleteness.

defects.issue: Headline 53.5% sensitivity at 99% specificity is attributed to 'asymptomatic screening context' and cited to the Nature Comms 2024 paper, but that paper is a matched case-control fragmentomics methods study reporting AUCs (CRC 84.7%, breast 90.1%, MM 95.0%) — it does not report 53.5% sensitivity at 99% specificity, nor any asymptomatic-screening sensitivity. The number is unverifiable vendor data presented as a screening performance claim.

Record classifies the test as MCED and leads target population with 'high-risk asymptomatic individuals.' The vendor explicitly positions OncoXPLORE as a tumour-naive comprehensive profiling / MRD / CUP / NSAS-triage tool, NOT an asymptomatic screening test. Listing it as an MCED screening test in the ECD database is misleading.

Record name 'OncoXPLORE+' does not match the vendor product name 'OncoXPLORE®' (no plus). The vendor's internal module is 'GIPXplorePLUZ'; the '+' appears to be a transcription artifact, creating a name that does not exist.

sensitivityType 'observed (case-control)' is correct for the AUC-based study but is inconsistent with presenting a 53.5%/99% 'asymptomatic screening' sensitivity, which would require a screening (incidence-adjusted) study.

Several key fields (LoD 0.3% VAF, technical sensitivity tiers, 441 genes detail) cite only 'Vendor data (Christophe Van Huffel, OncoDNA, Jan 2026)' — private correspondence, not independently verifiable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 53.5 (~53.5% for early-stage cancers at ~99% specificity in asymptomatic screening context)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Not found in the cited Nature Comms paper, which reports AUCs from matched case-control, not screening sensitivity. Sourced only to private vendor correspondence; no asymptomatic-cohort screening study exists yet (OncNGS Phase 3 still ongoing).

Vendor positions OncoXPLORE as comprehensive profiling / MRD / CUP / NSAS-triage, not asymptomatic multi-cancer screening. The vendor page explicitly says it is not a screening test for asymptomatic populations.

suspectClaims.currentValue: High-risk asymptomatic individuals, NSAS triage, therapy selection when tissue unavailable
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor messaging centers on MRD, CUP/tissue-of-origin, and therapy selection; 'high-risk asymptomatic individuals' as the lead use is not supported by current vendor positioning.
Vendor product is 'OncoXPLORE®' with no plus; '+' likely a transcription error from the 'GIPXplorePLUZ' module name.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Nature Communications 2024 study: 744 healthy individuals and patients (Stanley et al.)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cohort and authors are real, but this cohort validates fragmentomics cell-type signatures (AUCs), not the 441-gene OncoXPLORE assay as a screening product; presenting it as the test's screening validation cohort overstates its relevance.

proposedFills.proposedValue: OncoXPLORE
Multi-purpose tumour-naive profiling (MRD / CUP / NSAS-triage / therapy selection) — not asymptomatic MCED screening

Patients needing MRD monitoring, cancer-of-unknown-primary / tissue-of-origin determination, NSAS (non-specific symptoms) triage, or comprehensive genomic profiling for therapy selection when tissue is unavailable

Pan-cancer GIPXplore classifier: 81.62% overall accuracy at 95% specificity. Case-control AUCs (Stanley/Vermeesch, Nat Commun 2024): multiple myeloma 95.0%, early-stage breast 90.1%, colorectal 84.7%. No prospective asymptomatic-screening sensitivity/specificity has been published; OncNGS Phase 3 validation is ongoing.
Commercialisation phase led by OncoDNA from March 2026 via Early Access Programme; tumour-naive solution combining 441-gene capture-based NGS with GIPXplore fragmentomics

summary: OncoXPLORE (record mis-names it "OncoXPLORE+") is a real OncoDNA/KU Leuven tumour-naive multi-omics liquid biopsy, but the vendor positions it as comprehensive profiling / MRD / CUP / NSAS-triage — not asymptomatic MCED screening — so the record's MCED classification and "53.5% sensitivity at 99% specificity in asymptomatic screening context" (unsupported by the cited case-control Nature paper) are materially misleading. Technical specs (441 genes, 81.62% accuracy at 95% specificity, 88.7% TOO, RUO with CE-IVDR planned, March 2026 EAP) are largely accurate.

name: Freenome Lung Cancer Blood Test
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current (March 2026 AACR development data + PROACT LUNG NCT06122077), and well-sourced. Headline figures (90.7% sens @ 50% spec, 80.4% @ 75% spec, 673-sample cohort of 363 cases/310 controls, three subtypes, 50-80 LDCT-eligible population) all match Freenome's official press release. Roche ex-US deal (~$200M, Nov 2025), NVIDIA AI partnership, Exact Sciences CRC relationship, and H2 2026 LDT launch plan are all correctly characterized. Deterministic completeness (0.42) is low mainly because the test is pre-commercial — sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV/CPT/coverage fields are legitimately null because no locked operating point or commercial test exists yet, not because of poor curation. Score held below 90 by one factual discrepancy in Stage I sensitivity and lack of a peer-reviewed citation (data is conference/press-release only).

defects.issue: Record states '77.1% Stage I adjusted sensitivity at 50% specificity' but Freenome's official press release states 77.7% at 50% specificity. Likely a transcription error.

All performance fields are operating-point-based development data from a single 673-sample AACR presentation (March 2026), not locked/validated; record appropriately flags this but values are inherently preliminary and subject to change after PROACT LUNG.
Data exists only as a conference abstract / press release (AACR 2026); no peer-reviewed publication or PMID, so headline metrics are not independently verifiable beyond the vendor's own reporting.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 77.1% Stage I adjusted sensitivity at 50% specificity
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Freenome's official newsroom release reports 77.7% (not 77.1%) at 50% specificity for Stage I; current value appears to be a typo/misread.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 77.7% Stage I adjusted sensitivity at 50% specificity (development data, AACR 2026).

Methylation-only comparison reported 85.8% sensitivity at 50% specificity and 78.2% at 75% specificity, vs. 90.7%/80.4% for the full multiomics (methylation + protein) classifier, demonstrating incremental value of protein markers.

Roche ex-US deal (Nov 2025) is for 'kitted'/decentralized tests and is valued over $200M including a $75M equity instrument; Freenome retains all US rights and ex-US centralized testing rights. Exact Sciences holds exclusive US commercialization rights to Freenome's separate blood-based CRC test (acquired Aug 2025).

summary: Accurate, current, and well-sourced record for an investigational (pre-commercial) Freenome blood-based lung cancer screening test; figures match the March 2026 AACR development data and PROACT LUNG (NCT06122077), with only a minor Stage I sensitivity discrepancy (77.1% recorded vs 77.7% official). Low deterministic completeness reflects the test's pre-launch status (no locked sens/spec, CPT, or coverage), not poor curation.

name: MethylScan HCC
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced to the real Nature Communications 2023 validation (DOI 10.1038/s41467-023-39055-7 verified; 84.5% sens at 95% spec, AUC 0.94, n=554, marker panel CHFR/VASH2/CCNJ/GRID2IP + F12 all correct). Test confirmed still offered under this exact name on the vendor's live product page with CLIA/CAP accreditation. Points deducted for: vague reimbursement/CPT field when the real PLA code (0565U) is now public; the legal vendor name is EarlyDiagnostics Inc. (record says 'EarlyDx', the brand); internally inconsistent specificity (record pairs 84.5% sensitivity with 92% specificity, but the validated operating point is 84.5% @ 95% specificity); and stale evidence (numPublications=1, missing the AASLD 2025 MethylScan+AFP validation and the April 2026 PNAS multi-disease platform paper).

defects.issue: Record says only 'PLA code published April 2025' (vague). The actual published PLA code is 0565U, confirmed on the vendor's official product page. Should be populated with the real code.

Internally inconsistent operating point. Headline sensitivity 84.5% was reported AT 95% specificity (per the validation paper and sensitivityNotes), but the specificity field is set to 92%. Pairing 84.5% sens with 92% spec misrepresents the validated threshold; the matched pair is 84.5% @ 95% spec.
Vendor recorded as 'EarlyDx'; the company's legal/official name is EarlyDiagnostics Inc. (earlydx.com is the brand domain). Acceptable as a brand label but not the formal entity name.

Evidence is stale: lists only the single 2023 Nature Communications study. Misses the multi-site blinded AASLD 2025 (Liver Meeting) validation of MethylScan+AFP (Parikh, Univ. of Michigan) and the April 2026 PNAS multi-disease platform validation.
Labeled 'Stage I' but HCC is staged by BCLC (A/B/C/D), not TNM. The 73% value is BCLC Stage A. Notes clarify, but the field name is a category mismatch for this cancer.
CLIA/CAP/CA license numbers are now publicly disclosed (CLIA 05D2314626, CAP 9954593, CA CLF-90014301) and could be added to substantiate the LDT/lab claims.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: The pivotal study's headline 84.5% sensitivity is explicitly reported AT 95% specificity (confirmed in sensitivityNotes and source). 92% appears to be a different/secondary threshold; pairing it with the 84.5% sensitivity is internally inconsistent.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Has PLA code and CMS pricing determination; working with MolDx for LCD reimbursement
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Names a PLA code but omits the actual code (0565U) and the CMS pricing figure; 'working with MolDx for LCD' is unverifiable from public sources and may be stale.
tatNotes says 'Estimated turnaround time' — not a vendor-confirmed figure.

proposedFills.proposedValue: EarlyDiagnostics Inc.
95 (operating point matched to 84.5% sensitivity; AUC 0.94)
Multi-site blinded validation of MethylScan + AFP for early HCC detection presented at AASLD The Liver Meeting 2025 (N. Parikh, University of Michigan).

MethylScan platform validated for multi-disease detection from a single blood sample in a 2026 PNAS study; HCC remains the lead commercialized single-cancer assay, with lung and ovarian programs underway.

summary: MethylScan HCC by EarlyDiagnostics Inc. (brand 'EarlyDx') is still offered as a CLIA LDT single-cancer methylation blood test for HCC; the record's core performance data accurately matches the real Nature Communications 2023 validation.
