Main defects: vague reimbursement field that omits the now-public PLA code 0565U, an internally inconsistent specificity (84.5% sensitivity was reported at 95% spec, not the listed 92%), and stale evidence missing the AASLD 2025 and April 2026 PNAS validations.

name: Tempus xF+
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: 523-gene ctDNA liquid biopsy panel, CLIA LDT (not FDA-approved), Boulos et al. 2025 J Mol Diagn analytical validation (PMID 40287222), 7-10 day TAT, Streck cfDNA BCT collection, advanced solid tumors. The test is confirmed still offered under this exact name. Score held below 90 because biomarkersReported omits MSI-H and bTMB — two predictive immunotherapy biomarkers the vendor explicitly reports — and because of two citation/sample-detail discrepancies (analyticalValidationCitations uses a different PMID than the actual Boulos validation paper, and sampleTubeCount=1 conflicts with vendor/GTR guidance of 2 Streck tubes for xF+).

defects.issue: Omits MSI-H and bTMB (blood tumor mutational burden). The vendor (tempus.com xf page) and the Boulos et al. validation explicitly state xF+ reports MSI-H status and calculates bTMB — both are predictive immunotherapy biomarkers and a primary differentiator of the expanded 523-gene panel vs. the 105-gene xF. Their absence understates the test's capabilities.

Cites PMID 39820598 as 'Boulos et al. 2025 analytical validation,' but the confirmed Tempus xF+ analytical validation by Boulos et al. (J Mol Diagn 2025;27(5):383-394) is PMID 40287222 (used in numPublicationsCitations). The 39820598 PMID is unverified as the matching paper and may be the wrong reference.
Record states 1 Streck cfDNA BCT tube; vendor/NCBI GTR ordering guidance for xF+ specifies collection in two Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes. Likely understated.
Citation URL is truncated/broken: 'https://www.tempus.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Tempus-LS_' — incomplete link, not a working source.
policyName is null while a policyNumber (L38043) is given; coveredIndications and cptCode are thin/null, reducing verifiability of the COVERED status.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Labeled as Boulos et al. 2025 validation, but the actual Tempus xF+ analytical validation (J Mol Diagn 2025) is PMID 40287222. PMID 39820598 does not clearly correspond to this paper and may be mismatched.
Vendor/GTR ordering guidance indicates xF+ requires two Streck cfDNA BCT tubes, not one.
Vendor/GTR states results typically within 7-9 days; the record's '7-10 days' is close but slightly off from the cited source.

proposedFills.proposedValue: SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Rearrangements, CH variants, MSI-H, bTMB
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40287222/

Analytical validation by Boulos et al., J Mol Diagn 2025;27(5):383-394: 523-gene panel detecting SNVs/indels in 522 genes, CNGs in 7 genes, rearrangements in 10 genes; demonstrated accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducible precision, with concordance to xF and xT for MSI and gene coverage and support for bTMB calculation.

summary: Tempus xF+ is accurately recorded as a 523-gene ctDNA liquid biopsy CLIA LDT for advanced solid tumors with the correct Boulos et al. 2025 validation, and it is confirmed still offered under this name. Main gaps: biomarkersReported omits the vendor-reported MSI-H and bTMB, the analytical validation citation PMID appears mismatched, and the sample-tube count (1 vs. vendor's 2) and a truncated sample citation URL need correction.

name: MSK-IMPACT
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and well-cited on the load-bearing facts: 468-gene FDA-authorized config (DEN170058, Nov 2017), current ~505-gene expansion flagged in notes, tissue-only tumor/normal matched FFPE, CPT 0048U with Medicare ADLT rate $2,919.60 (all independently confirmed against MSK and findacode/AAPC). Main weaknesses: TMB is reported by the current assay per MSK's own page but is omitted from biomarkersReported; HRD is neither reported nor noted; and the primary genesAnalyzed field (468) is the stale FDA-config value rather than the current 505. No fabricated performance numbers detected; single record for the id (no duplicate).

defects.issue: Omits TMB (tumor mutational burden), which MSK's official msk-impact page explicitly states the current assay reports ('detects... tumor mutation burden, and microsatellite instability'). Reportable biomarker list is incomplete.
Primary value is 468 (the 2017 FDA-authorized configuration); the current clinical panel is 505 genes per MSK's official page. The genesAnalyzedNotes flags this, but the headline field is stale.
Method text says 'targets 468 cancer-associated genes' and lists SNVs/indels/CNAs/rearrangements/MSI but does not mention TMB or the current 505-gene panel, mirroring the biomarker omission.
'>20,000 patients sequenced since 2014' is undated/stale; MSK has reported far higher cumulative volumes (tens of thousands more) in later publications. Low-stakes but outdated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Select Rearrangements, MSI
suspectClaims.whySuspect: MSK's official page lists TMB as a reported output of the current assay; its absence here suggests the list reflects the 2017 FDA config, not the current clinical report.
Current MSK clinical panel is 505 genes (468 is the original 2017 FDA-authorized number); headline figure is stale even though the note acknowledges 505.
Sourced only to the vendor page as a round vendor-estimate with no enumerable citation; unverifiable as stated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Select Rearrangements, MSI, TMB
Current MSK clinical pipeline profiles 505 genes (solid tumors); the companion MSK-IMPACT Heme panel covers 468 genes. The 468-gene figure was the 2017 FDA-authorized (DEN170058) configuration.
Current assay reports MSI and tumor mutational burden (TMB) in addition to SNVs, indels, CNAs and select structural rearrangements.

summary: MSK-IMPACT record is accurate and well-sourced on FDA authorization, tissue-only scope, gene-panel history (468 FDA config / 505 current), and CPT 0048U / $2,919.60 ADLT pricing, all independently confirmed. The main gap is omission of TMB from the reported-biomarker list (MSK states the current assay reports TMB) plus a stale 468 headline gene count and an undated patient-volume figure.

name: Guardant360 Liquid CDx
qualityRationale: The headline facts are accurate and current: the May 20, 2026 FDA PMA approval, the "largest FDA-approved liquid biopsy panel" / 100x footprint framing, the 7 companion-diagnostic indications transferring from Guardant360 CDx (NSCLC, CRC, ESR1-mutated advanced breast cancer), genomic+epigenomic (methylation) profiling, ~7-day TAT, and broad Medicare/commercial coverage (~300M lives) are all corroborated by Guardant's own May 2026 press release and independent secondary coverage. The record is well-cited and vendor-verified. The main accuracy problem is the gene count: the record repeatedly states 744 genes, but Guardant's own 2024 upgrade press release and AMEA spec sheet consistently state 739 genes. Several quantitative analytical claims (LoD, specificity) rest on a binary spec-sheet PDF I could not parse, so they are plausible but unverified. Exactly one record matches the id+name (no duplicate-id defect).

defects.issue: Record states 744 genes in multiple fields, but Guardant's own 2024 'Smart Liquid Biopsy' press release ('evaluates biomarkers in 739 genes in total') and the AMEA/EU spec sheets state 739 genes. The 744 figure is unsupported by the cited sources and appears to be a transcription error.

LoD (e.g., SNV 0.20% VAF) and specificity (>=99.9%) figures cite a binary spec-sheet PDF that could not be machine-verified; values are plausible for this platform but currently unverifiable from the cited source as fetched.

Lists NCD 90.2 with coveredIndications 'Same as Guardant360 CDx' and null policyName/cptCode/notes; NGS NCD coverage for the post-PMA expanded panel is asserted by inheritance rather than directly cited for this specific test version.

numPublications=100+ cites a single PubMed ID (34838325) and notes 'same platform as Guardant360 CDx'; the publication count is an estimate carried over from the predecessor, not enumerated for this test.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Guardant's own 2024 press release states 739 genes ('evaluates biomarkers in 739 genes in total'); AMEA spec sheet also says 739. 744 is not supported by any located source, including the record's own cited press release.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Full 744-gene panel reported. 10x more genes than previous Guardant360 version
suspectClaims.whySuspect: The '10x more / lower tumor fraction' framing is from the 2024 launch which states 739 genes; the 744 figure conflicts.

suspectClaims.currentValue: >=99.9% for SNVs, Indels, CNAs, CNLs, Fusions, MSI, Promoter Methylation
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Single blanket >=99.9% specificity across all variant classes including newer epigenomic features is unusually uniform and rests on an unparseable binary spec sheet; warrants direct verification against the FDA SSED/labeling.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260520531436/en/FDA-Approves-New-Guardant360-Liquid-CDx-the-Largest-FDA-Approved-Liquid-Biopsy-Panel-with-a-100x-Expanded-Footprint

summary: Guardant360 Liquid CDx is accurately represented and still offered: the May 20, 2026 FDA PMA, 7 transferred CDx indications (NSCLC/CRC/ESR1 breast), genomic+epigenomic profiling, 7-day TAT, and broad Medicare/commercial coverage all check out. The principal defect is the gene count, listed as 744 where Guardant's own press release and spec sheets state 739.

name: OncoExTra
qualityRationale: Core facts (vendor/ownership, ~20,000-gene WES+WTS approach, tissue CGP, CLIA-LDT not FDA-approved, Medicare coverage, ~14-day TAT, advanced solid tumors) are accurate and current as of June 2026. Vendor string "Abbott (Exact Sciences)" is correct — Abbott completed the Exact Sciences acquisition on 2026-03-23 and vendor pages now state "Exact Sciences is now Abbott." Points deducted for: (a) a defining feature — patient-matched tumor-NORMAL sequencing requiring a matched normal blood draw — being absent from method and sampleRequirements (the latter lists only FFPE tissue); (b) HRD listed as a reported biomarker but not confirmable on vendor pages (which emphasize TMB/MSI/IHC); (c) a questionable oncotarget methodCitation; (d) thin/placeholder Medicare structured fields.

defects.issue: Record omits the test's defining feature: matched tumor-NORMAL sequencing. Vendor states OncoExTra uses patient-matched tumor-normal sequencing, which requires a matched normal (blood) sample. sampleRequirements lists only 'FFPE tissue; 10 unstained slides or tissue block' and omits the required normal blood draw, which is factually incomplete and could mislead ordering decisions.
Record says '10-14 days'; vendor consistently states 'approximately 14 days from sample receipt'. The lower bound of 10 is not vendor-supported.

policyNumber 'NCD 90.2' / coveredIndications 'Comprehensive genomic profiling' is generic; vendor describes Medicare FFS coverage under specific criteria (~96% zero out-of-pocket). policyName, cptCode, reimbursementRate are null; coverage basis is more MolDX/FFS-criteria than a clean NCD 90.2 citation.
Cites https://www.oncotarget.com/article/28285/text/ as method support; this article could not be verified as OncoExTra-specific validation and appears to be a weak/possibly mismatched citation.

suspectClaims.currentValue: HRD listed among SNVs, Indels, CNAs, Fusions, TMB, MSI, HRD
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor pages emphasize TMB, MSI, and IHC results but do not explicitly list HRD/genomic-instability scoring among reported biomarkers; HRD reporting for OncoExTra is unconfirmed and may be inferred rather than vendor-stated.
Vendor states ~14 days from sample receipt; the 10-day lower bound is unsupported.

clinicalUtility citation oncotarget article unverified; vendor-cited utility figures are '92% of reports with >=1 actionable variant' and '10.9% more alterations' — the record's framing is vague and not tied to the specific vendor-reported metrics.

proposedFills.proposedValue: FFPE tumor tissue (block or ~10 unstained slides) PLUS a matched normal blood sample; OncoExTra uses patient-matched tumor-normal sequencing.

Whole exome sequencing (WES) of ~20,000 genes combined with whole transcriptome sequencing (WTS) from FFPE tissue, using patient-matched tumor-normal sequencing to refine somatic variant calling and TMB; reports SNVs, indels, CNAs, fusions, MSI and TMB.
Approximately 14 days from sample receipt
In vendor clinical utilization data, 92% of OncoExTra reports showed >=1 clinically actionable variant, and the test detected ~10.9% more alterations than less-sensitive assays.
Exact Sciences became a wholly owned Abbott subsidiary; acquisition completed 2026-03-23.

summary: OncoExTra (Abbott/Exact Sciences) is accurately recorded as a still-offered ~20,000-gene WES+WTS tissue CGP CLIA-LDT with Medicare coverage and ~14-day TAT; the vendor/ownership string is correct post-Abbott acquisition. Main gaps: the defining matched tumor-normal (blood) requirement is missing from method/sampleRequirements, HRD reporting is unconfirmed, and a couple of citations (oncotarget, NCD 90.2) and the "10-14 day" TAT are weak or slightly off.

name: Northstar Select
qualityRationale: Core facts (vendor, 84-gene liquid CGP panel, QCT/smNGS method, MSI-H reporting, 0.15% LOD95, CLIA LDT non-FDA status, Medicare/MolDX L38043 coverage effective Feb 14 2025, CPT 0487U, J Liq Biopsy 2025 validation) are all corroborated by vendor pages, GenomeWeb, and the peer-reviewed publication. Deductions for a wrong clinical-availability date (claimed "since January 2023" — contradicted by 2024 ASCO abstract, Feb-2025 Medicare coverage, and Sept-2025 first publication), a stray Biodesix.com citation on the NCCN-alignment field, and a single unique record (no duplicate). Performance claims are accurate but derive from a vendor-sponsored study labeled as "independent validation."

defects.issue: States 'Commercially available in US since January 2023'. This is implausible/wrong: the validation was an ASCO 2024 abstract, Medicare coverage went effective Feb 14 2025, and the first peer-reviewed publication appeared Sept 2025. Northstar Select launched ~2024, not Jan 2023. Sourced only to a weak DelveInsight page.
Citation is 'https://www.biodesix.com/' — Biodesix is an unrelated competitor company, not BillionToOne/Northstar. Stray/incorrect citation; the NCCN-alignment claim is unsupported by this URL.

Labeled 'independent validation' but the head-to-head study (Bower et al., J Liq Biopsy 2025) is vendor-authored/sponsored by BillionToOne. Framing overstates independence; this is internal/sponsored validation, not third-party.
Lists '2025-Q4' while the Medicare coverage effective date is Feb 14 2025 (Q1); reimbursementNote and medicareCoverage.notes both correctly say Feb 14 2025, so this field is internally inconsistent.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Commercially available in US since January 2023
suspectClaims.whySuspect: All independent timeline markers (ASCO 2024 abstract, Feb 2025 Medicare coverage, Sept 2025 first publication) indicate a 2024 launch, not Jan 2023.

suspectClaims.field: sensitivity / specificity claims (51% more SNVs, 109% more CNVs, >99.9% specificity, 98% ddPCR concordance)
suspectClaims.currentValue: per Bower et al. J Liq Biopsy 2025
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Numbers match the publication but the study is vendor-sponsored head-to-head; superiority claims vs un-named comparators should be read as manufacturer data, not neutral benchmarking.
Specific CMS gapfill/ADLT rate for 0487U not independently confirmed in this audit; verify against CMS CLFS pricing file.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Commercially available in US; Medicare/MolDX coverage effective February 14, 2025
Medicare coverage via MolDX (Palmetto GBA LCD L38043) effective February 14, 2025 for advanced solid tumors
84-gene panel: 82 genes for SNVs/indels, 19 genes for CNA amplification, 5 genes for CNA loss, 9 genes for fusions; reports MSI status
https://www.northstaronc.com/ (replace stray biodesix.com URL)

summary: Northstar Select (BillionToOne) is a real, still-offered 84-gene liquid (ctDNA) CGP LDT with confirmed Medicare/MolDX coverage (L38043, CPT 0487U, effective Feb 14 2025) and a real Sept-2025 peer-reviewed validation; the record is mostly accurate but has a clearly wrong launch date ("since January 2023"), a stray Biodesix.com citation, and vendor-sponsored data presented as independent validation.

name: MI Cancer Seek
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and well-sourced on the load-bearing facts: FDA P240010 approval (2024-11-06), WES+WTS architecture, 228 reportable genes, 8 CDx claims, MSI/TMB/ERBB2/HRD/gLOH biomarkers, CPT 0211U at $8,455, 14-day TAT, pediatric+adult population — all confirmed against the Oncotarget/PMC12581394 validation paper and Caris official sources. Points deducted for two misleading explanatory notes (CDx-count arithmetic that doesn't sum; HRD overstated as a validated CDx use when it is an LDT/profiling biomarker not among the 8 FDA CDx claims) and one broken/irrelevant NCCN citation URL.

defects.issue: Note says '8 CDx claims: 1 pan-cancer indication plus 5 tumor-specific indications' which sums to 6, not 8. The PMC12581394 validation paper Table 1 lists 8 distinct CDx claims (e.g., PIK3CA breast; KRAS/NRAS-wt and BRAF V600E colorectal; BRAF V600E/K melanoma; EGFR ex19del and L858R NSCLC; MSI-H pan-cancer; MSS endometrial). The count of 8 is correct but the explanatory breakdown is internally inconsistent.

States 'HRD assessment validated for ovarian cancer PARPi therapy selection.' HRD/gLOH is reported as an LDT/profiling biomarker, NOT one of the 8 FDA CDx claims (which cover breast/CRC/melanoma/NSCLC/endometrial/pan-cancer MSI). Phrasing implies an FDA-validated ovarian CDx claim that MI Cancer Seek does not hold; risks misleading readers.
Citation is 'https://www.molecularmd.com/' — an unrelated/placeholder domain (MolecularMD, a former CDx company) that does not support any NCCN-alignment claim for MI Cancer Seek.
Second citation is a bare text string 'Oncotarget Aug 2025 MI Cancer Seek CDx validation' rather than a resolvable URL/DOI; the real reference is the Oncotarget/PMC12581394 validation paper.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Accurate as the reportable-gene tier, but flattening a tiered design (228 reportable at 1500x, 720 clinically relevant at 1000x, ~20,859 whole exome at 230x) into a single number can mislead; genesAnalyzedNotes mitigates this but the headline figure understates the WES scope vs DNA+RNA peers.

Ambiguous and unverifiable as stated; note conflates assay-specific publications with '165+ across the Caris Molecular Intelligence platform.' No clear source enumerates 30 MI Cancer Seek-specific publications.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Tiered sequencing: 228 reportable genes sequenced to ~1500x, 720 clinically-relevant genes to ~1000x, and ~20,859 whole-exome genes to ~230x average depth; whole transcriptome adds fusion/expression analysis.

proposedFills.proposedSource_note: keep value 8 but correct the note

proposedFills.proposedValue: 14 days

summary: MI Cancer Seek is still offered and the record is accurate and well-cited on all key facts (FDA P240010 WES+WTS approval, 228 reportable genes, 8 CDx claims, MSI/TMB/HRD/gLOH/ERBB2, CPT 0211U at $8,455, Medicare NCD 90.2 coverage). Only minor issues remain: a self-inconsistent CDx-count note, an HRD note that overstates the FDA CDx scope, and a broken NCCN citation URL.

name: MI Profile
qualityRationale: Record is substantially accurate and current: multi-omic WES+WTS+protein method, biomarkers (TMB, MSI, HRD, gLOH), FFPE sample, FDA-status nuance (MI Cancer Seek CDx approved Nov 2024; full MI Profile reflexes to LDT), and the MolDX L38121 Medicare LCD all verify against the vendor page and FDA/press sources. Score held back by a broken/irrelevant NCCN citation (molecularmd.com), a mildly stale gene count (22,000 vs vendor's current '23,000+'), the unnamed current LDT reflex component (MI Tumor Seek Hybrid), and the absence of a structured field capturing the 6 FDA CDx indications.

defects.issue: Citation points to https://www.molecularmd.com/ — MolecularMD is an unrelated (and effectively defunct/acquired) diagnostics company, not Caris. This is a wrong/broken citation that does not support any Caris NCCN-alignment claim.

No structured field captures that MI Cancer Seek carries 6 FDA companion-diagnostic indications (one pan-cancer MSI-H + breast PIK3CA, CRC KRAS/NRAS/BRAF, melanoma BRAF, NSCLC EGFR, endometrial). For a CGP/treatment-decision test, FDA CDx count/indications is a key fact and is only narratively implied.
Record states 22,000 (WES); current Caris vendor page states '23,000+ genes in one assay'. Mildly stale.

Current vendor architecture: MI Profile orders MI Cancer Seek (FDA CDx) NGS and reflexes to 'MI Tumor Seek Hybrid' (LDT) when specimens don't qualify. Record references an LDT reflex but does not name the current MI Tumor Seek Hybrid component.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Domain belongs to MolecularMD, an unrelated/acquired company — not a valid source for a Caris MI Profile NCCN-alignment claim; likely a copy-paste error.
Caris vendor page currently advertises '23,000+ genes in one assay'; 22,000 appears to be an older figure.
Cited to the MI Profile vendor page, but the current page does not display a turnaround time; figure is plausible historically but not presently verifiable at the cited URL.

proposedFills.proposedValue: MI Cancer Seek holds 6 FDA companion-diagnostic indications: pan-cancer MSI-H (pembrolizumab/dostarlimab); breast PIK3CA (alpelisib); CRC KRAS/NRAS WT (panitumumab) and BRAF V600E (encorafenib+cetuximab); melanoma BRAF V600E/V600K (BRAF/MEK inhibitors); NSCLC EGFR exon19del/L858R (EGFR TKIs); endometrial non-MSI-H (pembrolizumab+lenvatinib).
MI Profile orders MI Cancer Seek (FDA-approved CDx, Nov 2024) for NGS; reflexes to MI Tumor Seek Hybrid (CLIA LDT) when specimens do not meet MI Cancer Seek requirements.
Clinical and analytical validation of MI Cancer Seek published; WES+WTS-based CDx assay (228-gene CDx reporting, MSI/TMB).

summary: Caris MI Profile is accurately characterized as a still-offered multi-omic (WES+WTS+protein) tissue CGP service with correct biomarkers, FDA nuance, and Medicare LCD; main defects are a wrong NCCN citation (molecularmd.com), a mildly stale gene count (22,000 vs 23,000+), an unnamed current LDT reflex (MI Tumor Seek Hybrid), and no structured capture of the 6 FDA CDx indications.

name: IsoPSA
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and current: FDA PMA approved Dec 1, 2025 (confirmed via multiple sources), 14-site prospective validation, and the headline performance numbers (90.2% sens, 45.5% spec, AUC 0.783, PPV 47.7%, NPV 89.3%) all match published/FDA data. NCCN and AUA/SUO guideline inclusion, Medicare LCD L39284, and CPT 0359U are all verified correct. Score is held down by a fabricated/wrong PMID, mis-attribution of the n=888 performance data to the 2017 preliminary (n=261) paper, internally inconsistent PMA document numbers, and a sample-type (serum vs plasma) inconsistency.

defects.issue: PMID 27477528 is cited as 'Klein EA et al. Eur Urol 2017' but that is the WRONG PubMed ID. The actual PMID for the 2017 Eur Urol IsoPSA paper (Klein et al.) is 28396176. PMID 27477528 is an unrelated article.

The n=888 performance data (90.2% sens, 45.5% spec, AUC 0.783) are attributed to 'Klein EA et al. Eur Urol 2017', but that 2017 paper was a PRELIMINARY study of n=261. The n=888 figures come from the later prospective validation underlying the FDA PMA (J Urol 2019 / Urol Pract 2022), not the 2017 paper. Misattributed source.

Record cites two different FDA PMA document numbers inconsistently: 'P220023B.pdf' (for clinical/performance data) and 'P200048B.pdf' (for sample requirements / tube correction). Neither accessdata.fda.gov PMA URL could be verified in public results; the PMA number is unconfirmed and the two numbers cannot both be the approval document.

Listed as 'Blood/Serum' with Li-Heparin tube, but Li-Heparin yields PLASMA, and the CPT 0359U descriptor and FDA materials describe the analyte as plasma ('...plasma, algorithm reports risk of cancer'). Likely should be plasma, not serum.

Marketing-flavored unverifiable claim: 'More IsoPSAs ordered in first full year as LDT than Guardant360 tests in first 3 years' is a vendor comparison with no citable source and is not a clinical/coverage fact.

Classified as CGP (comprehensive genomic profiling). IsoPSA is a protein-structure prostate biopsy decision aid with no genomic content (genesAnalyzed correctly null). Categorization is a taxonomy mismatch, though correctly flagged in-record.

suspectClaims.currentValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27477528/ | Klein EA et al. Eur Urol 2017
suspectClaims.whySuspect: PMID 27477528 does not correspond to the Klein 2017 Eur Urol IsoPSA paper; correct PMID is 28396176. Wrong identifier.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 888 (attributed to Klein EA et al. Eur Urol 2017)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: The 2017 Eur Urol paper enrolled n=261. The n=888 cohort is the later prospective validation, not the 2017 study; attribution is incorrect.

suspectClaims.currentValue: More IsoPSAs ordered in first full year as LDT than Guardant360 tests in first 3 years
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor marketing claim with no citable, independent source.
PMA document number P200048B could not be verified; the record elsewhere cites P220023B for the same approval, an internal contradiction.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28396176/ | Klein EA et al. Eur Urol 2017 (preliminary, n=261)
Men ≥50 with confirmed PSA >4 and ≤25 ng/mL who are candidates for biopsy
Blood/Plasma
https://www.urologytimes.com/view/fda-approves-isopsa-test-as-aid-in-the-diagnosis-of-high-grade-prostate-cancer
Prospective multicenter validation across 14 US sites (FDA PMA pivotal); enrollment with PSA 4-100 (analytic cohort PSA 4-25) ng/mL, biopsy as reference standard

summary: IsoPSA is accurately represented as an FDA PMA-approved (Dec 1, 2025) blood-based protein-structure prostate biopsy decision aid, with correct headline performance, NCCN/AUA-SUO inclusion, Medicare LCD L39284 and CPT 0359U. Main defects: a wrong PMID (should be 28396176), the n=888 validation data misattributed to the 2017 n=261 preliminary paper, inconsistent/unverifiable FDA PMA document numbers, and a serum-vs-plasma sample-type inconsistency.

name: Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus
qualityRationale: Core facts verified against Thermo Fisher's official product page and gene-list PDF: 517 genes, amplicon (Ion Torrent) DNA+RNA, SNV/indel/CNV/fusion plus HRD/TMB/MSI signatures, RUO status, dual GeneStudio S5 / Genexus platforms. Vendor-verified by named Thermo Fisher contact (Dinesh Cyanam) March 2026, so currency is strong. Performance figures are sourced to the product flyer and a published validation study exists. Deductions: detailed analytical-performance numbers are cited only to a marketing flyer rather than the peer-reviewed validation paper; totalParticipants=20000 and numPublications=80 are round, unsourced estimates; reimbursement/clinicalAvailability are appropriately thin for an RUO kit but vague.

defects.issue: Value 20000 is a round number with no citation; appears to be an estimate of cumulative research use, not a defined study cohort. Unverifiable.
80 (with numPublicationsPlus=true) is unsourced and rounded; no citation to a publication list.

Extensive analytical-performance figures are cited only to a vendor product flyer (marketing collateral), not to the peer-reviewed validation study (MDPI J. Mol. Pathol. 2023) that reports analytical/clinical validation.
"Coverage varies" is vague; for an RUO research kit reimbursement is generally N/A — phrasing could mislead readers into thinking it is a billable clinical test.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Round, uncited number; no public source defines a 20,000-subject cohort for this RUO assay.
Round, uncited; no linked bibliography to substantiate the count.

Plausible and vendor-attributed, but vendor messaging emphasizes 'next day'/Genexus 24-27h; the 3-day GeneStudio S5 figure is from the flyer and should be treated as vendor-stated, not independently verified.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Add peer-reviewed validation reference: Conroy et al., 'Analytical Validation and Clinical Utilization of the Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus Panel for Comprehensive Genomic Profiling in Solid Tumors,' J. Mol. Pathol. 2023, 4(2):109-126.
517-gene content confirmed against Thermo Fisher official gene-list document; detects SNVs, indels, CNVs and fusions plus HRD/TMB/MSI signatures without add-on kits.
RUO (Research Use Only; not for use in diagnostic procedures) — confirmed on vendor product page.

summary: Accurate, recently vendor-verified record for Thermo Fisher's 517-gene RUO Oncomine Comprehensive Assay Plus; gene count, RUO status, DNA+RNA amplicon workflow, and HRD/TMB/MSI all confirmed against the official vendor page and gene-list PDF. Main weaknesses are unsourced round-number metrics (totalParticipants, numPublications) and performance data cited to a marketing flyer rather than the existing peer-reviewed validation study.

name: PGDx elio tissue complete
qualityRationale: Core CGP facts are accurate and current: 505 genes, FDA-cleared 2020, CE-IVDR July 2025, pan-solid tumor, MSI+TMB, NextSeq 550Dx IVD kit, CPT 0250U, Medicare $2919.60. All verified against Labcorp/PGDx official pages and AAPC. Score is held down by a stale Medicare effective date (claims 2025-Q4; actual effective Jan 1, 2022), and several unsourced/likely-fabricated numeric and payer fields (totalParticipants 5000, numPublications 30, commercialPayers UHC/Aetna, TAT 10-14 days) that are implausible or unverifiable for a kit product rather than a send-out lab service.

defects.issue: Claims '2025-Q4'. The $2919.60 rate for CPT 0250U was finalized by CMS and went into effect January 1, 2022 (PGDx/BusinessWire Jan 2022). The effective date is stale/wrong by ~3 years.

Lists 'UnitedHealthcare' and 'Aetna' as commercial payers with no citation. elio tissue complete is an IVD kit sold to labs, not a send-out billed to payers; these payer claims are unverifiable and likely fabricated. Sources note it is NOT a CDx and not auto-covered under CMS NGS NCD 90.2.
Value 5000 is unsourced. No public validation cohort of this size is citable; appears fabricated.
Value 30 is unsourced and unverifiable; appears fabricated.
'10-14 days' is unsourced. As an IVD kit run on-site by labs, turnaround is lab-dependent, not a vendor-specified value; unverifiable.

'~500 genes' is approximate while genesAnalyzed is precisely 505; should state 505. Amplification detection is limited to ERBB2 and 4 translocations (ALK, NTRK2, NTRK3, RET) per FDA labeling — 'select amplifications and translocations' is vague.
'plasma focus Dx' / 'PGDx analytics platform' phrasing is loose; elio plasma focus Dx is a separate FDA de novo authorized liquid biopsy product, not part of this tissue kit's offering.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: CMS rate for 0250U effective Jan 1, 2022, not 2025-Q4.
No citation; product is a lab IVD kit not billed to commercial payers as a send-out; sources state it is not a CDx and not under NGS NCD 90.2.
Round number, no citable validation cohort of this size.
Round number, unsourced.
Kit is run by customer labs; vendor does not specify a fixed turnaround.
Sources indicate elio tissue complete is not automatically covered under NGS NCD; '$2919.60 priced' is accurate but 'LCD coverage' is not clearly substantiated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Hybrid-capture NGS panel of 505 genes from FFPE tissue; reports SNVs, indels, select amplification (ERBB2) and translocations (ALK, NTRK2, NTRK3, RET), plus MSI and TMB
FDA-cleared (510(k)/de novo, 2020) for tumor mutation profiling; not a companion diagnostic (no CDx indications); CE-IVDR marked July 2025
>92% clinical success rate
FFPE solid tumor tissue

summary: PGDx elio tissue complete is still offered by Labcorp as a 505-gene FDA-cleared (2020), CE-IVDR (July 2025) pan-solid-tumor CGP IVD kit reporting SNVs/indels/select amps+fusions, MSI and TMB — these core facts are accurate. The record is undermined by a stale Medicare effective date (actually Jan 2022, not 2025-Q4) and several unsourced/likely-fabricated fields (5000 participants, 30 publications, UHC/Aetna payers, 10-14 day TAT); notably it is not a companion diagnostic and carries no CDx indications.

name: TruSight Oncology Comprehensive (TSO Comp)
qualityRationale: The record's load-bearing facts are accurate and current: 517-gene DNA panel, FDA PMA approval Aug 2024 (P230011), exactly 2 companion-diagnostic claims (NTRK fusions/larotrectinib pan-tumor; RET fusions/selpercatinib NSCLC), TMB and MSI reporting, NextSeq 550Dx platform, PLA code 0543U, and the CLFS rate of $2,989.55 effective Jan 1 2026 — all independently verified against FDA, Illumina press releases, and AAPC. Points deducted for an internally inconsistent/likely-wrong approval date in the changelog note (Aug 27 vs the consistently reported Aug 21, 2024), omission of the RNA panel (25 RNA genes) which is the actual basis of the CDx fusion claims, and three named commercial payers that could not be substantiated.

defects.issue: States 'FDA PMA approved Aug 27 2024'. FDA and all secondary sources consistently report the approval date as August 21, 2024. The 27th appears to be an error.
Describes only the 517-gene DNA panel; omits the 25-gene RNA panel. The FDA CDx claims (NTRK1/2/3, RET) are based on RNA fusion detection, so omitting the RNA component understates how the CDx works.

Lists UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna with no citation. No payer-specific policy for TSO Comprehensive could be located; likely a generic/unverified assumption rather than a documented coverage policy.
totalParticipants:10000 and numPublications:50 are round, unsourced figures with no citation; appear to be placeholders rather than verifiable values.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No payer-specific coverage policy for TSO Comprehensive could be found. As a distributable IVD kit run in-house, commercial payer dynamics differ from send-out lab services; these three look like an unsourced guess.
FDA and all news sources state August 21, 2024, not the 27th.
Round, uncited number; no validation study with this enrollment is referenced.
Round, uncited figure with numPublicationsPlus flag; not verifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: RNA panel covers 25 genes (RNA) for fusion detection, in addition to the 517-gene DNA panel; the NTRK and RET CDx claims are RNA-fusion based.
CMS CLFS reimbursement of $2,989.55 under PLA code 0543U, effective January 1, 2026; covered under NCD 90.2 for FDA-approved CGP with CDx claims.

Two CDx indications: NTRK1/2/3 fusions (larotrectinib/VITRAKVI, pan-solid tumor) and RET fusions (selpercatinib/RETEVMO, NSCLC). First FDA-approved distributable CGP IVD kit with pan-cancer CDx claims.

summary: An accurate, current record for Illumina's TruSight Oncology Comprehensive IVD kit — 517-gene DNA panel, Aug 2024 FDA PMA (P230011) with 2 CDx claims (NTRK/larotrectinib, RET/selpercatinib), TMB/MSI, NextSeq 550Dx, PLA 0543U at $2,989.55 (Jan 2026) are all verified. Main weaknesses are minor: a wrong approval date in the changelog note (Aug 27 vs 21), omission of the 25-gene RNA panel underlying the CDx fusion claims, and three uncited/unverifiable commercial payers.

name: therascreen EGFR RGQ PCR Kit
qualityRationale: Vendor and test name are correct and the product is still offered, but the record conflates two distinct QIAGEN products: the FDA-approved therascreen EGFR RGQ PCR Kit (tissue/FFPE) and the separate CE-IVD therascreen EGFR Plasma RGQ PCR Kit. It mislabels the sample type as Blood/Plasma, marks requiresTumorTissue as No, and frames sensitivity/LOD around plasma — all wrong for the FDA-approved tissue kit named here. It also miscalls the test "Tumor-agnostic" (it is single-gene EGFR, NSCLC-only) and omits the three FDA CDx drug indications (afatinib, gefitinib, dacomitinib). Performance numbers are cited only to a generic vendor URL and appear to be plasma-kit figures.

defects.issue: Listed as 'Blood/Plasma', but the FDA-approved therascreen EGFR RGQ PCR Kit is a tissue (FFPE) IVD. Plasma is a separate product (therascreen EGFR Plasma RGQ PCR Kit, CE-IVD, not FDA-approved). The record conflates two distinct products.
Set to 'No'. The FDA-approved kit named here requires FFPE NSCLC tumor tissue. Should be 'Yes'.
'Tumor-agnostic' is factually wrong. This is a single-gene (EGFR) test indicated only for NSCLC; it is tumor- and gene-specific, not tumor-agnostic.

'FDA-approved (tissue); CE-IVD (plasma)' mixes two products under one record and omits that it is a PMA-approved companion diagnostic (P120022) for three drugs: Gilotrif/afatinib (2013), Iressa/gefitinib (2015), Vizimpro/dacomitinib (2018).

Scorpions ARMS / allele-specific real-time PCR is correct, but the kit detects 21 defined EGFR mutations across exons 18-21 (incl. L861Q, G719X, S768I, exon 20 insertions), not just the listed exon 19 del/L858R/T790M. Description is incomplete.

80% with plasma framing is cited only to a generic vendor product page; this appears to be the plasma kit's clinical sensitivity, not the tissue kit's analytical sensitivity. Unverifiable as stated for the named FDA tissue kit.
'1% MAF' cited to generic vendor URL; tissue kit analytical LOD varies by mutation (~1-6.4%). Single flat figure is imprecise/unverifiable.
30000 participants cited to nothing specific; unverifiable round number.
150+ publications is an unsourced round figure.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: FDA-approved therascreen EGFR RGQ PCR Kit is an FFPE tissue assay; plasma is a separate CE-IVD product.
Single-gene EGFR test indicated only for NSCLC — not tumor-agnostic.
Reads as the plasma kit's clinical sensitivity; cited only to generic vendor URL, not validation data for the FDA tissue kit.
Cited only to generic vendor product page; no specific validation source.
Unsourced round number; no citation.
Unsourced round number.
Plausible for a same-day PCR run but cited to nothing; turnaround depends on lab workflow.

Yes

Allele-specific (Scorpions ARMS) real-time PCR detecting 21 defined EGFR mutations across exons 18-21 (exon 19 deletions; L858R, L861Q, G719X, S768I; exon 20 insertions; T790M) on the Rotor-Gene Q
Single gene (EGFR); 21 defined mutations across exons 18-21

summary: The record correctly identifies QIAGEN's therascreen EGFR RGQ PCR Kit (still offered) but conflates it with the separate CE-IVD plasma kit, wrongly labeling it as a Blood/Plasma, tumor-agnostic, non-tissue test; in reality it is a single-gene EGFR FFPE-tissue PMA-approved companion diagnostic (P120022) for afatinib, gefitinib, and dacomitinib in NSCLC. Performance figures (80% sensitivity, 98% specificity, 30000 participants, 150 publications) are plasma-framed and cited only to a generic vendor page, making them unverifiable for the named tissue kit.

name: OncoBEAM RAS CRC Kit
qualityRationale: Core identity is correct: Sysmex Inostics still offers the OncoBEAM RAS CRC Kit as a CE-IVD product in the EU and via CLIA service in the US; it is a BEAMing digital-PCR plasma assay for KRAS/NRAS in mCRC for anti-EGFR selection. But the record is weak on accuracy/evidence backing: the headline sensitivity/specificity (95%/99%) and the participant/publication counts (10,000 / 50+) are uncited or cited only to the vendor marketing page and conflict with peer-reviewed concordance data (overall tissue agreement ~86-93%, PPA ~82-95%). The panel is mischaracterized as a bare 2-gene panel without the well-documented "34 mutations across KRAS/NRAS exons 2-4" detail. Categorizing a 2-gene hotspot RAS assay as CGP (comprehensive genomic profiling) is itself a misclassification.

defects.issue: Claimed 95% sensitivity / 99% specificity cited only to the vendor page (sysmex-inostics.com/oncobeam-ras-crc, a non-resolving/marketing URL). Peer-reviewed validation shows overall plasma-vs-tissue agreement of only ~86.4% (PPA 82.1%, NPA 90.4%) in the 280-patient prospective study (PMC6734650) and 93.1% OPA in a 102-patient study. Presenting 95/99 as analytical performance is misleading and unverifiable.
Value 10000 has no citation and appears to be a fabricated round number. Published validation cohorts are on the order of 100-300 patients; no source supports 10,000.
Value 50 (with numPublicationsPlus true) is uncited and unverifiable; appears to be an invented round number.
A 2-gene RAS hotspot assay (KRAS/NRAS, 34 mutations) is not comprehensive genomic profiling. Classifying it as CGP is misleading; it is a targeted single-biomarker ctDNA test.
Omits the documented panel size: vendor and literature state 34 specific point mutations across KRAS and NRAS exons 2,3,4. Record says only 'exons 2-4' without the mutation count.

'0.02% MAF' cited only to vendor marketing page; not independently verified. BEAMing sensitivity in literature is often quoted ~0.01-0.04% but the specific 0.02% figure is unsourced beyond the vendor.

CE-IVD is correct for EU, but record omits that in the US it is offered only as a CLIA laboratory service (not FDA-cleared), and reimbursement note 'reimbursed in EU' is vague (specific German payer BARMER reimbursement exists).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited only to vendor page; peer-reviewed plasma-vs-tissue PPA is ~82-95%, and overall agreement ~86%. 95% is at the optimistic edge and not an analytical sensitivity figure.
Cited only to vendor page; published NPA is ~90-91%, not 99%. Unverifiable as stated.
No citation; round number inconsistent with published cohorts (hundreds, not thousands).
No citation; round number, unverifiable.
Cited only to vendor marketing URL that does not resolve cleanly; specific figure not independently confirmed.

Overall plasma-vs-tissue agreement 86.4% (positive percent agreement 82.1%, negative percent agreement 90.4%) in a 280-patient multicentre prospective study; 93.1% overall agreement in a separate 102-patient cohort.
CE-IVD kit available in the EU; offered as a CLIA-certified laboratory service in the US (Baltimore, MD).
Reimbursed by BARMER in Germany; coverage otherwise varies by EU country.

summary: The OncoBEAM RAS CRC Kit is still offered (CE-IVD in EU, CLIA service in US) and the core description is correct, but the record carries an uncited/misleading 95%/99% sensitivity-specificity pair that conflicts with peer-reviewed concordance data (~86-93%), apparently fabricated participant (10,000) and publication (50+) counts, and misclassifies a 2-gene/34-mutation RAS hotspot assay as comprehensive genomic profiling.

name: LeukoStrat CDx FLT3 Mutation Assay
qualityRationale: Core clinical facts are accurate and current: single-gene FLT3 PCR-based CDx detecting ITD and TKD (D835/I836) mutations in AML, FDA-approved as companion diagnostic for RYDAPT (midostaurin), XOSPATA (gilteritinib), and VANFLYTA (quizartinib), run on an ABI 3500-family genetic analyzer from bone marrow/peripheral blood. The first FDA-approved CDx for AML. Vendor and product name are unchanged and the test is still offered. Score is held below ~80 because two quantitative fields (totalParticipants=10000, numPublications=50) look like round placeholder values with no citation, the platform name is slightly off from the FDA-specified instrument, and the 3-day TAT is unverifiable for a distributed IVD kit.

defects.issue: Record says 'ABI 3500 Genetic Analyzer' but FDA approval P160040 and the VANFLYTA labeling specify the 3500xL Dx Genetic Analyzer (the regulated Dx instrument). Should be corrected to '3500xL Dx Genetic Analyzer'.

Value of 10000 is a round, uncited number with no obvious source; the pivotal CDx validation cohorts (e.g., QuANTUM-First n=539, RATIFY for midostaurin) are far smaller. Appears to be a placeholder rather than a verified participant count.
numPublications=50 with numPublicationsPlus=true is a round, uncited figure that reads as a placeholder rather than a counted literature total.
'3 days' turnaround is asserted without citation; for a distributed IVD kit run in third-party labs, TAT is lab-dependent and not a vendor-fixed value, so this is unverifiable.
sensitivity is null; acceptable for a qualitative mutation-detection CDx but leaves a key performance field unpopulated.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Round number with no citation; CDx clinical bridging cohorts are in the hundreds (QuANTUM-First n=539). Likely placeholder.
Round, uncited count flagged with a '+'; reads as an estimate, not a verified bibliometric total.
FDA-approved instrument is the 3500xL Dx Genetic Analyzer; generic 'ABI 3500' understates the regulated configuration.
Distributed kit run in customer labs; TAT is lab-specific and not vendor-guaranteed, so a single fixed value is unverifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 3500xL Dx Genetic Analyzer

PCR-based detection of FLT3 internal tandem duplication (ITD) and tyrosine kinase domain (TKD; D835/I836) mutations in genomic DNA from peripheral blood or bone marrow mononuclear cells; FDA-approved companion diagnostic

First FDA-approved companion diagnostic for AML. CDx for RYDAPT (midostaurin, newly diagnosed FLT3+ AML), XOSPATA (gilteritinib, relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML), and VANFLYTA (quizartinib, newly diagnosed FLT3-ITD+ AML; FDA-approved July 2023).

summary: Accurate, current record for Invivoscribe's LeukoStrat CDx FLT3 Mutation Assay, the first FDA-approved AML companion diagnostic (single-gene FLT3 ITD/TKD PCR test) for midostaurin, gilteritinib, and quizartinib. Main weaknesses are uncited placeholder-looking metrics (totalParticipants 10000, numPublications 50), a slightly imprecise platform name (should be 3500xL Dx), and an unverifiable fixed TAT.

name: PGDx elio plasma focus Dx
qualityRationale: Core identity (vendor/ownership, FDA De Novo status, IVD kit, NextSeq 550Dx platform, blood/plasma sample, tumor-agnostic, still offered) is correct and current. But the headline genomic claim — 70 genes — is factually wrong: the FDA-authorized kit (DEN230046, Aug 2 2024) covers SNVs/indels in 33 genes, CNAs in 5 genes, and translocations in 3 genes. Several performance fields (90% sens, 95% spec, 0.5% MAF LoD, 5000 participants, 30 publications) are round, vendor-page-cited or uncited numbers that don't match the FDA-reported analytical figures, and MSI reporting (a real authorized feature) is absent.

defects.issue: Record states 70 genes. The FDA De Novo-authorized PGDx elio plasma focus Dx detects SNVs/indels in 33 genes, CNAs in 5 genes, and translocations in 3 genes (per Labcorp press release and 360Dx/Targeted Oncology coverage of DEN230046). 70 likely confuses an earlier RUO/LDT focus panel with the FDA kit. The method field repeats this wrong '~70-gene' figure.

90% / 95% cited to the generic vendor product page, not validation data. FDA-reported analytical sensitivity is variant-type-specific (e.g. >0.31% VAF for actionable SNVs/indels, ~1.3-fold amplifications, 0.41% fusion read fraction). Single round percentages are misleading for a multi-analyte panel.
Should specify the 33/5/3 gene breakdown by variant type and note MSI is reported. CNA genes include CCND1, CD274, ERBB2, FGFR2, MET; translocations ALK, NTRK1, RET.
Record says 5-7 days; vendor/FDA materials state 4-5 day turnaround with >96% clinical success rate.
5000 participants and 30 publications are round, uncited figures not verifiable from any located source.
'Medicare LCD coverage' is vague and uncited; no specific MolDX/LCD or CPT reference provided.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: FDA De Novo authorization (DEN230046) and all press coverage describe a 33-gene SNV/indel panel + 5-gene CNA + 3-gene translocation content, not 70 genes.
Cited only to vendor product page; FDA reports variant-specific VAF-based analytical limits, not a single 90% figure.
FDA-reported analytical sensitivity is >0.31% VAF for actionable SNVs/indels and varies by variant class; 0.5% is an unsourced round approximation.
No located source supports a 5000-participant validation; appears fabricated/round.
Round figure with no citation; unverifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 33 (SNVs/indels in 33 genes; CNAs in 5 genes; translocations in 3 genes)
FDA De Novo authorized (DEN230046), granted August 2, 2024 — first kitted pan-solid tumor liquid biopsy IVD
4-5 days
Microsatellite Instability (MSI) reported, in addition to SNVs/indels, CNAs and translocations
FDA-authorized IVD kit; clinically launched for treatment selection April 2025

summary: PGDx elio plasma focus Dx is correctly identified as Labcorp's FDA De Novo-authorized (Aug 2024, DEN230046) pan-solid-tumor liquid biopsy IVD kit and is still offered, but the record's headline 70-gene count is wrong (FDA kit = 33 genes SNV/indel + 5 CNA + 3 translocation) and several performance fields (90/95% sens/spec, 0.5% LoD, 5000 participants, 30 publications) are round, vendor-page-only or unverifiable values. It is a tumor mutation profiling device, not a CDx.

name: MSK-IMPACT powered with SOPHiA DDM
qualityRationale: Every populated field is corroborated by the vendor's official product page: 505 genes, biomarker list (SNV/Indel/CNV/DNA fusions/MSI/TMB/TERT promoter/MET exon 14 skipping), 50 ng FFPE (min 10 ng) + 50 ng WBC gDNA matched-normal input, ~5-day TAT, 5% VAF with CUMIN barcoding, 99.3% PPA vs centralized MSK-IMPACT, RUO status, and Nov 2024 AMP launch. No fabricated numbers detected. Score held below 90 because citationPmids is empty (the 99.3% PPA analytical claim is vendor-page-only, not a peer-reviewed citation) and the record does not distinguish this from the sibling "MSK-IMPACT Flex powered with SOPHiA DDM" product, a possible source of confusion. Low deterministic completeness (0.41) largely reflects CGP fields (FDA CDx count, CPT code, Medicare/MolDX coverage) that legitimately do not apply to an RUO decentralized kit.

defects.issue: Empty. The headline 99.3% PPA analytical-concordance claim is sourced only to the vendor product page; no peer-reviewed PMID/DOI is cited. Validation data appears vendor-internal/poster-stage, so a real PMID may not yet exist — but the field should be confirmed empty intentionally.
Does not distinguish this assay from the sibling 'MSK-IMPACT Flex powered with SOPHiA DDM' offered by the same vendor; a reader could conflate the two SKUs.
States 'RUO kit (launched November 2024)' — accurate, but as an RUO product it is not clinically orderable; field name 'clinicalAvailability' is slightly misleading for a research-use-only kit.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 99.3% PPA for SNV/Indel detection vs centralized MSK-IMPACT
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Verified against the vendor page (matches), but it is a vendor self-reported analytical-validation figure with no independent peer-reviewed citation; treat as vendor-claimed, not independently confirmed.

proposedFills.proposedValue: ~5 days sample-to-report (library prep ~1.5 days)
RUO (Research Use Only); not for diagnostic use. The decentralized SOPHiA kit is RUO, distinct from MSK's centralized FDA-authorized MSK-IMPACT laboratory test.
Not reported (HRD/genomic instability score not listed among biomarkers on vendor spec)

summary: Record is accurate and faithfully matches SOPHiA GENETICS' official product page (505 genes, RUO, ~5-day TAT, 5% VAF/CUMIN, 99.3% PPA, Nov 2024 launch); the test is still offered under this name. Main weaknesses are an empty citationPmids (the 99.3% PPA figure is vendor-only) and no distinction from the sibling MSK-IMPACT Flex SKU; low completeness mostly reflects CGP fields (FDA CDx/CPT/coverage) that do not apply to an RUO kit.

name: NEO PanTracer LBx
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and current on most load-bearing facts: vendor (NeoGenomics), 514-gene liquid ctDNA CGP, CLIA LDT (not FDA-approved), MSI/bTMB reporting, 7-day TAT, 2x Streck cfDNA BCT tubes, CPT 81464, and Medicare MolDX L38043 coverage at $3,289 — all confirmed against the vendor page and the March 2026 press release. The AACR 2025 Abstract 4090 validation citation is real. Main deductions are for two stale/incorrect secondary gene counts (CNV and fusion) that conflict with the current vendor page, and a couple of minor citation/date inconsistencies.

defects.issue: Record states CNVs in 95 genes and fusions in 23 genes. The current vendor page (neogenomics.com/providers/test/NTG-PTLB-02SX) lists CNVs in 39 genes and fusions in 19 genes. The 95/23 figures appear fabricated or stale and contradict the cited source.

Record gives Medicare effective date as March 10, 2026; the NeoGenomics press release and GenomeWeb/CLP coverage describe the coverage announcement on March 16, 2026 under LCD L38043. The specific 'March 10' effective date is not corroborated in the cited press release (which discloses neither the rate nor CPT code), so the $3,289 rate and effective date should be re-sourced to the MolDX LCD/fee schedule rather than the press release.
Record says 'Streck cfDNA BCT' with 2 tubes but omits the vendor-stated 10 mL volume per tube ('two x 10 mL Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes'). Cosmetic completeness gap.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 95 genes for CNVs, 23 genes for fusions
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Current vendor page lists 39 genes for CNVs and 19 for fusions; the 95/23 values contradict the cited vendor URL and cannot be verified.
Cited press release (detail/323) does not disclose the rate or a March 10 effective date; the announcement was March 16, 2026. Rate/date need a primary MolDX/fee-schedule source.
Abstract 4090 is confirmed to exist, but the specific 96.9%/99.3% aggregate values could not be independently confirmed from public text; plausible but should be checked against the full poster.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 514 genes for SNVs/InDels, 39 genes for CNVs, 19 genes for fusions; plus MSI-High status and bTMB.
Comprehensive genomic profiling of cfDNA analyzing SNVs/InDels in 514 genes, CNVs in 39 genes, and fusions in 19 genes, plus MSI-High and bTMB.
Peripheral blood: two x 10 mL Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes
Medicare coverage under MolDX LCD L38043 announced March 16, 2026

summary: NEO PanTracer LBx is a currently offered NeoGenomics 514-gene liquid (ctDNA) CGP LDT, and the record is largely accurate (Medicare MolDX L38043, CPT 81464, 7-day TAT, MSI/bTMB, CLIA LDT all verified). The notable defect is stale/incorrect secondary gene counts — the record's 95 CNV / 23 fusion genes conflict with the vendor page's current 39 CNV / 19 fusion genes.

name: NEO PanTracer Pro
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-sourced against the live vendor catalog page (NTG-PTPO-03AX) and the Feb 12 2026 IR press release. FDA status (CLIA LDT), TAT (8-10d CGP / 2d IHC / 5d ISH), sample requirements, CPT codes, launch date, HRD/MSI/TMB reporting, and IHC marker list all verified. Score held below 90 only for rounded gene/IHC counts vs. exact vendor figures and for thin coverage detail.

defects.issue: Uses conservative rounded counts (400+ DNA SNV/InDel, 50+ CNV, 50+ RNA fusion). Vendor test page gives exact figures: 409 SNV/InDel, 61 CNV, 56 fusion, 6 splice (one NeoGenomics source cites 517/59/55). Could be sharpened to exact numbers.
Lists 13 IHC markers ('13+'); vendor test page shows 14 expression markers. Slight undercount.
Set to 500 (rounded marketing '500+'). Sum of exact DNA SNV/InDel genes is 409 (or 517 per one source); the single integer is ambiguous about DNA vs DNA+RNA scope.
'Coverage Varies' with reimbursementNote citing Medicare NCD 90.2 is plausible but uncited to a specific MolDX/payer policy; coverage backing is thin for a CGP test.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor test page enumerates 409 DNA SNV/InDel genes; a separate NeoGenomics product page cites 517. The rounded 500 does not match either exact figure and conflates DNA-only vs DNA+RNA scope.
Live vendor page lists 14 IHC expression markers, not 13.

proposedFills.proposedValue: DNA: 409 genes for SNVs/InDels, 61 genes for CNVs; RNA: 56 genes for fusions, 6 genes for splice variants. Plus 14 diagnosis-directed IHC protein markers bundled in a single order. (NeoGenomics also markets the panel as '500+ / 517 cancer-related genes.')
Plus 14 IHC protein expression markers: ALK, AR, c-MET, Claudin-18, ER, FOLR1, HER2, KI67, PD-L1, PGR, PTEN, ROS1, TP53 (plus one additional marker).
HRD status reported, primarily for ovarian cancer patients per vendor positioning.

summary: NEO PanTracer Pro (tds-31) is an accurate, currently-offered, well-cited record for NeoGenomics' Feb-2026 tissue CGP+IHC LDT; only minor precision gaps remain (rounded gene/IHC counts vs. exact vendor figures of 409/61/56/6 and 14 IHC markers, and thin coverage citation).

name: OncoDEEP Kit
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and vendor-verified (2026-05-31 by Cédric Balsat, OncoDNA): 638-gene DNA panel, FFPE tissue, CE-IVD, MSI/TMB/HRD reporting, not FDA-cleared, 5-day TAT all confirmed against vendor pages and the Annals of Oncology 1253P HRD validation. Single record for the id (no duplicate). Main weaknesses are completeness/nuance rather than fabrication: the record presents OncoDEEP as a DNA-only 638-gene assay and attributes all fusion detection to DNA intronic tiling, but the vendor's current product describes a comprehensive panel of 638 DNA genes PLUS a 22-gene RNA driver panel for fusion/splicing detection — so the architecture is DNA+RNA, not DNA-only. TERT promoter analysis (reported by vendor) is omitted from biomarkers. One citation URL (oncodeep-638-genes) and the methodCitations performance-summary PDF were not independently confirmable (404 on one fetch).

defects.issue: Record describes the assay as a 638-gene DNA panel with fusions detected only via 'intronic tiling for ALK, RET and ROS1'. Vendor's current product comprises 638 DNA genes PLUS a separate 22-gene RNA driver panel that detects fusions and splicing events. The DNA+RNA architecture is not captured; fusion detection is mischaracterized as DNA-only.
Vendor lists intronic tiling for ALK/ROS1/RET AND MET exon-14 skipping; record omits MET-ex14.
Vendor reports TERT promoter analysis for OncoDEEP; not listed in biomarkers (it is listed for the sibling OncoSELECT tds-33).

Performance-summary PDF (oncodna.com/content/uploads/2026/05/...) and the oncodeep-638-genes page returned 404 / unverifiable on fetch; live URL is oncodna.com/for-laboratories/solid-biopsy-oncodeep/.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Accurate as a category, but the implicit DNA-only framing in method understates a DNA+RNA combined assay (22-gene RNA panel for fusions/splicing).

suspectClaims.currentValue: 5 working days (vendor-reported target for decentralized in-house workflow)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Sourced only to a private vendor email; vendor public page states 5 days from DNA extraction to interpretation and ~48h post-sequencing, broadly consistent but the cited source is non-public.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Comprehensive genomic profiling kit for FFPE solid-tumor tissue combining a 638-gene DNA panel (1.8 Mb, with intronic tiling for ALK/ROS1/RET fusions and MET exon-14 skipping) with a 22-gene RNA driver panel for fusion and splicing-variant detection. Detects SNVs, indels, CNVs, LOH and TERT promoter variants and reports MSI, TMB and HRD. Paired with the OncoKDM interpretation platform for decentralized in-house testing.
SNVs, Indels, CNVs, LOH, Fusions, TERT promoter, MSI, TMB, HRD
22 RNA driver genes (fusion/splicing panel complementing the 638-gene DNA panel)
CE-IVD marked (EU); not FDA-cleared/approved

summary: OncoDEEP Kit (OncoDNA) is still offered as a CE-IVD 638-gene tissue CGP kit; the record is accurate on gene count, MSI/TMB/HRD, FFPE, CE-IVD and non-FDA status, but mischaracterizes the assay as DNA-only — it actually pairs the 638-gene DNA panel with a 22-gene RNA fusion/splicing panel — and omits TERT promoter and MET-ex14. No duplicate id; vendor-verified May 2026.

name: OncoSELECT Kit
qualityRationale: Core facts (vendor OncoDNA, 74-gene ctDNA RUO liquid-biopsy kit, biomarker types SNV/indel/CNV/fusion/TERT/splicing, RUO status, EU availability, no MSI/TMB/HRD) are accurate and match the current vendor page. Deductions: TAT is wrongly recorded as "not specified" when the vendor explicitly states 4-5 days; numPublications=0 is now stale (a multi-centre 4-lab validation poster exists); some method details (Streck tubes, 2x10mL, exact per-variant sensitivity figures) are unverifiable against the live vendor page, which instead specifies a 20ng cfDNA input and Twist/Illumina chemistry that the record omits.

defects.issue: Recorded as 'Not specified by vendor' but the vendor page explicitly states a 4-5 day turnaround (including ~48h OncoDNA scientist review). Factually understates available information.

Stated as 0 with note 'No peer-reviewed publications as of May 2026.' A multi-centre validation poster (four independent labs, LOD 0.25% VAF) now exists; still not peer-reviewed, but the supporting-evidence field is now incomplete/stale.

Omits the sequencing chemistry/platform: vendor states Twist Bioscience capture reagents, compatible with Illumina NextSeq/NovaSeq, with ~20 ng cfDNA recommended input. The record instead cites '2 x 10 mL Streck tubes' and per-variant sensitivity figures not confirmed on the current vendor page.
'2 x 10 mL Streck tubes' sample-collection detail is unverifiable against the live vendor page, which describes input as ~20 ng cfDNA from plasma.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Not specified by vendor (in-house kit workflow)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor lab page advertises 'actionable insights in 5 days' / 4-5 day workflow; TAT is in fact specified.

suspectClaims.currentValue: sensitivity >96.9%, specificity >99%, accuracy >99%, limit of detection 0.25-1%
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Live vendor page gives per-variant sensitivities (96.88% SNV, 96.93% indel, 97.6% fusion, >99% CNV) and LOD 0.25% VAF (SNV/indel), 0.5% fusion, 1% CNV; the record's aggregated '>96.9%' and 'accuracy >99%' framing is not directly stated and may be a paraphrase rather than a vendor-verbatim claim.
Not stated on current vendor lab page, which specifies ~20 ng cfDNA input; collection-tube detail unverifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 4-5 days in-house (kit workflow), including ~48h OncoDNA scientist report review
Twist Bioscience hybrid-capture targeted NGS panel, compatible with Illumina NextSeq/NovaSeq; ~20 ng cfDNA recommended input; paired with OncoKDM interpretation platform

MSI, TMB and HRD are NOT reported (confirmed absent on vendor page)

summary: OncoDNA's OncoSELECT is a 74-gene RUO ctDNA liquid-biopsy CGP kit, still actively offered; the OpenOnco record is mostly accurate but wrongly states TAT is unspecified (vendor says 4-5 days), shows a now-stale numPublications=0 (a 4-lab validation poster exists), and carries some method details (Streck tubes, per-variant sensitivities) not confirmed on the live vendor page, which instead specifies Twist/Illumina chemistry and ~20 ng cfDNA input.

name: NeXT Personal Dx
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, well-cited, and unusually current — all four Medicare coverage decisions (breast surveillance Oct 2025, NSCLC Feb 2026, immunotherapy May 2026, neoadjuvant breast May 2026) are confirmed against Personalis press releases, and core technical facts (tumor-informed WGS, ~1,800 variants, 1.67 ppm LOD / 3.45 ppm LOD95, >99.9% specificity) match the Oncotarget analytical validation paper. Points deducted for: internal inconsistency in the Medicare policy number (L38779 vs L38822), a sensitivity/validation cohort figure (n=493, 100% sensitivity) that does not match the published validation paper's described cohort (123 patients across 9 cancer types), and a fabricated-looking clinicalTrialsCitations DOI placeholder ("10.1016/j.cell.2025.12.XXX").

defects.issue: Inconsistent Medicare policy numbers: medicareCoverage.policyNumber="L38779" but coverageCrossReference.medicare.policies=["L38822"]. Both cannot be the governing LCD for the same test; at least one is wrong/stale and must be reconciled against the actual MolDX MRD LCD.

Contains a placeholder/fabricated DOI: "https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2025.12.XXX". The TRACERx Cell 2025 paper has a real DOI; ".XXX" is not a citable identifier and violates the no-fabricated-DOI rule.

Record states 100% sensitivity in a validation cohort of n=493, but the published Oncotarget/medRxiv analytical validation describes panels from 123 patients across 9 cancer types plus 2 cell lines. The 493 figure and the 100% sensitivity value are not traceable to the cited investor static-file and conflict with the peer-reviewed validation; analyticalValidationWarning is set but the headline number is still misleadingly precise.

Effective dates phrased loosely (e.g. "Feb 2026", "May 13 2026", "May 20 2026") mix announcement dates with effective dates; CMS effective date for the breast LCD is stated as Oct 7 2025 elsewhere — dates should be normalized to avoid conflating announcement vs effective.
TAT (21d/10d) and CPT ("MolDX with DEX Z-code") rely solely on private vendor communication (Dan Norton, Personalis, Dec 2025) with no public citation; unverifiable from public sources.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: 100% sensitivity is an analytical-validation artifact of a small/undocumented cohort; the cited investor static-file does not clearly support n=493, and the peer-reviewed validation cohort is 123 patients across 9 cancer types. A headline 100% clinical sensitivity is not credible for an MRD assay.
Published analytical validation (Oncotarget 2024 / medRxiv) describes 123 patients across 9 cancer types plus 2 cell lines, not 493. Source of 493 is unclear.
Conflicts with L38822 listed in coverageCrossReference for the same test; one is incorrect.

450 days (15 months) is from one breast-cancer cohort (Garcia-Murillas Ann Oncol 2025) and is not a universal NeXT median; NSCLC TRACERx showed ~6 months. Field stores a single number that is indication-specific and risks overstating performance.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Northcott et al., Oncotarget 2024 — analytical validation of NeXT Personal using panels from 123 patients across 9 cancer types plus 2 cell lines; detection threshold 1.67 ppm, LOD95 3.45 ppm, >99.9% specificity.

Replace the placeholder Cell DOI with the verified TRACERx NSCLC press/source pending exact DOI confirmation: https://investors.personalis.com/news-releases/news-release-details/personalis-receives-medicare-coverage-next-personalr-lung-cancer
Add pre-surgical/neoadjuvant breast (Stage II-III TNBC/HER2+) coverage confirmed via fourth coverage decision, supported by PREDICT-DNA (227 pts, JCO).
Immunotherapy monitoring across late-stage solid tumors Medicare coverage confirmed (announced May 13 2026, supported by VHIO data).

summary: Accurate, well-sourced, and very current record for Personalis NeXT Personal Dx (still offered under this name); all four 2025-2026 Medicare coverage decisions and core technical specs verify against vendor releases and the Oncotarget validation paper. Main defects are an internal Medicare policy-number conflict (L38779 vs L38822), a placeholder/fabricated Cell DOI in clinicalTrialsCitations, and a 100%-sensitivity/n=493 claim that does not match the published 123-patient validation cohort.

name: Signatera
qualityRationale: Record is highly accurate and current. Verified against vendor, FDA, and CMS sources: tumor-informed mPCR-NGS method, 16 variants tracked, ~0.01% VAF LOD, CPT 0340U PLA/ADLT, Medicare rate $3,920, and the May 15, 2026 FDA CDx approval for adjuvant atezolizumab in ctDNA+ MIBC (first blood-based MRD companion diagnostic) all match authoritative sources. Deductions are for vendor-sourced headline performance figures (sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV blended across tumor types), an over-100 publications soft claim, a vague leadTimeVsImaging value, and a minor internal inconsistency where medicareCoverage.reimbursementRate is null despite medicareRate=3920 being populated.

defects.issue: Null while top-level medicareRate is 3920 and CMS prices 0340U at $3,920; internal inconsistency, should be populated for consistency.
Value 300 (days) is generic/imprecise; notes cite 'ovarian ~10 months; NSCLC >7 months' which are tumor-specific, not a single 300-day figure. The single integer oversimplifies.
104 with numPublicationsPlus:true derives from a vendor 'over 100 papers' press release rather than an independent bibliometric count; soft/promotional.

Headline single values (94/98/98/96) are blended across tumor types and cited to an investor/ASCO press page rather than peer-reviewed per-indication data; notes appropriately disclose ranges but headline numbers are vendor-sourced.
Lists 'Stage I-IV NSCLC' whereas vendor/MolDX coverage is described elsewhere as NSCLC stage I-III surveillance; slight inconsistency between fields.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Single 300-day figure not supported by the cited notes (ovarian ~10 mo, NSCLC >7 mo are tumor-specific); appears to be a placeholder/averaged value.
Headline 94% is a longitudinal NSCLC-derived figure generalized across all tumors; notes show wide per-tumor ranges (80-99%), so a single 94% headline is potentially misleading without context.

Correct for standard clinical assay, but Natera now markets 'Signatera Genome' (WGS-based) configurations tracking far more variants; record should clarify whether mrd-7 covers the legacy 16-variant panel or the newer Genome product.

proposedFills.proposedValue: FDA-approved companion diagnostic (PMA) — May 15, 2026: Signatera CDx approved to select muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients (ctDNA-positive after cystectomy) for adjuvant atezolizumab; first blood-based MRD companion diagnostic. PMA submitted Feb 2, 2026; based on Phase III IMvigor011 (NEJM, Oct 2025). Otherwise CLIA LDT for pan-tumor MRD.
~0.01% VAF (>95% sensitivity at 0.01% tumor fraction; ~100% at >=0.05%)

summary: Signatera (Natera) is accurately and currently represented: tumor-informed 16-variant mPCR-NGS MRD assay, ~0.01% VAF LOD, CPT 0340U/ADLT at $3,920, and the verified May 15, 2026 FDA CDx approval for adjuvant atezolizumab in ctDNA+ MIBC. Only minor issues remain (a null Medicare rate field, a vague lead-time value, vendor-sourced blended performance/publication figures, and minor NSCLC staging inconsistency).

name: Haystack MRD
qualityRationale: Core technical facts are accurate and well-sourced (tumor-informed WES + matched normal, up to 50 variants, LOD 0.0006%/~6ppm, 100% analytical specificity, 4wk/5-7d TAT, CPT 0561U, FDA Breakthrough for Stage II CRC, Quest/Haystack Oncology ownership). But the record contains two material factual errors that mislead: (1) the headline "sensitivity: 95" is mislabeled — 95% is the LoD detection rate at 0.0006% tumor fraction, not a clinical or analytical sensitivity figure the vendor publishes; (2) the NEJM Apr 2025 study (NEJMoa2502029) is repeatedly mischaracterized as showing recurrence detection 1.4mo post-surgery vs 6.1mo imaging, when it is the dostarlimab/immunotherapy complete-response (organ-preservation) study where ctDNA identified clinical COMPLETE RESPONSE at a median 1.4 months — not recurrence lead time. The derived leadTimeVsImaging=42 days is a fabricated reframing. Medicare status is also overstated as COVERED when 0561U coverage is unestablished/not covered in multiple MAC regions.

defects.issue: Value 95 is mislabeled. 95% is the LoD95 detection rate at 0.0006% tumor fraction (the % of replicates detected at that ctDNA level), NOT a clinical or analytical sensitivity metric. Vendor does not publish a 95% sensitivity figure. Presenting it as 'sensitivity' is misleading.

The NEJM Apr 2025 study (NEJMoa2502029) is mischaracterized as 'MRD detected recurrence median 1.4 months post-surgery vs 6.1 months for imaging.' That study (Cercek et al., MSK dostarlimab in MMR-deficient cancers) used Haystack MRD as a correlative tool and found ctDNA identified clinical COMPLETE RESPONSE to immunotherapy at median 1.4 months vs >6 months by imaging — an organ-preservation/watch-and-wait finding, not post-surgical recurrence lead time. leadTimeVsImaging=42 days is a fabricated derivation from this misread.

Listed as COVERED under LCD L38779, but the record's own notes say 'Expected covered' (speculative) and multiple payers/MACs list CPT 0561U (Haystack MRD Monitoring) as Not Covered / coverage not established (e.g., Providence Sept 2024). Status should be NOT_ESTABLISHED/PENDING, not COVERED.
States 'Analytical validation (stages not specified)' yet a clinical sensitivity of 95 is populated — internally inconsistent with the sensitivity field being analytical LoD-derived.

Claims 'Validated for post-surgical MRD assessment and longitudinal surveillance' — the cited NEJM evidence is a neoadjuvant immunotherapy response-monitoring setting, not post-surgical MRD validation.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Not a published sensitivity. 95% is the LoD95 detection rate at 0.0006% tumor fraction; vendor FAQ frames it as 'detect 95% of cases at 0.0006% tumor fraction,' i.e. analytical detection probability, not sensitivity.

Derived from misreading the NEJM dostarlimab complete-response study as recurrence detection. The 1.4 vs 6.1 month figure is time-to-identify-complete-response to immunotherapy, not recurrence lead time over imaging.
0561U coverage is not established in multiple MAC regions (Providence lists Not Covered, Sept 2024); record notes say only 'Expected covered.'

Unsourced aggregate; no citation ties this number to a specific cohort. Sum of cited trial enrollments (150+25+158=333) suggests it is trial enrollment targets, not validated participants with results.
No citation supports a count of 18; only the NEJM paper and two ASCO GI 2026 abstracts are itemized.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Vendor reports the assay detects ctDNA in 95% of replicates at 0.0006% tumor fraction (LoD95) with 100% analytical specificity (zero false positives). This is an analytical detection rate, not a clinical sensitivity; stage-specific clinical sensitivity not published.

NEJM Apr 2025 (Cercek et al., dostarlimab in mismatch-repair-deficient locally advanced rectal/solid tumors): Haystack MRD ctDNA identified clinical complete response to immunotherapy at a median of 1.4 months versus >6 months by imaging — an organ-preservation/response-monitoring finding, not post-surgical recurrence lead time.
Up to 50
NOT_ESTABLISHED
Initial personalized panel build: up to 4 weeks; subsequent MRD timepoint results within 5-7 days of sample receipt.

summary: Haystack MRD (Quest Diagnostics) is still actively offered; core technical specs (tumor-informed WES + matched normal, up to 50 variants, LOD 0.0006%, 100% analytical specificity, CPT 0561U, FDA Breakthrough for Stage II CRC) are accurate and sourced. However the record has two critical errors: the headline 'sensitivity 95' is actually the LoD detection rate (not a sensitivity), and the NEJM Apr 2025 study is mischaracterized as recurrence-lead-time when it is an immunotherapy complete-response/organ-preservation finding; Medicare coverage is also overstated as COVERED.

name: FoundationOne Tracker (MRD)
qualityRationale: Core science is accurate and well-sourced: tumor-informed CGP-derived mPCR ctDNA assay, MTM/mL readout, LOD ~0.01% VAF, specificity 99.6%, all traceable to Zollinger et al. PLoS One 2024 (PMID 38753705) and consistent with the FoundationOne CDx tissue requirement. The MRD-vs-TRM split is correctly framed (MRD = Breakthrough Device/investigational; TRM = launched with Medicare coverage). The main quality problem is an internal contradiction in the coverage fields and an imprecise Medicare policy attribution, plus a slightly optimistic headline sensitivity.

defects.issue: Top-level medicareCoverage says status COVERED under LCD L38779 for this MRD record, directly contradicting coverageCrossReference.medicare (NOT_COVERED, investigational) and the record's own narrative that MRD use is investigational. L38779 is the MolDX MRD LCD; FoundationOne Tracker's actual Medicare coverage (effective June 2023) is for treatment-response monitoring of ICI therapy, not for the investigational MRD application this record audits. Mislabels an investigational use as COVERED.

Note 'Partnership with Natera - uses Signatera platform' is imprecise/misleading. FoundationOne Tracker uses Natera's mPCR technology but is a distinct branded assay designed from FoundationOne CDx CGP; it is not billed/covered as Signatera. Conflates two products.

Headline value 100 overstates; Zollinger 2024 reports >97.3% analytical sensitivity at >=5 MTM/mL with the most conservative 2-variant config; 100% holds only for qualitative PPA in precision/specific dilution conditions. Note partly clarifies but the single-number field reads as absolute clinical sensitivity.

States 'NOT yet FDA cleared' which is true for MRD, but omits that the TRM application is clinically launched and Medicare-covered, which could mislead readers scanning only this field (covered in other fields).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: This MRD record's narrative and crossReference both state MRD is investigational/NOT_COVERED. L38779 is the MRD LCD but Tracker's June-2023 Medicare coverage is specifically for ICI treatment-response monitoring (TRM), not MRD. The COVERED flag here is internally inconsistent and likely wrong for the MRD application.
Zollinger 2024 analytical sensitivity is >97.3% at >=5 MTM/mL (2-variant, conservative). 100% is not the general analytical-sensitivity figure.

Investigational MRD availability across EU/International is asserted via 'Roche global network for biopharma collaborations' with no public citation; unverifiable as a clinically available MRD offering outside the US.

proposedFills.proposedValue: FoundationOne Tracker received MolDX (Palmetto GBA) Medicare coverage effective June 17, 2023 for monitoring response to immune-checkpoint inhibitor therapy in qualifying beneficiaries with all solid tumors (TRM use). The MRD application holds FDA Breakthrough Device designation (Feb 2022) and remains investigational/not separately Medicare-covered.
>97.3% analytical sensitivity at >=5 MTM/mL with the most conservative 2-variant configuration (100% qualitative PPA in precision testing)
99.6% sample-level analytical specificity (plasma workflow)
CPT PLA code 0364U is associated with the FoundationOne Tracker personalized ctDNA monitoring service (TRM use); no clinical CPT for the investigational MRD application.

summary: FoundationOne Tracker is still offered (Foundation Medicine + Natera, tumor-informed CGP-derived mPCR ctDNA assay), with accurate, well-cited core science from Zollinger 2024. The audited MRD record is undermined by a critical internal contradiction: the top-level medicareCoverage flags it COVERED under L38779 with a 'uses Signatera platform' note, while the rest of the record correctly treats the MRD application as investigational and Medicare coverage as TRM-only.

name: Labcorp Plasma Detect
qualityRationale: Core architecture facts (tumor-informed WGS, buffy-coat germline, LOD95 0.005%/50ppm, specificity 99.4%, 14d/7d TAT, tissue required, April 2025 clinical launch via Early Experience Program for stage III colon, PROVENC3 validation) are accurate and well-sourced to Labcorp's official pages.
