Score is pulled down by a likely-fabricated 95% clinical sensitivity (Labcorp's published surveillance recurrence sensitivity is 77%, 10/13), an unsupported "2.3 months" lead-time figure (published median is 7.6 months), and mismatched publication citations (mesothelioma/head-and-neck papers cited for a colon-cancer test).

defects.issue: Record states 95% clinical sensitivity ('~95% in PROVENC3 stage III colon'), but Labcorp's published clinical sensitivity for recurrence detection in the surveillance setting is 77% (10/13). The 95% figure is unsupported by any located source and appears inflated/fabricated.

Claims 'up to 18 months earlier' and 'median 2.3 months lead time in chemotherapy monitoring study.' Labcorp's published median lead time before clinical recurrence is 7.6 months. The 2.3-month figure is unsourced and inconsistent with the official 7.6-month figure.

Cites 'Nature Medicine 2025 (mesothelioma); Clinical Cancer Research 2025 (head and neck)' as the publication basis, but the test's clinical validation is PROVENC3 in stage III colon cancer. These citations are mismatched/unverifiable for this product.

Cites Nature Medicine article s41591-024-03078-8 for the sensitivity claim; this DOI/article was not verifiable as a Labcorp Plasma Detect validation and does not support a 95% colon-cancer sensitivity.
States median '5000' SNVs tracked. Labcorp officially describes 'thousands of high-confidence tumor-specific mutations'; the specific median 5000 figure could not be confirmed on official pages.

Note says 'Labcorp acquired Invitae's oncology business' — Plasma Detect derives from the PGDx (Personal Genome Diagnostics) acquisition/technology, not Invitae. Potentially conflates two acquisitions.
Marked COVERED under L38779, but reimbursement section and cross-reference both say 'coverage emerging' with no public PLA/CPT and PARTIAL Medicare status — internally inconsistent.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Labcorp's published clinical sensitivity for recurrence in surveillance is 77% (10/13); no source supports 95%.

suspectClaims.currentValue: up to 18 months earlier; median 2.3 months lead time
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Official Labcorp figure is a median 7.6-month lead time; 2.3 months and 18 months are unsourced.

236 patients attributed to PROVENC3; published clinical sensitivity is reported as 10/13 events in surveillance, so the cohort sizing/denominators are unclear and the 236 figure is unverified against a citable source.
Vendor states 'thousands' of mutations; the precise median 5000 is not published on accessible official pages.
The two cited papers (mesothelioma; head and neck) do not correspond to the colon-cancer/PROVENC3 validation underpinning the listed indication.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Clinical sensitivity for recurrence detection was 77% (10/13) in the surveillance setting per Labcorp Plasma Detect colon cancer materials; LOD95 of 0.005% (50 ppm) with 99.4% analytical specificity.
Identifies molecular recurrence with a median lead time of 7.6 months before clinical recurrence in the surveillance setting.
Clinical launch April 23, 2025 via Early Experience Program for stage III colon cancer (research/biopharma launch April 5, 2024).
Plasma Detect Genome MRD - Baseline: 484442; Monitoring: 484551.

summary: Labcorp Plasma Detect is still offered (clinical Early Experience Program launched April 2025 for stage III colon cancer, tumor-informed WGS, LOD95 0.005%/50ppm, 99.4% specificity, 14d/7d TAT) and most architecture fields are accurate, but the record overstates clinical sensitivity as 95% (Labcorp's published surveillance recurrence sensitivity is 77%, 10/13), uses an unsupported 2.3-month lead time (official figure 7.6 months), and attaches mismatched mesothelioma/head-and-neck publication citations to a colon-cancer test.

name: Caris Assure
qualityRationale: Vendor, ownership, method (tumor-naïve WES/WTS cfDNA+cfRNA with buffy-coat CHIP subtraction), CPT 0485U, the $3,649 MolDX price, and the Nature Scientific Reports validation (real paper, s41598-025-08986-0; HR=33.4 MRD, HR=4.39 monitoring, 83.1–95.7% MCED sensitivity at 99.6% specificity, ~376K tissue + 7K paired training) are all accurate and citable. Score is held back by an unsourced/conceptually mismatched LOD ("0.05% VAF") for an AI-feature-based tumor-naïve assay, mislabeling the therapy-selection-oriented 0485U code as an MRD code, and internal tension between medicareCoverage="COVERED" and the cross-reference flagging MRD coverage as partial/overstated. The CURRENT record is largely accurate for the platform but overstates the maturity/specificity of the MRD application.

defects.issue: "0.05% VAF" cited to the Nature paper, but no public source (paper or vendor) states a VAF-based LOD. Caris Assure is a tumor-naïve AI/ML feature-based classifier, not a VAF-thresholded ctDNA assay, so a 0.05% VAF LOD is conceptually mismatched and appears fabricated/inferred.

0485U is described in CPT as a comprehensive cfDNA/cfRNA genomic profiling code (mutations, indels, CNV, fusions, MSI, TMB for solid tumors = therapy selection), not an MRD-specific code. Calling it a "tumor-naïve WES/WTS MRD assay" overstates its MRD specificity.

status="COVERED" with coveredIndications=["Liquid biopsy therapy selection"] at $3,649 conflates therapy-selection coverage with MRD. The record's own coverageCrossReference.medicare marks status PARTIAL and analysis flags the pan-solid MRD positioning as overstated — internal inconsistency for an MRD-category record.
Top-level 93.8% sensitivity derives from a small MRD validation cohort (n=86); fragile/cohort-specific figure presented as headline performance.

"Commercially available in US since late 2022" applies to the broader platform/therapy selection; the MRD application is not a separately marketed standalone MRD offering, so the date is misleading for an MRD record.
null; policyNumber given as generic "MolDX" rather than a specific LCD (cross-reference cites L38779 elsewhere — inconsistent).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No public source states a VAF-based LOD; tumor-naïve AI classifier does not report a conventional VAF detection threshold. Likely inferred/fabricated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Caris Assure PLA code ... Tumor-naïve WES/WTS MRD assay
suspectClaims.whySuspect: 0485U descriptor is comprehensive genomic profiling/therapy selection, not MRD-specific.

suspectClaims.currentValue: status COVERED, coveredIndications [Liquid biopsy therapy selection], rate $3,649
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Covered indication is therapy selection, not MRD; presenting it as MRD coverage in an MRD record overstates reimbursement for the MRD use, contradicting the record's own cross-reference (PARTIAL/overstated).

suspectClaims.currentValue: Commercially available in US since late 2022
suspectClaims.whySuspect: MRD application not separately commercially launched; date reflects platform/therapy-selection, not MRD.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Validation (Abraham et al., Scientific Reports 2025): MCED sensitivity 83.1–95.7% across stages I–IV at 99.6% specificity; MRD/recurrence HR=33.4 (p<0.005); therapeutic monitoring HR=4.39 (p=0.008). AI model (ABCDai) trained on >376,000 tissue WES/WTS profiles and >7,000 paired blood-tissue samples.

0485U is the Caris Assure PLA code (Caris MPI Inc.) for NGS cfDNA/cfRNA analysis reporting mutations, indels, CNV, fusions, MSI and TMB for solid tumors (comprehensive genomic profiling / therapy selection); it is not an MRD-specific code.
$3,649 MolDX price for Caris Assure with CHIP testing of WBCs (therapy selection indication)

AI-enabled whole exome and whole transcriptome sequencing (WES/WTS) of plasma cfDNA/cfRNA plus matched leukocyte (buffy coat) WBC DNA/RNA, analyzed by the ABCDai machine-learning classifier; tumor-naïve (no prior tissue profiling required), with leukocyte sequencing enabling CHIP subtraction.

summary: Caris Assure is still offered by Caris Life Sciences under this exact name and the record's core method and Nature Scientific Reports validation figures are accurate and well-sourced. The main quality issues are an unsourced/conceptually mismatched "0.05% VAF" LOD, mislabeling the therapy-selection CPT 0485U as an MRD code, and presenting therapy-selection Medicare coverage as MRD coverage (contradicting the record's own cross-reference).

name: Invitae PCM
qualityRationale: Core architecture (tumor-informed, tumor+normal WES design panel, AMP chemistry, 18-50 variants, FFPE+matched-normal requirement, ~80 ppm LOD) is accurate and matches vendor/literature. But the record has two critical accuracy problems: (1) sensitivity/specificity/LOD/lead-time all cite PMID 39848827 (Garcia-Murillas, Ann Oncol 2025), which is a WGS-powered ctDNA breast assay — NOT the Invitae PCM/AMP-WES assay; the citation is mismatched to the data. (2) medicareCoverage claims status COVERED under MolDX LCD L38779 (Palmetto), directly contradicting the record's own reimbursementNote and public reporting that PCM sits under Novitas, not MolDX/Palmetto. The product is also being retired/rebranded as 'Labcorp Plasma Detect ID MRD,' so the name 'Invitae PCM' is stale. Documented Blue Shield of California first commercial coverage (Mar 2023, all solid tumors) is omitted.

defects.issue: Claims Medicare 'COVERED' under MolDX LCD L38779 (Palmetto). PCM/Labcorp-Invitae is under Novitas MAC jurisdiction, NOT Palmetto/MolDX — the record's own reimbursementNote says exactly this ('Under Novitas MAC jurisdiction (not MolDX/Palmetto)'). L38779 is a Palmetto/MolDX policy and does not govern this test. Internal contradiction + factually wrong coverage claim.

All performance figures cite PMID 39848827 (Garcia-Murillas et al., Ann Oncol 2025), which describes a whole-GENOME-sequencing-powered ctDNA breast assay, not the Invitae PCM tumor-informed WES/AMP assay. The 76.9% (10/13) sensitivity figure is genuine PCM validation data (per GenomeWeb/Invitae), but it is attributed to the wrong publication. The 11.7-month/351-day lead time and 100% specificity (n=61) are from the WGS paper, not PCM.

Product is being retired/rebranded under Labcorp as 'Labcorp Plasma Detect ID MRD' (the WES tumor-informed variant); the Invitae catalog now redirects PCM to Plasma Detect ID MRD. Record name 'Invitae PCM' is stale; stillOffered is 'renamed.'

Omits the documented first commercial coverage: Blue Shield of California, effective Mar 1 2023, medically necessary for ALL solid tumors stage I-IV post-surgery. Record only lists Aetna PARTIAL, Cigna EXPERIMENTAL, UHC NOT_COVERED — missing the one clearly positive payer.

medicareRate 3878.45 'Priced' 2025-Q4 with adltStatus true is unverified against CMS gapfill/CLFS for PLA codes 0306U/0307U; combined baseline+monitoring pricing should be confirmed against the CMS Clinical Lab Fee Schedule.

Attributes TRACERx (~850 pts) as if it validates the PCM commercial assay; TRACERx is a research consortium using research-grade ctDNA methods. Co-development/branding overlap, but conflating consortium enrollment with PCM clinical validation overstates the evidence base (totalParticipants 1000).

suspectClaims.whySuspect: L38779 is a Palmetto/MolDX LCD; PCM is administered under Novitas, which does not adjudicate under MolDX. Public reporting (Precision Medicine Online/GenomeWeb) states Invitae deliberately is NOT under Palmetto. 'COVERED' is not supportable; at best coverage is 'emerging.'

This 100%/n=61 figure belongs to the Garcia-Murillas WGS breast study, not the PCM/AMP assay. PCM's own validation (76.9% sensitivity, 10/13) comes from a separate Invitae validation publication; the specificity value is mis-sourced.
11.7-month lead time is from the WGS-powered breast assay paper (PMID 39848827), not PCM. PCM/TRACERx NSCLC lead times reported are far shorter (median ~70 days).
Value is correct for PCM (10/13 relapsed patients per GenomeWeb) but the citation points to the wrong (WGS) paper, making it unverifiable as written.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Labcorp Plasma Detect ID MRD (formerly Invitae PCM)
renamed — PCM rebranded to Labcorp Plasma Detect ID MRD (WES tumor-informed variant) within the Labcorp Plasma Detect portfolio post-Aug-2024 acquisition

COVERED — Blue Shield of California, effective 2023-03-01, medically necessary for all solid tumors stage I-IV after surgical intervention for adjuvant/targeted therapy and/or relapse monitoring (first commercial coverage for PCM)
76.9% (10/13) detection of ctDNA in patients who relapsed during monitoring — Invitae PCM validation reporting (replace mismatched WGS citation PMID 39848827)
Administered under Novitas MAC jurisdiction, not Palmetto/MolDX; L38779 (a Palmetto/MolDX LCD) does not govern this test. Medicare coverage status is emerging rather than COVERED.

summary: mrd-16 'Invitae PCM' has an accurate assay architecture but two critical errors: performance metrics (sensitivity/specificity/LOD/lead-time) are cited to a WGS breast-cancer paper (PMID 39848827) that is not the PCM assay, and medicareCoverage claims 'COVERED' under MolDX L38779 (Palmetto) when PCM is under Novitas — contradicting the record's own note. The test is also being rebranded as Labcorp Plasma Detect ID MRD, and its documented first commercial coverage (Blue Shield of California, 2023) is missing.

name: CancerDetect
qualityRationale: Core clinical and analytical claims are accurate and well-sourced: BJC 2023 (PMC10338477) confirms 61.9% recurrence sensitivity, HR 8.40 (3.49-20.2), n=98 Stage II-III CRC, median 185 variants tracked; PLOS One 2025 (pone.0334282) confirms LOD 0.001% with 99.9% specificity. Vendor, ownership (IMBdx, Seoul), tumor-informed hybrid method, and CancerDetect/AlphaLiquid Detect naming are all correct and current. Score is held down by a critical citation mismatch: the LOD and LOD95 fields cite pone.0302129, which is the Foundation Medicine FoundationOne Tracker analytical validation — wrong assay entirely. A few unverifiable specifics (0.0005% VAF, LOD95 0.001%, 58 mutations/15 genes hotspot counts) and stale TAT/cohort claims.

defects.issue: Both cite https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0302129, which is the Foundation Medicine 'FoundationOne Tracker' analytical validation paper (Zollinger et al.), NOT IMBdx CancerDetect. The LOD/LOD95 numbers are backed by a citation to a competitor's assay. The correct analytical validation source for CancerDetect is pone.0334282.

Reports LOD ~0.0005% VAF (5 ppm) and limit of blank 0.0003% for bespoke regions. The published analytical validation (pone.0334282) headlines LOD 0.001% (10 ppm). The sub-0.001% / 5 ppm figures are not corroborated by a citable source and may be internal/conference values.

vendorNCCNAlignmentCitation points to https://www.cancerdetect.com/ and clinicalTrials notes reference cancerdetect.com; the live vendor page is imbdx.com/eng/about/detect.php. cancerdetect.com may not resolve to a verifiable IMBdx page — URL should be confirmed or replaced.

Claims tumor-agnostic hotspot panel of '58 clinically actionable mutations across 15 genes' — specific counts not confirmed against vendor/peer-reviewed source in this audit; verify against pone.0334282.
Lists only 'South Korea'. Vendor states AlphaLiquid services expanded to 20 countries / 33 hospitals and CancerDetect 'launched in Asia'; region availability may understate current footprint.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: The cited analytical validation (pone.0334282) and vendor materials state LOD of 0.001% (10 ppm). The 0.0005% (5 ppm) figure for bespoke regions is not corroborated by the peer-reviewed source and its citation actually points to a different company's paper (pone.0302129, FoundationOne Tracker).
Exact hotspot panel gene/mutation counts not verified against a public IMBdx source in this audit.
TAT note says 'reduced from previous 8-10 weeks'; current 28-day figure not confirmed against a dated vendor source, may be stale.

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0334282
proposedFills.source: Analytical validation of a hybrid-approach combining tumor-informed and tumor-agnostic bespoke ctDNA panel assay (PLOS One 2025, PMC12599964)

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0334282
proposedFills.source: Analytical validation of a hybrid-approach combining tumor-informed and tumor-agnostic bespoke ctDNA panel assay (PLOS One 2025, PMC12599964)
PLOS One 2025 analytical validation (pone.0334282): LOD reached down to 0.001% (10^-5) with 99.9% specificity

proposedFills.proposedValue: Clinical sensitivity 61.9% for predicting recurrence within 3 years at postoperative 3-week timepoint; 3-year DFS 32.2% (MRD+) vs 88.0% (MRD-), p<0.001; HR 8.40 (95% CI 3.49-20.2). n=98 Stage II-III CRC, median follow-up 36.3 months.
proposedFills.source: Hwang et al., British Journal of Cancer 2023 (PMC10338477)

proposedFills.proposedValue: Median 185 variants per patient (range 116-300) used for personalized panel design; median 66 somatic mutations (range 6-279) used for MRD monitoring.
proposedFills.source: Hwang et al., British Journal of Cancer 2023 (PMC10338477)

summary: CancerDetect (formerly AlphaLiquid Detect) by IMBdx is a currently-offered, Korean tumor-informed hybrid ctDNA MRD test whose record is largely accurate and well-sourced (61.9% recurrence sensitivity, HR 8.40, 0.001% LOD, 99.9% specificity all confirmed). The chief flaw is a critical citation error: the LOD/LOD95 fields cite a Foundation Medicine FoundationOne Tracker paper (pone.0302129) instead of IMBdx's own analytical validation (pone.0334282).

name: Signatera (IO Monitoring)
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and verifiable: Signatera is still offered by Natera as a tumor-informed ctDNA assay with a dedicated IO/immunotherapy response-monitoring indication, holds final MolDX/Medicare LCD coverage for pan-cancer ICI monitoring (L38779), and the supporting validation (INSPIRE/Bratman Nature Cancer 2020) and IMvigor011 trial are real. Score held below 80 because (1) the method/LOD fields describe the legacy 16-variant amplicon assay while the reimbursementNote simultaneously references the newer WGS-based "Signatera Genome" (~1,800 variants, ~1 ppm LOD) — an internal inconsistency; (2) the IMvigor011 entry is stale, presenting the Phase III as still enrolling though positive OS/DFS topline results were reported Aug–Oct 2025; (3) no specific peer-reviewed citation is attached to the IO validation claims, and (4) cash-pay figures are inconsistent across fields.

defects.issue: IMvigor011 (NCT04660344) is presented as ongoing/enrolling, but positive Phase III topline results were announced Aug 2025 and presented at ESMO Oct 2025 (Tecentriq cut risk of death by ~41%, DFS by ~36% in ctDNA-positive MIBC). Record omits the readout, making it stale.

Method describes only the legacy personalized amplicon NGS panel tracking 16 variants, but the reimbursementNote and current Natera offering reference 'Signatera Genome' (WGS-based, ~1,800 variants, ~1 ppm LOD). Method and lod fields are inconsistent with the Genome assay cited elsewhere in the same record.
States ~0.01% VAF (legacy amplicon). Natera now markets Signatera Genome detection down to ~1 ppm (~0.0001%); the LOD reflects the older chemistry without flagging which assay version applies.
Cash-pay figures conflict: vendorClaims cashPay = $4,150 while financialAssistance lists prompt-pay $349 and Medicare rate $3,500. Unreconciled within the record.
totalParticipants=1160 appears to sum trial targets (IMvigor011 ~760 + MODERN ~400) rather than enrolled IO-validation cohort; the figure is an estimate not tied to a validation publication.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Personalized amplicon-based NGS panels targeting 16+ patient-specific variants identified from tumor/normal sequencing
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Reflects the legacy mPCR Signatera; Natera's current/lead assay (Signatera Genome) is WGS-based tracking up to ~1,800 variants. The record mixes both generations across fields.

suspectClaims.currentValue: NCT04660344 IMvigor011 Phase III bladder cancer (760)... (described as ongoing)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Trial read out positively in 2025; describing it as in-progress is outdated.
Natera publicly claims Signatera Genome sensitivity to ~1 ppm (~0.0001% VAF), two orders of magnitude lower than the listed value; the LOD likely belongs to the superseded amplicon assay.

proposedFills.proposedValue: IMvigor011 (NCT04660344) Phase III MIBC reported positive topline Aug 2025 / ESMO Oct 2025: in ctDNA-positive patients adjuvant atezolizumab reduced risk of death by ~41% (OS) and recurrence/death by ~36% (DFS) vs placebo. MODERN (NCT05987241) ctDNA-guided bladder trial ongoing.

Bratman SV, Yang SYC, Iafolla MAJ, et al. Personalized circulating tumor DNA analysis as a predictive biomarker in solid tumor patients treated with pembrolizumab (INSPIRE study). Nature Cancer 2020 — early ctDNA kinetics (baseline to cycle 3, ~6 weeks) predicted immunotherapy benefit; ctDNA detected pre-treatment in 98% (92/94).

Tumor-informed ctDNA. Current Signatera Genome variant uses whole-genome sequencing of tumor/matched-normal to design personalized panels tracking up to ~1,800 somatic variants (legacy mPCR version tracked 16); plasma cfDNA deep-sequenced for serial response monitoring.
Signatera Genome validated to detect ctDNA down to ~1 part per million (1 ppm, ~0.0001% VAF); legacy mPCR assay LOD ~0.01% VAF.

summary: Accurate, still-offered Natera Signatera IO-monitoring record with valid Medicare/MolDX pan-cancer ICI coverage and real validation (INSPIRE/Nature Cancer); main weaknesses are a stale IMvigor011 entry (positive 2025 readout omitted) and internal inconsistency between the legacy 16-variant amplicon method/LOD fields and the newer WGS-based Signatera Genome referenced elsewhere in the same record.

name: Guardant360 Response
qualityRationale: Core analytical facts (tumor-agnostic Guardant360 hybrid-capture NGS, ≥50% VAF molecular-response definition, ~8-week lead time, 40+ studies, 2023 MolDX/Medicare coverage for ICI-treated metastatic solid tumors with baseline+4–10wk follow-up) are accurate and well-sourced. But the record is undermined by a wrong clinical-trial attribution (SERENA-6 used Guardant360 CDx, not Response), self-contradictory and unverified discontinuation claims (asserts DISCONTINUED / "sun-setted December 2025" while discontinuedDate says December 2024, yet the vendor HCP page is still live and GTR lists the test as Active), and a sampleCitations URL that points to the wrong product (Guardant Reveal). Currency and internal consistency are poor even though the underlying clinical data are mostly right.

defects.issue: Attributes SERENA-6 (n claimed 866) to Guardant360 Response. SERENA-6 used the Guardant360 CDx companion diagnostic, not Guardant360 Response, and published figures are ~3,256 screened / 315 randomized — 866 matches neither. Misleading clinical-evidence attribution.

Asserts 'DISCONTINUED - CLIA LDT no longer available' but no public source confirms formal discontinuation. The Guardant360 Response HCP page is still live and NIH GTR lists the test as Active (approved). The Nov 2025 news is that Reveal was EXPANDED to late-stage TRM, not that Response was retired. Status overstated as fact.
Internal contradiction: discontinuedDate='December 2024' but reimbursementNote says 'sun-setted December 2025.' Both are unsourced; cannot both be true.
Citation URL is the Guardant Reveal product page (guardantcomplete.com/.../guardant-reveal), not the Guardant360 Response page. Wrong-product source for a sample-handling field.
'$3,500' is asserted with no source; the 2023 Medicare coverage press release specifies no rate or CPT. Unverifiable.
LOD cited to Lanman 2015 (PLoS One) describes the original Guardant360 73-gene LDT analytical validation, not a Response-specific LOD; reasonable proxy but loosely attributed.

suspectClaims.currentValue: true / 'DISCONTINUED - CLIA LDT no longer available'
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor HCP page still live and NIH GTR (test 593444) lists the test as Active (approved) as of last review; no Guardant press release confirms retirement. Discontinuation is asserted, not evidenced.
Tied to SERENA-6, which is a Guardant360 CDx trial; published SERENA-6 enrollment is ~3,256 screened / 315 randomized. 866 is unexplained and likely wrong.
No public MolDX/CMS source cited; the coverage announcement gives no dollar figure.
Contradicts the 'sun-setted December 2025' note in the same record; neither date is sourced.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Clinical validity supported by 40+ studies using the Guardant360 platform to measure baseline and on-treatment ctDNA change across NSCLC, CRC, breast, gastric and other tumors. (Note: SERENA-6 is a Guardant360 CDx trial and should not be attributed to Guardant360 Response.)
Molecular response defined as a >=50% decrease in mean variant allele fraction (ctDNA) from baseline to on-treatment timepoint

Fee-for-service Medicare patients with metastatic/inoperable solid tumors on immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy; baseline test (Guardant360 CDx/LDT) before immunotherapy plus follow-up Guardant360 Response 4–10 weeks after therapy initiation (MolDX/Palmetto GBA, 2023)

As of Nov 10, 2025 Guardant expanded Guardant Reveal (tissue-free, methylation-based, 7-day TAT) to include late-stage therapy response monitoring, overlapping Guardant360 Response's use case; Response HCP page and NIH GTR listing (test 593444) remained active, so any retirement is not publicly confirmed.
https://www.guardantcomplete.com/hcp/solutions/guardant360-response/

summary: Guardant360 Response is a tumor-agnostic Guardant360 hybrid-capture NGS test that tracks ctDNA VAF change (≥50% decrease = molecular response) with 2023 MolDX/Medicare coverage; the record's clinical facts are largely accurate but it wrongly attributes SERENA-6 (a Guardant360 CDx trial), asserts an unconfirmed and self-contradictory discontinuation, and cites the wrong product page. Status is best marked unknown: the test still appears live (vendor page + active GTR listing) even though Guardant expanded Reveal to cover the same late-stage TRM use case in Nov 2025.

name: NeXT Personal
qualityRationale: Core technical facts are accurate and well-sourced: WGS tumor-informed, ~1,800 variants, LOD95 3.45 ppm / 1.67 ppm threshold all match the published Oncotarget analytical validation. Tempus xM co-commercialization and Medicare coverage (breast Nov 2025, NSCLC Feb 10 2026) are correct. Score is held down by (a) a near-certain duplicate of mrd-2 ('NeXT Personal Dx', same vendor/method/LOD), (b) an internal inconsistency in the MolDX LCD number (L38779 vs L38822), and (c) stale coverage missing the May 19 2026 4th decision for neoadjuvant TNBC/HER2+ treatment-response monitoring — which is precisely this record's TRM use case.

defects.issue: Duplicate of mrd-2 'NeXT Personal Dx' (same Personalis test, same WGS tumor-informed method, same ~1,800 variants, same Tempus xM co-brand). trm-3 is a treatment-response-monitoring variant of an already-listed MRD test; the two records overlap heavily and risk presenting one assay as two.

Inconsistent MolDX LCD reference: medicareCoverage cites L38779 while coverageCrossReference cites L38822. Both are real MolDX MRD LCDs but under different MACs (L38779 Palmetto, L38822 Noridian); the record should commit to the contractor-correct LCD rather than list both inconsistently.

Stale: missing the 4th Medicare coverage decision (CMS MolDX, May 19 2026) extending coverage to monitoring response to neoadjuvant therapy in Stage II-III TNBC and HER2+ breast cancer — directly the treatment-response-monitoring indication this TRM record is meant to capture. lastVerified 2026-04-08 predates it.
CPT code is null; a PLA/CPT code likely exists for the assay and is unfilled.
coverageCrossReference lastVerified (2026-01-15) is older than medicareCoverage lastVerified (2026-04-08); private-payer statuses may be stale.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: 493 corresponds to the analytical-validation sample count cited for the sibling mrd-2 record (sensitivity n=493), not the enrollment of the two clinical trials listed (B-STRONGER 422 + VICTORI ~71). The number appears carried over and mislabeled as trial participants.
Conflicts with L38822 used elsewhere in the same record; only one MolDX MRD LCD applies for the relevant MAC and the record does not resolve which.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Add: Monitoring response to neoadjuvant therapy in Stage II-III TNBC and HER2+ breast cancer (CMS MolDX coverage decision, May 19, 2026)
3.45 ppm LOD95 (detection threshold 1.67 ppm); linearity 0.8-300,000 ppm

Tempus is the exclusive commercial diagnostic partner for NeXT Personal Dx (marketed as Tempus xM) in breast and lung cancer and solid-tumor immunotherapy monitoring; clinical launch announced via Tempus MRD portfolio.

summary: trm-3 'NeXT Personal' is the treatment-response-monitoring framing of Personalis's tumor-informed WGS MRD assay (Tempus xM / NeXT Personal Dx), still offered; its technical facts (LOD, 1,800 variants, method) are accurate but it is a near-duplicate of mrd-2, carries an inconsistent MolDX LCD number, and misses the May 2026 4th Medicare coverage decision that maps directly to its TRM indication.

name: FoundationOne Monitor
qualityRationale: Core identity, vendor, tumor-naive approach, 324-gene F1LCDx backbone, ctDNA-tumor-fraction biomarker, and the IMbassador250/Sweeney CCR 2024 citation are all accurate and verifiable against vendor and peer-reviewed sources. But two material accuracy problems drag the score down: (1) the record asserts the test is an available CLINICAL LDT (isClinicalLDT:true, isInvestigational:false, "now available for clinical use"), whereas every current Foundation Medicine source states it is for INVESTIGATIONAL/RESEARCH use only, "currently in development for clinical use"; and (2) the LOD value "≥5 mean tumor molecules/mL (MTM/mL)" uses a Guardant-style unit that Foundation Medicine does not report for this %-tumor-fraction assay, is internally inconsistent with the method field, and is mis-cited to the F1 Liquid CDx analytical paper (Woodhouse 2020) which does not establish that threshold for Monitor.

defects.issue: Record states the test is an available clinical LDT (isClinicalLDT:true, isInvestigational:false, 'now available for clinical use'). Foundation Medicine's own pages and press releases state FoundationOne Monitor is for investigational/research use only and 'currently in development for clinical use.' Misleading regulatory/availability status.

LOD '≥5 mean tumor molecules/mL (MTM/mL)' uses Guardant-style MTM/mL units, not Foundation Medicine's. The assay reports ctDNA tumor fraction (%), making the LOD unit internally inconsistent with the method field. Cited to Woodhouse 2020 (PMID 32976510), the F1 Liquid CDx analytical-validation paper, which does not establish a 5 MTM/mL LOD for Monitor. Likely fabricated/mis-transcribed.
Lists only 'Advanced solid tumors'; vendor validation specifically cites mCRPC, NSCLC, HR+/HER2- breast, TNBC, and pan-solid immunotherapy settings. Thin/vague.

Describes status as 'Clinical LDT – built on FDA-approved FoundationOne Liquid CDx platform; not separately FDA approved.' More accurate per vendor: clinical-trial / investigational-use assay, not a clinically available LDT.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: MTM/mL is a Guardant unit; Foundation Medicine reports ctDNA tumor fraction as a percentage. Unit mismatches the method field and the cited Woodhouse 2020 F1 Liquid CDx paper does not define a 5 MTM/mL LOD for Monitor.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Available as clinical LDT in US and internationally via Roche... now available for clinical use.
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Current Foundation Medicine sources (monitoring-portfolio page, launch press release, ESMO 2025) describe it as investigational/research use only, 'in development for clinical use.'
Vendor materials label the assay 'for investigational use only' / 'clinical trial assay.'
Broad clinical-availability framing conflicts with investigational-use-only status; regional clinical availability unverifiable.

proposedFills.proposedValue: true
Reports ctDNA tumor fraction (%) using a multi-omic method (VAF, aneuploidy, fragmentomics) with CH/germline filtering; vendor does not publish a single numeric LOD threshold for Monitor.
Available for investigational/research use (biopharma and clinical trials); in development for clinical use as of 2025-2026.
Validation across mCRPC, advanced NSCLC, HR+/HER2- breast, triple-negative breast cancer, and pan-solid immunotherapy settings
June 2, 2023 (monitoring portfolio expansion launch)

summary: FoundationOne Monitor is a real, still-offered Foundation Medicine tumor-naive ctDNA-tumor-fraction assay built on the 324-gene F1 Liquid CDx backbone, but the record wrongly presents it as an available clinical LDT (it is investigational/research-use-only, in development for clinical use) and carries a fabricated/mismatched LOD in Guardant-style MTM/mL units mis-cited to the F1 Liquid CDx validation paper. Verified facts (mCRPC IMbassador250, Sweeney CCR 2024) are accurate; the availability and LOD claims are the key defects.

name: Northstar Response
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and well-sourced against the vendor's official page and the peer-reviewed Sci Rep 2025 validation (tumor-naive QCT methylation, >2200 loci v2, 0.01% LOD, 0.25% discrimination, CPT 0486U all confirmed). Correctly distinguishes that Medicare coverage went to sibling Northstar Select, not Response. Weaknesses are unverifiable/ambiguous secondary fields (ADLT designation, 2025-Q4 effective date, cryptic BCBSM note) and mild scope overstatement — not factual errors, so it sits in the low-80s.

defects.issue: Stated as 'Pan-cancer: all advanced solid tumors' but analytical/clinical validation covers 12 tumor types (validated specifically in lung, colorectal, pancreatic, GI). 'All advanced solid tumors' overstates validated scope; should be framed as pan-cancer panel validated across 12 tissue types.

Note 'Northstar Response PLA code (BCBSM EI status Jan 2025)' is cryptic and conflates BillionToOne with BCBSM (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan?) without explanation. adltStatus:true could not be independently confirmed for Northstar Response specifically; 0486U is documented as gapfill/MAC-priced for 2025 but ADLT designation is unverified.
'2025-Q4' effective date is not clearly sourced; CPT 0486U was effective in 2025 as a gapfill code but the specific quarter and what it refers to (code effective vs pricing) is ambiguous.

lod='0.01% tumor fraction' (vendor headline claim) while lodNotes cites '0.25%' discrimination from the peer-reviewed paper; both are real but the 0.01% is a vendor marketing claim and 0.25% is the validated discrimination threshold — the distinction is not flagged.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Could not confirm that Northstar Response (0486U) holds CMS ADLT designation. 0486U is documented only as a gapfill/MAC-priced PLA code for 2025; ADLT status requires a specific CMS application/approval not found in public sources.

Described as 'initial validation cohort of 100 advanced GI cancer patients' but peer-reviewed Sci Rep validation used 51 patients/60 samples (lung) plus small longitudinal cohorts, and JCO GI abstracts cite n=73+. The round '100' figure is not clearly tied to a single citable cohort.
Citable peer-reviewed full papers appear to be 2 (Sci Rep 2025; Clin Lung Cancer 2025); the remaining are ASCO/JCO abstracts. '5+' counts abstracts as publications without distinguishing.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Tumor-naive single-molecule NGS using patented Quantitative Counting Template (QCT) technology; v2 panel measures >2200 cancer-specific methylated genomic loci (expanded from the ~100 CpG islands / avg 90 loci in the originally published v1), enabling absolute quantification of methylated ctDNA molecules with single-molecule precision.
0.01% tumor fraction (vendor claim); validated to discriminate 0.25% absolute tumor-fraction changes (AUC>0.94) and <10% CV at 1% tumor fraction

In advanced NSCLC, Tumor Methylation Score changes measured 4-10 weeks after anti-PD1 immunotherapy significantly predicted real-world progression-free survival (P<0.0001), outperforming standard imaging assessment which did not reach significance.

0486U — PLA code for Northstar Response (BillionToOne Laboratory); NGS methylation analysis of ctDNA from solid tumors quantifying methylation as a correlate of tumor fraction. Gapfill/MAC-priced on the 2025 CLFS.
Not covered as of mid-2026; self-pay. Note: Medicare (Palmetto/MolDX) coverage effective Feb 14, 2025 applies to the sibling therapy-selection test Northstar Select, NOT Northstar Response.

summary: Northstar Response is a still-offered, commercially available BillionToOne tumor-naive methylation ctDNA treatment-response monitoring test (CPT 0486U), and the record is largely accurate and well-sourced (>2200 loci v2, QCT, 0.01% LOD, self-pay/pending Medicare all confirmed against vendor and Sci Rep 2025). Defects are minor: overstated 'all solid tumors' scope, an unverified ADLT flag, a cryptic BCBSM coverage note, and an unsourced '2025-Q4' effective date.

name: RaDaR
qualityRationale: The core narrative is correct: the original RaDaR (v1.0) was permanently enjoined and removed from the US market in the Natera patent litigation, and the successor RaDaR ST (mrd-5) is now offered. But the record has a wrong discontinuation date, an internal contradiction (declares DISCONTINUED while carrying an active COVERED Medicare block, live commercial payer, and active trials), broken/non-specific citations, and mixes successor (RaDaR ST) trial data into the retired record. The factual spine is right but the field-level accuracy and currency are poor.

defects.issue: Record says RaDaR was discontinued in 'February 2026' due to legal proceedings. The original RaDaR (v1.0) was preliminarily enjoined Dec 2023 and PERMANENTLY enjoined / removed from the US market on Sept 23, 2024. February 2026 is actually when the SUCCESSOR RaDaR ST launched (Feb 25, 2026), not when RaDaR was withdrawn. The date is conflated and wrong.

Internal contradiction: top of record sets isDiscontinued=true, fdaStatus='DISCONTINUED', reimbursement='N/A - Discontinued', and coverageCrossReference.medicare.status='DISCONTINUED', yet the medicareCoverage block still asserts status='COVERED' with active LCD L38779 and covered indications. A discontinued product cannot simultaneously be actively COVERED. Misleading.

A retired RaDaR 1.0 record lists the RaDaR ST bridging study (ISLB 2025, 166 pts, 97% concordance RaDaR ST vs RaDaR 1.0) plus a grab-bag of trials totaling totalParticipants=735. Successor-test (RaDaR ST) validation data is attributed to the discontinued predecessor; the 735 total is unverifiable and conflates the two products.

Claims 'Blue Shield of California covers RaDaR' for a discontinued product, cited only to the DeciBio homepage (https://www.decibio.com/), a non-specific, non-authoritative source. Coverage of a withdrawn assay is unverifiable and contradicts the discontinued status.

tatCitations URL is malformed: 'https://neogenomics.com/test-menu/radarr' (typo, double r — dead link). sampleCitations and lodCitations are truncated/placeholder ('.../literature/S', 'NeoGenomics RaDaR product specifications').

method says 'amplicon-based NGS panels'; NeoGenomics describes RaDaR/RaDaR ST as WES-derived deep-NGS personalized panels. LOD '0.001% VAF (~10 ppm)' is roughly consistent with the published RaDaR ST LOD95 of 11 ppm but predecessor v1.0 historically claimed deeper detection; value is approximate.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Permanent injunction removing RaDaR v1.0 from the US market was Sept 23, 2024 (preliminary Dec 2023). Feb 2026 is the RaDaR ST launch date, not the discontinuation date.
Contradicts isDiscontinued=true and coverageCrossReference.medicare.status=DISCONTINUED elsewhere in the same record.
Sum of a heterogeneous trial list that includes successor (RaDaR ST) studies attributed to the retired RaDaR 1.0; not a single verifiable cohort.
Cited only to decibio.com homepage; asserts active commercial coverage for a product that is discontinued/enjoined.
Record's own lodNotes concedes earlier 10^-5–10^-6 claims were unsupported; current 0.001%/10 ppm figure aligns with RaDaR ST (11 ppm LOD95), not necessarily the discontinued v1.0.

proposedFills.proposedValue: September 2024

RaDaR v1.0 was removed from the US market following a permanent injunction (entered Sept 23, 2024; preliminary injunction Dec 2023) in Natera's patent-infringement suit. The successor RaDaR ST launched Feb 25, 2026. Natera later (Dec 2025) voluntarily withdrew its appeal after the court invalidated the asserted patent claims against the modified assay.
RaDaR ST (NeoGenomics), launched Feb 25, 2026; tumor-informed WES-derived personalized panel tracking up to 48 variants; LOD95 of 11 ppm; Medicare-approved for initial indications.

December 2025: Natera voluntarily dismissed its appeal and NeoGenomics dismissed its cross-appeal, leaving in place the district court's August 2025 summary-judgment ruling that Natera's asserted patent claims against RaDaR v1.1 are invalid.

summary: trm-5 documents the original RaDaR, correctly flagged as discontinued/renamed to successor RaDaR ST (mrd-5), but the discontinuation date is wrong (Sept 2024 injunction, not 'February 2026' which was the RaDaR ST launch), and the record contradicts itself by keeping an active 'COVERED' Medicare block plus live payer/trial data on a withdrawn product. Citations are broken or non-specific.

name: BD OneFlow B-ALL MRD Kit
qualityRationale: The underlying product (EuroFlow-standardized BD OneFlow BCP-ALL flow cytometry MRD reagents on FACSLyric) is real and the high-level framing (tumor-naive, CE-IVD, EuroFlow, flow modality, same-day TAT) is accurate. But the record carries a non-canonical product name, a vendor URL that 403s and does not match BD's real product page, fabricated-looking 96%/95% sensitivity/specificity figures absent from any cited source, an understated LOD, and entirely speculative payer-coverage claims (Medicare + 3 commercial payers all "COVERED" with null policies and "generally covered" notes). Round, unsourced totalParticipants (10000) and numPublications (100+) further erode evidence backing.

defects.issue: No BD product is literally named 'BD OneFlow B-ALL MRD Kit.' The actual product is 'BD OneFlow BCP-ALL' (8-color Tube 1 + Tube 2 MRD panels). Name is non-canonical/invented.

96% sensitivity and 95% specificity 'vs final clinical diagnosis' do not appear in the cited BD page or EuroFlow validation. EuroFlow (Theunissen, Blood 2017;129:347) reports concordance (~93% with >4M cells) and >98% applicability, not these assay-level numbers. Appears fabricated.

Cited vendor URL https://www.bd.com/en-us/products-and-solutions/products/product-families/bd-oneflow-b-all-mrd-kit returns HTTP 403 and is not BD's real product page (real product lives on bdbiosciences.com). URL likely does not correspond to a real page for this product.
Stated LOD '10^-4 to 10^-5' understates the published EuroFlow performance of <=10^-5 (down to 2.5x10^-6) when >=4 million cells are acquired.

Aetna, Cigna, United and Medicare all marked 'COVERED' with null policy and null policyUrl, justified only by generic 'flow cytometry MRD generally covered.' No verifiable policy citation; speculative.
Describes a single '8-color standardized panel'; the validated BCP-ALL MRD assay uses two 8-color tubes (Tube 1 + Tube 2).
Round unsourced values (10000 participants, 100+ publications) with no citation.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Not found in any cited or located source; EuroFlow validation reports concordance/applicability metrics, not a 96% sensitivity vs clinical diagnosis.
No source reports 95% specificity 'vs final clinical diagnosis' for this assay; appears invented.
URL 403s and does not match BD's real product page (bdbiosciences.com OneFlow BCP-ALL); likely non-existent page.
Published EuroFlow data establish <=10^-5 (to 2.5x10^-6) with >=4M cells; record understates achievable LOD.
Round, unsourced; EuroFlow design used 319 BCP-ALL patients across 5 phases — no basis for 10000.
Round, unsourced numPublicationsPlus claim.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Coverage as flow cytometry MRD / COVERED
suspectClaims.whySuspect: This is an EU CE-IVDR reagent kit; no cited US Medicare policy. 'Covered as flow cytometry MRD' is a generic assumption, not a verified coverage determination.

proposedFills.proposedValue: BD OneFlow BCP-ALL (MRD)
EuroFlow-standardized 8-color next-generation flow cytometry; two-tube BCP-ALL MRD panel (Tube 1 + Tube 2) on BD FACSLyric/FACSCanto II
<=10^-5 (quantitative range), down to ~2.5x10^-6 with >=4 million cells acquired
CE-IVD (IVD Directive 98/79/EC / IVDR); not FDA-cleared. BD OneFlow BCP-ALL T1 CE-certified by BSI Netherlands (NB 2797)

Theunissen P et al. Standardized flow cytometry for highly sensitive MRD measurements in B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Blood. 2017;129(3):347-357 (319 BCP-ALL patients; applicable in >98%; sensitivity <=10^-5)

summary: Record describes the real EuroFlow-standardized BD OneFlow BCP-ALL flow cytometry MRD assay but under a non-canonical name with a dead/fabricated vendor URL, invented-looking 96%/95% performance figures, an understated LOD, and speculative payer-coverage claims. Core framing (tumor-naive flow, CE-IVD, same-day TAT) is correct; the quantitative and citation backing is largely unverifiable and needs correction.

name: Liquid Trace (Therapy Monitoring)
qualityRationale: Vendor, test name, method (combined cfDNA+cfRNA), tumor-naïve approach, and LOD values are accurate and verified against GTC's official Liquid Trace Solid Tumor page (LOD "0.1 to 0.01 non-hotspot, 0.01 to 0.001 hotspot, <0.001 tumor-informed/prior Hx" matches verbatim). The record is undermined by an unverifiable Medicare coverage representation: it attributes MolDX MRD LCD L38779 to "treatment response monitoring for solid tumors AND hematologic malignancies," but GTC's only publicly documented Medicare coverage (Palmetto GBA) is for its CGP heme DNA profile used for diagnosis/prognosis/therapy selection — not MRD/treatment-response monitoring, and not solid tumors. Performance fields (sensitivity, specificity) are null and the "15+ publications" claim cites only a generic publications landing page.

defects.issue: Attributes MolDX MRD LCD L38779 to Liquid Trace for 'treatment response monitoring for solid tumors and hematologic malignancies.' No public evidence GTC holds L38779 MRD/monitoring coverage. GTC's documented Palmetto GBA coverage is for its heme CGP DNA profile (diagnosis/prognosis/therapy selection), not MRD monitoring and not solid tumors.

Flat 'Medicare covered' for a pan-cancer solid+heme therapy-monitoring use overstates scope. Verifiable Medicare coverage is limited to heme liquid biopsy CGP (diagnostic), per GTC's own press release.

Both null with no quantitative analytical/clinical performance data; sensitivityNotes are qualitative marketing claims ('cfRNA detects mutations missed by cfDNA') without a citation to validation data.
'15+' publications cited only to a generic /category/publications/ landing page, not an enumerable list specific to Liquid Trace; unverifiable as stated.
Single free-text bucket 'Pan-cancer: solid tumors and hematologic malignancies' is vague for a monitoring test; no per-indication validation distinction.

suspectClaims.currentValue: L38779 (MolDX MRD LCD) covering treatment response monitoring for solid tumors and hematologic malignancies
suspectClaims.whySuspect: L38779 is the MolDX Minimal Residual Disease LCD; no public source ties GTC Liquid Trace to it. GTC's only documented Medicare coverage (Palmetto GBA) is CGP heme liquid biopsy for diagnosis, not MRD monitoring, and excludes solid tumors.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 'Unique coverage for both solid and heme malignancies'
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Vendor press release documents heme-only liquid biopsy coverage; solid-tumor Medicare monitoring coverage is not substantiated.

suspectClaims.currentValue: 'Only commercially available therapy monitoring test combining cfDNA and cfRNA ... LEADING PROVIDER FOR CSF LIQUID BIOPSY'
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Superlative 'only'/'leading provider' claims are vendor-supplied marketing language with no independent source; unverifiable.
Cited only to a generic publications category page; not independently enumerable for this specific test.

proposedFills.proposedValue: GTC's publicly documented Medicare coverage is via Palmetto GBA for its Hematology liquid biopsy CGP (177-gene DNA profile on cfDNA), used for diagnosis/prognosis/therapy selection in hematologic neoplasms. No public documentation supports Medicare MRD/treatment-response-monitoring coverage for solid tumors. Confirm current monitoring coverage directly with GTC.
0.1-0.01% (non-hotspot); 0.01-0.001% (hotspot); <0.001% (tumor-informed / prior history)

Combined cfRNA + cfDNA NGS liquid biopsy (peripheral blood or CSF). cfRNA transcriptome enables detection of additional mutations/fusions missed by cfDNA alone and supports immunoprofiling (e.g., CD19/CD20/CD33/CD4/CD8) and tumor-marker transcripts; serial VAF used to monitor tumor load (VAF reduction post-treatment indicates response).

summary: Liquid Trace by GTC is still offered and the record's method and LOD figures match the vendor's official page, but its central Medicare coverage claim (MolDX MRD LCD L38779 for solid+heme treatment-response monitoring) is unverifiable and likely wrong — GTC's only documented Medicare coverage is Palmetto GBA CGP coverage for its heme liquid biopsy (diagnostic, heme-only). Performance fields are null and the publication claim is thinly sourced.

name: Bladder EpiCheck
qualityRationale: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced: FDA 510(k) K203245 (May 2023) confirmed; the US pivotal study metrics (sensitivity 67%, specificity 84%, PPV 65%, NPV 85%, high-grade sensitivity 77% / NPV 95%, n=449) all match the Fleshner et al. 2024 publication cited; 15-marker urine qPCR methylation method confirmed against the FDA decision summary; test confirmed still actively offered (AUA 2025, EAU25, QIAGEN Rotor-Gene Q platform). Deductions are for thin/empty operational fields (CPT codes, TAT both unpopulated) and a scoping note that omits the expanded EU primary-detection/UTUC indication.

defects.issue: Record says 'PCR-based 15-marker DNA methylation urine test'; the FDA decision summary and vendor specify qPCR (real-time PCR) specifically. Cosmetic precision issue, not wrong.
Empty. No CPT/PLA code populated; a proprietary or stacked molecular code likely applies but none verified.
Both null; only free-text 'same-day testing available' noted. Vendor does not publish hard TAT, so unverifiable rather than wrong.

Frames test purely as NMIBC recurrence surveillance. Correct for the US 510(k) label, but omits that the EU CE-mark label was expanded to include primary bladder cancer and upper tract urothelial carcinoma detection in hematuria patients.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Value 21 is asserted but the citation (Urology Times) and the accompanying note reference '7 unique clinical studies' from a 2023 review. The 21 figure is not directly supported by the cited source and is unverifiable.

suspectClaims.currentValue: CE-marked in Europe since 2016; US coverage landscape developing; Netherlands reimbursement achieved 2023
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Netherlands 2023 reimbursement claim and 'CE-marked since 2016' are plausible but not backed by a citation in the record; should be sourced.

proposedFills.proposedValue: qPCR-based 15-marker DNA methylation assay on voided urine (real-time PCR)

US 510(k) cleared for NMIBC recurrence monitoring in conjunction with cystoscopy; EU CE-mark label additionally covers primary bladder cancer and upper tract urothelial carcinoma detection in patients with hematuria/urinary symptoms.
Fleshner et al., North American pivotal study and meta-analysis, Technology in Cancer Research & Treatment 2024 (n=449)

summary: Bladder EpiCheck (Nucleix) is an FDA 510(k)-cleared (K203245, 2023) 15-marker urine qPCR methylation test for NMIBC recurrence surveillance; the record's regulatory status and pivotal-study performance metrics are accurate and properly cited, and the test is still actively offered. Defects are minor: empty CPT/TAT fields, an unverified numPublications count, and a scope note that omits the broader EU label.

name: EasyM
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-sourced against vendor pages and peer-reviewed literature. Method, tumor-naive approach, clonotypic-peptide MS mechanism, CLIA-LDT status (Jan 2023, all US states except NY), no-coverage reimbursement, multiple myeloma indication, sensitivity-vs-SPEP/IFE multipliers, and the "79% in NGS-negative" detection rate all verified. DOI/PMID citations are real and on-point (CCR 2021 PMID 34210683; Haematologica 2024.285933). Main deductions: availability/regulatory notes predate the March 2026 Canada provisional license (stale), and a couple of performance fields (specificity 100, LOD framing) are framed in a way that is conceptually defensible but easy to misread as hard analytical metrics.

defects.issue: Does not reflect the March 2026 Ontario Ministry of Health provisional license allowing EasyM to be offered by prescription in Canada; availability framing is stale as of June 2026.

specificity: 100 cited to CCR 2021 reflects the patient-specific clonotypic design rather than a measured analytical specificity from a defined negative cohort; risks being read as a hard validated metric.
'1×10⁻⁵ g/dL' is described in notes as an LOQ, not a formally determined LOD; the lod field value is therefore an approximation labeled as LOD.
totalParticipants: 500 is not tied to a specific citation; validationCohortSize is only 26 (MCRN-001). The 500 figure is unverified and may aggregate across studies inconsistently.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: No single citation supports 500 participants; the named validation cohort is 26 (MCRN-001) and the Canadian PMCC/NDMM study enrolled ~70. The 500 figure is unsourced aggregation.
Lead-time figure conflates median/typical lead time with extreme outliers (38 months ≈ 1140 days). The 300-day point value is not clearly tied to a reported median in the cited CCR 2021 paper.
Represents design-level patient specificity, not a measured specificity from a negative-control cohort.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Canada: provisional clinical license granted March 2026 by the Ontario Ministry of Health, permitting EasyM to be offered by prescription to patients and clinicians in Canada.
Princess Margaret Cancer Centre (PMCC) Canadian prospective NDMM study completed enrollment April 2026 (~70 patients).
Independent comparison of EasyM clonotypic MS vs EuroFlow MRD assessment published in Haematologica (2024).

summary: EasyM (Rapid Novor) is accurately captured and still offered: a tumor-naive clonotypic-peptide mass-spec MRD test for multiple myeloma, CLIA-LDT, not FDA-cleared, not reimbursed. The record is current and well-cited; main weaknesses are a stale Canada-availability note (March 2026 provisional license missing), an unsourced 500-participant figure, and a few performance fields (specificity 100, LOD) framed as hard metrics when they are design-level/LOQ values.

name: K-4CARE
qualityRationale: Record is accurate, current, and well-sourced against the real peer-reviewed validation paper (PMID 38404964 / DOI 10.3389/fmolb.2024.1334808). Single non-duplicated id. Sensitivity (79% ctDNA detection), specificity (>99%), tumor-informed approach, tumor-tissue and matched-normal requirements, and US-unavailable status (Pangea collaboration announced 2026-01-21) all verified. Score held below ~80 by: a method-description claim ('bespoke multiplex PCR') not supported by the paper; missing LOD and CGP panel gene count (473 genes / 1.7 Mb) that are publicly available; and an MRD sensitivity figure (79%) that is a CGP-informed ctDNA detection rate, not a true post-surgical MRD clinical sensitivity, which is potentially misleading as labeled.

defects.issue: Claims MRD tracking uses 'bespoke multiplex PCR with ultra-deep sequencing (100,000X)'. The validation paper (DOI 10.3389/fmolb.2024.1334808) describes a 473-gene / 1.7 Mb CGP NGS panel from which top-ranked tumor-derived somatic mutations are used to track ctDNA in plasma; it does not describe a bespoke multiplex-PCR personalized panel (Signatera-style mPCR). The mPCR and 100,000X depth claims are unverifiable against the cited source.

79% is labeled as MRD sensitivity but in the source it is the 'detection rate of ctDNA in liquid biopsy' when CGP-informed mutations were used — an analytical/concordance detection rate, not a clinical post-surgical MRD sensitivity. Clinical utility was shown only in 2 case studies. Presenting 79% as sensitivity overstates clinical validation.
No LOD field despite the paper reporting LOD for SNV/indel/amplification/fusion detection; a key MRD parameter is absent though publicly available.
Omits the publicly documented CGP panel size (473 cancer-relevant genes, 1.7 Mb), which is central to how the personalized MRD targets are selected.

Listed as 1 (the Frontiers validation paper). A medRxiv preprint and conference materials (K-CONNECT APAC 2025) exist; count is defensibly conservative but the single peer-reviewed paper is the only validation evidence, underscoring thin clinical backing.

suspectClaims.currentValue: CGP-informed personalized ctDNA tracking using bespoke multiplex PCR with ultra-deep sequencing (100,000X)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Cited paper describes NGS-panel-based tracking of top-ranked somatic mutations from a 473-gene CGP panel, not bespoke multiplex PCR; '100,000X' depth and 'multiplex PCR' are not stated in the source.
Source defines this as the ctDNA detection rate in liquid biopsy using CGP-informed mutations, not a clinical MRD sensitivity; clinical utility limited to 2 case studies.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Lung, Colorectal, Breast, Liver, Gastric, Ovarian (6)
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Paper benchmarked CGP across 10 cancer types and applied ctDNA tracking in 'selected cancers' without enumerating exactly these 6; the specific 6-type MRD list is not clearly traceable to the cited source.

proposedFills.proposedValue: Comprehensive genomic profiling via a 473-gene (1.7 Mb) targeted NGS panel; top-ranked tumor-derived somatic mutations identified by the panel are then tracked in plasma to detect ctDNA for residual-disease surveillance.
473 cancer-relevant genes (1.7 Mb)

79% refers to the ctDNA detection rate in plasma when CGP-informed somatic mutations were used for personalized tracking; CGP analytic sensitivity was >99% for SNV/indel and amplification, 94% for fusion. Clinical MRD utility demonstrated in 2 case studies only.

Jan 21, 2026: Gene Solutions and Pangea Laboratory (Brea, CA) announced a strategic collaboration to validate Gene Solutions' ctDNA LDTs (including K-4CARE) in CLIA/CAP settings for US offering; no US launch date confirmed.

summary: Accurate, current, single-instance record for Gene Solutions' K-4CARE, a Vietnam/Singapore CGP+personalized-ctDNA-MRD test not yet available in the US (Pangea validation collaboration announced Jan 2026), well-anchored to a real Frontiers validation paper. Main weaknesses: the MRD method description (bespoke multiplex PCR / 100,000X) is unsupported by the source, the 79% figure is a ctDNA detection rate mislabeled as clinical sensitivity, and publicly available details (473-gene panel, LOD) are omitted.

name: CatCHimera
qualityRationale: Record is accurate and current. Every load-bearing claim — tumor-informed HBV viral-integration (vh-DNA) approach via capture-NGS + ddPCR, 95.8% sensitivity / 95.5% specificity (with AFP+PIVKA-II), 158-day mean lead time over CT, ~1.5 cm detection limit, NPV 92% / PPV 71%, 98% pre-surgical detection, FDA Breakthrough Device Designation (April 2026), TFDA LDT in Taiwan, developed with Pei-Jer Chen / NTU — is corroborated by independent sources (GenomeWeb, PR Newswire, CLP Mag) and traces to genuine peer-reviewed literature (Hepatology 2020, PMID 32171027; AASLD 2023 abstract). The main weakness is citation thinness: nearly every field cites a single biospace press release rather than the primary publications that actually exist, and several deterministic fields (LOD in conventional ppm/%VAF terms, turnaround time, gene/integration-site panel detail) are absent or expressed unconventionally. Completeness 0.57 reflects these gaps, but the populated data is correct.

defects.issue: All performance figures cite only a single biospace.com press release, even though the underlying numbers come from a real peer-reviewed source (Cell-Free Virus-Host Chimera DNA, Hepatology 2020, PMID 32171027) and an AASLD 2023 abstract. Primary sources should be cited directly.

LOD expressed as '~1.5 cm tumor' rather than a molecular detection limit (ppm/%VAF/copies). This is a tumor-size correlate from the Hepatology paper, not a conventional analytical LOD; field is technically populated but not comparable to peer MRD assays.

Set to 'No', but the assay is tumor-informed: it derives patient-specific HBV integration junctions, which in the validation study were determined from tumor tissue by capture-NGS. The note explains the plasma-based approach, but 'No' may overstate that no tissue is ever needed to define the personalized targets.
No turnaround time provided (field absent); not publicly disclosed by vendor.

suspectClaims.whySuspect: Tumor-informed assays typically require tumor tissue to define patient-specific targets; the pivotal Hepatology 2020 study determined vh-DNA integration sites in tumor tissue by capture-NGS in 148 patients. Whether the commercial workflow defines targets from plasma alone is unconfirmed.

Accurate but context-dependent: the 95.8% sensitivity is for recurrent HCC only when vh-DNA is COMBINED with AFP + PIVKA-II, not vh-DNA alone. Stored as a flat assay sensitivity, which could mislead cross-test comparison (note field does clarify).

proposedFills.proposedValue: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32171027/
proposedFills.source: Wang T-H et al. Cell-Free Virus-Host Chimera DNA From Hepatitis B Virus Integration Sites as a Circulating Biomarker of Hepatocellular Cancer. Hepatology. 2020;72(6):2063. PMID 32171027

proposedFills.proposedValue: Preoperative plasma vh-DNA concentration correlated with tumor size (r=0.73), with a detection limit corresponding to ~1.5 cm tumor; smallest recurrent HCC with detectable circulating vh-DNA was 0.8 cm.
Among 113 patients with postoperative follow-up, vh-DNA PPV for recurrence 71% (17/24), NPV 92% (82/89); combined vh-DNA + AFP + PIVKA-II gave 95.8% sensitivity / 95.5% specificity for recurrent HCC.
NCT05823584 — Cell-free DNA From Junction of Hepatitis B Virus Integration in HCC Patients for Monitoring Post-resection Recurrence
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9139492/ (Circulating Virus-Host Chimera DNAs in the Clinical Monitoring of Virus-Related Cancers) and PMID 32171027

summary: Accurate, well-corroborated record for CatCHimera, a tumor-informed HBV viral-integration (vh-DNA) MRD liquid biopsy for HBV-related HCC that received FDA Breakthrough Device Designation in April 2026 and operates as a TFDA LDT in Taiwan. All performance claims trace to real peer-reviewed data (Hepatology 2020, PMID 32171027; AASLD 2023); the main weakness is that nearly every field cites one press release instead of the available primary literature, plus a non-standard LOD and missing turnaround time.

name: Quest Flow Cytometry MRD Blood Test
qualityRationale: All present facts are accurate and verified against Quest's official press release (Feb 2, 2026) and clinical FAQ328: next-generation flow cytometry, peripheral blood (no bone marrow biopsy), no baseline sample required, 5-day specimen stability, comparable sensitivity to NGS, CLIA LDT. Nothing factually wrong, but record is thin (completeness 0.34): missing publicly available LOD (5x10^-6), sample volume (2x5 mL), TAT (24-72 hrs), test code (38287), and antibody panel. Headline problem is structural: id 'mrd-30' is shared with a different record (Myriad 'Precise MRD'), a critical data-integrity defect. Minor name mismatch vs vendor's official 'Quest Flow Cytometry MRD for Myeloma'.

defects.issue: Duplicate id: 'mrd-30' is used by TWO different records in mrd.json — this Quest test (line 5676) AND Myriad Genetics 'Precise MRD' (line 4843). Duplicate primary keys break lookups/dedup/MCP retrieval. One must be reassigned a unique id.
Record name 'Quest Flow Cytometry MRD Blood Test' does not exactly match vendor's official product name 'Quest Flow Cytometry MRD for Myeloma' (Feb 2, 2026 release / FAQ328).
LOD/sensitivity is null. Quest publicly reports analytical sensitivity of 5x10^-6 with a single tube of blood — a core MRD spec and sourceable.
Turnaround time is null. Quest FAQ328 states results in 24-72 hours; coverage cites 3-5 day TAT. Sourceable.
Sample volume null. FAQ328 specifies 2 x 5 mL whole blood in sodium heparin (green-top) tubes (10 mL total). Sourceable.
No CPT/test code. Quest test code 38287 published on FAQ328 (CPT not stated).
Record carries no source citation fields despite well-documented Quest press release and FAQ being available.

suspectClaims.currentValue: Reported as sensitive as next-generation sequencing for myeloma MRD detection.
suspectClaims.whySuspect: Accurate as a vendor claim, but Quest's quantitative LOD is 5x10^-6 whereas best NGS (clonoSEQ) reaches 10^-6 — NGS is ~10x more sensitive. 'Comparable' is marketing framing presented without the quantitative caveat.
Defensible, but the test uses a fixed aberrant-plasma-cell immunophenotype panel rather than a patient-specific clone; the immunophenotyping nuance is unstated.

proposedFills.proposedValue: 24-72 hours (3-5 days)
10 mL (2 x 5 mL whole blood, sodium heparin green-top tubes)

Next-generation multiparameter flow cytometry using an 11-marker panel (CD19, CD20, CD27, CD38, CD45, CD56, CD81, CD117, CD138, CD200, CD229) to detect aberrant plasma-cell immunophenotypes in peripheral blood; sensitivity 5x10^-6; requires no baseline/pre-treatment sample.
Quest Flow Cytometry MRD for Myeloma
Available at approximately 7,000 Quest Diagnostics locations across the US; launched February 2, 2026.
Quest test code 38287

summary: The Quest Flow Cytometry MRD blood test record is factually accurate but thin — it omits publicly available LOD (5x10^-6), TAT (24-72 hrs), sample volume (2x5 mL), and the 11-marker panel from Quest's own FAQ and Feb 2026 launch release. The critical defect is structural: id 'mrd-30' is duplicated, also assigned to Myriad's 'Precise MRD' record, and must be reassigned.
